name: CeraVe Foaming Facial Cleanser
ingredients: AQUA / WATER / EAU, COCAMIDOPROPYL HYDROXYSULTAINE, GLYCERIN, SODIUM LAUROYL SARCOSINATE, PEG-150 PENTAERYTHRITYL TETRASTEARATE, NIACINAMIDE, PEG-6 CAPRYLIC/CAPRIC GLYCERIDES, SODIUM METHYL COCOYL TAURATE, PROPYLENE GLYCOL, CERAMIDE NP, CERAMIDE AP, CERAMIDE EOP, CARBOMER, METHYLPARABEN, SODIUM CHLORIDE, SODIUM LAUROYL LACTYLATE, CHOLESTEROL, DISODIUM EDTA, PROPYLPARABEN, CITRIC ACID, TETRASODIUM EDTA, HYDROLYZED HYALURONIC ACID, PHYTOSPHINGOSINE, XANTHAN GUM

couche3.note: efficace peau grasse, doux; moussant → prudence très sèche
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this facial cleanser effective, particularly for oily skin, and appreciate that it doesn't dry out their skin while leaving it smooth and hydrated. Moreover, the product is gentle, removes makeup well, and offers good value for money, with one customer noting it's a big savings compared to CVS or Walgreens. Additionally, they like that it's fragrance-free and perfect for sensitive skin. However, opinions about the foaming action are mixed, with some saying it foams up nicely while others report it doesn't produce any foam at all.

reviews.author: Teethnclaws
reviews.text:
I purchased a 3oz bottle of this from my local store, which lasted a long time by the way. I decided I loved it enough to order the larger value size.





I have experimented with different washes for years, different acne remedies for years, and never quite got 100% perfect skin for my trouble. I've spent thousands (no exaggeration) on washes, skincare, treatments, doctors. I've tinkered with YT suggestions, DIY potions, all natural, multi-step kits, suggested regimens like 5 or 10 step Korean skincare. I've eliminated foods from my diet, attempted to regulate hormones, etc. None of it did much to improve my overall complexion. Recently, I started struggling with blackheads and unflattering texture along my jawline alongside my acne and needed to change it up.





After watching Dr. Dray on YT (great dermatologist out of TX who promotes a less is more approach to skincare), I purged everything that had, especially anything with superfluous fragrance and dyes and went back to basics. Per her suggestion, I had been using the CeraVe hydrating cleansers. I have sensitive skin and while I'm oily in places, I've heard hydrating cleansers are ideal overall. The hydrating version did nothing for my oil, the cause of my blackheads. I'm sure it would be fine for someone who has dry skin, but I needed something to cut the oil without over-drying. This foaming version of the same brand's cleanser breaks down the excess oil without overdrying the face and works well as a second step if you do the two-step oil cleansing method (which I do to remove sunscreen and makeup). It's fragrance-free, dye-free, pH balanced, loaded with ceramides, etc..you know the drill. All the good, little to no bad as far as I can tell.





Ultimately, I've found [for me] I have to wash morning and night. None of this, splashing a little warm water on my face in the morning and you only have to wash once. It is not enough for someone with skin that likes to lean on the oily side. It's just not. Under or overwashing can both lead to other issues. So, for me 2x is plenty. I use this fantastic foaming cleanser both times, all over my face. As part of my routine, I use The Ordinary's SA 2% (only on the acne prone areas), brush my teeth to give it time to soak in, then apply their Niacinamide 10%/zinc serum. The combination of this wash, the SA, and Niacinamide has cut my oiliness considerably without drying me out. I don't feel like an oil slick mid-day. Still, I top it off with a little of the CeraVe regular moisturizer, CeraVe Daily Moisturizing Lotion with Hyaluronic Acid and Ceramides for Normal to Dry Skin. I don't need a lot, but everyone needs moisturizer, & then I have a great water-gel sunscreen. I use a 30 to 50spf daily on my face, neck, chest, and ears.





PM - I  double-cleanse the spf and makeup off using this wash as my 2nd step after an oil-based remover, and after I've gently wiped away the excess balm or oil with a fragrance-free baby wipe or tissue. No point in struggling to remove everything with a wash alone. I don't expect any single wash to remove my spf and makeup and I'm pretty minimalist. So if you love smearing copious amounts of foundations, powders, etc. on your face, you really should try to remove as much of it with removers or an oil-cleanse before you try to wash it with this. I use this wash, SA 2% (again only on the acne prone areas), the Niacinamide, the CeraVe moisturizer and I'm done.





I've been doing this routine for a few weeks now and the blackheads are disappearing, the hyperpigmentation is vastly improving (fading), no new acne has appeared, and the uneven texture along my jawline is almost gone. The only difference in recent months has been swapping out a hydrating wash for this foaming one for my combo, mature, sensitive skin. I can't say enough good things about it: inexpensive, effective, non-drying, not harsh, etc. I wish I had gone to this sooner, but many of us with serious skin issues are often fooled into thinking we have to pay a fortune and pile on the products in order to get great results. My advice, strip everything to the basics and build from there. I'm so pleased with this wash. I hope they don't do something stupid like reformulate it for some reason. Best to leave a great thing alone.

reviews.author: Kyle Utterback
reviews.text: I have searched for a long time to find the right facial cleanser for my skin. My skin is very sensitive, tends to be oily, and many cleansers either dry it out, cause redness, or leave my face feeling irritated and uncomfortable. This cleanser has been a completely different experience. It makes my face feel incredibly clean and soft without ever drying it out. I've had no irritation, redness, or burning at all. My skin always feels refreshed and comfortable after using it. I also started using the CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion, and I really like the combination of the two products. Together, they leave my skin feeling healthy, balanced, and well-moisturized without feeling greasy.

reviews.author: Medi
reviews.text:
I’ve been using this cleanser for a few weeks now, and it has become a staple in my daily routine. I have combination-to-oily skin, and finding a cleanser that actually removes excess oil without leaving my face feeling tight and dry is a challenge. This one hits the perfect balance.





The gel texture lathers up into a nice, lightweight foam that cuts right through daily sunscreen and light makeup. Because it’s packed with ceramides, hyaluronic acid, and niacinamide, my skin barrier feels supported rather than completely stripped after a wash. It’s also completely fragrance-free, which is great if your skin is easily irritated.

reviews.author: Chenyu Li
reviews.text: Been using this CeraVe foaming cleanser daily for months and my skin has never looked better. It removes oil and buildup without that tight, stripped feeling afterward. The formula with ceramides and niacinamide actually leaves my skin feeling smooth and hydrated. Fragrance free is a big plus — no irritation at all. The 16 oz bottle lasts forever since you only need a small amount. Dermatologist recommended for a reason.

reviews.author: Ale
reviews.text:
Only cleanser I’ve used since I found a routine for my skin. No other cleansers has touched my face.


Love that it’s fragrance free.


After one wash skin looks great and feels great. Non drying however Skin can dry out if you over wash.


Pump functions great.


Because it’s non comedogenic it does well with opening pores to recieve moisturizer serums and toners.

couche3.note: démaquillant doux hydratant, top sèche/sensible; nettoyage mitigé
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the cleanser effective at removing makeup and appreciate its gentle, creamy texture that feels like rubbing butter on the face, leaving skin feeling fresh and hydrated. The cleansing performance and value for money receive mixed reviews - while some praise its effectiveness and consider it excellent value, others find it not worth the price. The scent receives mixed feedback, with some noting it has zero scent while others find it bad-smelling, and several customers report receiving containers that are less than half full.

reviews.author: Camille Crocker
reviews.text: Okay, I bought this on a whim. I have pretty severe sebaceous hyperplasia. I have had chemical peels.  I’ve had them burned off, frozen, I’ve used topical retin A from my dermatologist. It always rages back. I saw a review say this stuff shrinks them and makes them much less visible. I was put off by the price, but let’s face it, I’ve dropped a lot more than this.My first wash,  I did not use anything to remove my make up. I wanted to see if this got rid of it by what was left when I used my toner. Initially, I thought this stuff would be greasy. IT IS NOT! It COMPLETELY MELTED MY MAKE UP AWAY! I have used it daily for about two weeks. It is unlike anything I have ever used. It leaves my face clean, but not dry. It isn’t oily either, like some make up removers that then require soap and water. I have purchased most of the face cleaners in Ulta. I have never, in my oily 57 years, used anything like this. The price point is amazing considering what it does. It even removed my waterproof mascara???? I don’t know what is in this, and frankly I do not care. It’s hot where I live, and my SH is not growing. My skin feels clean. It’s not dry. It’s not greasy. I write reviews only occasionally, but I want others who have struggled with oily skin issues for years to know there’s something out there for us. I can’t speak to dry skin, but it feels extremely gentle. Am I rid of my excess oil completely? No, but I have other solutions in my toolbox for that. I will do without other things to buy this again and again. I love other products from The Ordinary, but THIS one is the Holy Grail. I still have bumps from SH. However, they are almost flat, and much less noticeable. For my fellow sufferers out there, this helped me. They’re no longer the first thing I see when I look in the mirror. I don’t feel like people see them and wonder what the heck is on my face. Many thanks to The Ordinary for a decently priced, effective product that does what it says.

reviews.author: Destiny
reviews.text:
I've been really happy with this squalane cleanser as a makeup-removing balm. It does a great job breaking down makeup and sunscreen without needing a lot of product or excessive rubbing. Everything melts away pretty easily, which makes taking off my makeup at the end of the day much less of a chore.





One thing I especially like is using it in the shower. It's convenient to apply, rinse off, and continue the rest of my skincare routine all in one place. For an even deeper cleanse, I like to follow it up with The Ordinary Glucoside Foaming Cleanser. The combination works really well for me and helps ensure that all of my makeup is completely removed.





Another plus is that it doesn't irritate my skin at all. My skin feels clean and comfortable afterward rather than stripped or tight. It also has a very neutral scent, which I appreciate since I don't enjoy heavily fragranced skincare products.





Overall, this is a simple but effective cleansing balm that does exactly what I need it to do. If you're looking for a gentle makeup remover that gets the job done without irritating your skin, I'd definitely recommend giving this one a try!

reviews.author: Jagdeep Batth
reviews.text:
Pros:


• Very gentle on the skin and does not leave it dry.


• Melts makeup and sunscreen easily when rubbed between hands.


• Leaves skin soft and hydrated after washing.





Cons:


• Does not foam like traditional cleansers.


• Texture feels thick until you warm it in your hands.


• Heavy waterproof makeup may need a second cleanse.





Overall, a great gentle cleanser that cleans well while keeping skin hydrated.





ਇਹ ਕਲੀਨਜ਼ਰ ਬਹੁਤ ਹੀ ਨਰਮ ਹੈ ਅਤੇ ਚਿਹਰੇ ਨੂੰ ਸੁੱਕਾ ਨਹੀਂ ਕਰਦਾ। ਮੇਕਅਪ ਅਤੇ ਸਨਸਕ੍ਰੀਨ ਨੂੰ ਅਸਾਨੀ ਨਾਲ ਹਟਾ ਦਿੰਦਾ ਹੈ ਅਤੇ ਸਕਿਨ ਨੂੰ ਨਰਮ ਅਤੇ ਮੋਇਸ਼ਚਰਾਈਜ਼ਡ ਛੱਡਦਾ ਹੈ।

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text: This is kind of magical to me. It starts as a balm, becomes an oil when you emulsify it in your hands, then spreads beautifully as you massage it onto your face, dissolving EVERYTHING, including sunscreen and waterproof mascara, and then rinses off with zero residue, with your barrier 100% intact and your skin not depleted. Squalane is much like skins sebum which was a brilliant choice to use as the base instead of all the plant or synthetic oils used in all the other oil cleansers and balms.

reviews.author: Rae
reviews.text: I wouldn’t say it’s the best but it gets the job done after two washes. Not really good for waterproof mascara. Skin is left feeling soft.

name: Cetaphil Hydrating Gentle Skin Cleanser
ingredients: Water, Glycerin, Cetearyl Alcohol, Panthenol, Niacinamide, Pantolactone, Xanthan Gum, Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate, Sodium Benzoate, Citric Acid

couche3.note: doux sans parfum, top sensible/sèche
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this facial cleanser effective and gentle on skin, particularly noting its suitability for sensitive skin and its non-irritating properties. They appreciate its creamy texture that feels like silk, its fragrance-free formula, and consider the 20oz size a good value. While some customers report their skin looks healthy after use, others mention it can cause redness and rashes. The product receives mixed feedback regarding makeup removal, with some saying it just wipes off while others find it doesn't remove makeup well.

reviews.author: Alyssa Stephenson
reviews.text: OMG I'm amazed at how wonderful this is...My skin is very sensitive and I am currently repairing my skin barrier from another product stripping it badly. Clinique makes it...its all about clean foaming facial cleanser for very dry to dry skin and it's awful. This stuff is really great for sensitive skin and fragrance free and has no harsh chemicals in it. I love it so much, I bought another bottle for later so I am not going to run out anytime soon. You only have to use a little bit to clean your face and it's totally worth the money. It'll take awhile for me to use it up...its very affordable and I highly recommend it. Its great for my skin barrier and care. My skin feels so soft and hydrated after cleaning. Its not meant to lather up and you need to read the directions. You wet face and hands and put a tiny bit.. maybe a nickel size amount, in the hands and massage it on the face. Wipe clean with a soft cloth and don't use HOT water. Lukewarm is better, and pat dry with a towel gently. Apply moisturizer if you want. Cetaphil moisturizing lotion is awesome.

reviews.author: Stina
reviews.text: The ingredients are clean and simple and it doesn't strip my skin. I tend to have dehydrated skin that feels dry even washing with just water. I sometimes get an itchy/tight feeling with sodium cocoyl isethionate but the tiny amount in this is fine. The only thing is it doesn't always thoroughly clean my face even if I use it twice. It struggles getting zinc sunscreen off even if the sunscreen is not waterproof. To remove dead skin you sometimes have to scrub with a washcloth or something. It seems like with cleansers you have to pick between mild and effective. Or maybe I need to oil cleanse first. Either way, I liked this enough to repurchase it. It's pretty cheap. I tend to get clogged pores and I don't feel like this makes them any worse. It is a good everyday cleanser that won't bug my skin.

reviews.author: Chuck
reviews.text: I just received this face wash today.  I immediately used it in the shower.  I love Cetaphil products for my face because my eyes are extremely sensitive to so many chemicals and scents and Cetaphil does not irritate my eyes at all.  This face wash doesn't lather like a traditional face wash.  It is creamy and you massage it into the skin and it rinses off nicely and leaves my skin feeling soft and clean.  It comes in an easy to use pump bottle that is convenient to use in the shower.  The size is very good for the price.  It isn't a thick wash...it is sort of a thin lotion with a creamy off-white color.  No fragrance that I can detect.  Just great for washing my skin gently and not irritating my eyes!

reviews.author: Linda T.
reviews.text:
A Dermatologist told me years ago that CETAPHIL gentle cleanseR can be use on your private areas  “WITHOUT having to rinse it off!” Just cleanse, pat dry, and your good to go!!


It’s emollient, gentle on delicate skin, non irritating, scent free, and gets the job done!!


Comes in an assortment of sizes-small purse size or travel size too so you’ll always be ready to cleanse properly when needed at home or out. No Bidet needed!!!


It’s in every bathroom of mine near the toilet paper roll for use by all… love the pump bottle!

reviews.author: Dani | SAHM
reviews.text: I’ve really been liking this Cetaphil Hydrating Gentle Skin Cleanser. It leaves my skin feeling clean and refreshed without that tight, dry feeling some face washes cause. It’s super gentle, doesn’t irritate my skin, and works great for everyday use, especially if you have sensitive or dry skin. The creamy texture feels nice and it removes dirt and oil without stripping moisture. A little goes a long way, and the bottle lasts a while too. Definitely a staple in my skincare routine!

name: CeraVe Hydrating Facial Cleanser
ingredients: AQUA / WATER / EAU, GLYCERIN, CETEARYL ALCOHOL, PEG-40 STEARATE, STEARYL ALCOHOL, POTASSIUM PHOSPHATE, CERAMIDE NP, CERAMIDE AP, CERAMIDE EOP, CARBOMER, GLYCERYL STEARATE, BEHENTRIMONIUM METHOSULFATE, SODIUM LAUROYL LACTYLATE, SODIUM HYALURONATE, CHOLESTEROL, PHENOXYETHANOL, DISODIUM EDTA, DIPOTASSIUM PHOSPHATE, TOCOPHEROL, PHYTOSPHINGOSINE, XANTHAN GUM, CETYL ALCOHOL, POLYSORBATE 20, ETHYLHEXYLGLYCERIN

couche3.note: hydratant doux, sensible/sèche, sans parfum
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this facial cleanser effective and moisturizing, leaving their skin feeling clean and hydrated with a soft, smooth texture. The product is gentle on sensitive and dry skin, and customers appreciate that it's fragrance-free. While some customers report that it removes all makeup easily, others say it doesn't work well for this purpose. The skin sensitivity aspect receives mixed feedback, with some customers reporting clearer skin while others experienced breakouts.

reviews.author: Yuliia Kuznetsova
reviews.text:
I’ve been using the CeraVe Hydrating Facial Cleanser for almost half a year now, and it has honestly become a staple in my skincare routine. My skin tends to be dry and sensitive, and this cleanser keeps it feeling soft, clean, and comfortable without drying it out.





The formula is very gentle and creamy, and I love that it contains hyaluronic acid, ceramides, and glycerin. After washing my face, my skin feels hydrated and calm instead of tight or irritated. It’s also fragrance-free, which is a huge plus for sensitive skin.





Over time, I’ve noticed my skin barrier feels healthier and less reactive, especially during colder or drier weather. It works perfectly for daily use and pairs well with other skincare products without causing breakouts.





I also appreciate that it’s certified by the National Eczema Association, which gave me more confidence trying it long term. Overall, this is one of the best gentle cleansers I’ve used, and I’ll definitely continue repurchasing it.

reviews.author: Don L
reviews.text: I picked this up after my dermatologist recommended CeraVe for my dry, eczema-prone skin and I'm pretty happy with it. It cleanses gently without stripping any moisture and my skin feels hydrated and calm after every wash — no tightness, no irritation. The hyaluronic acid and ceramides combo is clearly doing something right. That said, if you're switching over from the CeraVe Renewing SA Cleanser like I did, be prepared for a noticeably different texture. The SA Cleanser has a lighter, more gel-like feel whereas this one is significantly richer and has more of an oily texture that can feel a bit heavy, especially if you have combination skin or tend to get oily in your T-zone. For strictly dry skin it's probably perfect, but it took some getting used to for me personally. Still a solid cleanser backed by good ingredients and the National Eczema Association certification gives it extra credibility. Just know what you're getting into texture-wise before making the switch.

reviews.author: Erika
reviews.text: I have very sensitive and eczema prone skin and this face wash is the only one that I will use. It is so gentle and it works so well on my skin to remove makeup and anything else. It doesn’t really foam, but it lathers on nicely and a little bit goes a long way. It is really gentle and moisturize my face. It’s not greasy at all and it doesn’t leave any residue behind. Recently, I’ve been wearing a lot of sunscreen went outside with my toddler and I am so impressed by how well it removes that. The bottle is big and lasts a very long time making it such a great value. This is my favorite face wash and I will be using it for many years to come.

reviews.author: Danileidy Alvarez Monzon
reviews.text: This facial cleanser works very well and leaves my skin feeling clean, soft, and hydrated. It has a gentle formula that does not irritate my skin or leave it feeling dry or tight after washing. It removes dirt and excess oil effectively while maintaining the skin's natural moisture. The product is fragrance-free and suitable for daily use. I would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a mild and effective facial cleanser.

reviews.author: Ikram
reviews.text: I absolutely love the CeraVe Hydrating Facial Cleanser. It is incredibly gentle on the skin, leaving it feeling deeply moisturized and clean without that tight, stripped feeling. The texture is wonderfully smooth and perfect for normal to dry skin types. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a reliable, dermatologist-loved daily cleanser!

name: Vanicream Gentle Facial Cleanser
ingredients: Purified Water, Glycerin, Coco Glucoside, Sodium Cocoyl Glycinate, Acrylates Copolymer, Caprylyl Glycol, Mica, Sodium Chloride, 1,2-Hexanediol, Titanium Dioxide, Sodium Hydroxide, Disodium EDTA

couche3.note: doux sans parfum, bon sèche, zéro irritation
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this facial cleanser effective at removing makeup and appreciate its moisturizing properties, particularly noting it's good for dry skin and doesn't cause irritation. The product is gentle on the skin, leaving it feeling soft and clean, and customers like that it's fragrance-free. They report positive results for skin sensitivity, reducing redness and breakouts, and consider it good value for money, describing it as a wonderful face wash for the price.

reviews.author: lr21
reviews.text:
I have very delicate skin that is prone to acne and eczema. I also have a lot of allergies that prevent me from using most  skin products so I was pretty nervous about trying a new one, but I gave it a chance.





The first couple of days I noticed some itchiness in my skin, but it was effectively clearing dead skin and reducing whiteheads so I kept using it and the itching went away.





My skin does feel a bit dry after using it, and it probably would be better alongside the vanicream moisturizer; however, I try to only start new products one at a time so that I can easily pin point things that may be bothering me.





As far as pricing, I think it's pretty affordable considering it comes in a fairly large bottle and you only have to use a pea-sized amount each wash.





It also doesn't leave any weird films or grease, and doesn't seem to have any weird smells.





Altogether, really like the product over the week or two that I have been using it, and my skin looks a lot cleaner and more evenly textured.

reviews.author: Claire
reviews.text:
I've tried a lot of facial cleansers over the years, and this one has become a staple in my routine. The fragrance-free formula is gentle enough for my sensitive skin and cleans thoroughly without leaving my face feeling greasy, tight, or stripped afterward.





One of the things I appreciate most is how soft and smooth my skin feels after washing. It removes leftover makeup surprisingly well, even the bits I occasionally miss during makeup removal, while still being gentle enough for daily use.


I've also noticed an improvement in occasional breakouts since incorporating it into my routine.





The pump dispenser deserves a mention as well. It dispenses the perfect amount of cleanser, and unlike many pump bottles I've used, it doesn't seem to clog or become difficult to use over time.





Overall, this is an effective, gentle cleanser that works well for sensitive skin, leaves my face feeling clean and comfortable, and has become one of the easiest skincare products for me to consistently repurchase.

reviews.author: kbhatia
reviews.text:
So far so good. My dermatologist recommended this brand as a daily skincare routine, and I think I’ll be using it from now on. I had been using CeraVe for quite some time, and it seemed to work okay, but I like the lather VANICREAM provides. It results in a cleaner feel even if it is a placebo effect. I also prefer the irritant-free formula just because it puts my mind at ease using something without unnecessary ingredients, not because I absolutely need it.





Side note: My doc recommended this during a perioral dermatitis flare-up caused by medication. The CeraVe was potentially irritating my skin even more at the time. Otherwise, I would’ve just continued using the CeraVe. This appears to be good for extremely sensitive skin, including dermatitis and acne flares.

reviews.author: EJ
reviews.text: This cleanser is great for my acne prone skin. Its great for sensitive skin since its so gentle and fragrance free. I've gone through 3 bottles and keep coming back to it. My skin tends to feel very dry after washing my face with certain cleansers but not with this one! This stuff works great!

reviews.author: Carrie Dee
reviews.text:
Fantastic for my sensitive, acne prone aging skin.


It is dramatically improving my skin barrier and has limited my skin congestion without causing skin irritation.


Completely fragrance free!!

name: Neutrogena Hydro Boost Cleansing Gel
ingredients: water, glycerin, cocamidopropyl hydroxysultaine, sodium cocoyl isethionate, sodium methyl cocoyl taurate, sodium chloride, sodium hydrolyzed potato starch dodecenylsuccinate, potassium acrylates copolymer, hydroxyacetophenone, polysorbate 20, phenoxyethanol, fragrance, linoleamidopropyl PG-dimonium chloride phosphate, sodium hydroxide, citric acid, polyquaternium-10, disodium EDTA, ethylhexylglycerin, hydrolyzed hyaluronic acid

couche3.note: hydratant doux quotidien, OK sensible; parfumé
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this facial cleanser effective at removing makeup while leaving skin moisturized, soft, and supple. The product is gentle enough for daily use, particularly suitable for sensitive skin, and customers report their skin looks healthy and clearer after use. They appreciate its pleasant scent and consider it comparable to more expensive cleansers, with one customer noting it's dermatologist-tested for sensitive skin.

reviews.author: Mel83
reviews.text:
To be honest, I was always the type of person who didn't put any thought into "skincare" during my younger years (even though I have had a lifelong addiction to cosmetics). In my mid-30s I finally started developing a skincare routine with various serums, moisturizers, masks, etc but still didn't think too much of cleansers...in my mind, something that just sat on top of wet skin for a few seconds only to be washed off again just didn't seem like it was an important part of my skincare routine because the cleanser isn't kept on the skin for very long. I basically lived on makeup removal wipes for my entire 20s and 30s, which I now know aren't so great for skin. Hindsight is 20/20.





About 6 months ago, shortly before I turned 39, my lifelong "very oily" skin suddenly turned dry. Like, almost overnight. I have always had a "grease pit" for a face, with large pores and very oily t-zone as well as horrendously oily cheeks - I never used a moisturizer previously because my skin was so oily all the time. Now, my skin suddenly was parched and tight feeling and had flakey patches that just wouldn't go away, it looked horribly embarrassing and made my foundation look gross. Looking back, I think I may have used too many facial masks or treatments (in an attempt to fade some dark spots and even out my skintone) and damaged my skin barrier and turned my skin "sensitive". I began to realize that the makeup removal wipes probably were drying my skin up even worse, and started to use actual cleansers instead of wipes.





I have an Ipsy subscription so I had a few brand new sample/travel sized cleansers sitting around under my bathroom cabinets. A few of them dried my skin up even worse, but 2 of them seemed to actually hydrate my skin after use...so at that point I realized that cleansers really DO make a difference in a skincare routine. The travel sized cleansers didn't last too long before running out and the full sized versions of those cleansers were pretty expensive and I'm on a tight budget, so I ventured out to find a less expensive hydrating (and sensitive skin friendly) cleanser. I have used several Hydro Boost products in the past and had a good experience - and I'm a sucker for the particular color of blue used for the Hydro Boost line - so I chose the hydrating cleanser to try. To my surprise, it hydrated my skin even better than the 2 more expensive brands I had previously used in travel size!





After using the Hydro Boost cleanser, my skin does not feel "tight" and dry (which is a problem I've had with many other cleansers). I still moisturize almost immediately but I do appreciate my skin not feeling uncomfortable between cleansing and moisturizing. After adding this cleanser to my new routine (which includes a few other skincare items from more expensive brands) my skin feels almost as good as new, my flakes have all vanished. The other new skincare items were starting to repair my skin, but it wasn't until I added the Hydro Boost cleanser to the routine that my skin fully seemed to be fully repaired. I skipped the Hydro Boost cleanser for a few days to see if it really made a difference and my skin became slightly dry again with a few flakes, so I knew at that point that this cleanser was really making a difference - it takes the tightness away, hydrates, and also seems to make my skin more smooth. I also love the scent!





The fragrance is a little strong so if you are very sensitive to such things you should probably get the fragrance free version of Hydro Boost, but I just love the scent personally, although it does seem to disappear immediately after the cleanser is washed off. I am going to stock up on this cleanser, and I really hope it is never discontinued.

reviews.author: JoJo
reviews.text: I've been searching for a quality face wash product that I would really love with ingredients I can trust and a smell I won't Hate!  I think it's finally this one.  I don't seem to have "sensitive" skin but do like to treat it with care now that I have finally figured out that I need to!  I've been lucky and still have good skin at my advanced age. What it would be like if I had treated it much more gently all my life and with much more care!  So though I may not need products specifically for sensitive skin, or unscented products, I do want to use things which have helpful properties.  I read up on which products that dermatologists recommend most.....a wide variety.  I have tried a few and some not on the list , and have not repurchased any of them.  I love this one so far.  I am not into wasting $$ and dropping it in the trash bin!  Some skin washes, serums, moisturizers and the like can cost quite a bit.  Like most women,  we want to look as well as we can for as long as we can.  And that definitely means caring for our skin.  Right?  Right!  So I am sticking with this delightful feeling and smelling face wash. As an aside, Neutrogena Rapid Wrinkle Repair regenerating cream works wonders too.  I have given these products some time to test for results and I am really happy with what I am noticing in the mirror.  I am noticing improvement in forehead lines, some of which were becoming a bit deep, and some lip lines and a couple starting under the end of my eyebrow.  I admit to an occasional day where I "fudge" a little on my complete 2x daily easy facial routine.  But the improvements are showing up to my own critical eye in about 60-90 days.  So I think it's really worth the effort!

reviews.author: PD
reviews.text: Great product, and cleans beautifully.  Leaves my face feeling good...however... I personally am allergic to fragrances...no perfumes.  I wasn't expecting the makers of this product to use perfumes or scents in their cleanser..  Guess I should have read the label more carefully before buying.  I use it, but the smell sends me in sneezing and coughing fits.  So it's just sitting there.  Sad, because I do love the way it cleans.  If you're not allergic or sensitive to scents, you'll probably do better than I did with it.

reviews.author: Elianna Feliz Segura
reviews.text:
The best face wash I’ve tried so far. It leaves your skin incredibly soft, clean, and fresh without that tight, dry feeling. What I love the most is the value  you get a great amount of product for the price, and a little goes a long way, so it lasts.





It helps remove dirt, oil, and makeup while still keeping your skin hydrated and healthy looking. After using it consistently, your face looks smoother, brighter, and more even. Perfect for daily use, and honestly… totally worth every penny.

reviews.author: Jkp
reviews.text: I was so excited to find this Tate McRae kit because my daughter loves her. The cleanser is perfect for her tween skin, it is gentle and moisturizing. Removes all her makeup with a nice foamy lather. She loves the texture of the wipes. The appearance of her skin is super glowy and soft. Love this!

couche3.note: top grasse/acné, ne décape pas
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this cleanser effective for oily and acne-prone skin, taking off makeup well without over-drying, and appreciate its gentle, baby-soft feel. The product foams up well and lasts a long time, with one customer noting it cleanses without stripping the skin. While some customers find it worth the price, others consider it overpriced. The fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some loving the scent while others find it awful.

reviews.author: Mar
reviews.text:
I’ve been using this liquid cleanser for a while now and it honestly exceeded my expectations. One single pump is enough to wash my entire face, even after long stressful days when my skin feels extra oily and heavy. The texture feels like a soft gel, it foams nicely without being too much, and it leaves my skin feeling genuinely clean without that tight, stripped feeling some cleansers give. It also has no fragrance at all, which I really appreciate because it doesn’t irritate my skin or feel harsh in any way.





What I like most is that it simply does its job perfectly. It removes oil, sweat, and daily buildup really well while still feeling gentle and comfortable on the skin. A little product goes a long way, so the bottle lasts much longer than I expected. After trying so many cleansers that either dry out my skin or barely clean it, this one feels like one of those underrated products people should talk about more.

reviews.author: Nelory fierro
reviews.text: I’ve been using La Roche-Posay Toleriane Purifying Foaming Cleanser and I absolutely love it! It leaves my skin feeling super clean and refreshed without making it feel dry or tight. The formula is gentle, lightweight, and perfect for sensitive skin. It also helps control oil while still keeping my face soft and hydrated. A little goes a long way, and it works great both morning and night. Definitely one of the best facial cleansers I’ve tried — highly recommend! 💙

reviews.author: Arianny Zambrano
reviews.text: am absolutely loving this La Roche-Posay Effaclar Purifying Foaming Gel! It is the perfect cleanser for my oily skin, as it effectively removes excess oil and impurities without leaving my skin feeling tight or dry. I appreciate that it has an optimized pH and a non-comedogenic formula, which keeps my complexion clear and refreshed. It has become a staple in my daily routine, and I highly recommend it to anyone struggling with oiliness

reviews.author: Rawan smadi
reviews.text: This cleanser works very well for my skin. It leaves my face feeling clean without making it feel tight or overly dry. It is gentle, easy to use, and a good choice for daily cleansing. I would recommend it.

reviews.author: Jennifer Plummer
reviews.text: It gently removes dirt, makeup and oil.  Doesn’t dry out my skin.

couche3.note: doux hydratant, sensible; démaquillage mitigé
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this face cleanser effective and gentle, leaving their face feeling clean and amazing. The product is moisturizing, doesn't dry skin, and is dermatologist-recommended, with a nearly scentless formula. Regarding makeup removal, while some say it thoroughly removes makeup, others report it doesn't work at all. Skin sensitivity experiences are mixed - while some say their skin looks better and feels wonderful, others report breakouts and burning sensations.

reviews.author: Amanda D.
reviews.text:
This is a really solid everyday cleanser, especially if you have dry, sensitive, or easily irritated skin. It feels gentle from the first use and does not leave my face feeling tight or stripped afterward.





The texture is more creamy than foamy, so it does not give that bubbly “deep clean” feeling, but that is actually what makes it so comfortable. It cleans without making my skin feel dry or irritated, which is a big plus if you wash your face morning and night.





In real-life use, I like this most for:





Morning cleansing when I want something gentle before moisturizer and sunscreen





Nighttime cleansing after a long day when my skin feels dry or sensitive





Cold weather use when harsher cleansers make my face feel tight





After wearing light makeup or sunscreen, especially as a second cleanse





What stands out:





Very gentle on sensitive skin


Leaves skin feeling hydrated, not squeaky-clean or dry


Creamy texture feels soothing


No strong fragrance


Good for daily use


Works well with a simple skincare routine





A couple honest notes:





It does not foam, so some people may feel like it is not “cleaning enough” at first


Heavy makeup may need a makeup remover or cleansing balm first


Oily skin types may prefer something more clarifying


The texture is mild and creamy, not a dramatic cleanser





Bottom line:


This is a dependable, gentle face cleanser that does exactly what it should. It cleans without irritating or drying out the skin, making it a great choice for everyday use, sensitive skin, or anyone trying to keep their skincare routine simple and barrier-friendly

reviews.author: Qimmah “Q”
reviews.text:
Honest First Impressions: La Roche-Posay Toleriane Hydrating Gentle Cleanser for Sensitive Skin





I just started using this cleanser today, and even on first use, I can tell it’s a solid option—especially for sensitive, dry-to-normal skin.





The texture is extremely smooth and gentle. It feels lightweight on the skin, almost like a soft silk-like cleanser as you massage it in. It doesn’t feel harsh or stripping at all. Instead, it leaves my skin feeling clean, soft, and still hydrated afterward.





What I really like is how easily it rinses off without leaving any residue or tightness. My skin still feels comfortable and calm after washing, which is important for maintaining a healthy skin barrier.





I personally rinse with cool water instead of warm, as it helps my skin feel more refreshed and less irritated, and this cleanser works well with that approach.





It’s fragrance-free, gentle, and feels like it was made for sensitive skin that needs both cleansing and hydration at the same time. The bottle size is also a good value for the price.





Overall, first impressions are very positive. If you’re looking for a gentle everyday cleanser that supports hydration and doesn’t strip the skin, this is definitely worth trying.

reviews.author: Shaun Murphy
reviews.text:
Love this facial cleanser! Makes my skin soft and doesn't dry my skin like others do. I have less redness after only a short time using Laroche Posay, the varicose red veins decreased the more I use it!


This Laroche Posay facial cleanser is perfect for my normally dry skin. My skin does not feel tight after washing compared to other name brand cleansers.


This cleanser doesn't foam up like soap, therefore doesn't strip the skin of natural oils.


Over time my skin continues to be less red and leaves skin very soft.


The fragrance is very light  and fresh, barely noticeable. I am sensitive to strong scents, this Laroche Posey has never caused any reactions at all.


Perfect for combination skin.


Highly recommend for combination skin leaving me with healthy, soft and radiant skin!

reviews.author: Juliette
reviews.text: This moisturizer has been such a nice addition to my skincare routine. It feels lightweight but still really hydrating, and it sinks into the skin quickly without leaving a greasy residue. My skin feels soft, smooth, and refreshed after using it, and it works well under makeup too. I also like that it doesn’t have a heavy scent or irritate my skin. Perfect for daily use, especially if you want something simple but effective!

reviews.author: briana canterino
reviews.text: Good for dry cracked skin and rough eczema patches . I wouldn’t use it on my son who has severe eczema and flares bc he says it does burn him so I took one star away because it is difficult to have to Remeber this can’t always be used. Buts it’s a good moisturizer when not flared and for those with just dry skin. It’s also sensitive enough for the face which is good to be able to use it all over

name: Paula's Choice CLEAR Pore Normalizing Cleanser

couche3.note: clarifie acné sans dessécher, sans parfum
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this facial cleanser effective at clearing skin and removing makeup and oil, while being gentle on the skin and leaving it moisturized without drying. The product receives positive feedback for its fragrance-free formula and easy application, with one customer noting it works well for difficult-to-remove makeup. Regarding skin compatibility, while some find it good for sensitive skin, others report it burning their skin, and opinions on value for money are mixed, with some finding it very valuable while others consider it pricey.

reviews.author: Beauty Lover. 💖
reviews.text:
I've trier other Paula's Choice products, and have liked them. I decided to buy this Paula's Choice facial cleanser, and am very happy I did.





First off, I'm really happy about the way this product arrived. It was sealed on the outside, which is really important to me when dealing with skincare products. There was no leaking or issues with the bottle, which I also appreciate.





As for the cleanser itself, this is a gentle yet effective one. Its clear in color, and lathers nicely. It does a great job at removing oil, residues and makeup from my skin-without making my skin feel too dry.





There's not much of any fragrance to this product, which may be of some importance to some people. My skin feels refreshed, and the feeling lasts several hours.





I like that this cleanser is gentle enough for my combination skin; I have dry areas of my skin, plus some areas that get a little oily. Additionally, I have some areas that have enlarged pores or are prone to breakouts. This cleanser has helped with those issues, while still helpful at moisturizing my dry areas.





It's an excellent cleanser that does exactly what I hoped it would, and I've only been using this for a few days now. I love the results I've seen thus far, and am continuing to use this cleanser every day. I like that a little goes a long way, and the price seems reasonable to me. I would recommend this product to others.

reviews.author: Erin
reviews.text: I’ve been using this cleanser for about 4+ years now and keep coming back to it. I have really sensitive skin, so I have to be careful with what I use, but this one leaves my skin feeling clean and fresh without drying it out. It’s gentle, hydrating, and still does a great job getting all the dirt and makeup off. Plus, there’s basically no smell, which I love. I use this twice a day, every day, and probably use more than I’m supposed to. Even then, one bottle still lasts me about a month.

reviews.author: SB
reviews.text: Paula’s Choice, is the best of the best- in skin care.  It is affordable, effective. Cruelty-Free. This cleanses skin, without having to “double cleanse” in saying that, if you have costume make-up on, etc. I would remove makeup first with whatever you chose to use, then cleanse. The 3 step system is fantastic too, for Acne. I use this bc my skin gets flared-up, while having problematic skin. It’s a deep cleanse, yet gentle.  Love love love.

reviews.author: Addison
reviews.text: So far. This is my favorite cleanser for my skin. I have acne, sebaceous filaments, and closed comedones.  It helps a lot with making my pores smaller as well. I really like it for oily skin too. ONLY THING IS. The bottle after a lot of use has had some leakage recently. On the back it just leaks. But its whatever. Its still my favorite cleanser so far.

reviews.author: eliza garcia
reviews.text: I’ve only been using this product for a few weeks but I am already seeing a difference in my pores. My nose is clearing up and my pores look smaller. I have sensitive and oily skin but this product does not irritate me.

name: First Aid Beauty Ultra Gentle Cleanser

couche3.note: doux sensible, hydratant
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this facial cleanser effective at removing makeup and clearing skin, while being gentle on sensitive skin and moisturizing throughout the day. The product receives positive feedback for its texture, with one customer describing it as having a light creamy feeling, and customers appreciate its small size where a little goes a long way. They like the travel size option and consider it worth the price. Opinions about the fragrance are mixed, with some customers noting it doesn't have a scent while others find it weird.

reviews.author: Pugangelx3
reviews.text:
I ordered the travel size to test the cream-to-foam formula before committing to the full size. I’ve liked other products from this brand and have trusted their quality, so I had high expectations.





The cleanser dispenses with a promising creamy texture, but once applied to damp skin, it quickly breaks down into a thinner, lotion-like consistency with very light bubbling. It doesn’t maintain a true foam long enough to effectively remove makeup, and I found I would likely need a second cleanse for thorough removal.





The scent is neutral and pleasant, and it feels gentle on the skin. I think it would work very well as a morning cleanser or as a second cleanse—just not as a primary makeup remover.





I’ve appreciated this brand since their original standout products, and I hope the focus remains on maintaining their high performance standards rather than the recent rapid expansion of adding so many new products to the range.





The photos included show the result after cleansing for 1 minute and 30 seconds. Overall, it’s still a gentle formula from a brand I respect—I just hoped the foam performance would hold up a bit longer.

reviews.author: Kylee Dunaway
reviews.text: Good sized bottle, my face feels softer and cleaner, I’m not sure about its makeup removal ability since I use a makeup remover prior to using. Easy to use and actually foamy. Feels natural and clean.

reviews.author: BuffaloSadie
reviews.text: The best cleanser. Very gentle yet feels so clean. It’s a cream to foam cleanser and feels very luxurious!

reviews.author: Mary C
reviews.text: This is THE BEST cleaner to take off stubborn and waterproof makeup. Great for sensitive and aging skin

reviews.author: GiaGem
reviews.text: Love this cleanser. It's gentle but thorough. My skin never feels super dry or tight afterward!

couche3.note: top acné/grasse, ni sec ni gras
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this facial cleanser effective, particularly for acne-prone skin, and appreciate that it doesn't leave skin feeling dry or oily. The product is gentle on sensitive skin and provides a soft, smooth feel. While some customers say it foams up nicely, others report it doesn't produce the expected lather. The fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some finding it wonderful while others describe it as terrible. Customers consider it good value for money, though durability receives mixed feedback, with some noting it lasts a long time while others report issues with the pump mechanism rusting.

reviews.author: Susan Estrella
reviews.text: I've been using it for a week and I really feel the difference. Deeply cleans without drying out the skin. Controls excess oil and helps keep pores cleaner, reducing small imperfections. It has a light texture and a soft aroma, it rinses easily and leaves the skin fresh. An excellent product if you are looking for an effective cleaning without irritation.

reviews.author: frank
reviews.text: I've been using this cleanser for years now. It can be a bit drying if you have very sensitive or dry skin but it's great for combination skin with an oiliness tendency. It doesn't really work for removing makeup though, I use an oil cleanser for that. It feels very cleansing.

reviews.author: Yanet
reviews.text: “I always use it to clean my face because I have a little acne. My skin feels clean and soft afterward. I can’t stop using it.”

reviews.author: Jalpan Pansuriya
reviews.text: Been using since a year now. By far the best cleanser! Keeps my face acne free.

reviews.author: Cesar Alzate
reviews.text: Excelente producto.

name: Krave Beauty Matcha Hemp Cleanser
ingredients: Camellia Sinensis Leaf Water, Water (Aqua/Eau), Glycerin, Dipropylene Glycol, Coco-Betaine, Coco-Glucoside,1,2-Hexanediol, Butylene Glycol, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride, Methyl Gluceth-10, Xanthan Gum, Cellulose Gum, Benzyl Glycol, Camellia Sinensis Leaf Powder, Sodium Polyacrylate, Hemp Seed Oil, Citric Acid, Ethylhexylglycerin, Disodium EDTA, Sodium Chloride, Panthenol, Sodium PCA, Camellia Sinensis Leaf Extract, Avena Sativa Kernel Extract

couche3.note: nettoie sans décaper, hydratant, doux
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this cleanser effective, noting it cleans without stripping necessary oils and leaves skin feeling clean and hydrated. Moreover, the product is gentle on sensitive skin, lathers beautifully, and lasts for weeks or months. They appreciate that it's not strong-scented and consider it excellent value for money, with one customer mentioning its pretty green color from matcha.

reviews.author: Sean Bozarth
reviews.text:
I’ve been using the KraveBeauty Matcha Hemp Hydrating Cleanser for several weeks as part of my daily skincare routine, and it’s easily one of the most pleasant face washes I’ve tried. What really stands out about this product is how balanced it is — it gets the face clean without stripping away natural moisture or leaving that tight, dry feeling that so many cleansers do.





From the first wash, the texture felt soft and gentle. The cleanser has a smooth, gel-like consistency that spreads easily and creates just enough slip to feel like it’s doing its job without feeling too foamy or harsh. When I rinse, my skin never feels stripped or rough afterward; instead, it feels calm and comfortable, which is especially noticeable on days when my skin is a little sensitive.





The combination of matcha and hemp seems to work really well together. The matcha gives a fresh, clean feeling without any tingling or irritation, and the hemp really helps lock in moisture. Even after washing away sunscreen or light makeup, my skin still feels plump and hydrated. That’s a big deal because I often notice dryness or tightness with other cleansers.





One thing I really like about this cleanser is that it feels suitable for everyday use. I’ve used it both in the morning and at night, and it performs consistently well without leaving a residue or making my skin feel dry later. If I’m wearing makeup, I usually double-cleanse, but this still removes the day’s buildup nicely without needing to scrub. It’s also vegan and cruelty-free, which is a bonus for me personally.





Another small but nice detail is that it doesn’t have a strong scent. There’s a very light, fresh smell that doesn’t linger —just enough that you know it’s there, but not enough to be overwhelming. The pump bottle is also convenient and dispenses a good amount without wasting product.





If I were to point out any minor downside, it’s that the cleaner isn’t designed to remove heavy waterproof makeup on its own. That’s not really a flaw — just a reminder that it’s best as part of a double-cleanse in those situations.





All in all, the KraveBeauty Matcha Hemp Hydrating Cleanser delivers exactly what it promises: a gentle, hydrating, and effective daily wash that’s kind to skin and feels comforting to use. I’d recommend it especially if your skin leans toward sensitive or dry, or if you just want a cleanser that cleans without stripping. It’s become one of the staples in my routine, and I find myself reaching for it every day.

reviews.author: MashedPotato
reviews.text:
Okay, hear me out. This is one of THE BEST face washes out there. I have very sensitive skin that reacts badly to most cleansing products. Most cleansers, especially foaming ones, damage my skin's barrier and cause rosacea flare-ups. I used to rely on the Gleanser, but unfortunately the newest reformulation broke me out, although I still can use it occasionally and only a small amount. I then read reddit posts about the gentlest washes, and this one was highly recommended by many, and I 100% agree with folks. I've been using this product for a couple of months, and it's definitely super gentle and non-irritating. It feels super REFRESHING, maybe because of the nicest, faintest smell (it smells very naturally of green tea), and it cleanses really well. I use this twice a day. It mostly washes my PM moisturizer very well (I splash my face with luke-warm water first, then use a small amount of this cleanser lathered with water in my hand for 10 seconds).





My only complaint is that I want a large bottle with a pump as the tube isn't the most convenient.

reviews.author: Gio G.
reviews.text:
This is the best cleanser I have used. I was using a homemade cleanser made with Castile soap and I learned that I have been stripping my skins moisture barrier so I had a lot of dryness and redness. But just because something is homemade and nontoxic, does not mean it’s right for your skin!





Well, I’ve found my holy grail cleanser. This is so moisturizing and I feel like my skin feels more supple after cleansing instead of dry and tight. I absolutely love the consistency- it kind of reminds me of aloe gel but then it foams up gently and does such a good job of cleansing without over-cleansing. I have no irritation and I only use a tiny dab to cleanse at night and only wash with water in the morning so I think this will last me a while. I also love that it’s non toxic and the scent is pleasant. I have no qualms at all.

reviews.author: Leigh
reviews.text: I have been using this product since it was first launched. It is my favorite cleanser I have ever used. It is the perfect balance for me of clean skin that feels soft to the touch after. My skin does not feel tight after. I am someone who is incredibly acne prone. This product has never once caused me to breakout. I like that it gentle enough not to irritate the skin. I have used the many formulations over the years, probably all of them honestly, none have ever disappointed me. This is a very high quality, well crafted product. I hope this is never discontinued. So cool to watch Liah go from a small two product launch so many years ago, and watching the brand slowly become more and more popular. Would definitely recommend this one to anyone who wants an effective gentle cleanser.

reviews.author: A. Robinson
reviews.text: This cleanser has become a staple in my routine! The longevity is great since only a small amount is needed each time—making it excellent value for money. It makes my skin feel and look great, leaving it soft, hydrated, and balanced without any dryness. The scent is light and refreshing, never overpowering. While the lather isn’t foamy, it still provides a really good cleanse that feels gentle yet effective. The foaminess is minimal, but I actually love that because it doesn’t strip my skin. A high-quality cleanser I’ll keep repurchasing—it’s truly one of the best daily washes I’ve tried!

couche3.note: doux sensible; peu d'avis, valeur mitigée
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this face cleanser effective, with one mentioning it clears skin instantly. Moreover, they appreciate its gentle formula, particularly noting it's suitable for sensitive skin. However, opinions on value for money are mixed, with some saying it's worth the price while others find it slightly pricy.

reviews.author: Nationally accredited  Family Nurse practitioner
reviews.text:
I got sent a sample of this when I was a teenager (I am now 29) in a beauty subscription box. For context, I have very sensitive skin that reacts badly to most if not all new products I’ve tried.





This product cleared my skin instantly and made the overall appearance smooth and glowy. It is slightly pricy, but lasts a long time and is absolutely worth the price.





I will never not have this product so I highly recommend!

reviews.author: Mary McDonald
reviews.text: My go-to face cleanser. My skin is kinda sensitive and somewhat oily. This is super gentle. Doesn't dry my skin out. My teenage daughter uses it as well and it keeps her skin clear and soft too. Scent is light and refreshing. A little pricey, but soo worth it. A little does go a long way.

reviews.author: Danah M. Lucas
reviews.text: My daughter loves this face cleaner because it is gentle yet effectively clean without irritating her face. This is a much better deal than at a department store or Sephora.

reviews.author: Melody Fletcher
reviews.text: A mild cleanser that removes what it should, not what it shouldn't, and doesn't burn my eyes.

reviews.author: Shut off Spark
reviews.text: This product is amazing. It is not abrasive at all. I feel like my face is clean not dry.

name: Drunk Elephant Beste No.9 Jelly Cleanser

couche3.note: démaquille, doux 2x/j, sans parfum
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this cleanser effective at removing makeup and making their skin feel fresh and clean, with a soft, whipped texture that's gentle enough for twice-daily use. They appreciate that it's scentless, lasts a while, and is a regular part of their face washing routine. While some customers say it's good for oily skin, others report it dries their skin, and opinions on value for money are mixed.

reviews.author: teabeapea
reviews.text:
I'm officially on the Drunk Elephant bandwagon, which is saying something, since I'm not part of the beauty blogging/brand influencer community who gets these items gratis. Nope, I'm spending my hard earned cash on these products -- and I have to say, I'm very happy with the results.





For most of my life, I used whatever soap was in the shower to wash my face. I only recently purchased a facial cleanser (also reviewed). But now I'm on a mission to use healthier products on my face -- reading ingredients and learning more about what to avoid.





Like all of Drunk Elephant's packaging -- the Jelly Cleanser comes in a sturdy box while being easy to open. The tube and safety seals are easy to open, too.





The instructions suggest placing a dab in the palm of your hand and placing it under water, then rubbing your hands together to create a foam. The thing I'm noticing about these pricier products is that a dab truly is all that's needed. It spreads smoothly and covers quickly and rinses easily. I also like the scent. My skin feels fresh and clean and soft -- but not super dried out.





I've knocked off stars on some of the other Drunk Elephant products because of the pump design. But this tube looks good and l have no doubt I'll be able to wring every drop from it. The price tag is HEFTY (especially to someone like me who has used body wash or bar soap on her face), but the fact that it does the job with just a dab keeps it five stars.

reviews.author: Stacey Monroe
reviews.text:
I absolutely love the Beste No. 9 Jelly Cleanser by Drunk Elephant! This cleanser is gentle yet super effective—my skin feels clean, fresh, and balanced every single time I use it. The jelly texture is lightweight and satisfying, and it lathers just enough to remove makeup, sunscreen, and daily grime without stripping my skin.





What really stands out is how non-drying it is. My skin feels soft and calm afterward, never tight or irritated. It works beautifully for all skin types, making it a great unisex option, and it’s fragrance-free, which I really appreciate for sensitive skin days.





A little goes a long way, so the 5 oz bottle lasts a while and feels worth the price. If you’re looking for a reliable, no-fuss cleanser that actually delivers, this one is a must-have in your routine. I’ll definitely be repurchasing! ✨🧼

reviews.author: Jax
reviews.text: I have used Drunk Elephant products for many years now and will use nothing else. I get tons of compliments on my skin everyday! This cleanser is my first step every night. It removes everything including my mascara and never dries out my face or causes any issues for my thin sensitive skin

reviews.author: Melissa P.
reviews.text: Love this face cleanser. It's great for my sensitive skin and doesn't dry it out.

reviews.author: Suzanne Sterkel
reviews.text: Best face cleanser. Takes all makeup off and does not dry out my face.

name: Youth To The People Superfood Cleanser

couche3.note: hydratant top sèche; parfumé → prudence sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the facial cleanser effective and appreciate its moisturizing properties, particularly noting it's great for dry skin and doesn't dry out the face. The product receives positive feedback for its gentle, soft feel, lovely fragrance, and good lathering properties. While some customers say it doesn't irritate their skin at all, others report it made their face feel like it was burning. The pump functionality receives mixed reviews, with several customers reporting unworkable pumps. Value for money opinions are divided between those who find it worth the price and those who consider it too expensive.

reviews.author: Lee S
reviews.text: This facial skin cleanser always makes me laugh when I read the print on the bottle! “Kale + Green Tea, Spinach, Vitamins: Superfood Cleanser.” Sounds like I should be DRINKING this stuff! How healthy can you get? But in all seriousness, Youth To The People facial skin cleanser is indeed healthy. I have sensitive skin, and this cleanser is gentle and magical. A very small amount will cleanse your skin while moisturizing as well. The fragrance is fresh and pleasant. My understanding is that some people have problems with the pump. Fortunately, I have never had a pump problem. All I’ve ever had is great facial skin cleansing! If you think it’s too expensive and you want to try a “knockoff” product, that’s up to you. I have found that I need so little of the cleanser that Youth To The People is NOT expensive. I sincerely hope you will try it.

reviews.author: Ginny P.
reviews.text:
This cleanser is amazing but the package is TERRIBLE. My skin feels clean and the smell is fresh and not overwhelming. It's good for my sensitive combination skin.





But the glass bottle? In the shower? Hello??? Who approved this hazard?





It looks nice, yes. Very “rich woman with eucalyptus in her bathroom.” But the second your hands are wet, this thing becomes a bar of soap’s evil cousin. It's so slippery and I fear for my life every time I use it. I am going to end up in the ER one day when this bottle slips out of my lathered hands and shatters in a million sharp pieces on my tub.





Great cleanser. Truly. But please put it in something that does not threaten to explode into artisanal shrapnel in my bathtub.

reviews.author: Barbara S.
reviews.text: I thought it was a gimmick--I'm not going to eat this! Why do I need kale on my face? Was I ever wrong! This crystal clear liquid comes out slowly so you can easily control the amount. You need very little to get just the right amount of suds that release a beautiful fresh scent. And what you're left with is skin that's super clean, soft, glowing, with no residue at all. I hated to apply anything else because it feels so wonderful.

reviews.author: Rebecca Stone
reviews.text: I had jumped around between different face cleansers for years trying to find one that didn't feel like it left residue or cause any irritation. I have been using the YTTP kale and green tea face wash twice a day for over three years now and love it. It leaves my skin feeling fresh, doesn't cause dry skin, is gentle for sensitive skin, and doesn't have an unpleasant scent like so many cleansers on the market.

reviews.author: Smartshopper63
reviews.text:
This is the BEST face cleanser! Both my daughter and I use it. We have fairly sensitive skin, but after using you can already feel the difference. Te pump provides enough where you don’t have to pump it multiple times to waste product, this product lasts and you can buy refills! Skin is smooth and it’s clean.


I’m 40 & my daughter is 11 it works. Buy it.

name: CeraVe Moisturizing Cream
ingredients: AQUA / WATER / EAU, GLYCERIN, CETEARYL ALCOHOL, CAPRYLIC/CAPRIC TRIGLYCERIDE, CETYL ALCOHOL, CETEARETH-20, PETROLATUM, POTASSIUM PHOSPHATE, CERAMIDE NP, CERAMIDE AP, CERAMIDE EOP, CARBOMER, DIMETHICONE, BEHENTRIMONIUM METHOSULFATE, SODIUM LAUROYL LACTYLATE, SODIUM HYALURONATE, CHOLESTEROL, PHENOXYETHANOL, DISODIUM EDTA, DIPOTASSIUM PHOSPHATE, TOCOPHEROL, PHYTOSPHINGOSINE, XANTHAN GUM, ETHYLHEXYLGLYCERIN

couche3.note: riche, top eczéma/sèche/sensible, non gras
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective, particularly for eczema and aging dry skin, and appreciate that it works well under makeup. The cream is non-greasy, leaving no residue or oily feeling, and has a silky texture that's not heavy. Customers report it's wonderful for sensitive skin, helps relieve itchiness, and is great value for money.

reviews.author: DadBuysStuffTV
reviews.text:
Ten months in and I have barely made a dent in this tub. I am genuinely starting to consider my options — mixing it into smoothies, using it as a cooking fat, applying it to every surface in the house. Something has to be done because at the current rate of consumption this container will outlive me.





In all seriousness, the value proposition here is almost absurd. CeraVe Moisturizing Cream is a legitimately great product — dermatologist recommended, ceramides, hyaluronic acid, fragrance free, non-comedogenic, works on face and body, gentle enough for sensitive skin. It does everything a moisturizer should do without irritating your skin or smelling like you bathed in a candle.





The 19oz tub is the right call if you actually plan to use moisturizer regularly. A little goes a long way, which is both the best and most frustrating thing about it. Best because you're getting incredible value per ounce. Frustrating because you will be looking at this same tub for the foreseeable future with no end in sight.





Great product, generational quantity. Buy it once, pass it down.





One star deducted because I now have to figure out what to do with the remaining 18.5 ounces.

reviews.author: Jamie Jorgensen
reviews.text:
I have psoriasis and very dry, sensitive skin, so finding a moisturizer that works without causing irritation is important to me. CeraVe Moisturizing Cream has become one of my favorite daily moisturizers. It provides long-lasting hydration and helps keep my skin feeling soft and comfortable throughout the day.





One of the things I appreciate most is that it doesn't burn or sting when I apply it. Many lotions I've tried in the past can be uncomfortable on dry or irritated areas, but this cream feels soothing and gentle from the moment I put it on. It absorbs well and doesn't leave my skin feeling greasy, even though it is thick and very moisturizing.





I also like that it can be used on both my face and body, which makes it convenient and cost-effective. The hyaluronic acid and ceramides help lock in moisture, and I've noticed that my skin stays hydrated much longer compared to other products I've used.





If you have dry, sensitive, or psoriasis-prone skin and are looking for a reliable moisturizer that delivers excellent hydration without irritation, I would definitely recommend giving this cream a try.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
I’ve only been using this moisturizer for about 2 weeks, but so far I really like it. It keeps my skin feeling hydrated throughout the day without feeling greasy or heavy. The texture is smooth, absorbs nicely, and doesn’t irritate my skin at all.





I also noticed my skin feels softer and less dry since I started using it. It’s simple, gentle, and works well for everyday use, especially if you have sensitive skin. So far I haven’t experienced any breakouts or strong fragrance issues, which I appreciate.





Too early to say long-term results, but my first impression has been very positive and I would buy it again.

reviews.author: Brayden
reviews.text: Simply put, it’s perfect. Truly, I hate moisturizers, but this one just absorbs into the skin in a few minutes and disappears. It’s gentle on the skin and very hydrating, and it stays on all day. You won’t feel sticky or uncomfortable even if you end up sweating. It seems to stay, but it doesn’t seem to cause acne or clog any pores. It washes away very quickly when under constant flowing water, as in a shower.

reviews.author: Keïla Judith Jean
reviews.text: I love CeraVe Moisturizing Cream! It provides long-lasting hydration without feeling greasy or heavy. It works great on both my face and body, leaving my skin soft, smooth, and healthy-looking. The formula is gentle, absorbs well, and is perfect for daily use, even on sensitive skin. Great quality and excellent value for the price. I highly recommend it!”

name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
ingredients: AQUA / WATER / EAU, GLYCERIN, CAPRYLIC/CAPRIC TRIGLYCERIDE, NIACINAMIDE, CETEARYL ALCOHOL, POTASSIUM PHOSPHATE, CERAMIDE NP, CERAMIDE AP, CERAMIDE EOP, CARBOMER, DIMETHICONE, CETEARETH-20, BEHENTRIMONIUM METHOSULFATE, SODIUM LAUROYL LACTYLATE, SODIUM HYALURONATE, CHOLESTEROL, PHENOXYETHANOL, DISODIUM EDTA, DIPOTASSIUM PHOSPHATE, CAPRYLYL GLYCOL, PHYTOSPHINGOSINE, XANTHAN GUM, POLYGLYCERYL-3 DIISOSTEARATE, ETHYLHEXYLGLYCERIN

couche3.note: léger non gras, bon grasse/acné, hydratant
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective and super hydrating, particularly noting it's good for oily and acne-prone skin. They appreciate its lightweight, non-greasy feel, and consider it great value for money. Regarding skin compatibility, while some customers say it doesn't irritate sensitive skin, others report burning sensations. Similarly, experiences with breakouts are mixed, with some saying it doesn't cause breakouts while others experienced severe reactions.

reviews.author: doug wells
reviews.text:
In the past I have used high end skincare products and wasted money. This is absolutely one of the best PM OIL-FREE facial moisturizers. This has helped my skin improve significantly over the last few months. Its super super light weight and pea size dots cover my entire face. I add a pea size to my cheeks, forehead and chin. It all soaks into my pores evenly without clogging them. Due to me using a serum by CeraVe as well.


(Very important part of my personal skin-care routine)


I have extremely sensitive skin and nearly break out with everything I use.


However this helps being fragrance-free & OIL-FREE!


Over the years I always thought high end brands were better than the lower cost friendly ones. (NOT THE CASE! At least for me.)


I highly recommend this product for someone who is starting out using facial products or someone who has indulged in high end.


This does WORK! And it leaves my skin feeling fresh and lightweight. I started seeing differences in my skin texture (for the good) within about a month after using it daily. I then backed off from it and use it every other day. Since I now have my skin where I need it to be.

reviews.author: Three
reviews.text:
I’ve tried a lot of moisturizers over the years, and this falls into what people call “boring skincare” — and honestly, that’s exactly why it works so well.





If you have acne-prone skin and deal with redness or irritation, this is one of those rare products that checks both boxes without overcomplicating things.





The consistency is lightweight but still hydrating. It spreads easily and absorbs quickly into the skin without leaving any greasy or heavy residue. It gives my skin the hydration it needs without clogging pores or triggering breakouts. I’ve had no issues with acne while using this, which is not something I can say for most moisturizers.





It also has Very minimal to no fragrance, which I prefer — especially great for sensitive or reactive skin.





Overall:


This isn’t a “flashy” product, but it’s consistent, gentle, and effective. It’s the kind of moisturizer you can rely on daily without worrying about irritation or breakouts.

reviews.author: Kyle Utterback
reviews.text: I've been using the CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion every night, and I absolutely love it. My skin feels healthier, softer, and more youthful since I started using it. It provides excellent hydration without feeling heavy or greasy, and it has never irritated my sensitive skin. I pair it with the CeraVe Foaming Facial Cleanser, and the combination has worked incredibly well for me. Together, they leave my skin feeling clean, balanced, smooth, and well-moisturized. After struggling for years to find products that didn't dry out my skin or cause redness, this routine has been a game changer.

reviews.author: chriskardaneli
reviews.text: I recently started using Tretinoin and needed a face moisturizer to use with it. Did some research and heard this one would be great to use with it to keep my face hydrated and to use the “sandwich method.” Well, this moisturizer is great! Keeps face hydrated while using the Tretinoin. Feels very nice on the skin. Very lightweight. It’s not oily or dry. The pump bottle works nicely, and it is easy to use.

reviews.author: Tiann
reviews.text: I’ve tried so many night creams, but I always come back to this CeraVe PM lotion. It’s ultra-lightweight but incredibly hydrating. I love that it has ceramides and hyaluronic acid to help restore my skin barrier while I sleep. It’s oil-free and never breaks me out. A total holy grail product for anyone wanting healthy, glowing skin!

name: Cetaphil Moisturizing Cream
ingredients: Water, Glycerin, Petrolatum, Dicaprylyl Ether, Dimethicone, Glyceryl Stearate, Cetyl Alcohol, Helianthus Annuus (Sunflower) Seed Oil, PEG-30 Stearate, Panthenol, Niacinamide, Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Oil, Tocopheryl Acetate, Pantolactone, Dimethiconol, Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer, Carbomer, Propylene Glycol, Disodium EDTA, Benzyl Alcohol, Phenoxyethanol, Sodium Hydroxide, Citric Acid.

couche3.note: top sèche, crémeux non gras, sensible OK
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer works well for dry skin, keeping it hydrated throughout the day and being recommended by dermatologists. The product receives positive feedback for its texture, being creamy yet not heavy, and its non-greasy formula that absorbs well into the skin. Customers appreciate that it's fragrance-free, suitable for sensitive skin, and consider it good value for money.

reviews.author: Reanna Simpson
reviews.text:
I’ve never really been big on moisturizing my face, but I decided to start with this product and I’m glad I did. Other moisturizers I tried before made my skin feel oily, but this one leaves my skin feeling soft and hydrated without that greasy feeling.


I also like that it’s fragrance-free and gentle on my skin. After using it consistently, my skin looks smoother and has a nice natural glow. A little goes a long way, and it works well for both face and body.


Overall, a simple but really effective moisturizer — I’ll definitely keep repurchasing!

reviews.author: Ceekaie
reviews.text: I am star struck! This is the best moisturizer I have ever used. My skin is extremely dry and itchy at times. This soothes it while deeply moisturizing my skin. I tested it on a particularly dry spot, my heels. For 2-days, I did not reapply it to my heels after showering...yet 2-days later, my feet were still soft and deeply conditioned. It has a light fresh and clean scent. This will be/is my go to moisturizer from now on. It is Awesome!!!

reviews.author: Erica
reviews.text: This is the first moisturizer that I have repurchased 3 times. I have eczema so my skin is on the dry side & my face is very fickle. I'm able to use this on my body as well as my face with no problems. For me it isn't greasy absorbs well into my skin & keeps it soft & supple until my next application. No fragrance which I absolutely love.

reviews.author: Flayva
reviews.text: I WENT TO SEE A DERMATOLOGIST FOR MY EXCESSIVELY DRY SKIN AND SHE RECOMMENDED THIS FACIAL MOISTURIZER...BOY DOES IT WORK, IT HELPED MY DRY SKIN AND I WON'T BE WITHOUT IT, FRAGRANCE FREE, SENSITIVE SKIN, IT MOISTURIZES AND SOFTENS MY SKIN SO WELL...I HIGHLY RECOMMEND =-)

reviews.author: Renee Tremain
reviews.text: Doesn’t leave you feeling oily and leaves skin glowing. My face feels moisturized all day. The cost of tub is very reasonable and goes a long way. Easy to apply and the consistency is great too. It rubs in easily and doesn’t have a fragrance.

name: Vanicream Moisturizing Cream
ingredients: Ingredients: purified water, petrolatum, sorbitol, cetearyl alcohol, propylene glycol, ceteareth-20, simethicone, glyceryl stearate, PEG-30 stearate, sorbic acid, BHT.

couche3.note: eczéma/sèche/sensible, sans parfum, non gras
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective, particularly for eczema and dry skin, and appreciate its non-greasy, silky texture that leaves skin soft. The product is fragrance-free and works well for sensitive skin, with one customer noting it's gentle enough for babies with eczema.
