While some customers like its consistency, others find it too thick, though they still consider it good value for money.

reviews.author: Naju
reviews.text:
I bought Vanicream while trying to repair a damaged skin barrier.  My skin was extremely dry, irritated, sensitive, and reacting badly to a lot of products, so I needed something very simple and gentle.





This cream ended up being one of the few things my skin could actually tolerate consistently.





The texture is VERY thick, but surprisingly not greasy. I wouldn’t call it lightweight either, but it feels more comforting than heavy, especially when your skin feels dry and stripped. I mostly used it on damp skin after applying water or Avène Thermal Spring Water first, and it layered well with the rest of my barrier repair routine.





Most importantly, it didn’t sting my skin and didn’t cause breakouts for me, which was a huge relief because my skin was reacting to almost everything at the time.





I also appreciate how basic and versatile it is. It’s the kind of product that works well when your skin is overwhelmed and you need to simplify everything. I can see why so many people with sensitive or compromised skin recommend it.





If I had to describe it simply, this feels less like a luxury moisturizer and more like a reliable “safe” product to keep around for when your skin barrier is struggling.





Overall, really happy with it and will definitely repurchase.Great size for your money. A little goes a long way and this will last you for a long time.  Staple in my house.

reviews.author: Mom of 5
reviews.text:
This definitely hydrates!!! I have to say that it is greasy but I don't care! I live in Arizona, so we need all the help we can get. It's worth it for what it does.





I'm 46 and starting to see the inevitable wrinkles showing up,  especially after I put in my makeup. Now, I put this on before my makeup and it helps it not crease. My wrinkles seemed to have disappeared. My skin is so soft compared to what it used to be like.





Also, my daughter's eczema was so bad that half her arm was bloody, the crease of her neck,  under her eyes,  and the back of her legs were all scabbed over. I tried so many products for her and nothing worked. This!?!? Healed every last spot within a week. Completely gone I'm so happy.  And she doesn't have any scarring!

reviews.author: Kitty
reviews.text: Research shows that Vanicream products are free of chemical irritants which is why I purchased it. This cream has helped with the eczema on my hands. I like that it absorbs into the skin quickly to hydrate and moisturize the skin. It is non-greasy and doesn't leave a sticky film. I find that it has a lightweight consistency compare to the Aquaphora cream I used to use. The Vanicream provides soothing relief for my eczema flareups. This makes me happy. I recommend this cream for sensitive dry skin.

reviews.author: Gina Marciniak
reviews.text: I keep coming back to Vanicream products because they just work. This cream is thick and moisturizing without feeling irritating or overly scented. My skin can be sensitive and this has been great, especially during dry weather. I also love the pump container because it’s easy to use and feels more hygienic than sticking your hand in a jar. Simple, effective, and worth buying again.

reviews.author: Cydney Farmer
reviews.text: This stuff is nice and works well, but I did hope it would be a tad bit thicker. It has a decent weight to it but it’s not as dense as I thought it would be. Keeps skin moisturized and from drying out and cracking. Great size for the price.

name: Neutrogena Hydro Boost Water Gel
ingredients: Water, Dimethicone, Glycerin, Dimethicone/Vinyl Dimethicone Crosspolymer, Phenoxyethanol, Polyacrylamide, Cetearyl Olivate, Sorbitan Olivate, Dimethiconol, C13-14 Isoparafin, Dimethicone Crosspolymer, Chlorphenesin, Carbomer, Laureth-7, Sodium Hyaluronate, Ethylhexylglycerin, Fragrance, C12-14 Pareth-12, Sodium Hydroxide, Blue 1

couche3.note: gel léger non gras, top grasse/mixte, hydrate
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer hydrating and appreciate its lightweight, non-greasy formula that absorbs quickly into the skin. The texture leaves faces smooth, and customers consider it good value for money. Regarding skin compatibility, while some say it doesn't irritate their skin, others report breakouts. The fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some finding it wonderful while others say it's heavily scented.

reviews.author: Emily
reviews.text: I LOVE THIS GEL!!! Let me start of by saying that I do not have bad skin. I average about 7 breakouts a year. I have used Proactiv+, the "proclaimed gift from God" for skin, and this is better than any Proactiv+ product, in my opinion. Proactiv+ has a 3 step program that always left my skin a bit oily when I woke up from a nights sleep, took days to rid my face of mild acne, and it is TOO EXPENSIVE!! The Hydro Boost gel left my skin soft as a baby's bottom and oil-free. 10 hrs after putting it on, which was before bed, I woke up looking radiant! I already use a face brightening scrub, but this enhanced the brightness in my skin tenfold. It gave me a more even skin-tone, which I LOVE because I am weening myself off of makeup. The clearer my skin is, the quicker I can stop clogging my pores with makeup! I have never used Neutrogena face products before, but I will continue to use this as long as they sell it. It doesn't have a harsh smell, it applies smoothly, and it lasts! It does not have the consistency of an actual gel (like the thick, jell-o ones you use for your hair), it is more of a light, airy cream that has a blue tint to it. I love to overdo everything. I always feel that the more product I use, the better off I'll be, but trust me when I say that you do not need that much product. Which is another pro. When I first saw that this little container was $15, I was hesitant because I am used to buying products over the counter that costs less than $10, but you really get your money's worth. I see this lasting me two months, and I wash my face twice a day. An extremely little dab covers my whole face. I am so glad I came across this product. In case you wanted to know, my daily regimen is as follows: scrub in cleanser (with my clean, washed hands...never clean your face with dirty hands) for 2min. to exfoliate, apply astringent to further clean my face of any dirt the scrub didn't get, and then I moisturize with the gel. My skin has never looked better and I am truly thankful for this affordable product. Excellently priced and does a great job. As I said before, I don't have bad skin...so if you're looking for a miracle to help with cystic acne or anything of the sort..I wouldn't look to this product for salvation. However, it definitely helps with the occasional break out. I am also adding a clay mask to my face regimen, which is supposed to exfoliate and unclog pores so hopefully that even furthers my skin success. I wish you all good luck in determining which product is right for you. I know narrowing it down can be hard, but I hope this review provides some perspective!

reviews.author: Michelle Kim
reviews.text:
My husband absolutely loves the Neutrogena Hydro Boost Water Gel, and it’s easy to see why! The lightweight, non-greasy formula absorbs almost instantly, leaving his skin feeling refreshed, hydrated, and comfortable all day long. Unlike heavier creams that can feel sticky or sit on the surface, this water gel penetrates deeply, giving long-lasting hydration without clogging pores or making skin feel oily. This is perfect for normal to oily skin types and easy to use!





We also appreciate the clean, subtle scent that doesn’t overpower, making it ideal for everyday use. What really differentiates this product from others on Amazon is how consistent it performs even after long days or outdoor activity. The 24-hour hydration claim isn’t just marketing fluff. It genuinely keeps skin feeling nourished and smooth.





For anyone looking for a high-quality moisturizer that works quickly, feels weightless, and keeps skin balanced, this is a must-have. My husband has already made it a staple in his daily routine, and I can see why it’s so highly rated.

reviews.author: Lissetty M
reviews.text:
I’ve been using this moisturizer for a few months now, and it really does hydrate the skin well. I had stopped doing skincare for a while, but after buying this cream I’ve actually noticed a good difference in my skin. It has a gel-like texture that’s very easy to apply and absorbs quickly, which is perfect for my combination skin.





The size is quite small and the price is definitely on the higher side, but I assume that’s because of the quality and the fact that it’s a well-known dermatologist-recommended brand.





What I don’t like is the smell. I had read about it in the reviews before purchasing but didn’t think it would be that noticeable — and it is. For a product at this price point and from this brand, you would expect a better fragrance, so I am not sure I’ll buy it again.

reviews.author: Destiny&Logan
reviews.text: This Neutrogena Hydro Boost Water Gel is one of the best moisturizers I’ve used! It’s super lightweight and absorbs quickly without leaving any greasy feeling. My skin feels instantly hydrated and smooth, and it gives a nice healthy glow. I love that it doesn’t clog pores and works great under makeup too. A little goes a long way, and it keeps my skin feeling moisturized all day. Definitely a go-to product!

reviews.author: Nevada Girl
reviews.text: This is a nice moisturizer. It goes on easily and soaks right in with no sticky residue. Skin feels cool and soft and accepts makeup after use.

name: LRP Toleriane Double Repair Moisturizer
ingredients: AQUA / WATER / EAU • GLYCERIN • SQUALANE • DIMETHICONE • ZEA MAYS STARCH / CORN STARCH • NIACINAMIDE • AMMONIUM POLYACRYLOYLDIMETHYL TAURATE • MYRISTYL MYRISTATE • STEARIC ACID • CERAMIDE NP • POTASSIUM CETYL PHOSPHATE • GLYCERYL STEARATE SE • SODIUM HYDROXIDE • MYRISTIC ACID • PALMITIC ACID • CAPRYLOYL GLYCINE • CAPRYLYL GLYCOL • XANTHAN GUM. (CODE F.I.L. : N296584/1)

couche3.note: léger non gras, sensible, ne fait pas pousser l'acné
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer hydrating and appreciate its lightweight, non-greasy texture that feels soft on the skin. The product is particularly suitable for sensitive skin, as it doesn't irritate or cause breakouts, and customers consider it good value for money. They like that it's fragrance-free and doesn't leave a greasy residue.

reviews.author: JustAShopper
reviews.text:
La Roche-Posay Toleriane Double Repair Moisturizer is one of those simple but very effective skincare products. It has a lightweight, non-greasy texture that absorbs quickly into the skin without feeling heavy or oily.





It does a great job at hydrating and calming the skin, especially if you have sensitivity, dryness, or a compromised skin barrier. The combination of ceramides, niacinamide, and glycerin helps support the skin barrier and lock in moisture throughout the day, which is really the main strength of this product.





It also works well under sunscreen and makeup without pilling (as long as you don’t overapply), and it’s fragrance-free, which makes it a safe choice for most skin types.





Pros:





-Lightweight and fast-absorbing texture


-Strong hydration without greasiness


-Helps repair and support skin barrier


-Fragrance-free and gentle for sensitive skin


-Works well under SPF and makeup





Cons:





-May not be rich enough for very dry skin


-Basic formula (no “extra” active skincare benefits beyond hydration/barrier support)





Overall: A dependable everyday moisturizer that focuses on hydration and skin barrier repair without irritation. Great for sensitive, normal, or combination skin looking for a simple, dermatologist-recommended

reviews.author: Dacian
reviews.text:
This has become one of my favorite skincare products. I previously used Neutrogena Hydro Boost along with a separate moisturizer because I wanted hydration without feeling greasy, but this product feels like the two had a child and combined the best qualities of both.





It provides all-day hydration, yet it never leaves my face feeling oily, heavy, or sticky. The texture is lightweight and absorbs quickly, making it comfortable to wear throughout the day and under sunscreen.





My skin feels moisturized for much longer than it did with my previous routine, and I appreciate that I can get the hydration I want without that shiny, over-moisturized feeling. Highly recommend for anyone looking for a lightweight moisturizer that still delivers lasting hydration.

reviews.author: Jenna
reviews.text:
Pros: On the more affordable side as far as lotions go. Great for sensitive-skin and eyes. Truly fragrance free. Light and non-greasy.





Cons: Will pill if skin is not thoroughly cleaned and dried before applying.





Overall, I have extremely sensitive eyes so trying different face lotions usually ends up in red, watery eyes. This moisturizer keeps the eye irritation at bay and my skin moisturized, so I still use it daily with no intentions of trying anything different. However, the pilling can get frustrating.

reviews.author: chris
reviews.text: I've been very happy with this moisturizer. It goes on smoothly, absorbs quickly, and leaves my skin feeling soft and hydrated without feeling greasy. I have sensitive skin and haven't had any irritation. It works well under makeup and keeps my face moisturized throughout the day. A little goes a long way, and the bottle lasts a good amount of time. I would definitely purchase this again and recommend it to anyone looking for a gentle daily moisturizer. Fragrance free and a great value for money.

reviews.author: Customer Karey-Ann
reviews.text: This was a good addition to my routine. It makes my skin feel smooth and really moisturised doesn't leave any residue. I haven't noticed any skin sensitivity issues and it seems to absorb pretty well. It is value for money and it is true that a little goes a long way. The box was crushed that it came in, but the product itself was fine.

name: First Aid Beauty Ultra Repair Cream

couche3.note: riche sèche/sensible, non gras
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective for dry skin, noting it doesn't irritate sensitive skin and leaves it soft and smooth. The cream is non-greasy, with a mild scent, and customers report their skin looks plump and hydrated after use. While some customers consider it well worth the price, others find it expensive, and there are mixed experiences with skin sensitivity.

reviews.author: Shayronna
reviews.text: This cream is soft and smooth as cool whip… fantastic… oh, it stung a weee bit for just a minute … SO WORTH IT!  I have always had very sensitive skin, insanely sensitive ….  I’ve tried soooo many products, always making certain they are scent free and for sensitive skin.  A few have been successful, for a while then, whether they change the ingredients or my skin just becomes immune, I end up having to stop using and begin the search again.  Well, I had been using Laroche Posse for the past 3 years, no problem, no sting, no drying skin, no red blotches… AND THEN… one day WOW!  I washed my face as I always do, applied the moisturizer and HOLY CATS!  My face instantly burned, and it stung like mad, I knew something had changed, I rinsed the moisturizer off with cold water trying to calm it, patted my face dry but, it was no use; my face felt like someone had stuck it in a deep fryer on about 400 degrees It was terrible….  Almost immediately my face started getting those big red patches (so attractive), my face looked awful .. The next day, it was even worse, my eyes were even swelling and burning.  Obviously this face warranted some Devine intervention and FAST action.  Where else do ya go?  Hello Amazon, I read the reviews carefully looking for others like me who suffer from raging skin, reviews for this brand were awesome so many had similar stories to mine.  Thank goodness for Amazon, I did the searches, scrolling past all those that have already been in my line-up… And based on the number of positive reviews for FIRST AID BEAUTY products these sounded like an answered prayer to me, I could not order fast enough, I’ve been down this road before so, I didn’t get my hopes too high but, I was desperate, I needed help fast.  I won’t keep you in suspense anymore… I about met the Amazon driver getting out of her truck, I shot in the house and excitedly washed my face with the First Aid cleanser OMG!  I felt some relief in the works, it stung a little but after two days suffering through extreme burning face, this little sting was a relief… I followed the instructions and applied the rescue/moisturizer… Have I mentioned my face had been burning, and was horrid red blotchy mess for nearly 3 days at this point?  Well, it was, it still hurt, and at this point my face was sort of raw feeling so, it stung a little when I first applied the First aid products but, as my face dried, the sting began to subside, I repeated the process a few hours later, there was noticeable improvement, it still stung when first applied but, after a few minutes the sting subsided and it felt great, there was a light beginning to shine at the end of this long, painful tunnel… I wasn’t going to give up, by bed time I could have cried I was so happy with the progress.  The next morning I woke up and the severe sting was gone, I washed my face and applied moisturizers again, by the 2nd day, the red blotches were nearly gone and I was smiling again, grateful to be free from pain and that my face no longer would scare small children, I am so happy to have found the First Aid products, I’m praying I never have reaction to First aid because this stuff is phenomenal!  Once the rawness of my face calmed after the 3rd day.. THE STING WAS GONE EVEN AFTER washing and applying moisturizer to my face YAY ME!  😀 It’s a little pricey but, when you’re face burns, stings and looks that bad, you’re willing to get off your wallet some, not sorry bout that, it was worth every penny.  Oh look!  Amazon is here with my First Aid Mask 😃 looking forward to trying that too.  Seriously, if you’re like me and have crazy sensitive skin… Give these products a try, I don’t know anyone who’s face is more sensitive than mine and all I can say is, these products worked for me.  If I were you I’d try them..

reviews.author: Skylar
reviews.text:
I've been aware about this cream for a long time now, but I always skipped it because I felt like it wouldn't actually do much for me, but kept it on my radar regardless. It recently went on sale for $30, so I grabbed it because I was about to run out of the moisturizer that I had been using.





I've got normal to dry leaning skin that gets flushed easily due to stimulant medications. At night I need to lock in that moisture after toners & serums, but keeping my skin calm is priority.





This cream definitely keeps my skin moisturized without feeling so heavy that I need a power washer to get it off in the AM. I've not noticed that it's done anything for my skin tone yet, but I've not used it for long.





What I love about this is that the price point is beyond reasonable for the size of the jar. This definitely will last me a while. It's gentle enough to use on sensitive skin, it's definitely moisturizing, it's like... a cozy blanket for my skin that I don't FEEL on my skin which is actually really nice. I want my skin to feel like skin. Not weighed down.





What I don't like is that even though it feels and looks like I'm putting on a lot, I kind of feel like I need a little more just before bed after it dries down and my skin absorbs it. I expected it to be a little heavier just because of the texture, but I guess that's also a pro for some people because it does absorb quickly.





Overall, totally worth it in not just the formulation (the ingredients are amazing) but the price point is perfect. I've tried moisturizers that are triple the price of this and didn't live up to the brands claims. I've used moisturizers that are $15 and gotten decent results, but the quantity and quality of this cream alone will make me repurchase when I run out.

reviews.author: Ayesha
reviews.text:
This moisturizer is excellent. I wanted a moisturizer that didn’t give such a dewy finish- which I absolutely don’t mind because I have normal to dry skin. However, sometimes I just want my skin to look like soft skin and not like I just stepped out of a sauna lol I noticed almost an immediate change when I started back using this after running out before, even though I was using good quality skincare products and sunscreen. This is pretty much a staple for me in terms of facial moisturizer. I definitely think there’s a notable smoothness in texture that’s attributed to this product.


I also like that it’s very soft, a whipped consistency and very easily movable because anything you put on your face every day that requires a lot of tugging I imagine would eventually loosen the skin? 🥴 I may be completely incorrect about this, but in my head, it makes sense.


I’ve never had any sort of reaction to this. I think it would be great for sensitive skin. It’s not greasy whatsoever and has a very light scent.

reviews.author: Adelaide
reviews.text:
I'm obsessed with this cream. It leaves the skin feeling so hydrated and soft without leaving a greasy residue on the skin. It's lightweight enough to apply to your face while also thoroughly hydrating the rest of the body. I have acne prone skin and haven't had any issues with applying this to my face.


I would especially recommend this to anyone in food service. My hands used to be constantly red and/or cracked from constant washing and exposure to drying chemicals, but this cream has been a lifesaver!

reviews.author: daniel
reviews.text: It is very hydrating, would recommend! Good for sunburn, hydration at night into morning. Not greasy. No fragrance, very soft and soothing, seems good for sensitive skin here.

couche3.note: oil-free léger, top grasse/mixte/acné
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective for dehydrated combination skin and appreciate its lightweight formula that absorbs quickly. The product receives positive feedback for its texture and appearance, with one customer noting it creates a dewy, non-greasy look. While some customers say it doesn't irritate their skin and consider it worth the price, others report it burns sensitive skin and find it pricey.

reviews.author: Lourdes
reviews.text:
I am debating if I like this or not.  I've been using it for over a month, and not sure if it's making my skin get those skin-colored bumps. I wear this and a foundation from Chanel or Estée Lauder. Sometimes I wear powder foundation from Tarte or Jane Iredale.





It's a bit hard for me to just wear foundation without something to cover some unevenness of my skin.  Also, not sure if it's a cream or lotion, or gel- it's hard for me to tell. I am not getting pimples from it but again, not sure if it is causing skin-colored bumps, which I believe they call over-active sebaceous glands.  I have dry skin and this is okay to use. I also use Tret on my skin, to even the tone and try and get rid of those skin-colored bumps especially on the chin area.





There is no pump on this, but a squeeze tube, which is easy to dispense the product. I also like that there is no fragrance. The moisturize easily spreads on my fingers and I tap on my skin. It helps with the tightness a bit, although I still see some of the flaky dry areas still flaky.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text: I’ve been through 2 or 3 of these and keep coming back, even if I try to find less expensive options. This one is just special. I’m very, very oily mind you and it’s probably not great for dry or even combo skin. But this moisturizes just enough for me and leaves no shine. It’s very lightweight and perfect under SPF and makeup! I’m sure I’ll continue to purchase this, but I really wish PC wasn’t so pricey

reviews.author: Rachel
reviews.text: This is my FAVORITE moisturizer. I wasn't sure if it actually was the culprit to clearing up my skin until I ran out and tried using a different product for a few weeks. It keeps my skin extremely hydrated, never oily, and I use it in the morning and at night. I don't think I'll use anything else EVER.

Perfect prep for makeup: hydrates deeply and prevents any peeling or unevenness.


The moisturizer is unscented, but I lean towards moisturizers with a pleasant fragrance.

reviews.author: Kindle Customer
reviews.text: Never write a review but had to for this. Great product, only moisturizer that has never made me break out.

couche3.note: hydrate bien, top sèche, léger non gras
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective, noting it hydrates well and absorbs quickly without leaving a greasy feel. They appreciate its lightweight texture, silky smoothness, and how it works as a game-changer for dry skin, keeping it feeling good throughout the day. Customers like that it's fragrance-free and consider it worth the price.

reviews.author: Joey
reviews.text:
I bought this because I have extremely dry, very sensitive skin and most moisturizers either sting, break me out, or just sit on top without actually helping. I was mainly looking for something that could get me through the entire day without constant reapplication.





This cream has been the most reliable option I’ve found. It’s rich without feeling greasy, absorbs well, and actually keeps my skin comfortable all day. With other moisturizers, I’d be reaching for more by midday, especially in colder or drier weather. With this one, I apply it once in the morning and my skin still feels hydrated hours later. It applies great.





The lack of scent is a big plus. Fragrance tends to irritate my skin, and this hasn’t caused any redness or reaction. It layers well under sunscreen and makeup, which isn’t always the case with thicker creams.





The price is undeniably high, and that’s the biggest downside. That said, a little goes a long way, and for how well it performs, I’ve decided it’s worth it for me. If you have very dry, sensitive skin and can manage the cost, this is a solid investment.

reviews.author: Megan B.
reviews.text: I started using this years ago but have switched at times to different moisturizers just to try something new and/or cheaper. But I always come back to the Ultra Facial Cream and really should just stop getting sucked in by marketing for other creams. This cream is the only thing I’ve found that keeps my dry, mid-40s skin soft, smooth, and flake-free. While it feels light, it’s deeply moisturizing, it absorbs quickly, and it leaves my skin with a healthy glow - not greasy or matte. It layers well under sunscreen and over my retinol serum. While it is on the pricier side, a little goes a long way, and IMO it’s worth every penny. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: Eliazia Alhashmi
reviews.text: I’ve tried so many moisturizers over the years, and this is honestly the only lightweight facial cream that has truly worked for my sensitive skin. Most products either feel too heavy, cause irritation, or break me out. It absorbs quickly, doesn’t leave a greasy feeling, and keeps my skin hydrated all day. I also love that it doesn’t have a strong scent, which is a huge plus for sensitive skin like mine. My face feels smooth, calm, and balanced every time I use it.If you struggle with sensitivity and want something light but still really effective, I highly recommend giving this a try. I absolutely love it!

reviews.author: MoD
reviews.text: I've tried many moisturizers over the years, and this one is a favorite. It feels rich without being greasy and keeps my skin hydrated all day. My skin feels softer and smoother after using it, and it works well under makeup. A little goes a long way, making the jar last longer than expected. Definitely worth the splurge.

reviews.author: Candace Hurst
reviews.text:
This feels like a dream going on. No greasiness. Easily applied and worked into the skin. I have sensitive skin and so far have had absolutely no issues with burning or break outs.


I can tell my skin is much more moisturized. I can’t wait to see how much results turn out after more regular use.

couche3.note: polyvalent sèche+grasse, léger, sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective, noting it works well for both dry and oily skin types, and appreciate that it doesn't leave a greasy feeling. The product is lightweight, soft, and absorbs quickly, making it suitable for sensitive skin, and customers like that it layers well under makeup. While some customers consider it good value for money, others feel it's overpriced for its basic nature.

reviews.author: Ham Beef
reviews.text:
I’ve been using Clinique face lotion for a few weeks now, and I’m honestly so impressed. My skin feels incredibly hydrated without being greasy, which is hard to find. It absorbs quickly, layers well under makeup, and has noticeably improved the overall texture of my skin.





I also love that it’s gentle and doesn’t irritate my sensitive areas—no breakouts, no redness, just smooth, balanced skin. A little goes a long way, so the bottle lasts longer than expected.





If you’re looking for a reliable, lightweight moisturizer that actually delivers, this is definitely worth it. 5 stars!

reviews.author: Lily
reviews.text: Only complaint is that its a bit expensive for the size i got because it was so expensive, I didn’t realize I got 0.5 ounces, but at least I know now. The product quality is great. It really hydrates and softens my skin. It’s just very small for the price, unscented to so beautiful.

reviews.author: Hp
reviews.text: I have tried several different moisturizers, but this is just the one that works for me.  It is not a heavy moisturizer and it doesn’t block my pores.  Not irritating to my skin and the price is reasonable.  I find it last a long time and a little goes a long way.

reviews.author: Brandi Norris
reviews.text: I love this stuff!!! I’ve been using this moisturizer on and off for well over a decade and now that I’m in my 40’s, it suites my skin better than ever.  I have oily skin and this doesn’t feel heavy or break me out.

reviews.author: Melissa
reviews.text:
I have been using this daily since I was in my 20s. I turn 70 this year abd people always think I’m younger.





It has no scent and nothing to burn or irritate you—great for sunburned skin. Men use it, too.

couche3.note: apaisant rosacée/sensible/sèche, anti-rougeurs
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this skin cream hydrating and effective, particularly for dry patches and rosacea, with one noting it works almost immediately. They appreciate its suitability for sensitive and damaged skin, and its ability to alleviate redness and irritation. Customers describe the cream as soft, smooth, and not too heavy or greasy, while also being gentle and soothing. Opinions on value for money are mixed, with some finding it a good value while others consider it expensive.

reviews.author: Elizabeth Browning
reviews.text:
I so wish I had known about this skincare line a long time ago, but just glad I did find it at all. I have rosacea (including ocular rosacea) diagnosed by a dermatologist. I had been through all the typical stores and websites, trying so many things over the last 15 years. Yes, most were supposed to be for people with skin problems. EVERY SINGLE TIME I tried something, within five minutes, my skin was bright red. My skin reacted to EVERYTHING. I started to believe there was not one single product on the entire planet that my skin would not react to! It was so frustrating. Purchase, fail, repeat. Thing is, I had tried their spring water spray. I sort of ignored all the other products until I was talking to someone randomly and they asked if I had tried Avene. Honestly I decided maybe this was providence and that I had better try it just to be on the safe side. What would life be like if I had ignored their advice and not tried it? If this company ever goes out of business, I will have to live with terrible skin because I already know there is nothing else out there. I had read a review whereby someone said their doctor told them the longer they used the products, the better their skin would get. This is completely true. But even initially, all I cared was that I could relieve the dryness with SOMETHING that did not turn my skin RED IMMEDIATELY!  Even THAT, I would have been happy with! Now for the rest of the benefits. I had the good intuition to purchase two of these when they were on sale. I am so glad I did. I use it like crazy every chance I get. It just feels so good going on and I like touching my skin even after the product dissipates. No redness, no irritation, it actually CALMS my skin. And makes it like, well, "real skin." I'm glad I also got the thicker version in the tube, Cicalfate. This is my go-to for dryer areas and in the winter. The two together along with the water spray, if you like to use something like that, are a great overall program. Definitely other things I would like to try. Again, my goal was to help others who have skin problems because everything else out there has no concept whatsoever of calming the skin, but the exact opposite -- irritating it worse (even if those products are supposed to help with redness or irritation). And I felt the company truly deserves a shout-out because they GET IT when it comes to skincare products.  Whatever they've done, they are experts, far more than all the other skincare lines and I don't care how long those have been around. If it doesn't immediately tell you your skin "likes it" and shows it by not reacting or immediately getting better, why on earth would you pay your hard-earned money to buy it and keep using it?  It's worth every penny, don't bat an eyelash, your skin is worth that and more.  If it's on sale, get a lot of it because trust me, you are going to want and need it. You will be so glad you got more than one. I definitely am! THANK YOU, AVENE, WE LOVE YOU HERE IN AMERICA! YOU UNDERSTAND SKINCARE LIKE NO OTHER COMPANY!





Update:  Wanted to comment that I'm still so grateful for a product that does FAR MORE than what you expect! I recently bought more. I watch my skin calm down the minute I put it on. I don't know entirely how the ingredients do what they do, but to the company ... please don't fix what isn't broken! Too many companies have a good thing and then change it.  Please don't, the products are perfect and far beyond expectation!  I also wanted to mention it seems the Tolerance tube will get EVERY LAST BIT out, you won't be wondering if there is anything left you are discarding. The Cicalfate tube is a bit different, it's more like a squeeze tube.  But I feel I can still get the product out to use it all.  That said, you get your money's worth. I'm also starting to use the Cicalfate on my hands in the winter, too (which are sadly starting to show my age). But this product line has literally CHANGED MY LIFE! BUY IT, it's the REAL DEAL!

reviews.author: A Humble Opinion
reviews.text:
This is my go-to cream whenever I have redness or skin reactions and need "recovery" time from other beauty products, like retinols or active moisturizers. It is especially awesome after a visit to the esthetician and the removal of milia. I recently had an overabundance and made an appointment where it took her over an hour to remove 75% of them - I couldn't handle any more - leaving me to look like Frankenstein and his brides scary child. But I used this with some bio-oil only on my face for three days and everything healed beautifully.


I've used this product for years when I have reactions. It's a bit pricy for every day use, but I always keep it on hand, especially in the winter when my sensitive skin reacts more often. It's supposed to be save for patients who have skin irritation from cancer and radiation treatment as well. Very gentle, not greasy, absorbs very quickly and completely unscented and fragrance free.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
I’ve been using Avène tolerance control for a few weeks now, and the difference in my skin is unbelievable. The texture is rich without being heavy, and it absorbs beautifully. My skin feels soothed, hydrated, and noticeably softer from the very first application.





What I love most is how gentle and calming it is—especially on days when my skin feels irritated or sensitive. Redness is reduced, dry patches are gone, and my complexion looks healthier overall. It feels like the product truly nourishes instead of just sitting on the surface.





If you’re looking for a reliable moisturizer that delivers both comfort and visible results, Avène is absolutely worth trying. I’m very happy with it and will definitely continue using it!

reviews.author: Jeanie
reviews.text:
I absolutely love the Avène Tolerance Control Soothing Skin Recovery Cream — it’s become a sanctuary for my sensitive skin. From the very first application, I noticed how calming and gentle it feels. The texture is rich yet lightweight, absorbing beautifully without any greasiness.





This cream has been a game‑changer for days when my skin feels irritated, reactive, or overwhelmed. It instantly soothes redness and discomfort, and over time my skin feels stronger, more balanced, and resilient. What really stands out is how comfortable and nourished my skin feels all day long — no tightness, no irritation, just pure relief.

reviews.author: Tiffany
reviews.text: I have fair, sensitive skin & since being in peri-menopause I suddenly have rosacea. This cream is SO gentle on the skin but so instantly calming. It hydrates so well & a little goes a long way. I will be buying this over & over from now on!

name: Belif Aqua Bomb (ex « True Cream Aqua Bomb »)
ingredients: Water/Eau, Dipropylene Glycol, Glycerin, Methyl Trimethicone, Alcohol Denat., Dimethicone, Cyclopentasiloxane, 1,2-Hexanediol, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride, Peg/Ppg/Polybutylene Glycol-8/5/3 Glycerin, Pentaerythrityl Tetraethylhexanoate, Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Extract*, Calendula Officinalis Flower Extract*, Nepeta Cataria Extract*, Rubus Idaeus (Raspberry) Leaf Extract*, Baptisia Tinctoria Root Extract*, Stellaria Media (Chickweed) Extract*†, Alchemilla Vulgaris Leaf Extract†, Equisetum Arvense Leaf Extract†, Urtica Dioica (Nettle) Leaf Extract†, Sodium Hyaluronate, Niacinamide, Polymethylsilsesquioxane, Panthenol, Peg-150, Pentaerythrityl Tetraisostearate, Phenyl Trimethicone, Hydroxyethyl Acrylate/Sodium Acryloyldimethyl Taurate Copolymer, Carbomer, Tromethamine, Squalane, Peg-40 Hydrogenated Castor Oil, Dimethiconol, Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer, C14-22 Alcohols, Polysorbate 60, Butylene Glycol, Macadamia Ternifolia Seed Oil, Malachite Extract, Triethylhexanoin, Trisodium Edta, Arachidyl Glucoside, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter, Hydrogenated Lecithin, Sorbitan Isostearate, Glyceryl Stearate, Stearic Acid, Ceramide Np, Cholesterol, Peg-100 Stearate, Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Leaf Oil‡, Citrus Aurantifolia (Lime) Oil ‡, Pelargonium Graveolens Flower Oil ‡, Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Peel Oil ‡, Citral , Citronellol , Limonene, Geraniol, Linalool. *Napiers Original Formula †Napiers Aqua Formula ‡Fragrances Of Botanical Origin

couche3.note: gel hydratant léger, dewy, polyvalent
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective at hydrating dry skin, with a smooth, non-greasy texture that feels light on the face. The product absorbs quickly into the skin and creates a cute dewy glow, with one customer noting it evens out their complexion. While many customers consider the price worth it, opinions about value for money are mixed.

reviews.author: L.Cho
reviews.text:
Belif is one of my all-time favorite skincare brands, and I’ve been using their products for almost 10 years, so you can imagine how much I trust them. This moisturizer is an irreplaceable, must-have product in my routine!





I’ve been using Belif The True Cream Aqua Bomb for years, and it’s always been my go-to moisturizer, especially during the summer months. The reason I love this product is its lightweight, refreshing texture. It doesn't feel sticky at all, which is a big plus when the weather gets warmer. The formula provides just the right amount of moisture, leaving my skin feeling hydrated and soft without any greasy residue.





I’ve probably used more than 10 jars (100ml) over the years, and it’s always been consistent in providing the hydration I need. Even though it’s a gel-cream consistency, it gives my skin a decent amount of moisture and nourishment, making it perfect for those hot and humid days. I feel that my skin looks plump and refreshed after using it, and it absorbs quickly, so it’s ideal for layering with other skincare products.





As an added tip, this cream works great when paired with the Aqua Bomb Toner from the same line for even better hydration and a refreshed feel. Unfortunately, I just finished my jar yesterday and threw it away, so I don’t have a picture to share this time.





Just a note: This is not a nourishing or regenerative cream, so I recommend using it when you’re in need of hydration rather than for deep nourishment or skin regeneration.





If you’re looking for a lightweight, non-greasy moisturizer with a fresh, cooling effect that hydrates deeply, I highly recommend giving this a try!

reviews.author: Sarah Morgan
reviews.text:
I’ve tried so many moisturizers in the last couple of years, but I LOVE this one. I got it for the first time a few years ago as part of an Ulta promotion. It was the little, travel-sized pot. I didn’t buy more for the longest time because of the price, but I finally bit the bullet. The price is seriously worth it!





I used Olay Regenerist before switching; the heavy scent and thick cream feeling just sat poorly on my face, but this Belif Aqua Bomb is so REFRESHING. It’s like Neutrogena’s Hydro Boost moisturizer in terms of consistency/viscosity but ten times better. It feels so silky, light and cool but isn’t oily. Now with the Aqua Bomb, my skin stay moisturized all day without overloading it. (I have sensitive combination skin and am 30 years-old.)





If you’re not sure about it, get the smallest one and try it. Totally worth it and now a forever staple in my routine.

reviews.author: F
reviews.text: One of my favorite daily creams. It has a gentle scent with a hint of lemon that is not overpowering. It is a lightweight cream but highly nourishing and hydrating. It absorbs in just a few seconds. I love it because I can use it in all seasons: it protects my skin in below zero temperatures with heavy snowfall, but in summer it is so light that it doesn’t feel greasy or make me sweat. A real gem of a cream.

reviews.author: Hilary Skelton
reviews.text: My favorite daily moisturizer! I’ve been through about four jars already. Doesn’t irritate my skin, very light fragrance with a non-greasy feel. I started layering it over my Peach & Lily serum and my skin is never dry!

reviews.author: Nicole K
reviews.text: Refreshing and hydrating, not sticky and hydration lasts

couche3.note: léger top peau grasse/été, dewy
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective, particularly noting its suitability for oily skin and summer use. The cream receives positive feedback for its silky texture, lightweight feel, and ability to make skin look radiant with a dewy glow. Customers appreciate that it's gentle on sensitive skin and doesn't leave a greasy residue, while the divine fragrance adds to its appeal. Opinions on value are mixed, with some finding it worth the price while others consider it too expensive.

reviews.author: KREOLE Troi
reviews.text:
I absolutely love the TATCHA “The Water”cream moisturizer. It’s lightweight, absorbs quickly, and leaves my skin feeling refreshed without any greasy residue. I’ve added it to my daily facial routine, and it makes my skin look smooth and hydrated throughout the day.





The texture is silky and cooling, which makes application feel like a little luxury. I appreciate that it layers well under makeup and keeps my skin balanced. Overall, this moisturizer is a wonderful addition to any skincare routine, worth every penny for the quality and results.

reviews.author: Kelsea
reviews.text: Great lightweight moisturizer. It wasn't a perfect match for me because I ended up needing something a little thicker for the dry patches on my face, but I used the rest of this as my morning moisturizer and it worked perfectly. It absorbs well, was great for my sensitive skin, and I liked how it smells. The only downside is how expensive Tatcha is as a brand, but if you have the extra money, I really feel that it's worth the splurge.

reviews.author: Denise
reviews.text: The product is great. It's not greasy, goes on smooth.  Leaves my skin soft. I gave it 4 stars because the jar I got before was bigger. The both say 50ml, 1.7oz, but I don't know why the jar is small than the first one I bought.  It makes me wonder if I really got the 50ml in the small  bottle.

reviews.author: Anagka
reviews.text: It’s really hard to believe, but this actually does quench my dry skin without giving the feeling of yucky cream. It goes on so easily and absorbs right into the dry spots while remaining as light as can be.

reviews.author: elizabeth carson
reviews.text: TTATCHA really makes skin feel smooth, and looks great.

name: Drunk Elephant Lala Retro Whipped Cream

couche3.note: Creme riche tres hydratante, ideale peau seche/mature et lhiver ; huiles potentiellement comedogenes pour peau grasse ; nombreuses plaintes de contrefacon sur Amazon ; chere.
couche3.customers_say: Reconstruit depuis les avis : creme hydratante riche tres appreciee, ideale peau seche et en hiver, texture fouettee non grasse, parfum leger ou absent. Bemols : tres chere, une huile peut obstruer les pores (peau grasse), et de nombreux acheteurs signalent des contrefacons/colis abimes.

reviews.author: Mandi Mills
reviews.text:
I’ve tried so many moisturizers over the years, but Drunk Elephant Lala Retro Whipped Cream is the only one my face actually takes. From the first use, it felt rich and luxurious without feeling greasy or heavy. A little really does go a long way—the tiny jar lasts me a few months even with daily use. I love that it’s packed with ceramides, fragrance-free, and free of silicones and essential oils, so it feels clean and gentle on my skin while still giving serious hydration.





After a few weeks of using it consistently, my skin feels softer, smoother, and much more balanced. Even on dry or rough patches, it soothes and nourishes without causing any breakouts or irritation. I love that it’s cruelty-free and dermatologist-tested, so I feel good about putting it on every day. This moisturizer has earned a permanent spot in my routine—I won’t go back to anything else.

reviews.author: Jay
reviews.text: This my favorite moisturizing cream of all time. It is a bit expensive but when I tell you it makes your skin feel like butter...I am not kidding. I was introduced to this product as a sample in sephora years ago and have been hooked ever since.  And I am someone who has done a lot of in-depth research into skincare, almost to the scholarly level. For reference I have oily, slightly sensitive and acne prone skin.

reviews.author: Carole Stine
reviews.text: This cream has helped me so much with my eczema I would highly recommend if you struggle with it as well. You don't need much and one of these lasted from July 2025 to Jan 2026, it's not too thin and not to thick. It's really good quality for the price.

reviews.author: Diane D
reviews.text:
Wonderful Face Cream!  This cream goes on light and leaves your skin feeling so soft!


it feels nourished and moist.  It has a great Scent as well!

reviews.author: donna mitchell
reviews.text: Feels like a luscious layer of moisture that’s needed. No scent , a little goes a long way. I will buy again. I put 4 stars  because it’s pricey

name: Weleda Skin Food
ingredients: Water Helianthus Annuus (Sunflower) Seed Oil Lanolin Prunus Amygdalus Dulcis (Sweet Almond) Oil Beeswax (Cera Alba) Alcohol Alcohol Polyglyceryl-3 Polyricinoleate Glycerin Limonene Viola Tricolor Extract Hydrolyzed Beeswax Sorbitan Olivate Rosemary Leaf Extract Chamomilla Recutita (Matricaria) Flower Extract Calendula Flower Extract Arginine Zinc Sulfate Fragrance Linalool Geraniol Citral Coumarin

couche3.note: baume très riche top très sèche; lourd/collant → pas grasse
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective, particularly for dry skin and hands, and appreciate that it doesn't irritate sensitive skin. The texture receives mixed feedback - while some say it feels soft and luxurious, others find it heavy and sticky. The fragrance, thickness, and greasiness are also mixed aspects, with some finding it divine-smelling and thick, while others describe it as strong-smelling and too heavy.

reviews.author: BA
reviews.text:
I’ve used Weleda lip balm in the pink container for over a decade. It’s the only thing I put on my lips.





But never bought Skin Food because i never needed extra oil or moisturizing (ive always had normal skin).





But now, in my 50s, i became alarmed to see my facial skin becoming unusually dry. Tight dry facial skin. Very uncomfortable but also it looked awful.





I suspect a body wash i started using caused this, moreso than hormonal changes. Because for over a decade I've only used a specific glycerin based  bar soap. But i switched to a body wash last month and i do believe it dried my skin out badly.





Anyway, it freaked me out, so i bought this and within 3 days of use my facial skin looks and feels so much better. Supple, moisturized,  and luminous.





Totally back to normal, if not an improvement.





I am not a claiming miracles, but as for the restorative and moisturizing benefits, I have seen for myself what Skin Food can do.  It works.





I will always keep this on hand.





One month UPDATE:





Still very happy. About to order more.


I use it on my face, neck, hands, and feet, daily, but will be using something else on my feet from now on, so I can stretch it out for two months at a time.





Love.

reviews.author: MS
reviews.text: I absolutely love this product. I find it to have a pleasant, mild smell and a nice consistency. It is thick but spreads really nicely and gives kind of a sheen to the skin. It absorbs quite well on the body and face. Compared to other plant based creams I’ve used, this one is much less greasy and leaves the skin nicely moisturized without any after effects. I have discovered a specific use for it that cancels the need out for another cosmetic product, namely brow gel. I find this leaves my eyebrows softened and keeps them in place, also gives a bit of shine that is even nicer than the eyebrow gel I’ve bought from sephora. I especially like that Weleda Skin food products are gentle enough to use as an eye cream when you need that extra hydration to fight winter dryness.

reviews.author: TAC
reviews.text: At age 65 I was introduced to Weleda by a professional makeup artist who used this as a primer under my makeup. Not only has my makeup looked amazing... so many compliments... but, my skin does too!! The product is quite thick, so you need to rub it on your finger-tips to soften it up. I pat it on, bc rubbing it on pulls my skin. A little goes a long way, fyi. My skin soaks up the moisture which Weldeda refers to as 'Skin Food', AND IT IS. PTL. Personally, I love the fragrance; it is very outdoorsy and calming. Lastly, do not get me started on the price! It is so affordable! Big name moisterizers, not to mention primers, can cost 4 to 5 times the price. For that reason alone this product is worth a try.

reviews.author: lchalley
reviews.text: So nice. Thick and creamy with a faint clean citrus-vanilla-grass scent. Great on backs of hands and shins, feet, cheekbones and around mouth (or wherever you’re dry) in winter.  Absorbs quickly for how thick it is, leaves the faintest glow. I haven’t had good results layering over other lotions, it gets sticky as a top layer. Works best for me as a stand-alone, even with the other products in line (light moisture face lotion, body lotion, and body butter). Highly recommend for dry skin.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text: I love it! I put it on my face at night before bed. It’s thick but it absorbs well. Great moisturizer and gives you a good glow. I only wish there were scent options. The lemony smell isn’t for me, but I deal with it because I like the product.

name: CeraVe Hydrating Mineral Sunscreen SPF 30 (Face)
ingredients: ACTIVE INGREDIENTS: TITANIUM DIOXIDE (6%), ZINC OXIDE (5%) INACTIVE INGREDIENTS: WATER, GLYCERIN, C12-15 ALKYL BENZOATE, DIMETHICONE, GLYCERYL STEARATE, ISODODECANE, BUTYLOCTYL SALICYLATE, STYRENE/ACRYLATES COPOLYMER, PROPANEDIOL, STEARIC ACID, PEG-100 STEARATE, SORBITAN STEARATE, NIACINAMIDE, PEG-8 LAURATE, CERAMIDE NP, CERAMIDE AP, CERAMIDE EOP, SORBITAN ISOSTEARATE, CARBOMER, CETEARYL ALCOHOL, CETEARETH-20, TRIETHOXYCAPRYLYLSILANE, DIMETHICONOL, SODIUM CITRATE, SODIUM LAUROYL LACTYLATE, SODIUM DODECYLBENZENESULFONATE, MYRISTIC ACID, SODIUM HYALURONATE, CHOLESTEROL,ALUMINUM HYDROXIDE, PALMITIC ACID, PHENOXYETHANOL, CHLORPHENESIN, HYDROXYETHYL ACRYLATE/SODIUM ACRYLOYLDIMETHYL TAURATE COPOLYMER, CAPRYLYL GLYCOL, CITRIC ACID, PANTHENOL, XANTHAN GUM, PHYTOSPHINGOSINE, POLYHYDROXYSTEARIC ACID, POLYSORBATE 60, ETHYLHEXYLGLYCERIN

couche3.note: SPF minéral sensible; white cast + épais (prudence grasse)
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the sunscreen effective for sensitive skin protection and appreciate that it's dermatologist-recommended. The product receives mixed feedback regarding its texture - while some find it non-greasy and easy to apply, others mention it's tough to rub in. Moreover, customers disagree on the moisturizing properties, with some saying it provides good hydration while others report dryness. The white cast is a significant concern for many customers.

reviews.author: Angel
reviews.text:
When it comes to sunscreen, the market is saturated with options claiming to deliver sun protection with a side of skincare. But does CeraVe’s 100% Mineral Sunscreen SPF 30 deliver more than just promises? Let’s dive into this travel-sized powerhouse that’s redefining the way we think about sun care.





Packaging and Portability: The Travel Companion You Deserve





At 2.5 oz, this sunscreen is perfectly sized for life on the go—whether you’re jet-setting across continents or heading to the gym. Its sleek design ensures it won’t take up unnecessary space in your bag, while the screw-top cap prevents any dreaded sunscreen explosions. Travel-size convenience meets full-size efficacy.





Formulation: Science in a Tube





CeraVe isn’t just a skincare brand—it’s a science lab bottled up for your face. This sunscreen combines zinc oxide (10%) and titanium dioxide (5.5%), offering broad-spectrum UV protection that blocks both UVA (aging rays) and UVB (burning rays). But what truly sets it apart is the marriage of sun defense with a lineup of dermatologist-approved skincare superstars:


1. Hyaluronic Acid: Keeps your skin hydrated and plump, battling the dryness mineral sunscreens can sometimes cause.


2. Niacinamide: Calms redness, minimizes pores, and gives your skin that smooth, even finish.


3. Ceramides: These unsung heroes repair and reinforce your skin barrier, ensuring you don’t just survive under the sun but thrive.





Texture and Application: A Lesson in Patience





Unlike chemical sunscreens that vanish instantly, this mineral formula takes its time. Upon application, you’ll notice a slight white cast—a badge of authenticity that proves its mineral origins. The trick is to warm the sunscreen between your fingers and apply it in layers, like building a skincare masterpiece. The result? A velvety matte finish that doubles as a primer under makeup.





Oil-Free but Full of Love





If your skin tends to produce enough oil to fry an egg, CeraVe’s oil-free formulation is your saving grace. It sits lightly, doesn’t clog pores, and won’t trigger breakouts—a godsend for sensitive and acne-prone skin.





Performance: Under the Sun and Beyond





From scorching beach days to winter hikes, this sunscreen holds its ground. It doesn’t sweat off or melt into oblivion, and its water-resistant formula lasts through most outdoor adventures (though you should always reapply every two hours). Unlike many mineral sunscreens that feel heavy, this one breathes, making it suitable for all seasons.





The Good, the Great, and the Room for Growth





The Good: The blend of UV protection and skincare ingredients feels like you’re doing your skin a favor with every application.


The Great: Its versatility—it’s gentle enough for kids, safe for the most sensitive skin, and an ideal first step for skincare minimalists.


Room for Growth: The initial white cast might deter those with deeper skin tones, but with proper blending, it eventually dissipates.





Final Thoughts: More Than Just Sunscreen





CeraVe 100% Mineral Sunscreen SPF 30 isn’t just a sunscreen—it’s a daily commitment to protecting your skin while improving its health. It’s the rare product that understands that skincare is about balance: protecting what’s outside and nurturing what’s within.





Whether you’re a sunscreen skeptic or a devotee, this zinc-and-titanium duo deserves a permanent spot in your routine. In a world where skincare can often feel overwhelming, CeraVe brings us back to basics—with an edge.

reviews.author: TheyCallMeMacGyver
reviews.text: One of the more reasonably priced options. It’s a mineral sunscreen. Don’t dab it all over and expect to double back and spread it out. Doesn’t work that way. Get it on and spread it as quickly as possible. It turns into a dried on film fairly quickly so you’re in a race. Works as advertised. Lasts all day, even when in the water. Leaves a bit of a white sheen sorta. Kinda actually gives a purplish tone on Caucasian skin.  It’s not purple by any means. It’s kinda funny seeing all kinds of people all walking around with their purpley (is that a word?) self. Getting it off in the shower is an experience too.

reviews.author: Nancy
reviews.text: Great sunscreen for people will sensitive skin! This is one of the very few sunscreens that does not cause my face to break out. The bottle arrived super quickly and was actually bigger than expected! The only reason this did not get 5 stars from me is because applying the product it was so thick, the sunscreen never really disappeared, it left a white tint to the skin even if you continued and continued to rub it in, at least it was protective! A positive for this sunscreen is that a little did go along way, hopefully it will last me awhile then!

reviews.author: Tina Wallace
reviews.text: Great product. Does not melt into eyes as day goes, wears very comfortable and leaves face looking nice after wear. Of course leaves face a little white while wearing.

reviews.author: Ralph Davis
reviews.text: Rubs  on white, and very difficult to rub the white away.  Probably very good protection from the sun, but leaves a white residue

ingredients: Active Ingredients: Avobenzone (3%), Homosalate (15%), Octisalate (5%), Octocrylene (10%) Inactive Ingredients: Water, Butyloctyl Salicylate, Styrene/Acrylates Copolymer, Silica, Dimethicone, Potassium Cetyl Phosphate, Benzyl Alcohol, Beeswax, Caprylyl Methicone, Aluminum Starch Octenylsuccinate, Glyceryl Stearate, PEG-100 Stearate, Cetyl Dimethicone, Caprylyl Glycol, Ethylhexylglycerin, Behenyl Alcohol, Acrylates/Dimethicone Copolymer, Xanthan Gum, Chlorphenesin, Dimethicone/PEG-10/15 Crosspolymer, Sodium Polyacrylate, Hydrolyzed Jojoba Esters, Fragrance, Disodium EDTA, Ethylhexyl Stearate, Tocopheryl Acetate, BHT, Jojoba Esters, Trideceth-6

couche3.customers_say: Customers find this sunscreen to be the best they've used, providing reliable protection and working well under makeup. The product is easy to apply and doesn't feel greasy or leave a white cast, though opinions on these aspects are mixed. Skin compatibility receives mixed feedback - while some say it doesn't irritate their skin, others report it stings their eyes. The fragrance also gets mixed reviews, with some noting it has no unpleasant scent while others find it very strong.

reviews.author: David Velasquez Henao
reviews.text: Excellent product. The sun is extremely strong where I live, and I've been using this for several days now; it lasts a very long time on the skin. However, if you have very sensitive skin, I wouldn't recommend using it on your face—in my case, it caused a breakout. But for the neck, body, and arms, it’s excellent. I highly recommend it, and it doesn't leave a bad feel on the skin or an unpleasant smell. If you use it in the water, it is recommended to reapply every hour and 20 minutes, otherwise you might get sunburned.

reviews.author: Linda NYC
reviews.text: First day, I tried it on my face. I had to immediately wash if off with cleanser because It burned my eyes really badly. The irritation lasted all day, even though I'd removed the sunscreen. A week later, I tried it on my forearms. When I brushed my hair out of my eyes, the proximity of the sunscreen to my eyes made them burn and water again. So I gave the tube to a friend whose eyes are not sensitive to chemical sunscreens, and she loves it!  No white cast, moisturizing, and it works.

reviews.author: Mr.Dependable:)
reviews.text:
As a bald dude, I fine the fact that it is matt good. Check it before you leave or, if you use to much,  looks like white face upon drydown and in dry areas. I like it. I do. And it protected me from the hot sun of the middle east. No burn.


Just needs a second check after about 10 minutes. But very good at what it does!!!

reviews.author: Natalia
reviews.text: It does provide good protection from the sun but it can look white-ish on certain skin colors.

reviews.author: Chip James
reviews.text: Great sunscreen!  Goes on smooth and light and absorbs quickly.  Use on my face daily.

name: Beauty of Joseon Relief Sun SPF 50
ingredients: Water, Rice Fermentation Liquid, Pumpkin Fermentation Extract, Soybean Fermentation Extract

couche3.note: SPF hydratant sous maquillage; peu d'avis
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the sunscreen effective, with one noting it's a great option for daily use. The product is hydrating, with one customer mentioning their skin feels smooth and hydrated after application, and they like its appearance, with one noting it works well under makeup. The texture receives positive feedback, with customers saying it feels great after application. Regarding skin irritation, customers have mixed experiences.

reviews.author: Danileidy Alvarez Monzon
reviews.text: I really like this sunscreen. It feels lightweight on the skin, blends in easily, and doesn’t leave a white cast. My skin feels hydrated and smooth after using it, and it works well under makeup too. Great option for daily use, especially if you have dry or sensitive skin.

reviews.author: Patricia
reviews.text: I tried this rice sunscreen for the face. I’m not impressed. I didn’t care for it. It’s very light it absorbs fast. I didn’t care for. It will not waste my money no longer on this brand.

reviews.author: MHB
reviews.text: this is great.. it really works well... has a very light moisture to your skin... not greasy at all.. can be worn all over the face including the eyelids.. I will buy this again

reviews.author: Orlando Miguel
reviews.text: Desde la aplicación sentí que es muy bueno

reviews.author: Dariela Hernandez
reviews.text: Perfect

couche3.customers_say: Customers find this sunscreen effective and appreciate that it doesn't leave a white cast on dark skin. Moreover, the product is gentle on skin, works well under makeup, and provides a beautiful, all-day glow. Additionally, they like its smooth, non-greasy texture and moisturizing properties, with one customer noting it's particularly suitable for dry skin.

reviews.author: SJ
reviews.text:
Too oily and made my eyes burn.





Update:6/10/26- To be fair, I wanted to give this sunscreen another try and was pleasantly surprised. There was no burning/stinging eye sensation that I initially experienced.


I also learned to not slather so much product on my face to reduce oiliness, flushing and prevent breakouts. This sunscreen can give a nice glow with just a small amount (at least for me).





* I have rosacea and live where it’s hot and humid most days.*

reviews.author: Felicia Riley
reviews.text: For someone with a darker complexion, this is a really good sunscreen because it doesn’t leave a white cast or hue. It goes on light and smooth, and it feels comfortable on the skin. The coverage is good, and it provides great protection. Overall, I highly recommend it and would definitely continue using it.

reviews.author: Customer Karey-Ann
reviews.text: This was a good addition to my routine. It doesn't leave any residue, is easy to use, and smells nice. I haven't noticed any skin sensitivity issues and it seems to absorb pretty well. It doesn't make my skin oily. Lovely to find one that doesn't leave a film of white over the skin.

reviews.author: ShebaDiva
reviews.text: My son loves this sunscreen.  He has tried them all and this one checks all the boxes.  First, it doesn't leave a grey tint.  Second, it is very moisturizing (he is taking Accutane which dries his skin).  Finally, it really works and protects his face.  This is a 100/10.

reviews.author: GHD
reviews.text: No white cast left  on beautifully melenated skin. I love the light glow it leaves. Even though I moisture my face too, my skin feels so soft, hydrated and protected. Customer for life.

name: Vanicream Facial Moisturizer SPF 30
ingredients: Zinc oxide 19.5%; Purified water, C12-15 alkyl benzoate, isopentyldiol, squalane, glycerin, coco-caprylate/caprate, bis-octyldodecyl dimer dilinoleate/propanediol copolymer, glyceryl stearate, PEG-100 stearate, polyhydroxystearic acid, cetearyl alcohol, sucrose stearate, ceramide EOP, ceramide NG, ceramide NP, ceramide AS, ceramide AP, carnosine, phytosterols, hydrogenated lecithin, dimethicone, triethoxycaprylylsilane, polyacrylate crosspolymer-11, caprylyl glycol, 1,2-hexanediol

couche3.note: SPF minéral sèche/sensible; texture lourde (prudence grasse)
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the moisturizer effective for dry and sensitive skin, with good sun protection through mineral sunscreen. The texture receives mixed feedback - while some describe it as silky, others find it heavy and greasy. The absorption rate is also debated, with some saying it absorbs quickly while others report it doesn't absorb well. The white cast is a concern for some customers, though many note it doesn't leave a noticeable white cast. Skin sensitivity reactions are mixed, with some finding it non-irritating while others report burning sensations.

reviews.author: Lakes
reviews.text:
I’ve only been using this moisturizer with zinc oxide (sun protection) for a couple of weeks, and so far it’s great. Glides on smoothly, unlike thick sunscreens that can get blotchy, and it absorbs quickly. My skin feels very soft and fine lines disappear, so I know the moisturizer is working. I’ve had problems with the drying effects of sunscreen in the past. No discernible scent, which is a plus.





It does leave a faint whitish cast that disappears after a minute or two, and my skin tone looks even. The very faint “glow” or hint of zinc oxide reduces some of the redder spots on my sensitive skin, so I am able to use this alone as foundation. Makes it easy to reapply every two hours to continue the sun protection.





It’s important to remember that no sunscreen product lasts all day—always follow the directions and reapply to keep sun protection active.





I really like the safety rating this cream received from EWG—this is rare for sun protection products. It’s not perfect, but since I have to moisturize and use sunscreen, this product gives me all that without added worry about health and safety concerns.





I live in the Pacific Northwest and we haven’t had many full-sun days since I started using it, but so far the sunscreen factor seems to be working. Because this product glides on so easily I am able to avoid areas of missed coverage. I will update this review when I’ve tested it in the summer, but I expect that I’ll need a full sunscreen if I’m spending all day outside.





Reading other reviews, I’m concerned to see that people use this as both day and nighttime moisturizer. Zinc oxide is one of the safer sunscreen options, but give your skin and overall health a break by using this only during the day (even on cloudy days). Wash your face in the evening and apply a non-zinc moisturizer overnight. I think Vanicream makes one if you like the way this product feels.

reviews.author: Lulu
reviews.text: I’ve been in search for a good 2 in 1 moisturizer + sunscreen for a long time. I’ve tried other brands (Differin & Cetaphil) but they burned my eyes so badly and didn’t have good moisturizing power. Then I came across this! One good thing about this product is that it has mineral sunscreen without white cast. Chemical sunscreens are the culprit to my burning, teary eyes which I cannot stand. This does not burn my eyes at all, although I apply it pretty close to my eyes. Plus, it has amazing ingredients for a moisturizer. Squalane is one of the top ingredients which helps regulate sebum and lock in the moisture. And it’s packed with 5 different kinds of ceramides, which fortifies your skin barrier to keep it strong and healthy. I put it on as the last step of my morning skin care routine, lightly dab extra oil with a Kleenex, then start putting on makeup. It’s a fabulous base for all kinds of foundations; no cakiness, no smudging, just grips the foundation beautifully. The price point is super reasonable, too. This will be my staple from now on.

reviews.author: SY
reviews.text:
Used it this morning for the first time. So far I like it. Feels very gentle on my skin, did not irritate my skin in anyway. I have very sensitive, reactive skin. So I'm really glad I found this facial moisturizer with spf. Spf moisturizer is very important to me. I use the Vanicream vitamin C serum and their facial cleanser. I listened to a dermatologist and she recommended the brand Vanicream for sensitive skin people. So far I have loved their products. I have the regular daily face moisturizer arriving today so I can't wait to give that one a try too.


One thing I will say about this spf moisturizer, do NOT use if you have oily skin. This product is very moisturizing so it is perfect for someone with dry skin. A little goes a long way. Not pasty at all. When I first put it on I could feel the oily substance but it immediately absorbs into your skin. I've had it on for 2 hours now and it's completely settle in my skin and I can't feel any residue what's so ever. Not sticky at all.

reviews.author: Brie Olsen
reviews.text: I absolutely love my vanicream moisturizing lotion, daily facial moisturizer and now this if I know I’m going into the sun. It is thicker and whiter than the facial moisturizer. It doesn’t make your skin feel gross and sunscreen try though. It technically is fragrance free but smells a little like sunscreen because of the zinc oxide active ingredient. Easy to dispense with the cap on the bottom, easy to apply and no worry about leaks. Great value for the money.

reviews.author: Gina
reviews.text: Lightweight moisturizer that absorbs easily without feeling greasy. Works well under makeup and doesn’t irritate sensitive skin. No fragrance and very minimal formula, which makes it easy to use daily without issues. Good for everyday wear when you want basic hydration plus SPF in one step.

couche3.note: SPF fond bien non gras; léger white cast
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this sunscreen effective, particularly noting it works well under makeup and melts into the skin. The texture receives positive feedback for being silky and non-greasy, though some report it leaves a noticeable white cast. While some customers say it doesn't irritate their skin, others mention it stings their eyes, and opinions on value for money are divided between those who find it worth the price and those who consider it ineffective.

reviews.author: Amy Liesenfelt
reviews.text: This sunscreen was a game changer for me. I live in a sun, soaked climate year-round and finding a sunscreen to wear every day that’s not only healthy for your skin, but feels good and smells like the beach on your body has been a years long mission. La Roche-Posay sunscreen fills all of the gaps. I’ve been using this brand for a few years now and will never use anything else. I use this particular sunscreen for my body and the broad spectrum protection is fantastic. I spend a lot of of time in the ocean doing hours long activities and I’ve never gotten burned if I reapply as directed. It is easily removed with a gentle cleanser or soap unlike many zinc oxide brands, which still leave a smelly residue. This sunscreen is silky and absorbs very quickly and has very minimal transfer to darker clothing. I highly recommend this company and all of their products. I use their tinted facial sunscreen daily also and can cleanse my face fully and I do not get irritation or acne from it. Layering makeup on top of it is a breeze once it dries. This sunscreen is more costly, but the benefits are well worth it. For me, it’s worth every single penny.

reviews.author: Adrian
reviews.text:
recently tried La Roche-Posay sunscreen and I’m really impressed! 🌞 The texture is lightweight, non-greasy, and absorbs quickly without leaving a white cast, which is a big plus for daily wear. It feels gentle on my skin and doesn’t clog pores, making it perfect for sensitive or acne-prone skin. I also love that it provides strong, broad-spectrum protection while still feeling breathable. Overall, it’s become my go-to sunscreen—I actually enjoy applying it every morning because it feels more like skincare than sunscreen. Definitely worth it!





I would be honored if you would consider me to receive a PR package. I’d love the opportunity to try more of your products and create authentic content that highlights the benefits of La Roche-Posay skincare.





Thank you so much for your time and consideration!





Warm regards,


Andrea Mohanlall

reviews.author: Carmen Suárez
reviews.text:
I’ve been using this sunscreen for about a week now, and honestly, I’ve liked it even more than I expected. The skin on my face feels much softer and healthier, and one thing I’ve definitely noticed is that my dark circles are gradually looking a bit lighter and less tired. I love it because it doesn’t feel heavy, absorbs quickly, and doesn’t leave a greasy residue.





I also like that it works well under makeup or just on its own for everyday use. For now, I’m giving it 4 stars because I haven’t been using it for very long yet and want to see more results, but so far, I definitely feel a difference in my skin. If I continue to see more positive changes, I’ll come back to update my review.

reviews.author: Tzieleah Recla
reviews.text:
This sunscreen feels much lighter than many high-SPF formulas I’ve tried and blends into the skin without leaving a heavy white cast. The texture spreads easily and gives a hydrated finish without feeling greasy, which makes it comfortable for everyday wear on both the face and body. I also liked that it layered well under makeup without pilling.





I used this during long outdoor days and it held up well while still feeling comfortable on the skin. The SPF 60 protection is reassuring, especially for travel, beach days, festivals, or daily sun exposure. Overall, this is a reliable sunscreen option if you want strong protection with a more wearable, skincare-like texture.

reviews.author: Father of 3
reviews.text:
This sunscreen is definitely more expensive than many other options, but the quality makes it worth it. It goes on smoothly, absorbs well, and doesn't leave a heavy or greasy feeling on my skin. The SPF 60 protection gives me confidence when spending time outdoors, and it works well on both my face and body.





I've been using it regularly and have been very pleased with the results. If you're looking for a high-quality sunscreen and don't mind paying a little extra, I would recommend giving this one a try.

name: EltaMD UV Clear SPF 46
ingredients: Active Ingredients: Zinc oxide, Octinoxate Inactive Ingredients: Purified Water, Cyclomethicone, Niacinamide, Octyldodecyl Neopentanoate, Hydroxyethyl Acrylate/Sodium Acryloyldimethyl Taurate Copolymer, Polyisobutene, PEG-7 Trimethylolpropane Coconut Ether, Sodium Hyaluronate, Tocopheryl Acetate, Lactic Acid, Oleth-3 Phosphate, Phenoxyethanol, Butylene Glycol, Iodopropynyl Butylcarbamate, Triethoxycaprylylsilane

couche3.note: SPF top sensible/acné, léger non gras
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this sunscreen excellent for sun protection, particularly noting its effectiveness for sensitive and acne-prone skin. The product applies smoothly, feels light on the face, and is not greasy or oily. While some customers say it's worth the price, others find it too expensive, and opinions about the tint are mixed, with some appreciating it while others note a slight white cast. Customers disagree about skin irritation, with some reporting breakouts while others have no issues.

reviews.author: Kailash
reviews.text:
EltaMD UV Clear has become an essential part of my daily skincare routine, and I can't imagine going without it now. I was initially drawn to it because of the dermatologist recommendation and the zinc oxide formula, and after using it consistently, I can confidently say it lives up to the praise.





What sets this sunscreen apart from others I've tried is how lightweight and non-greasy it feels on the skin. It applies smoothly, absorbs quickly, and leaves no white cast, which has been a dealbreaker with other mineral sunscreens in the past. It works beautifully as a primer under makeup and doesn't interfere with any other skincare products I layer it with.





The oil-free formula is ideal for those with combination or acne-prone skin. I noticed that it doesn't clog pores or cause any breakouts, which was a concern I had when switching to a mineral-based SPF. The niacinamide in the formula is also a nice bonus for calming and evening out skin tone over time.





The SPF protection is solid and the formula is broad-spectrum, covering both UVA and UVB rays. I feel genuinely protected wearing this daily, whether I'm commuting or spending time outdoors.





The price is on the higher end for a sunscreen, but the quality justifies it. A little goes a long way, and the bottle lasts a reasonable amount of time. Highly recommend to anyone looking for a reliable, skin-friendly daily SPF that performs well under all conditions.

reviews.author: Paulson
reviews.text:
I’ve only been using this sunscreen for a few days, but so far it feels like the best sunscreen I’ve tried for my skin.
