It looks very natural, does not sting, and has not caused irritation, dryness, oiliness, clogged pores, or that uncomfortable heavy feeling some sunscreens leave behind. My skin still looks hydrated and healthy, but not shiny or greasy.





I’m also currently using prescription tretinoin for acne, so finding a sunscreen that feels gentle and comfortable is very important to me. This one works really well with my routine, and I honestly think I may have finally found the right sunscreen for my skin.

reviews.author: Mel Dalton
reviews.text: I've been very impressed with EltaMD UV Clear SPF 50. It goes on smoothly, feels lightweight, and doesn't leave a greasy residue. It absorbs quickly and works great for oily and blemish-prone skin. I appreciate that it's non-comedogenic and doesn't clog my pores or cause breakouts. The zinc oxide provides excellent sun protection without the heavy feel of many mineral sunscreens. My skin stays balanced throughout the day, and it wears well under moisturizer or makeup. Highly recommend for anyone looking for effective daily sun protection that's gentle on sensitive skin.

reviews.author: Marivel C
reviews.text: I absolutely love this sunscreen! It’s lightweight, non-greasy, and blends beautifully into my skin. The tinted formula helps even out my skin tone while providing excellent sun protection. It’s perfect for daily use, especially for sensitive or acne-prone skin. I will definitely purchase it again!

reviews.author: AO99
reviews.text: My family loves this sunscreen. Gets the job done. Wish it was not this expensive. And also, makes my oily skin shine a lot despite saying non-greasy.

couche3.note: SPF teinté léger, sensible/acné, ne bouche pas
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this sunscreen provides reliable protection and appreciate its lightweight formula that's not heavy or greasy on the skin. Moreover, the product is gentle on sensitive and acne-prone skin, leaving it smooth and moisturized without clogging pores. However, the tint receives mixed feedback - while some find it nice, others say it's too dark. Additionally, customers disagree on whether the product is non-greasy.

reviews.author: Esmeralda R.
reviews.text:
Absolutely love this tinted sunscreen! The lightweight formula feels incredibly fresh on my skin and absorbs quickly without leaving a greasy or heavy feeling.





The tint blends beautifully with my skin tone, helping to even out my complexion while still looking natural. One of my favorite things about this sunscreen is the healthy glow it gives my skin. It leaves my face looking radiant and refreshed.





You don't even need foundation after applying it because the tint provides just enough coverage to give your skin a smooth, polished look. It's the perfect combination of sun protection and a natural beauty boost.





If you're looking for a sunscreen that feels light, protects your skin, and leaves you with a beautiful glow, I highly recommend this product!

reviews.author: R. L.
reviews.text: I needed a tinted sunscreen as I have some mild melasma and I've been told that a tinted sunscreen is one of the most important things I can do to keep that under control. I researched the different sunscreens and this one was highly recommended. It is expensive and I was a little hesitant but I decided that I would try it. I have to say that this sunscreen is a quality product. I could feel it the first time I put it on. Went on silky smooth and looked and felt  great on my skin. A nice matte finish. Honestly, this is the  nicest sunscreen I ever used and it seems to be working well for my skin so I will continue using it.

reviews.author: Oksana Osypchuk
reviews.text:
I’ve been using this SPF for almost 2 years now and I still love it! The tint adjusts beautifully to my skin tone and gives such a natural, healthy glow without looking heavy or greasy. It applies so smoothly, feels lightweight, and has a very pleasant smell.





Most importantly, it doesn’t break me out at all, which is super important for my sensitive skin. I wear it almost every day and it works perfectly under makeup too. The packaging is also super convenient and easy to use, especially for everyday skincare and travel.





Definitely one of the best sunscreens I’ve ever tried — worth every penny!

reviews.author: lorban
reviews.text: I’m a 54 year old white woman who wasn’t good about sun protection. I had a MOHS procedure in February for basal cell carcinoma on my nose, so I’m taking it seriously now, but I don’t like a lot of products. This is excellent. It doesn’t feel heavy, it doesn’t burn my eyes, and it gives nice coverage - not too much. I never review products but this one is worth it!

reviews.author: Ashley Sweeney
reviews.text: HSA eligible, good tint, goes on smooth. Knocked off a star because it feels greasy and has a weird smell to it. It’s not a bad smell and doesn’t smell like sunscreen, just not my fav. It doesn’t clog my pores and looks good.

couche3.note: SPF 40 mineral teinte (oxyde de zinc), leger, sans effet blanc, hydratant ; tres bien tolere peau sensible ; un peu riche pour peau tres grasse ; sur en grossesse.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this sunscreen effective at protecting their face and appreciate its lightweight, smooth application. The tinted moisturizer receives positive feedback for being very light and not leaving a white cast, while providing good hydration without making the skin oily or greasy. While some customers report no breakouts or irritation, others mention it can cause skin breakouts.

reviews.author: L.B.
reviews.text:
I’ve tried quite a few daily sunscreens, and this one stands out for how refined it feels on the skin. The texture is elegant, moisturizing, and absorbs beautifully without the heavy, greasy finish that so many SPF products leave behind. It gives my skin a smooth, comfortable feel while still delivering dependable daily protection with zinc oxide and SPF 40.





What I appreciate most is how seamlessly it fits into a morning routine. It layers well, feels gentle, and leaves my skin looking polished rather than coated. For dry skin, it strikes a really nice balance between hydration and sun protection, which makes it feel more like a true skincare step than just sunscreen.





Overall, this is a very elevated everyday SPF — simple, effective, and well formulated. If you want a face sunscreen that feels sophisticated and consistently pleasant to wear, this is an excellent choice.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
This is the best sunscreen I have ever used. As a 55-year-old woman who struggles with melasma, I’ve tried countless products over the years. EltaMD UV Daily Tinted SPF 40 has been a game changer for me.





It goes on smoothly, feels lightweight, provides great moisture, and doesn’t leave my skin feeling greasy. The tinted formula helps even out my complexion while providing excellent sun protection. Most importantly, it has helped keep my melasma from worsening, even during the Texas sun and outdoor activities.





I’ve repurchased it multiple times and won’t be without it. If you have melasma, sun sensitivity, or simply want a high-quality daily facial sunscreen, I highly recommend giving this one a try.





Worth every penny! ☀️💛

reviews.author: Rock
reviews.text:
I was actually searching for a tinted serum when I came across this product. I added it to my cart and was considering it for a day or two when a friend on Facebook posted that it was one of her favorites. After seeing how beautiful and natural it looked on her skin, I decided to give it a try.





I'm so glad I did. I absolutely love the light tint and how natural it looks. It provides the sun protection I need without feeling heavy or looking like traditional makeup. My skin looks healthy and even-toned while still looking like my skin.





A little goes a long way, and it blends in beautifully. It's lightweight, comfortable to wear, and has quickly become a staple in my daily routine.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text: This is my favorite tinted moisturizer. It is very lightweight and perfect for daily wear. Provides just the right amount of coverage while also protecting from sun damage. My only complaint is the pump. The bottle is full of product, but as you can see in the video, nothing is coming out. I’ve been buying this brand for years and have never had a problem, so not sure if it’s the change in packaging or I just got a defective pump.

reviews.author: Brianna Milian
reviews.text: Great daily sunscreen! It’s super lightweight, non greasy and feels more like you’re putting on moisturizer. Since I’ve started using it daily I’ve noticed a more even skin tone on my face. There is a pearl finish version I also love for that glowy look.

couche3.note: SPF texture primer invisible, léger; un peu sec
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this sunscreen provides excellent protection for fair skin and appreciate its smooth, silky texture that feels like a makeup primer. The product is lightweight, works well under makeup, and leaves no white cast. While many customers consider it worth the price, others feel it's too expensive.

reviews.author: MK
reviews.text:
Absorbs fast, weightless, no white cast, and does not aggravate my sensitive skin nor stain clothes.





Despite the oily appearance of the formula (which is related instead to the silicone), it feels surprisingly light and moisturizing, and I love that there’s no alcohol, which can irritate and dry my skin. It is my favorite daily sunscreen, and I actually prefer it to many of the Japanese brands famous for “cosmetic elegance.” My only complaints have been the cost (but there are frequent sales) and the lower water/sweat resistance. Although I hate rushing to reapply with even light exercise, the sunscreen is so comfortable to wear that reapplications and consistent use are not as taxing as they could be. For the cost, I wish the UVA protection was higher, but that’s probably more of a comment for the FDA.

reviews.author: Woof
reviews.text:
I love this stuff. Pricey, yes. But it's GOOD.





I've never loved putting sunscreen on my face, but long ago accepted that it's the smart thing to do. I don't care for the smell of the usual stuff--the products with all the chemicals ending with "-one" or "-ate"... stuff that may or may not be bad for you. Better, probably, than a sunburn, of course. But I prefer the mineral stuff. Problem is, most of the mineral ones leave an unwelcome tint. The worst of them look like clown makeup.





This stuff, though. Wow. It uses minerals, not those chemicals, and a tiny amount goes a long way. It's smooth, super easy to apply, and odorless. Best of all, it's really invisible.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
I get it. I genuinely get the hype behind this product. It's AMAZING. It is the ONLY sunscreen that I've ever tried that applies like, feels like, behaves like, and wears like an actual primer.


Because I am only slightly darker in skin tone than freshly fallen snow, I wear sunscreen year around. This stuff WORKS, year around, regardless of my skin being dry (winter) or sweaty (summer), and makes my makeup last all day without pilling, oxidizing, clumping, or streaking.





So to whatever chemical nerd geniuses that work at Supergoop that came up with this product? WELL DONE. This pasty pale girl salutes you.

reviews.author: Angie Moore
reviews.text: I love the smooth silky finish. I thought it would be greasy, but it absorbed quickly, leaving a smooth finish. It's a perfect primer as well. My makeup went over it easily and stayed throughout the day. I'm going to buy a large tube.

reviews.author: Tamara W.
reviews.text: Go on like silk and it’s clear. It doesn’t feel thick or sticky on my face. I put my foundation over it and it doesn’t peel. Nice sunscreen,  small tube though and kind of expensive.

couche3.note: SPF minéral léger, primer, hydrate
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this sunscreen effective, noting it works well as a primer under makeup and protects better than other products. The texture receives positive feedback for its smooth, non-tacky feel, and customers appreciate its moisturizing properties. The white cast and value for money receive mixed reviews - while some say it doesn't leave a white cast and consider it worth the price, others disagree. The fragrance also gets mixed reactions, with some finding the scent not overwhelming while others find it unbearable.

reviews.author: Lee
reviews.text:
No paid review here I purchased this with my own hard earned money. I am currently on prescription hydroquinone tretinoin cream from my dermatologist for discoloration, acne, fine lines, etc. I have combo acne prone skin, in my 30s and light skin tone. My skin is currently dryer than normal due to the prescription cream I am on. Being on this prescription cream it’s extremely important for me to wear sunscreen every single day and not skip it! I can only wear mineral sunscreen at the moment due to my skin being more sensitive than normal. Typically I would wear a chemical sunscreen prior to my hydroquinone tretinoin treatment. Typically I really don’t care for mineral sunscreens because they have always felt gross and left an unpleasant film on my skin. This mineral sunscreen is one of a kind. I can tolerate very few mineral sunscreens and this is one of them. Here is my list of pros of this sunscreen and also my list of what I look for in a sunscreen:


-light weight/easy to blend


-not greasy


-minimal to no white cast


-doesn’t clog pore or break me out


-sits well over moisturizer


-works well under makeup


-no pilling


-water resistant 40 mins


-not drying





Here are the cons of this sunscreen:


-It smells like Johnson and Jonson baby shampoo upon application (smell does fade away though)


-it is expensive I try to catch it on sale


-you have to remember to shake it before every use





I just reordered a new bottle at the Black Friday sale price so I got the big size. Normally I get the small size. Overall I am very happy with this sunscreen even though it has a light scent upon application and it’s pricey. The most important things to me are that it’s not greasy (I have sensory issues to greasy lotions) and most importantly it keeps my skin protected and safe while on my prescription treatment. I can confirm as a light skinned person that I do not burn while wearing this sunscreen. I cannot afford to burn or else it will set me back in my skin treatment. I have been using this for a while and I would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a high quantity mineral sunscreen that plays well with makeup and isn’t greasy. The finish is a nice natural stain finish that drys quickly. No more waiting around forever for my mineral sunscreen to dry down like the majority of mineral sunscreens. If you are looking for a dewy/radiant finish you may want to opt for something else, although it is not drying at all on me. In comparison to other mineral sunscreens I’ve tried the white cast is easily blended out. It is not noticeable on me. They also have a tinted version but it is actually too dark for me. If you are a person of color the tinted version is a medium beige color that is very similar in texture and finish to this untinted version. In the morning I am short on time and it’s quick to put my moisturizer and then this isdin right on top without having to do a bunch of work to blend to make it look presentable. My makeup glides on smooth with no problems. I wouldn’t go as far as to call it primer like but it does work as a nice base for makeup. I haven’t found anything that makes it ball up and pill. It is just an added bonus that I can wear it for active days since it resists sweat. I don’t have to scrub to get it off at the end of the day either. My cerave face wash takes it off just fine. The only thing I would say is if you simply cannot handle any type of scent in your skincare products, unfortunately you may want to skip this one. If you are fine with a light scent that fades you will probably be alright. Although I do wish it was unscented because the scent is really unnecessary. The active ingredients are all mineral 11% zinc but it does have a chemical component in the inactive ingredients so if are intolerant to that you may want to check out the ingredient list. I throw it in my purse and take it with me for travel as well and I haven’t experienced any leaking out of the bottle. I can’t think of much more to say other than it’s really one of a kind because the texture is sooo light.

reviews.author: Debra S.
reviews.text:
I could rave all day about this incredible sun screen!  It goes on seamlessly, creamy smooth, invisible once applied, comforting to my skin, non irritating mineral ( not chemical)  and fragrance free to me. I apply Isdin liberally on my face, neck chest every single day. The bottle is LARGE  3.4 ounces many expensive brands are 1.5 oz; although it is pricey it goes further than many smaller $40.00 sunscreens.  If you are interested in keeping your skin healthy, read about ISDIN and decide for yourself.


I’m reviewing this product from my perspective since the results have been better than expected.





Isdin sun screen helps IMPROVE past skin damage as well as protecting you from daily sun damage with broad spectrum 50+, 100% mineral with DNA repairsomes water resistant.





Purchase tinted or non tinted ultralight emulsion.  I mix the two for my fair complexion and it gives me a glow.





Pictures are


1. clear sunscreen (orange color on bottle)


2. the tinted sunscreen (gray text on bottle)

reviews.author: MackerelSky
reviews.text: I haven't used it long term so I can't speak to how effective it is in repair work, however it is very good as a sunscreen. Applies easily and stays put. My only issue is it MOST DEFINITELY HAS A WHITE CAST. I don't understand how the product statement claims that there is no white cast. This is a false claim. I don't know how it would work if you applied make up over it. But if you don't use make up, then expect noticeable white cast.

reviews.author: Kimberly A Estes McCarty
reviews.text: Amazing mineral sunscreen with a beautiful consistency and no white cast. It plays well with other skincare and is a great primer for foundation. I can even use it around my extremely sensitive eyes. I’ve tried so many different mineral sunscreens and this one is worth every penny!

reviews.author: leonie sherman
reviews.text: It’s absolutely worth it. The best moisturizing sunblock I’ve tried- and I’ve tried dozens! It’s light, doesn’t leave a film or greasy feel amd 100% effective.

couche3.note: SPF hydratant, zéro white cast, OK rosacée/sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this sunscreen provides excellent protection and moisturizes skin well, with a silky smooth texture that doesn't leave a white cast. They appreciate its effectiveness, with one customer noting it works well for rosacea, and its non-irritating formula that's comfortable to wear. Customers describe it as lightweight, and while some find it worth the cost, others consider it pricey.

reviews.author: Aidan James Rhodes
reviews.text:
I am someone with incredibly sensitive, acne-prone skin, and I live in the desert. Sunscreen can cause me to break out horribly, especially if I sweat it. I have probably spent a couple hundred dollars trying sunscreens only to have them not work for me.





Seeing the price on this, I was very hesitant, but it was SO worth it. I'm not exaggerating when I say this is the happiest my skin has been, ever.  Reapplying 2-3 times per day has restored my skin barrier and left my skin dewy, soft, and glowing - not to mention that my skin tone has evened out significantly from wearing sunscreen daily.





I am so happy to have found a daily sunscreen that I look forward to putting on every day. No significant scent, goes on without a cast, and does not leave any grease or residue. Best product I have found in a very long time.

reviews.author: Taylorm
reviews.text:
By far the best facial sunscreen I’ve owned:





- Spreads very well so the bottle lasts for a pretty long time





- Feels great on my skin, like a light moisturizer! No oily residue. I have dark skin and appreciate that there is absolutely no white cast.





- The sunscreen scent is very mild





- My skin is sensitive and I’ve been having zero acne problems





All in all, a great product!

reviews.author: Jewels
reviews.text:
This is the only sunscreen i’ve liked.





I’ve tried CeraVe and face reality. Both sat too heavy and didn’t absorb into the skin well.





This one? Wow. It’s hydrating and smells like sunscreen at first but it absorbs so well and leaves my skin looking really healthy. I have sensitive skin, and this has not broken me out. I love it and i’m very glad i tried it.

reviews.author: Maya
reviews.text: 5th bottle. I have been on various skin medication and this is the only sunscreen that has worked along with actives, blends beautifully and provides proper coverage.

reviews.author: Kindle Customer
reviews.text: ...And I love it.  Non-greasy, no strong smell, and great protection.  I wear it daily as a base under my moisturizer.  I also put it on the backs of my hands/wrists.

name: Garnier SkinActive Micellar Water
ingredients: AQUA / WATER, HEXYLENE GLYCOL, GLYCERIN, DISODIUM COCOAMPHODIACETATE, DISODIUM EDTA, POLOXAMER 184, POLYAMINOPROPYL BIGUANIDE FIL B162919/3

couche3.note: micellaire doux tous types, ne pique pas
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this micellar water effective at removing makeup, particularly eye makeup, while being gentle on the skin without causing stinging or irritation. The product leaves skin moisturized and is suitable for all skin types, with one customer noting it's better than makeup wipes. They appreciate its ease of use, especially for mascara, and consider it a good value, comparing it favorably to more expensive alternatives like Bioderma's micellar water.

reviews.author: Khaleesi
reviews.text:
Great make-up remover.  All you need is a cotton ball or pad with a little dab of this solution.  You don’t have to rub hard, it takes the make-up off with little effort.  Small enough to bring in your purse.  No oily or irritated skin after use.


Pros


- Effectiveness – works great without a lot of effort; don’t need to scrub


- Makeup removal – takes off all types of make-up


- Moisturizing – does not leave your face feeling dry


- Quality – you get your money’s worth because you don’t have to use a lot


- Gentleness – no scrubbing with a rag, cotton balls work.


- Skin compatibility – excellent for sensitive skin, mine is very sensitive and this works great


- Value for your money – the value is in not having to use a lot, it last


- Ease of use – just a dab on a cotton ball or pad is all I need





Cons


- none

reviews.author: Carlie Tomaszewski
reviews.text: This Garnier Micellar Water is my skincare essential! It gently removes makeup, dirt, and impurities without harsh rubbing or rinsing. Perfect for sensitive skin - no irritation whatsoever. The 2-pack value is excellent. It leaves skin feeling clean, hydrated, and refreshed. Removes even waterproof mascara effectively. I use it every night and my skin has never been better!

reviews.author: Sage
reviews.text:
Love this!! I use it every night to take off my makeup! First I wash my face really good, then go in with this and those washable makeup pads and it gets off my eyeliner/other makeup off! It also leaves my skin feeling good <3





Id recommend it to makeup wearers, or even non makeup wearers too! Sometimes I’ll wipe my body with it too if I don’t shower that night.

reviews.author: rbp47
reviews.text: I’ve been using this for years and always keep a bottle on hand.  It removes makeup easily, feels gentle on my skin, and doesn’t leave behind any greasy or sticky residue. I especially like using it to remove eye makeup or as a quick first cleanse before washing my face. Simple, effective, affordable, and one of my forever repurchases.

reviews.author: CRISTAL
reviews.text: This is my go to! This micellar water is amazing, removes all makeup, residue, cleans out my pores and leaves it smooth and soft. I have very dry skin, no irritation. This is the ideal size and lasts a few months, even with daily use. I got the two pack and gave one to my teen daughter, she loves the product as well.

name: CeraVe Hydrating Foaming Oil Cleanser
ingredients: AQUA/WATER, GLYCERIN, PEG-200 HYDROGENATED GLYCERYL PALMATE, COCO-BETAINE, DISODIUM COCOYL GLUTAMATE, PEG-120 METHYL GLUCOSE DIOLEATE, POLYSORBATE 20, PEG-7 GLYCERYL COCOATE, SQUALANE, CERAMIDE NP, CERAMIDE AP, CERAMIDE EOP, CARBOMER, TRIETHYL CITRATE, SODIUM CHLORIDE, SODIUM HYDROXIDE, SODIUM COCOYL GLUTAMATE, SODIUM BENZOATE, SODIUM LAUROYL LACTYLATE, SODIUM HYALURONATE, CHOLESTEROL, CITRIC ACID, CAPRYLOYL GLYCINE, HYDROXYACETOPHENONE, CAPRYLYL GLYCOL, CAPRYLIC/ CAPRIC TRIGLYCERIDE, TRISODIUM ETHYLENEDIAMINE DISUCCINATE, PHYTOSPHINGOSINE, XANTHAN GUM, BENZOIC ACID, PEG-150 PENTAERYTHRITYL TETRASTEARATE, PPG-5-CETETH-20, PEG-6 CAPRYLIC/CAPRIC GLYCERIDE

couche3.note: démaquille, hydrate sèche, ne décape pas, sensible OK
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this cleanser effective at removing makeup and restoring moisture to dry skin, while being gentle and soft on the skin. They appreciate that it doesn't irritate sensitive skin and doesn't cause breakouts, with one customer noting it doesn't strip the skin barrier. The foaming action creates a rich lather, and customers consider it good value for money and appreciate its unscented formula.

reviews.author: Khaleesi
reviews.text:
I love this cleanser.  It does not leave my face feeling dry, but just the opposite.  Its not too oily, but still soft enough that my face feels naturally smooth.  I like that it is in a container that I can carry and use throughout the day.





Pros


Effectiveness – cleans my face extremely well


Moisture – keeps my face from feeling dry, I don’t like using lotion on my face


Gentleness – does not irritate my skin like some soaps do


Skin sensitivity – soap gave me tiny rashes, this does not


Foaming – lathers up into smooth foam


Value for money – great price, not expensive compared to other skin products


Fragrance-free – no lingering smell means less chemicals were used


Skin feel – skin feels really smooth and moist after washing





Cons


None

reviews.author: Gaffe0880
reviews.text:
I've started using an number of other CeraVe products and decided to give this Hydrating Foaming Oil Cleanser a try as a body wash. It seems to work well and doesn't have any fragrance, which is one thing I really like about CeraVe products. I have added using this product to my regimen of using their hydrating face wash and oil control shampoo. Overall, my skin is much better and I see mush less acne and clogged pores.





I knocked it down 1-star because, in order to use it as a body wash, I've got to do like eight or nine pumps to get enough product out.

reviews.author: Jenai Morris
reviews.text: This cleanser is very gentle and works especially well for dry or sensitive skin because it cleans without stripping moisture. The foaming oil formula feels smooth and soothing on the skin, removing dirt and buildup while still leaving the face soft afterward. It doesn’t have a strong scent, which is great for sensitive users, and it doesn’t leave a tight or dry feeling after washing. It also works for both face and body, making it versatile. Overall, it’s a reliable everyday cleanser that focuses on hydration and comfort.

reviews.author: rbp47
reviews.text: I really did not think I was going to like putting an oil cleanser on my face 😅 I pictured feeling greasy and immediately regretting my life choices. But I actually LOVE this stuff. It cleans my skin really well while still feeling gentle and moisturizing instead of stripping everything dry. My skin feels softer and calmer afterward, and it doesn’t leave that heavy oily feeling I was worried about. Now I totally understand why people are obsessed with oil cleansers.

reviews.author: Martin Theiss
reviews.text: I use this on my face, hands, and body. It sits by my sink for face/hands, and then I put it in the shower for body. It works everywhere and leaves skin clean and soft. My skin never feels tight or dry with this cleanser. This is an all-purpose cleanser for those with dry skin.

name: The INKEY List Oat Cleansing Balm

couche3.note: baume doux sensible, démaquille bien
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the cleansing balm effective at removing makeup, including sunscreen and mascara, and appreciate that it's gentle on sensitive skin without causing breakouts or irritation. The product receives positive feedback for its quality and value for money, with customers noting it's perfect for the price. The consistency receives mixed reviews - while some find it silky smooth, others say it feels greasy. Moreover, the ease of removal and residue are also mixed aspects, with some saying it melts away makeup effortlessly while others find it difficult to rinse off and leave a film on the face. The scent receives mixed reactions, with some appreciating that it doesn't have a strong smell.

reviews.author: Miss History
reviews.text:
A million thanks to the Sephora team member who casually pointed me towards the INKEY list--the entire line has been blowing my mind! If you're looking for a nice, unfussy balm to take off your makeup before using another cleanser to wash your face (aka double cleansing), this is a fantastic option. I've tried all kinds of makeup removing balms from low to high price points, and the INKEY List Oat Cleansing Balm is my new favorite. You get plenty of it for the price, which is great since I don't believe most brands when they say a dime-sized amount will take off all your makeup in every circumstance. Even if a little does go a long way, I wear a full face of makeup every day and need at least a quarter's worth of any balm to get everything off to my liking. This bottle is going to last me a good while, and it will go even farther for people who prefer the less is more model.





More importantly, the formula is awesome, one of those simple and to the point gems. It's both gentle and very effective, a win/win for super sensitive/oily/prone to some serious hormonal acne skin like mine. It's a little thicker than some of the other balms I've tried, but that's been a plus for me. It feels like it's really gripping the makeup on my face rather than just slip-sliding around the top layers of it. This balm also just FEELS clean, if that makes sense--it's not chemical-y at all, it doesn't have any added scents, but it also doesn't smell too strongly or too strangely without them.





Most makeup removing balms out there don't sting my eyes, but plenty do get into my eyes and leave a bit of a blurring film. It's another win/win situation with this balm: it removes every bit of makeup off my eyes (no morning raccoon eyes!) but doesn't stick around once I wipe it clean. For clarity, I do use a makeup eraser to wipe the balm off, which I think is a game-changing step no matter what balm you use.





After I'm done double cleansing, my skin never feels tight, dry or itchy in between the cleansing and moisturizing steps, and the gentle formula in this balm is a big part of that. I've seen a couple reviewers who say their skin felt greasy after using the balm, but for my part, I've never experienced that. That might be more of an issue if you want to use this as a standalone cleanser, but my skin just feels soft and makeup-free when I use it, even before I reach for the cleanser to complete my double cleanse.





In all, I love this simple and effective balm, and I'll definitely be adding it to my subscribe and save list. For the price and the quality, it's won me over from reaching for any other brand I've tried before.

reviews.author: Sean
reviews.text: I'm newer to the INKEY brand and have only tried the Oat Cleansing balm but am super impressed! It definitely takes off makeup and SPF from the day and just leaves your skin feeling super calm. Since this is a cleansing balm you will want to do a second cleanse as it's very oily and find it leaves a layer on your skin, but that's easily washed off with a second cleanse and really how you want to use this. It does have some grainy texture that may be a little to much for some, but for me I love it and feels it gives a more thorough cleanse. Definitely will repurchase this product!

reviews.author: SF
reviews.text: My stylist recommended this after I told him that everything I've ever used to wash my face makes it super dry and...yup, this does NOT. After I wash, my face feels so clean and soft.  It doesn't foam up or anything, so I use a sonic brush on my face and, after rinsing, it's smooth and soft.

reviews.author: KBGL
reviews.text: It works but it kind of smells earthy.  Which isn't bad just it smells a lot like oats.  It works on my sensitive skin.  Its not made me break out in a rash.  But it smells like what you feed horses.

reviews.author: Anna B
reviews.text: This is so wonderful! It is a gentle and truly disrupts the makeup (so it can be removed with a cleanser).

couche3.note: micellaire top sensible, démaquille waterproof
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this micellar water effective at removing makeup, particularly waterproof mascara, and appreciate its gentle formula that's especially suitable for sensitive skin without causing irritation. The product leaves skin feeling fresh and moisturized, and customers find it easy to use. While some customers report it doesn't sting their eyes, others mention it can be harsh. Customers consider it good value for money.

reviews.author: Glitchzig
reviews.text:
I wanted to try this product for a very specific purpose, I have been looking at facial cleansing wipes to use occasionally at bedtime instead of stripping my face by doing a full wash with cleanser and water. I rarely wear makeup, I spend my energy on finding the best skincare products for my skin and sunscreen. So at bedtime usually, I’m just taking off sunscreen and the day’s grime, or if I didn’t go out, the day’s moisturizing products. I tried several brands of moist facial cleansing wipes, and they left behind a greasy film and my face still feels like it needs a full wash.  I would never want to skip properly cleansing my skin, and I usually do a full routine while I shower, but it would be nice to be able to occasionally do something quicker and more convenient without sacrificing on quality. I was hoping this product might cleanse well without needing to be rinsed or breaking me out on occasions where I want something quick and convenient, or maybe am travelling and need a basic routine.





Wow, this product is not only perfect for that, it actually makes my skin soft and clean, you’d never know I skipped my regular cleansing and just used this Bioderma product with a cotton pad before my nighttime moisturizers/serum. It completely takes off the day’s water resistant facial sunscreen and any sweat or grime and leaves my skin very clean, soft, not dry, and it feels like there is no residue whatsoever. I also don’t break out or get clogged pores, it is just a clean, soft texture. One cotton pad with product is all takes for my entire face to look and feel just as clean as if I had used my regular products (a cleansing balm followed by a foaming cleanser and lots of rinsing with water).





I have used this micellar water quite a bit since receiving it, and have not broken out or had clogged pores from “cutting corners” so to speak. And it cleanses very well. I can’t believe how well it cleanses without needing to be rinsed, without irritating my skin, without clogging pores or causing breakouts, and is suitable to use and then put on my serum and nighttime moisturizer. I’ve even used it the next morning to remove the nighttime moisturizers and then put on my SPF.





I worried that I was going to cause my skin not to look as good if I didn’t properly wash and rinse with water but this product is so amazing. I have not yet used it to see how well it removes makeup since I rarely wear any, but just for skincare and cleansing without a full wash with regular water, it is so amazing. Not going to sugarcoat it, this is me wanting a convenient routine when I travel but this is also me being lazy on nights when I’m very tired or mornings when I am in a hurry.





I consider this one of my few holy grail skincare products. I have picked up a smaller size for travel. I will keep this on hand always. I’m not even looking at moist wipes anymore. (This product is more gentle and leaves no residue as compared to a dermatologist recommended brand of face wipes). I’m most surprised that it cleans my face so thoroughly and that not rinsing does not cause any irritation or breakouts. Just wow. Totally worth the price. I was going to try other brands but this product has been so good there is no need to.

reviews.author: Elizabeth M.
reviews.text:
I love this micellar water. It removes my makeup on the first pass with no harsh rubbing or irritation. Even around the eyes, it feels soothing rather than stinging, which isn’t always the case with makeup removers. It’s also fragrance free and lightweight, so it doesn’t leave behind any greasy or sticky residue.





Another major plus is the no-rinse formula. You can use it quickly with a cotton pad and be done, which makes it perfect for busy nights or when you just don’t feel like doing a full routine! It leaves your skin feeling clean, calm, and moisturized. Seriously gentle yet effective.

reviews.author: Jill
reviews.text:
I really like this micellar water. I used to use a different brand, but I feel like this one does a better job removing my makeup while still being gentle on my skin.





I have sensitive skin with rosacea, and I wear liner, mascara, and eyeshadow daily. This removes everything from my eyes without needing a lot of rubbing or effort, which I really appreciate.





After using it, my skin feels very clean but also soft, not stripped or dry.





Overall, this has become my favorite micellar water and I’ll definitely keep repurchasing it.

reviews.author: DiG
reviews.text: This is a great micellar water! I have very sensitive, acne-prone skin, and it hasn’t caused any redness or irritation at all. It removes makeup very easily and leaves my skin feeling clean and refreshed afterward. I also like to follow up with a mild face wash as part of a double cleanse routine; overall, it feels gentle, effective, and perfect for sensitive skin.

reviews.author: Oxana
reviews.text: I’ve tried many micellar waters, and this one is by far my favorite. It removes makeup easily without irritating my skin and leaves my face feeling clean and refreshed. Perfect for sensitive skin and a staple in my skincare routine!

name: DHC Deep Cleansing Oil
ingredients: olea europaea (olive) fruit oil, caprylic/capric triglyceride, sorbeth-30 tetraoleate, pentylene glycol, tocopherol, stearyl glycyrrhetinate, rosmarinus officinalis (rosemary) leaf oil, phenoxyethanol

couche3.note: huile démaquillante hydratante, douce; prudence acné
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the cleansing oil effective at removing makeup and appreciate its moisturizing properties, noting it leaves skin soft and clean without drying. The product receives positive feedback for its gentleness, with customers describing it as silky and smooth. While some customers find the scent mild, others describe it as weird, and opinions about value for money are mixed, with some considering it a great value while others find it expensive. Skin sensitivity experiences are mixed, with some customers saying it doesn't irritate sensitive skin while others report burning around their eyes.

reviews.author: Miyi
reviews.text:
I rarely stay loyal to skincare products for years because I’m always testing and trying new things, but DHC Deep Cleansing Oil is one of the very few products that has remained a permanent part of my routine. I’ve repurchased this multiple times over the years, and every time I try another cleansing oil, I eventually come back to this one because the performance is just consistently good.





The first thing that stands out is how effective it is at removing makeup. I wear waterproof mascara, eyeliner, sunscreen, and long-wear foundation regularly, and this breaks everything down incredibly fast without needing to aggressively rub my skin. I usually apply one pump onto dry hands and massage it onto my dry face for about 30 seconds. You can literally feel the makeup dissolving almost immediately, especially around the eye area where stubborn makeup usually takes extra effort to remove.





What I really appreciate is that it removes heavy makeup in one application. I don’t have to go back in multiple times or use separate eye makeup removers anymore. Even waterproof mascara that normally leaves residue around the lashes comes off cleanly. That alone saves me so much time at night when I’m exhausted and just want my skincare routine done quickly.





Once water is added, the oil transforms into a light milky texture that spreads evenly and rinses away very easily. A lot of cleansing oils claim to rinse clean but still leave behind a greasy film that makes me feel like I need another cleanser immediately afterward. This one actually rinses off properly with water and leaves my skin feeling clean, soft, and balanced instead of heavy or sticky.





Another reason I’ve continued using this for years is because it doesn’t make my skin feel stripped. My skin tends to get dehydrated easily if I use harsh cleansers, especially during colder months, but this leaves my face feeling comfortable and hydrated. The olive oil and vitamin E in the formula definitely help with keeping the skin soft after cleansing. My skin usually feels smoother and calmer afterward instead of tight and irritated.





I also haven’t experienced clogged pores from this, which surprised me because I was originally nervous about putting an oil cleanser on my face. Instead, it actually helps loosen buildup and sunscreen better than many foaming cleansers I’ve tried. My skin texture always looks cleaner and more refreshed after using it consistently.





The bottle also lasts a very long time because you honestly don’t need much product. One pump covers my entire face easily, including eye makeup removal. Even with regular use, the full-size bottle lasts me months, so I feel like the value is actually very good considering how little product is needed each time.





The included travel size is another nice bonus. I keep the mini bottle in my travel bag or purse because it’s convenient for trips and prevents me from having to transfer product into another container. It’s small but still lasts quite a while.





One more thing worth mentioning is the price. I’ve purchased this from different places over the years, and this listing is usually cheaper than buying directly from the official store. Whenever I see it on sale, I stock up because it’s one of those products I genuinely never want to run out of.





Overall, this is one of the most reliable cleansing oils I’ve used. It removes makeup quickly, rinses clean without residue, feels gentle on the skin, and lasts a long time. If you wear makeup regularly, use waterproof products, or simply want a cleansing oil that actually performs the way it promises to, this one is absolutely worth trying.

reviews.author: Naztazia
reviews.text: This has to be the best face cleanser/makeup removers I have ever used in my 42 years of existence. Now before I tell you about the product - let me tell you about my skin: Uber sensitive, oily T-section, very prone to breakouts, blackheads, eczema, blotches, allergic reactions, you name it. I just look at some cleansers and my skin breaks out. I wear contact lenses, and my eyes are sensitive too. Now the last thing I would ever think of putting on my face is anything that has an "oil" in it, yet alone a product that IS an "oil." First of all, logic dictates that oil and water don't mix, plus if you've ever dripped salad oil on your chin, you know it's impossible to get out - and your skin feels greasy even after 2 showers later. And then you break out with a pimple there because your pores got clogged. Right? Now getting back to this product. I was watching one of those shopping channels on TV, and this really pricey cleansing oil comes on. And the lady just rubs the cleansing oil into her skin with her dry fingers (no water) and the makeup just melts off. She rubs it into waterproof lipstick, takes a white cloth and the whole thing wipes right off in one swoop! Noo... that's impossible. Then she does it again on her eyes - dry hands, pumps a few drips of the oil into her hand, massages it into her eyelids, and within 10 seconds later even the waterproof mascara gets wiped away with the cloth. Now there was no way I was going to pay $120 a bottle for their cleansing oil, so I went on Amazon to see if there was a comparable alternative. I came across this DHC one and figured, what the heck? Wow! I cannot believe it - this stuff works incredibly! I did it the same way as on TV, and my eyeshadow, waterproof eyeliner, and waterproof mascara, even the glue from false eyelashes just comes right off. I just pump 2-3 pumps of oil in my hand, close my eyes, rub my face with this oil for like 10-12 seconds, feel around for the cotton pad on the counter without opening my eyes, and wipe away the oil around my eyes, lips. Then I cup my hands with warm water and splash it on my face several times. Supposedly you don't have to use water, but I do. The oil comes right off - no oily feeling at all. And my skin is smooth. Now this sounds crazy - but the blackheads around my nose area - 100% gone! Somehow this oil (over a week's time) works better than any clay mask I have ever used in removing clogged pores. Just in case you also wear contacts, you know contacts plus any oil is bad and makes red, itchy, dry eyes. So I usually remove my contacts (just in case) first, and then wash my face. Or if I have to put my contacts back in right afterwards, I do use a normal cleanser for my face afterwards in my eye area, just to ensure there's no trace of oil. So far (been through 3 bottles) I have not had any problems. No breakouts, no scrubbing to get off makeup, no itchy skin, no blackheads - actually I think it might be protecting my skin somehow because even during this itchy pollen season, I'm perfectly fine. Love love love this stuff!!!

reviews.author: Lesley
reviews.text:
I use this every single day to remove my sunscreen at the end of the day. If you wear SPF daily you know how hard it is to fully get it off with a regular cleanser. This oil breaks it down completely without scrubbing or tugging at your face.





The gentleness is what keeps me coming back. I massage it onto dry skin for about a minute and everything dissolves. Sunscreen, any makeup residue, whatever built up during the day. It rinses clean with water and leaves zero greasy residue behind. My skin feels fresh after, not stripped or tight.





No fragrance and no colorant. I appreciate that because my skin reacts to basically everything unnecessary in a formula. Zero skin irritation after months of daily use. The ingredients are simple and effective. Olive oil based with vitamin E and it doesn't try to do too much. Just cleans thoroughly and gets out of the way.





My skin appearance has honestly improved since I started using this consistently. I think properly removing sunscreen every night was the missing step in my routine. Pores look cleaner, fewer random breakouts, and my other products absorb better on freshly cleansed skin.





The medium 4.1 oz bottle lasts me about two months with nightly use. Already on my third bottle and it's another subscribe and save staple.

reviews.author: Lindsay
reviews.text: i’ve been repurchasing this for almost 8 years now. hands down this is a holy grail product for me and i have been recommending it to everyone. it takes my makeup off great and it leaves my skin feeling so smooth and soft. its very lightweight and it is very easy to use. i use this for about 10-20 minutes before i use a water based cleanser to take it off. it got rid of little bumbs i have and i genuinely do feel blackheads and dead skin coming off as i use it. this made my skin do a 180 and i will def be using this until the day i die. i love this and recommend it so much.

reviews.author: Sarah
reviews.text: Product I have been purchasing for almost a decade. Excellent at getting out black heads around the nose and mouth. However, if you do not thoroughly double cleanse after, you will be sure to experience a break out. It has a mild scent that doesn’t bother me, typically I get headaches from too strong of a scent. Overall, it’s a gentle way to cleanse and protect your skin health.

name: Farmacy Green Clean Cleansing Balm
ingredients: cetyl ethylhexanoate caprylic/capric triglyceride peg-20 glyceryl triisostearate synthetic wax peg-10 isostearate echinacea purpurea root extract carica papaya (papaya) fruit extract moringa oleifera seed extract zingiber officinale (ginger) root oil helianthus annuus (sunflower) seed oil sorbitan sesquioleate citrus aurantifolia (lime) oil citrus aurantium bergamia (bergamot) fruit oil glycerin citrus aurantium dulcis (orange) peel oil

couche3.note: baume démaquille, hydratant; a un parfum
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the cleansing balm effective at removing makeup, particularly waterproof mascara, and appreciate its moisturizing properties that leave skin soft and clean. The product is gentle on eyes, doesn't irritate skin, and has a divine fragrance. While some customers consider it worth the price tag, others find it too expensive.

reviews.author: DIANA DIAZ
reviews.text:
I love this! As a mom, I don’t have time for complicated skincare routines, and this balm is perfect. It melts away all my makeup—including waterproof—super gently. My skin feels soft, hydrated, and fresh after every use. Super natural, easy to use, and no greasy residue. Definitely a staple in my routine!


Perfect for a quick, gentle cleanse!

reviews.author: Jana W.
reviews.text: I have really delicate dry skin. I don't often wear makeup, but when I do, I need to get every bit of it off or else I break out or my pores clog and I end up with what seems like instant blackheads. I've never understood how people can wear heavy cosmetics without ruining their skin. But then, maybe covering it all up is why they wear it in the first place. A-N-Y-W-A-Y...years ago I discovered a Lancome cleanser that melted and removed every trace of makeup and left my skin soft and clean without feeling dry or stripped of moisture. It was a cream-to-oil cleanser that liquefied at body temperature that was designed for oily skin and cleansed without water. In fact, mixing it with water wrecked the deep cleansing and makeup removing effectiveness of it. Once all my makeup AND eye makeup was dissolved, I could just wipe everything off with a damp cloth and then rinse my face in warm water. Presto. I didn't have oily skin, but the woman at the counter raved at what a miracle makeup remover it was, so I tried it, and it proved to be everything she'd said. Naturally, Lancome discontinued the product and I could never find anything like it. I was able to find more on the eBay and hoarded what I could find, but eventually, I ran out, and no one seemed to offer anything like it...until now. FARMACY NATURAL MAKEUP REMOVER IS THE BOMB! A little goes a long way. It looks like a cream, but it liquefies on contact with my skin. I go all over my face (and eyes!) in light circular motions until everything is completely loose and dissolved, and then wipe it all away with a damp cloth. Just like before. Then I rinse, rinse, rinse with warm water. My skin is fresh and clean with no trace of makeup left behind. It completely dissolves long lasting lipstick/lip liner and water proof eye makeup in nothing flat. No dryness, no redness, no stripping. It's just grand. I am so relieved to have found this product! I recommend it highly...but DON'T mix it with any water until you are ready to remove it from your face. Be sure to use a damp wash cloth to wipe your face before you rinse, otherwise I don't think everything comes off. If needed, after rinsing, if you want to follow up with your regular cleanser, or do a repeat with this cleanser, that would be up to you, but depending on your primer/foundation/powder etc., you shouldn't need to. I never have. I don't even need moisturizer after cleansing. I just use this stuff, rinse, pat my face dry, and head off to bed. Sweet! And the scent is wonderful. Refreshing and relaxing all at the same time. One other thought. As I mentioned before, my old Lancome product was made as a cleanser for oily skin, not especially as a makeup remover. It's only a guess, but if I had really oil skin, I'd try this cleanser whether I wore makeup or not. I think this product is probably going to prove to be way more than just a makeup remover.

reviews.author: Alice H. Patlovich
reviews.text: It's absolutely the best makeup remover cream.  Totally clean, no fragrance and feels very smooth going on, washes off easily and leaves your face completely clean. It's amazing!

reviews.author: Joan Crum
reviews.text: It’s a fabulous cleanser. It liquifies instantly, cleanses thoroughly, quickly, easily washes off with water. You’ll never skip your night cleansing routine!

reviews.author: Jolene Gatto
reviews.text: This stuff is clean and unscented, and it works amazingly well on my very sensitive skin! Just rub a little onto dry face and rinse well. It “melts” your makeup off and works with cool or warm water.

name: Clinique Take The Day Off Cleansing Balm

couche3.note: baume doux non irritant, neutre, démaquille
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this cleansing balm effective at removing makeup and appreciate its gentle, non-irritating formula that leaves skin soft and smooth. The product has a neutral scent and comes in convenient travel-sized containers. While some customers consider it worth the price, others find it not worth the cost, and there are mixed experiences with eye irritation.

reviews.author: Paris Sanders
reviews.text:
This is a very long must have review, so please be prepared....


Absolutely love, love. love this!!!


The product does exactly what its supposed to do. It's a little pricy but worth it.


Get it in large I feel it's more worth it goes along way.


I've had the small on just because I had a budget.


Who hasn't had a budget?


You do not need a whole lot. Probably the size of a nickel really.


You can wipe it off with a damp cloth or Bounty Paper Towle (whatever you use for spills).


After wiping it off you can either get into the shower, leave it on for 10 to 30 minutes or wash your face in the sink.


When I leave it on for that amount of time it feels like it leave my face nice and moisturized.


Then go to my other facial products to finish off my night for bed...





If you're able to do buy the Moisture Surge 100H Auto-Replenishing Hydrator Oil Free Face Moisturizer.


It's recommended to have they come hand in hand.


I would definitely recommend getting them both.


Now that's also little pricey, I buy this in the smaller bottle each and every time.


I feel that you don't need the bigger bottle.


Also, just a small amount like a nickel size.


It says to apply to neck area, I have a different cream for that (I buy at Meijer's in the trial size lol).


Like I said this girl is on a budget!!!


We all wear make up very day maybe.


You should wear after you do your Toner, then follow your other steps.


I feel after toner make you skin more soft when you are applying other make-up needs.


This moisturize is really for the day time, well that's when I use it.


I feel you need more moisture during the day.


Your face gets a beating during the day.


All types of air flying dirt lets call it.


That is truly when you need all the moisturizer you can get.


At night you face has that time to settle down with all the rest to get back to normal.


That's just my opinion to each's  own really.


Unless you're going to wipe off all the make-up you had on, make yourself up all over for a night on the town or date definitely reapply.


Remember to use The Day Off Cleansing Balm Makeup Remover.


You should wear after you do your Toner, then follow your other steps.


I feel after toner make you skin more soft when you are applying other make-up needs.


I do hope this helps

reviews.author: KB
reviews.text:
A good product for occasionally removing extra makeup that's built up over time and difficult to remove. Generally I use micellar water with pads to remove eye makeup and makeup wipes after for the rest of my face. I notice that after time there is often eye shadow or excess eye liner on the edges of my eyes that looks like dark spots and hard to remove, and I want to avoid scrubbing it. This product is really great for rubbing into those areas before face wash to fully remove makeup spots that have become resistant. It smells nice and comes off really easily with face wash.





I do wish it was a bit cheaper, but a little does go a long way. I'd estimate I've gotten 15-20 uses from the small travel-size container, though I only use it on half my face.

reviews.author: Ben and Hannah
reviews.text: I love this makeup remover! It has the faintest smell that is very pleasantly soft, herbal and slightly earthy. It doesn’t smell like melting plastic or chemicals like many other fragrance free cleansing balms I’ve used. This cleansing balm takes my tinted SPF and mascara off with one wash. When I wear Clinique’s bronzing stick and highlighter, it takes it right off without scrubbing. It washes off very quickly without having to waterboard myself lol. It leaves my skin feeling hydrated and comfortable. The size is awesome! You definitely get what you’re paying for!

reviews.author: Yvonne
reviews.text:
I was pleasantly surprised. Left my skin super clean and soft. Doesn’t take much either. Will be my go to going forward.


Been using it now for a while. It is still fabulous on my skin, but mascara is not as easy to get off and it does burn my eyes a little.

reviews.author: Olivia Makowsky
reviews.text: I need to buy this in a bigger size already, it takes off my mascara SO well and I feel like it’ll keep my eyelashes more healthy

name: Then I Met You Living Cleansing Balm
ingredients: Olea Europaea (Olive) Fruit Oil, Vitis Vinifera (Grape) Seed Oil, Cetyl Ethylhexanoate, PEG-20 Glyceryl Trii-sostearate, PEG-8 Isostearate, Cera Microcristallina (Microcrystalline Wax/Cire Microcristalline), Hippophae Rhamnoides (Sea Buckthorn) Oil, Ceresin, Synthetic Wax, Orbiignya Oleifera Seed (Babasu) Oil, Diospyros Kaki (Persimmon) Fruit Extract, Aniba Rosaeodora (Rosewood) Wood Oil, Citrus Paradisi (Grapefruit) Peel Oil, Rosemarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Leaf Oil, Tocopheryl Acetate, 1,2-Hexanediol, Bis-Diglyceryl Polyacyladipate-2, Ethylene/Propylene Copolymer

couche3.note: baume démaquille, double nettoyage, hydratant
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the cleansing balm extremely effective at removing makeup, particularly eye and face makeup, and appreciate its double cleansing capabilities. The product receives positive feedback for its moisturizing properties, leaving skin feeling soft and refreshed, and its silky, buttery texture that's easy to work with. Moreover, they like its heavenly fragrance and non-greasy formula that doesn't leave residue. While many find it worth the high price, opinions about value for money are mixed.

reviews.author: SEJOONG LEE
reviews.text:
Amidst my skincare journey, I've discovered a true gem in the Then I Met You Living Cleansing Balm. This product has brought a refreshing and delightful twist to my routine. The orange-ish scent is out of this world.





Upon opening the jar, I was immediately greeted by an enchanting aroma that added a touch of luxury to my cleansing ritual. The delicate scent adds a sensory dimension to the experience, transforming my skincare routine into a moment of relaxation and indulgence.





The texture of the cleansing balm is incredibly smooth, allowing it to glide seamlessly onto my skin. The balm's consistency ensures effortless application, effortlessly dissolving makeup and impurities without any tugging or irritation. This aspect has elevated my cleansing routine, turning it into a serene and effective process.





One of the key highlights of this cleansing balm is its ability to provide a thorough cleanse while maintaining the skin's natural moisture. After use, my skin feels refreshed and nourished, rather than stripped or dry. This gentle yet effective approach aligns perfectly with my sensitive skin's needs.





The packaging of the Then I Met You Living Cleansing Balm is both elegant and practical. The jar's design is not only visually appealing but also functional, making it easy to scoop out just the right amount of product.





The Then I Met You Living Cleansing Balm has introduced a touch of elegance and luxury to my skincare routine. The alluring scent, seamless texture, and effective cleansing properties have redefined my cleansing experience in the best way possible. If you're seeking a cleansing balm that elevates your routine with a sensory delight while ensuring a gentle yet thorough cleanse, I wholeheartedly recommend giving this product a chance.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
I have tried quite a few oil cleansers in the last 6-7 years. This is the best one. Better than Farmacy, better than Youth to the People, better than Naturium, and better than any other japanese or korean brands I have tried.


It removes difficult makeup and sunscreen in 60 seconds (that is how long I like to massage my cleansers in) and it feels so silky on the skin. It has a nice slip to it so i never feel that I am tugging at my face. It all melts into my hands as soon as I begin to rub them together. My biggest pet peeve with cleansing balms is that they never fully melt into my hands and i am left with big cleanser chunks between my fingers. That is not the case with this balm. It is perfect.


It does not leave a residue either, if that is something you look for. Personally I never mind either way because Im going to follow with a regular cleanser right after. But this rinses of clean and leaves my face feeling soft.


There is a very pleasant scent to this balm as well. I don’t find it too strong and it doesn’t irritate my skin, either.


Finally, the price is a bit wild for the amount, but honestly, I truly think i will keep coming back because I have never used a better balm and I don’t believe i ever will.

reviews.author: unevenstar
reviews.text: Since I started using Then I Met You Liovng Clransing Balm, I can’t wait to wash my face in the morning and keep checking the clock at night to see if it’s time to go wash my face before bed.  I am truly obsessed with this product.  First, it smells so good I can’t wait to open the lid.  My delight only improves when my make-up melts away including my very difficult to remove mascara.  It is very gentle on the eye area, and I can even open my eyes to check and see if it’s all gone or if I need to rub it in a bit more.  There is no stinging whatsoever unlike every other make-up remover I have ever used.  I also don’t get that weird blurry eye thing after I rinse. In addition, my face feels hydrated when I’m done rinsing but not oily. I have T-zone oily skin.  I have been using for almost a month, and I feel I have barely used any product at all.  My face loves it!  I think the quality is amazing and  it’s worth every penny - especially as it seems it will last for quite a while.  I will not hesitate to purchase again.

reviews.author: Amber Nierenberger
reviews.text: Just tried this tonight and I am impressed with it. It smells great and seem to cleanse pretty well. I don’t normally wear make up so I didn’t have any to take off with it, but I did use it to cleanse my face after the day and then followed it up with my normal cleanser. It went on smooth and had a pretty even consistency, but I will say that it felt a little greasy/oily however it did not leave a weird residue on my face or anything like that. It had a decent amount of moisture to it so it didn’t leave my face feeling dry either. It felt like a good product.

reviews.author: Kelley Foreman
reviews.text: The best cleansing balm I've tried! I have neither dry nor oily skin that is sensitive with rosacea.  This cleanser has the perfect texture and leaves me so soft and clean, but not tight and dry. What takes it over the top is the scent, though!  It has orange and rosemary essential oil and smells citrusy and fresh, not not heavy.  This is an expensive product, but it is worth it!  It's like a mini spa for my face!

couche3.note: BHA top acné/grasse/filaments; prudence sèche/sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the salicylic acid solution effective for acne and sebaceous filaments, while also appreciating its quality and value for money. The product makes skin smooth and feels great after use, with customers noting it promotes a healthy skin glow and makes their face look clearer. While some customers report no irritation, others mention it can burn their skin, and while some find it effective without being overly drying, others note it can be drying. The skin lightening effects are mixed, with some customers reporting reduced redness while others experience severe redness.

reviews.author: Yuliia Kuznetsova
reviews.text:
I’m really impressed with this serum. The Ordinary consistently delivers effective skincare at a great value, and this Salicylic Acid 2% Solution is no exception. It has been a great addition to my skincare routine for helping with occasional breakouts and improving overall skin clarity.





The texture is lightweight, absorbs quickly, and doesn’t feel heavy or greasy on the skin. It layers well with my other skincare products and works nicely both in the morning and evening routine. I also appreciate that it doesn’t have a strong fragrance.





After using it consistently, I noticed my skin looked smoother, pores appeared less congested, and blemishes seemed to calm down faster. It helps keep my skin looking clearer and more balanced without over-drying when used as directed.





A little goes a long way, so the bottle lasts quite a while. For anyone with blemish-prone or oily skin looking for an affordable and effective treatment, this is definitely worth trying. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Andre
reviews.text:
I’ve been using this for a few months now, and I’m really impressed with the results. The texture is lightweight and absorbs quickly into the skin without feeling greasy or heavy, which makes it easy to include in both my morning and nighttime routine.


What I love most is how it helps keep my skin looking clearer and smoother. It gently exfoliates inside the pores, which has noticeably reduced the appearance of small breakouts and congestion over time. My skin looks more even and less dull overall.


A little goes a long way, so the bottle lasts quite a while, making it very affordable for the quality. I also appreciate that it layers well with other skincare products without causing irritation when used properly.


Overall, this is a simple but very effective serum that delivers visible improvements with consistent use. Definitely a staple if you’re dealing with occasional breakouts or want to maintain clearer-looking skin.

reviews.author: Mike
reviews.text:
This The Ordinary Salicylic Acid 2% Solution is a great option for dealing with occasional breakouts and clogged pores. The serum is lightweight, absorbs quickly, and doesn’t leave a greasy feeling on the skin.





I’ve noticed it helps reduce the size and redness of pimples within a few uses, and it also works well for keeping blackheads and congestion under control with consistent use.





It’s strong enough to be effective, but if you overuse it, it can cause a little dryness—so starting slow is definitely the way to go. It layers well under moisturizer without pilling.





Overall, this is an affordable and effective acne-fighting serum that works well for blemish-prone skin when used correctly.

reviews.author: mo
reviews.text: The Ordinary acne serum has been a great addition to my skincare routine. It feels lightweight on the skin and absorbs quickly without leaving any greasy residue. After using it consistently, I’ve noticed fewer breakouts and my skin looks calmer and more even overall. It’s gentle enough for daily use but still effective at helping manage acne and congestion. For the price, it’s a really solid product that delivers noticeable results over time.

reviews.author: Twee
reviews.text:
I love their products. Great price, great quality.





The salicylic acid formula is strong enough to exfoliate and treat psoriasis.





Use with care. I find this is not good as a daily serum.

name: COSRX BHA Blackhead Power Liquid
ingredients: Salix Alba (Willow) Bark Water, Butylene Glycol, Betaine Salicylate, Niacinamide, 1,2-Hexanediol, Arginine, Panthenol, Sodium Hyaluronate, Xanthan Gum, Ethyl Hexanediol

couche3.note: BHA doux, ne pique pas, OK sensible, lisse
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the toner effective and appreciate its gentle formula that doesn't burn or tingle, with one noting it's suitable for sensitive skin. The product makes skin feel smoother and more soft, and customers like that it has virtually no scent. Regarding consistency, while some say it soaks in nicely, others find it feels greasy. Skin sensitivity experiences are mixed, with some reporting clearer skin while others experienced breakouts. Value for money opinions are divided between those who find it worth the price and those who consider it overpriced.

reviews.author: Meredith
reviews.text:
This may sound strange, but I don't get blackheads. My pores are oily and congested, so most of my breakouts are whiteheads, or just small red bumps near the surface of my skin. So this product wasn't really at the top of my "must try" list. However, after my skin began flaring up this past summer and my usual 

Acne.org 16 oz. Treatment (2.5% Benzoyl Peroxide)

 didn't appear to be working on its own, I started doing lots of research on acne products in hopes of finally getting my skin under control.





First thing you should know, this product is a chemical exfoliant because it contains beta hydroxy acid (BHA). BHAs are good at getting deep into your pores and clearing out old sebum and dead skin. The most commonly known BHA is salicylic acid, which is found in most North American acne products (and has never worked for me when used on its own). However, instead of being formulated with salicylic acid, the Coxrx Blackhead Power Liquid uses betaine salicylate, which is apparently a gentler BHA and more suitable for sensitive skin. I’ve been using this product every night for the past 8 weeks and I’ve had no issues with it…it goes on easily and doesn’t burn or make my skin feel tight, in fact, it doesn't feel like I have anything on my skin.





Lastly, lots of different skincare blogs/message boards agree that after you’ve applied a chemical exfoliant, you should leave it on your skin for at least 20 minutes (or longer) in order to give it time to work on your skin. Apparently this has to do with the various pH levels of skincare products…I’m not an expert, but I’ve been following those general guidelines and leaving the Cosrx Blackhead Power Liquid on my skin for a minimum of 20 minutes before applying anything else and it seems to be working for me. (The wait time is also why I’ve been applying it at night and not in the morning, but you can apply it whenever)





The second product I’ve been using in conjunction with Cosrx Blackhead Power Liquid is 

Acne.org 16 oz. Treatment (2.5% Benzoyl Peroxide)

. Benzoyl peroxide works by killing acne causing bacteria. I've been using the Acne.org benzyol peroxide gel on and off for years (another well-known brand is Paula’s Choice). The gel has a lower level of benzoyl peroxide (2.5%) which means it is gentle enough to use on a daily basis. Overall it has worked at keeping my acne under control, however, a few months ago it stopped being as effective.





I decided to combine the two products together since the BHA’s clean deep within the pores and remove dead skin and sebum, while benzoyl peroxide kills acne causing bacteria. After the first day of trying this routine my acne had already begun to clear up and fade. Within a week my skin was noticeably clearer. After 8 weeks of consistent use my skin is almost totally clear. The biggest change I’ve notice is that my pores are less clogged and congested and do appear smaller. I do occasionally get hormonal acne, but as long as I leave it alone and follow the routine, it clears up quickly.





Lastly, about a week ago I began incorporating 

Alpha Hydrox AHA Enhanced Creme, Anti-Wrinkle Exfoliant - 2 oz

 to cap off my nighttime routine. Like BHA's, alpha hydroxy acids (AHA's) are chemical exfoliants that exfoliate the upper layers of your skin, which in turn reduces active acne, improves skin texture, and brightens skin. Once I started adding the AHA moisturizer to my nighttime routine my old acne scars began to lighten and fade, and my skin felt smoother. You can totally skip this part and just use a regular moisturizer, but I felt that it took my skin to the next level.





So here is a breakdown of the skincare regime I've been using for the past 8 weeks as well as some of the products I have been using:





Night


1. Remove makeup. (

Bioderma Sensibio H2O Micelle for Sensitive Skin 500 ml.,pack 2

).


2. Wash face with a gentle cleanser and warm water. (

Cosrx Low Ph Good Morning Gel Cleanser 150ml

)


3. Gently pat skin dry with clean towel. Wait a few minutes for skin to get totally dry.


4. Apply the Corsx Blackhead Power Liquid to my skin (I use my fingers to apply). Wait 20-30 minutes to allow it to absorb.


5. Apply Acne.org Benzoyl Peroxide gel to my skin. (The amount you use varies depending on your skin sensitivity. I found that I don't need too much when I use it with the BHA). Wait 5-10 minutes for it to absorb into skin.


6. Apply moisturizer. (

Alpha Hydrox AHA Enhanced Creme, Anti-Wrinkle Exfoliant - 2 oz

 or 

Olay Complete All Day Moisturizer with Broad Spectrum SPF 15 Sensitive, 6.0 fl oz (Pack of 2)

)





Morning


1. Cleanse skin with cleanser and warm water. Gently pat skin dry skin with a clean towel.


2. Apply benzoyl peroxide gel as a spot treatment to any active pimples. Wait 5 minutes to allow it to absorb.


3. Apply moisturizer. (

Olay Complete All Day Moisturizer With Sunscreen Broad Spectrum SPF 15 - Sensitive, 4 fl. Oz.

)


4. Apply Sunscreen. (

La Roche-Posay Anthelios 60 Ultra-Light Facial Sunscreen Fluid, Water Resistant with SPF 60, 1.7 Fl. Oz.

)





A few notes-


*If you're going to use any chemical exfoliants (or benzoyl peroxide) you absolutely MUST USE SUNSCREEN EVERYDAY. All of these products make your skin more sensitive to the sun and increase the risk of sunburn! So make sure you're using a good sunscreen.





*If you're new to chemical exfoliants start slow. Maybe applying them every other night. My skin can handle nightly use, but proceed with caution.





*If you've never used benzoyl peroxide before, start with a very small amount. It can dry your skin out, causing redness and irritation when you first start using it.





*DO NOT pick at your skin! I know this is a tough one (especially if you have active acne), but it's important to leave your skin alone.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
Started using this following the very popular grit method for getting rid of subcutaneous filaments. As stated in the method i apply, allow it to sit on my skin for 20 minutes and then apply a clay mask and an oil cleanse.





Using this protocol ive had pretty great success keeping my skin clear and smooth of those pesky little bumps. I also use retinal nightly which has helped a lot but i mention it to note if u are using retinal you should definitely take a day off to when using this product or you risk irritation and skin barrier problems. This is definitely gentler than salicylic acid while being comparatively as effective in my experience.





Overall if you are looking to clear your skin of filaments, this definitely works.

reviews.author: Tania Mora
reviews.text:
I’ve had a good experience with this BHA liquid. The texture feels lightweight and absorbs well without feeling heavy or greasy. It fits easily into a skincare routine and feels comfortable to use a few times a week.





With consistent use, my skin felt smoother and looked more refreshed overall. I also liked that it felt gentle compared to stronger exfoliating products and layered well with moisturizer.





Overall, this is a nice option if you’re looking for a lightweight exfoliating product that leaves skin feeling cleaner and smoother over time.

reviews.author: Mevis
reviews.text: Great, I was having so much trouble finding effaclar K+ from LRP, because it was so good for my acne and breakouts. I actually was a little nervous because I break out so easily. But this has done wonders, the first time I used it it felt oily to the touch and got scared, but it is not oily, and it dried very quickly!

reviews.author: Smell too strong.
reviews.text: Using this BHA+AHA TONER. Had pretty great success keeping my skin clear and smooth of those pesky little pumps.

couche3.note: BHA star, clarifie, lisse, réduit rougeurs
couche3.customers_say: Customers report that the exfoliant works wonders, transforming their skin and keeping it clear, while making it feel smooth and improving texture. The product helps even skin tone and reduces redness. Regarding moisturizing and value for money, opinions are mixed - while some find it moisturizing and worth the price, others say it can be drying and consider it expensive for the amount. Skin sensitivity experiences are also mixed, with some reporting no irritation while others experience burning sensations.

reviews.author: Audrey S.
reviews.text:
My skin type:





- Very Dry, easily dehydrated


- Oily nose


- Prone to stubborn acne and closed comedones


- Redness on chin and cheeks (not rosacea)





I already use a nightly Tretinoin cream (.04% concentration) to help with my acne and premature signs of aging but decided with my derm that incorporating a BHA would be a good idea to help with my seriously stubborn closed comedones and some irritating textural issues. It was between this and the Cerave SA lotion and I ultimately decided on this due to the higher concentration of salicylic acid.





It's moisturizing, and feels sticky for the first 5-10 minutes after application but seems to sinks in to something of a satin finish on me and I can no longer feel it on my skin. You just have to be a little patient. My skin feels well hydrated and plump after it settles and looks really smooth and my pores aren't as obvious.
