It sits just fine under makeup for me but I'm not a new user to chemical exfoliants so I don't have lots of dead skin pilling up or sloughing off when I use it, my skin is, for the most part, already very smooth outside the acne and CCs. Occasionally I'll apply it further down my neck/decolletage than usual and WOW do I old skin just ball up and peel right away - it's so gross but also satisfying. So those that don't exfoliate often or are new to BHA/AHAs will likely have issues with foundation until their skin adjusts (but it will adjust). My advice in those cases is to either use this at night for the first few weeks or just accept going barefaced for a little while if you can.





I'd strongly suggest not applying this product over anything that you feel does not dry down fully, the times I've attempted to use it after serums or heavier products it takes forever to sink in and sometimes never fully does. I save all my serums and essences for after this has absorbed and it's far easier to work with it as a result. My steps go like this most days: ACWell Licorice toner --- PC 2% BHA --- 10% Niacinamide serum --- TO Matryxil serum --- Secret Key Black Snail cream or Syn-ake cream w/ 3 drops Stratia Fortify oil --- Sunscreen.





It works fairly quickly IMO, on my neck where I typically don't apply my Tret at night I noticed the peeling and balling of dead skin on day one and on my face where I have a patch of really awful CCs I noticed them getting smaller and smoother by around day 3-4. I'm going on about 8 days now and they're barely visible unless I'm right up in the mirror and feel as if they're almost gone when I touch my fingers to that area. I do have some slight peeling of the skin around my mouth and nose but it's not terrible (likely because I already exfoliate the heck out of my skin with the tret).





Some tips and things keep in mind:





1)


DO NOT RINSE IT OFF. I don't understand some of the top reviews advising people to do that, it says on the bottle not to rinse and nearly all chemical exfoliators are meant to be left on until the next cleansing specifically so they can do their job. If you rinse it off you're either going to prevent it from properly exfoliating or end up irritating the hell out of your skin. If you have oily skin and feel this might be heavy on you than you can probably just use this on bare skin and  then sunscreen if you use in the AM or a lightweight lotion/cream and be done with it, but you need to leave it on your skin.





2)


You have got to use sunscreen while you're using this product! Any BHA or AHA exfoliant causes increased sun sensitivity and you have to address that in your routine. You should already use sunscreen daily but it becomes a must when you incorporate a chemical exfoliator or you risk sun damage.





3)


Less is more! Don't slop this on your face or soak a cotton pad in it, a little goes a long way and you'll be kicking yourself if you apply way too much and have to wait 20+ minutes for it to sink in. Use a shake or two and add more if needed but go easy.





4)


Expect some irritation initially. Chemical exfoliants can be harsh and rough even on sturdy skin and you have to give yourself time to adjust. Over-exfoliation is a possibility but sometimes just introducing a BHA or AHA into your routine can cause some sensitivity. Don't panic and just be gentle to your skin. Use ceramides (like Cerave Skin Renewing cream) or fatty oils to soothe your face if needed and try to stay away from any harsh products or acids like Vitamin C right after applying this to avoid really burning your face.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
I’ve used quite a few exfoliating products over the years, but I keep coming back to this one because blackheads seem determined to make themselves at home in my pores. No matter how consistent I am with the rest of my skincare routine, they always find a way to show up, especially around my nose and chin.





What I like most about this exfoliant is that it works without feeling harsh. It has a lightweight liquid texture that applies easily and absorbs quickly. Unlike physical scrubs that can leave my skin feeling irritated or over-exfoliated, this provides gentle chemical exfoliation with none of the rough scrubbing. My skin feels smoother after use, and I don’t get the tight, stripped feeling that some acne-focused products can cause.





After using it regularly, I notice fewer clogged pores and a visible reduction in blackheads. My pores also look less noticeable, and my skin texture appears more even overall. It helps keep congestion under control before it turns into larger blemishes, which is a big reason why it has remained a staple in my routine.





For me, this is one of those rare products that manages to be both effective and gentle at the same time. If blackheads and clogged pores are an ongoing battle, this has been one of the most reliable products I’ve found for keeping them under control while still being comfortable enough for regular use.





Summary: This exfoliant has earned a permanent spot in my skincare routine. It helps reduce blackheads, keeps my pores looking clearer, and improves overall skin texture without irritating my skin. For anyone dealing with persistent pore congestion, it’s easy to see why this product has such a loyal following.

reviews.author: Deb
reviews.text: I have used products that are either too weak to notice any difference or so strong that my skin peels so badly that I'm embarrassed to leave my home. Paula's liquid exfoliant is absolutely amazing! It goes on like water and my initial impression was that it would do nothing. In fact, after my first (and literally every application since), I've been shocked at the improvement in my skin. Last week, I ran out of Paula's and decided to use my old tretinoin before bed. The next morning, my skin was coming off like a snake's skin in layers! It was so embarrassing that I actually called in sick to work, because my skin looked so horrible! I immediately ordered more of Paula's exfoliant. I had yet to venture out of my home, as my skin was coming off in literal sheets! And my face itched! If I scratched it, my skin would flake off like the worst dandruff anyone had ever seen. I was so embarrassed that I called in sick again that day. Thankfully, the Paula's exfoliation liquid came in that afternoon. I immediately applied it, and I reapplied it before bed that night. The following morning, my skin looked perfect! No more flaking or peeling off in sheets. No more itching. Paula's Skin Perfecting Exfoliant is both incredibly gentle and incredibly powerful! I honestly don't know how it can be so perfectly both gentle and powerful, but I will never go without it again. In fact, I'm buying it again today because of Prime Day and it's on sale. I wish I could afford to buy 50 refills now. I'm seldom this effusive in my praise, but I feel far more strongly about how fantastic this product is than I've been able to express here.

reviews.author: Cathy Rowell
reviews.text: I am almost 40 and have had acne since puberty. I have tried so many products. The products that showed some improvement (like Proactiv) changed the texture of my face. Literally made my face feel like a basketball. I’ve tried some products that I’ll notice an improvement after a week or so, and the it would just stop working. I have never used something that so drastically helped my face in such a short amount of time. Notably, within 5 days my blemishes were almost gone. Within a week, my chin felt smooth instead of feeling the bumps of every clogged pore. The discoloration of my face, such as the redness, hasn’t totally disappeared, but it is improving. My face doesn’t feel oily throughout the day. My skin feels tighter, like I got a facelift. Moisturizer along with this product is important. And it is important to follow the directions and start slowly with this product. Use it every other day for the first week, gradually working your way up to daily and then twice a day. It is a shock to your skin and can dry it out if you go overboard. But for what I needed this product for, it has exceeded my expectations in every way. I couldn’t recommend it more. Just a heads up, it is applied like a toner. I expected a face wash, but it is a liquid that you apply to cotton and rub on your face. I wash with CeraVe SA cleanser, follow it with this, and apply whatever moisturizer you prefer—I’ve been using moisturizer with SPF so as to not damage my skin more than I already have.

reviews.author: kwi
reviews.text: This stuff works great. However, the packaging is not. It is messy and you lose product whenever you dispense it.  For the price, I don’t want to waste a drop, but product ends up around the top and it makes this messy. Will I continue to purchase? Yes. But only because I have tried other BHAs and they seem a bit harsher on my very delicate skin. I am in the process of trying other brands that have better packaging and once I do, I will not replace.  Already, I have found a budget vitamin C that has the same percentage of actives as PC- and a much, much better dispenser. PC sold out a while ago and the brand has just not been impressing me lately. Still, this BHA works well, is non irritating on my fair, delicate, over 50 skin. I just wish the bottle had a stopper that helped it dispense the product in a more controlled manner.

name: Naturium BHA Liquid Exfoliant 2%
ingredients: Salicylic Acid,Bioactive Fruit Acids

couche3.note: BHA encapsulé, top mixte, hydrate
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the exfoliant effective, particularly for combination skin, and report that it leaves their face feeling hydrated and smooth. The product receives positive feedback for its price point and ability to even out skin tone and texture, with customers noting a glowing appearance. Regarding gentleness and fragrance, opinions are mixed - while some find it gentle and pleasant-smelling, others report it's harsh on sensitive skin and has a bad smell. Moreover, customers disagree on the product's absorption properties, with some saying it absorbs beautifully while others note it soaks up too much product. Additionally, several customers report leakage issues with the product.

reviews.author: Organized Digital Life
reviews.text:
I've been using this Naturium BHA Liquid Exfoliant 2% almost every day, and it's become a regular part of my skincare routine. What I like most is that it helps keep my pores looking clear, especially around my nose where I tend to notice congestion the most.





I've tried exfoliating products in the past that left my skin feeling tight or irritated, but this one has been very gentle on my skin. It absorbs quickly, doesn't feel greasy, and layers well with the rest of my skincare products.





After consistent use, my skin looks smoother overall, and I notice fewer clogged pores than when I'm not using it regularly. If you're looking for a simple exfoliant that feels effective without being harsh, this one is worth trying.

reviews.author: Ali
reviews.text: Love this very gentle. My skin is in recovery from retinol and this is gentle enough for me during this time. This is great if u have blackheads and want makeup to lay better. Also when u dispense the product it doesn’t come rushing out which saves product. I saw the founder using this and nikki la rose. I trust them immensely.

reviews.author: Gabe
reviews.text: It’s a great exfoliant and I have no acne when I use it and my face is really clear. My main issue is actually the bottle it’s in. I travel a lot and this bottle isn’t sealed so if you turn it over it will leak, making it impossible to travel with.

reviews.author: Marissa Holder
reviews.text: This is my new favorite product. It is not as harsh as a scrub and leaves your skin feeling smooth and looking healthy. Worth the price. Will buy again.

reviews.author: Nichole H.
reviews.text: Love how clear my skin is when using this.

couche3.note: AHA glycolique, éclaircit taches; prudence sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this toner effective as a chemical exfoliant that delivers visible results, with many noting it's better than pharmaceutical-grade alternatives. The toner improves skin appearance by lightening hyperpigmentation and making it clearer, while also making it smoother and more hydrated. Moreover, it helps minimize pores and unclog them, and customers consider it an affordable skincare essential. Regarding skin sensitivity, opinions are mixed - while some find it gentle on sensitive skin, others report it being harsh.

reviews.author: Anna Zhu
reviews.text:
While this product is officially marketed as a facial skin toner, I actually purchased it based on a recommendation to use it to get rid of stinky armpit odors and it has completely exceeded my expectations. If you are only using this on your face, you are missing out!





Here is why this has become a staple in my daily routine:





An Incredible Underarm Deodorant: I started splashing a small amount of this toner onto my underarms after showering, and it works brilliantly at eliminating stinky armpit smells. Because glycolic acid lowers the pH level of your skin, it makes the environment less hospitable to odor-causing bacteria. It keeps me completely fresh all day long without relying on heavy fragrances.





Smooths Out Skin Bumps: I also use a little bit of this on other areas of my body that are prone to unwanted bumps, rough texture, or razor burn. The 7% glycolic acid gently exfoliates the dead skin cells, and I’ve noticed a massive reduction in bumps and a much smoother skin texture overall.





Packaging & Value: The bottle is quite large at 81 fl oz and comes with a convenient nozzle applicator, making it incredibly easy to dispense onto a cotton pad or directly into your hands for body application. Given how little you need to use at a time, this bottle lasts for months, making it an amazing value. The only concerns i have are how do I take this with me when I’m travelling!





A Quick Tip: Because this is an active alpha hydroxy acid (AHA), it can sting slightly if applied immediately after shaving. I recommend waiting a bit before applying it to freshly shaved skin!





Conclusion:


This is an incredibly versatile, effective, and budget-friendly product. Whether you want to even out your skin tone, smooth out body bumps, or tackle stubborn underarm odor, I cannot recommend this enough!!

reviews.author: Human person
reviews.text:
Glycolic acid is a superstar in the world of skincare and hair care even.


I don’t know how I lived so long without it. It does so much. It’s compatible w my sensitive skin too.


You can use this on a greasy scalp or if you have dandruff, it’s the best pre shampoo treatment ever.  If you. Put some in a spray bottle, you can mist it all over your body and it will help w uneven skin tone , body acne and it’s the absolute goat for pre and post shaving treatment to keep ingrown hairs away. This bottle will last a while and it will do so much. Spray your back after the shower for backne prevention.. tackle hyperpigmentation issues on legs with a good spray down after the shower or whenever. It’s such a good multi user product it’s an excellent investment in your skin care

reviews.author: Adf
reviews.text: The best toner. Doesn’t break me out, I like the tingle, my face and skin has been so soft and glowing. It’s been about a month and I’ve paired it with a light face wash and moisturizer, and my face has been better than ever. Helps with large pores, and lasts a long time. I will definitely purchase this again. It’s a decent sized bottle and a little goes a long way. I’ve been wearing much less makeup because it’s helped so much. It works well especially being consistent. I think it would work for any skin type, but my skin in on the dry and dull side but this has helped tremendously and dosent dry my skin out more. It feels bouncy as well. Again, I do pair this with other light products, but this is a staple in my routine.

reviews.author: Aby Hinojosa
reviews.text:
This toner is an absolute miracle worker! I’ve been using it a few times a week, and the improvement in my skin's texture and radiance is incredible. It gently exfoliates, leaving my face feeling so smooth and looking noticeably brighter.





It is also amazing for clearing up dark spots and evening out skin tone. A little goes a long way, so the bottle lasts for months, making it an unbeatable value for the price. Just remember to use sunscreen during the day since it's an acid! It has completely transformed my skincare routine and I can’t recommend it enough

reviews.author: wiceD
reviews.text: The Ordinary Glycolic Acid 7% Toning Solution has become one of my favorite skincare products. It gently exfoliates my skin, leaving it smoother, brighter, and more even-looking after just a few uses. I’ve noticed an improvement in skin texture and a healthy glow without spending a lot of money. The bottle lasts a long time, making it an excellent value. It’s effective, affordable, and a great addition to any skincare routine. Highly recommended!

name: The Ordinary Lactic Acid 10% + HA
ingredients: Aqua (Water), Lactic Acid, Glycerin, Pentylene Glycol, Propanediol, Sodium Hydroxide, Sodium Hyaluronate Crosspolymer, Tasmannia Lanceolata Fruit/Leaf Extract, Acacia Senegal Gum, Xanthan Gum, Isoceteth-20, Trisodium Ethylenediamine Disuccinate, Ethylhexylglycerin, 1,2-Hexanediol, Caprylyl Glycol.

couche3.note: AHA lactique, lisse/éclat; prudence sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the lactic acid serum effective at exfoliating and transforming their skin, making it smooth and clear with a glowing appearance. The product quickly absorbs into the skin and is considered a great addition to skincare routines, with one customer noting it can be used daily or every other day. While some customers find it gentle enough for sensitive skin, others report it can be harsh. The color receives mixed feedback, with several customers noting it has a yellow appearance.

reviews.author: Mike-N-Jessica
reviews.text:
As a 40-something year old mom juggling kids, work, and everything in between, I don’t have time (or budget!) for complicated skincare routines. I wanted something simple, effective, and affordable—and the Ordinary Lactic Acid Serum has been a lifesaver.





✨ Gentle but Effective Exfoliation


My skin can get a little dull and uneven, especially with stress and lack of sleep. This serum gives me that gentle chemical exfoliation I need without harsh scrubbing. After a couple of weeks, my skin looked smoother, brighter, and just healthier.





🌸 Great for Sensitive Skin


I was nervous about acids since my skin can be reactive, but this formula is surprisingly gentle. I started every other night and worked up to nightly use. No stinging, no peeling—just a soft glow.





💲 Budget-Friendly & Simple


As a mom, I appreciate that it’s affordable and doesn’t come with extra fluff. The packaging is straightforward, but the product delivers. I don’t feel guilty adding it into my routine.





⏱ Easy to Work Into a Routine


After cleansing, I put a few drops on, let it sink in, then follow with a moisturizer. Done. It doesn’t take more than 30 seconds, and that’s exactly what I need in the chaos of mom-life.





🌟 Noticeable Results


• Softer, smoother skin texture


• Less flaky patches around my nose/chin


• Makeup goes on more evenly


• A subtle “glow” that makes me look more rested than I actually am!





💡 Mom-to-Mom Tip


Don’t skip sunscreen in the morning! Any exfoliating serum makes your skin more sensitive to the sun. I learned the hard way.





Overall, this serum feels like my little self-care secret. It’s simple, affordable, and it makes me feel a bit more put together—even on days when I’m running on coffee and dry shampoo.

reviews.author: Michelle
reviews.text: Let me start this of by saying I never write reviews. I have been having trouble with my skin on my cheeks and my nose. All of a sudden, I've been breaking out with little red acne for the first time in my life. I tried everything including retinol cream. nothing worked. It just kept getting worse and then I looked up what I could use for this redness and these breakouts and this product was recommended. When I say that I asked for a fast delivery and I tried it the first night the next morning everything was gone. I'm not sponsored. I am just a random person. Who bought this product and saw overnight results. I'm on day four and I actually use it twice a day now and people are complementing my skin and telling me how it is dramatically changed and it looks great. I don't even know what it is, but I'm going to keep using it and I'm going to recommend it. Doesn't remove wrinkles not that I can see. I have a very thin face, but the skin looks like I had a chemical peel. All the redness is gone.  I'm using it for the breakouts and the redness and it's a game changer.

reviews.author: Chelsea
reviews.text:
I alternate this and 0.25% tretinoin every other day and it has been the perfect combo for my acne prone, very fair, very sensitive skin.





After spending a ton of money trying everything under the sun to get my cystic acne under control this combo has been my holy trail for less than $20 combined. (I get my tret from All Day Chemist for $6 a tube).





For PM moisturizer I use a couple drops of jojoba oil mixed with a pea size of Vanicream in the tub, and only Neutrogena 60 SPF sunscreen in the morning before makeup.





My skin is so happy with this light routine - I've tried azaleic and glycolic acid from The Ordinary and some other products too but nothing has been as good as this. It feels very nice on the skin and absorbs quickly with no sticky or greasy residue. They also have a 5% solution which I recommend starting with if this is a new addition to your skin care routine.

reviews.author: kenzie
reviews.text: I’ve been using this twice a week as a very gentle exfoliator. My skin always feels extra smooth without any major irritation most acids cause. Easy to mix into lotion, and has a nice lightweight consistency.

reviews.author: Jordyn Emmert
reviews.text: This is a daily use product for me. Love how soft and supple it keeps my skin (I'm 30)

couche3.note: AHA mandélique doux, OK sensible/acné, éclaircit
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective for sensitive skin, particularly for cystic acne, and appreciate its gentle formula that doesn't cause irritation. The product helps with skin appearance, making it clearer and brighter, with dark spots lightening and skin tone becoming more even. While customers like the smooth texture and find it worth the price, opinions about moisturizing effects are mixed, with some finding it moisturizing while others describe it as oily. Skin irritation experiences are also mixed, with some customers reporting no breakouts while others experience red rashes in the chin area.

reviews.author: KC
reviews.text:
EDIT: i discovered Azelaic acid and it works much better for both cystic acne and pih without drying out my skin! I paused using the mandelic acid due to purging.





I had cystic neck acne and I’m now 30 years old and upset I’m just learning about the amazing wonders of mandelic acid. I deep dived into it and decided to get this. I only SPOT TREAT with this stuff to help heal my cystic acne and prevent pih and treat hyperpigmentation. I have brown skin and this stuff is amazingggggg at clearing up blemishes fast because it’s also antibacterial. I used it twice a day spot treatment only for the past 2 days and I’ve already seen an improvement. The cyst is FLAT and my hyperpigmentation is fading. I use the Dennis gross aha bha face wash, then spot treat with this 10% mandelic acid, then apply sunscreen 10 mins after. At night it’s the same minus sunscreen and just moisturizer or the Dennis gross texture serum. This stuff is amazing for the price!

reviews.author: Kadir
reviews.text: This product works extremely well for my sensitive skin. It doesn’t burn, sting, or cause any irritation, and most importantly, it hasn’t triggered any breakouts. It leaves very little residue behind maybe a slight oily feel initially but it absorbs well and dries down nicely. Since incorporating it into my routine, I’ve noticed a reduction in the number of breakouts I get, and it has also helped fade some of the dark spots left behind by previous acne. The formula is lightweight, comfortable to wear, and feels great on the skin. Considering the affordable price, I think it’s an excellent product and offers great value for money. In fact, I’m already on my second bottle.

reviews.author: Disappointed
reviews.text: I’ve only used it twice so far, but the results are always showing. I have very sensitive/combination/acne prone skin and have struggled with blemishes/acne scars for over a decade. I use Kate Somerville’s sulfur cleanser, The Ordinary’s Mandelic Acid, Azaleaic Acid and Dr. Althea’s 345 moisturizer and have noticed a VISIBLE reduction in my acne, dark spots and rosacea since incorporating mandelic acid. This is a dream product. I don’t mind the oily consistency, as this is my nightly routine and I just go to bed. I’ve woken up feeling extremely moisturized and with barely any redness on my face. I would definitely recommend

reviews.author: Candy
reviews.text:
For the price, it does its job :P simple effective formula and  ingredients


I have oily dehydrated skin and like using this during the nighttime a few days after using tretinoin, it helps exfoliating resurfaced skin like closed comodones.





Only gripe is I hate the oily texture, like not matter how much I wait it doesn’t dry down. But like it’s cheap? And it works?? So I can’t care

reviews.author: LL
reviews.text: Very gentle active that doesn't cause irritation but is effective. I use it 2x/week in the PM and my skin looks/feels so lovely in the morning. I have rosacea and acne and this didn't cause any issues for me.

name: Pixi Glow Tonic
ingredients: Glycolic Acid (AHA). Ginseng. Aloe Vera

couche3.note: tonique AHA doux, glow, OK normale/sèche
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the toner works wonders on their skin, providing a nice balanced glow from chin to forehead. The product is gentle on skin, particularly suitable for normal/dry skin, and customers appreciate its hydrating properties without causing dryness or peeling. They like its fragrance and report that scars fade with prolonged use. While some customers consider it a great value, others find the bottle size too small for the price.

reviews.author: J. Nolte
reviews.text:
Original review: 5 stars


I have only used this product for three days, so I will share an update in case anything changes as I use this product long-term! I have very dry skin that is prone to acne and redness. I mostly struggle with big pores and blackheads but I typically also have some pimples close to my mouth or on my forehead. Enter the Pixi Glow. I have used it five times now (mornings and evenings), but it seems like people vary a lot in how they use it. Some seem to use it twice a day, others once a day, and others yet use it only a few times a day. I will let you know if I end up using it less often! For now, I have not experienced any dryness or shedding/ peeling of my skin (however, I do moisturize very heavily due to my dry skin, so that might have helped). I don't know by which magic this tonic works but I am seeing a huge difference in my skin on the third day already! No redness, no pimples, and smaller pores. It's crazy - I have struggled with my skin since puberty and my skin has not looked this nice in more than a decade. I am seriously in love. If I notice side effects, or if it stops working, I will be back. For now, I am very happy to have given it a chance. BTW, the smell is very pleasant - reminds me of roses or other flowers.





Updated review: 4 stars


After achieving amazing reviews at the stars, I was pretty disappointed once the toner started to lose its magic. One week in, it seemed to stop working for me. I now only use it once a day (in the evening) and feel like using it more sparingly (I originally used it twice a day) works better for me - I still see some results every morning when I wake up but they are not at great as when I first started using the toner. I still don't have any issues with dry skin but I do moisturize twice a day, so it could be because of that. Will leave new review if anything else changes.

reviews.author: Kindle Customer
reviews.text: This product has completely transformed my skincare routine! My skin looked brighter and more radiant after just one use.

reviews.author: Kathleen T. DeShong
reviews.text: I think this is a Quality toner, it always gives my skin a supple feeling.

reviews.author: ask jona
reviews.text: Works... makes my skin glow with no irritation.

reviews.author: K. Gorman
reviews.text: Great product. Did not irritate my skin.

couche3.note: AHA mandélique doux, acné/sensible, éclat
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective for acne-prone skin and appreciate its gentle formula for sensitive skin. Moreover, the product receives positive feedback for its texture, with customers noting smoother, glassy results without irritation, and its ability to brighten and even skin tone while reducing redness. Additionally, the serum absorbs nicely and customers consider it good value for money. However, several customers mention the product feels very sticky.

reviews.author: ennish207
reviews.text: I’m really impressed! It’s super affordable and a little goes a long way. The texture is slightly thicker than most serums, but it still spreads easily and doesn’t feel sticky or heavy on the skin. What stood out most to me is how gentle it is. My skin can be pretty sensitive, but I had no irritation, redness, or stinging when using this. Despite being mild, it works quickly. I noticed my blemishes clearing up faster and my skin looking more even within a short time. It’s a great option if you want an effective exfoliating serum that’s budget-friendly and suitable for sensitive skin.

reviews.author: CC
reviews.text:
I am absolutely loving the Good Molecules Mandelic Acid Serum! It’s gentle yet effective.  My skin feels smoother, brighter, and more even after just a few uses. I’ve noticed my pores look smaller and my texture has improved without any irritation, which is perfect for my sensitive skin.





The serum absorbs quickly and layers beautifully under my moisturizer. It’s such a simple addition that makes a noticeable difference. Highly recommend if you want a gentle, affordable exfoliating serum that actually works!

reviews.author: McKenzie
reviews.text: Purged my skin a little at first even only using 1-2 times per week. Once my skin got used to it I liked it. Also works to exfoliate Brazilian area in between waxes

reviews.author: Ashley
reviews.text: Works great for acne prone skin. It needs to be slowly added into your skincare routine to prevent drying out. The texture is smooth and rubs in nice.

reviews.author: Sarah Webster
reviews.text: very good product

couche3.note: AHA 8% éclat/lisse; irritation mitigée (prudence sensible)
couche3.customers_say: Customers report that the exfoliant works wonders for their skin, making it feel smooth and supple throughout the day, with brighter and clearer appearance. The product receives mixed feedback regarding skin irritation and sensitivity - while some find it gentle and non-irritating, others report severe reactions. Similarly, opinions on value for money are divided, with some considering it a great value while others find it too little for the price. The product's drying effects also receive mixed reviews, with some noting quick fading of marks while others find it excessively drying.

reviews.author: L.
reviews.text:
I absolutely adore this stuff. I have had skin issues and blemishes for over a decade and my skin is finally much clearer than I ever thought possible and that is completely thanks to chemical exfoliation.





If you cannot afford Paula's Choice I highly recommend CorSx or other asian beauty brands that have AHA/BHA that are cheaper





This is the first AHA I tried, and it is the only AHA I use now. It prevents acne, improves my skin texture and helps me get rid of CCs





Important: if you have closed comedones (those little black things in your nose that you might think are blackheads) chemical exfoliation and oil cleansing could be a miraculous thing for you (as it was for me)





The strange way I oil cleanse with this product to get as many grits as possible (grits are CCs, sebum, dead skin that you can get out of your skin with oil cleansing, it is so wonderfully satisfying and fun to do this! please try this at least once to see how your skin responds, my skin looks so clear and smooth the day after it's amazing).





apply Paula's Choice BHA 2% (or a similar product, stridex in the red box works as well but only in the red box and not the green because the green does not have the proper PH, if you are new to any of this I highly recommend researching online!)  - wait 20 minutes for it to work





apply Paula's Choice AHA 8% - wait 20 minutes (a lot of people say you don't have to wait, but I find it makes a huge difference - also you could just use one of these, but I've found that both together yield the best results.)





apply your oil of choice, I use: 

Life-Flo Organic Pure Safflower Oil, 16 Ounce

 it has plenty of 'slip,' and gets me more grits than any other oil (I also mix the oil with other oils like rosehip if I feel like it) - wait five minutes (you can skip that if you're sick of waiting)





now massage your face!! so much stuff will come out it's insane. after you do that for at least 5-10 minutes (be gentle! don't rub your face too harshly) - wash your face with a good cleanser (I prefer Cerave foaming cleanser because it has a low ph and it is good for acne-prone skin - also, cleansers should not make your face feel tight or 'squeaky' at all! do not strip your skin) - I also use a konjac sponge for gentle removal (I get these for 89 cents on ebay).





Other people recommend putting on a mask like the aztec healing clay mask (which I don't recommend for dry skin) between oil cleansing and the chemical exfoliation for more grits, but that has not worked for me so I don't use the mask.





I do this kind of reverse process (normally you apply chemical exfoliators after you cleanse) at least three times a week and my closed comedones are significantly diminished if not entirely gone! (they refill themselves if you stop, there's no permanent way to remove them so this is the best method I've found for emptying them).





If you have acne, if you have skin-texture issues, if you want clearer, healthier skin - this product is a must for you! Chemical exfoliation is much gentler than physical exfoliation and much more effective in my experience.





This is a holy grail product for me and I urge you to try it out! (IMPORTANT NOTE: if you are new to chemical exfoliants please work up to using it multiple times a week! don't stress your skin out by using it constantly and only use it once a day. Make sure to use plenty of sunscreen with an AHA because it will make your skin more photosensitive!)

reviews.author: Jenny
reviews.text:
I have sensitive, acne prone, combination skin. I started breaking out at 24 and it was on and off. Then, two years ago, I started breaking out to the point where family and friends would ask what was going on. There was no part of my face that did not have acne or congested pores. It was embarassing and painful!





Well, let me tell you — this stuff got rid of all my skin problems! I had a mixture of milia, regular acne, and cystic acne on my face. I was using 16% glycolic acid, which only led to angry breakouts. Once I switched over to this, I started seeing fantastic results. Warning: clogged pores or burgeoning pimples will come to the surface or “purge.” However, you will know if something is not working for you if you start breaking out in angry and inflamed acne (especially in areas you don’t usually break out in). This was thankfully not the case for me.





It took about four months for my skin to completely clear. It even cleared up stubborn spots that other products were ineffective in.





My skin is very sensitive and this has not irritated it. One tiny drop is enough to spread on my entire face. I noticed that it stings a bit and can be drying when I use more than usual.





I am so thankful I found this product! I had a baby two months ago and my skin has remained clear postpartum. This was not the case with my first two babies! Even when I’m tired and fatigued, my skin still has a fresh glow from the product. I haven’t had a “glow” since my early 20s. I am now 31.





Please try this! I was desperate and prayed this would work for me. And it actually did- so it might work for you too! I started out with the travel product and I just ordered a full size.





This is my routine for my fellow acne sufferers: FAB beauty face wash, cerave am light moisturizing fluid, Clinique moisture surge (at night), and ddf 5% benzoyl peroxide in the am. I use PC aha gel at night only.





Update 5/28/19





I have consistently used this every night since my last review. My skin has continued to IMPROVE. I can now leave the house WITHOUT foundation! This is something I haven't been able to do since my college days and that was a decade ago! When I do break out on my chin, my pimple either never surfaces and disappears or comes to a head (which I pop) and heals within a day. A DAY! This is AMAZING for someone who previously had cysts that would remain under the surface for one week, and then would take another week to heal. It was embarrassing to leave the house or when I had unexpected visitors. If you constantly have breakouts, then you probably understand how it can lead you to not want to leave the house, avoid sitting in front of the window, avoid the outdoors because your acne appears more visible, despise overhead lighting (casts unflattering shadows on your face due to textured skin). Even though it's "just acne," your life is often affected because you feel so self-conscious. I cannot recommend this product enough. This is the one product I currently cannot live without. You must try this one! What can you lose?!

reviews.author: Hummingbirder
reviews.text:
I found out a long time ago, I can't use AHA on my face.  Paula's Choice wasn't the only AHA product I tried.  All of them sting my face, so I use BHA on my face.  The first time I bought the Skin Perfecting 8% AHA Gel, I decided not to waste it and used it on my hands.  I finished that bottle very happy with the results, but didn't buy it again for a long time, because I always have BHA product.





But for several years, I have had a great deal of dryness on the base knuckles of my forefingers and middle fingers.  It's as though the oil glands stopped working altogether, and as a result, I had very red knuckles in the winter.  Though I've had great results with BHA, it wasn't cutting the mustard, so to speak on my hands.  So I decided to try the 8% AHA again, before winter set in.





Shortly before bedtime, I wash my hands halfway up my forearms, then apply one pump to the backs of my hands and wrists.  I follow that with a couple applications of a good moisturizer, such as 

Olay Quench Body Lotion, Normal to Dry Skin with Green Tea 8.4 fl oz (250 ml)

 or 

Gold Bond  Ultimate Restoring Lotion, With CoQ10, 13-Ounce Bottle

.  Of course, I have to apply moisturizer during the day, too, but this I do as a nightly treatment.





I couldn't be more pleased, either.  This gel absorbs quickly, and helps draw moisturizer deeper into my skin.  My hands are much smoother, my knuckles look better, and due to the exfoliation, a lot of sun damage disappeared.  What started as an idea to help my knuckles succeeded, and then some.  My hands look and feel great!





I would caution you not to use this on broken skin; it will only irritate it.  So, if your skin is cracked, you'll need to get it healed up some before starting use of either AHA or BHA.





It takes two weeks to a month for results to show.  If you're ready to start your exfoliation regimen, prepare to be amazed.  Two wings up!

reviews.author: Jae Martinez
reviews.text: My complexion has not been so kind to me as of late and it's been really frustrating. I have darker skin so I deal with hyper-pigmentation from sporadic breakouts and with having combination/oily skin it's tough to find the right mix of products that make a difference. For some reason my skin has become really dull as well. With that I decided to dramatically change up my regular skin care routine in the hopes that I can get my fresh face back. My two constant problem areas are my neck & under my chin as well as the random breakouts & resulting dark spots. I have always tended to stuck to one product line but I've found a few items from a few different lines that have worked and have created 4 different routines. With the addition of Paula's Choice, my routine has kicked into high gear. The nightly use of this product has made a HUGE difference in the week I've used it. I have a morning and evening routine which alternates between acne, cleansing, exfoliating & moisturizing series of products. The one constant each night has been Paula's Choice Skin Perfecting Gel. I used this product at night after using my facewash (rotating between Kiehl's/Dermalogica/ProActive) & toner (Mario Badescu) and I have had no adverse reactions. My skin tone has improved, my spots seem to be getting lighter and my face is looking healthy, brighter and more firm again. I've tried peels, wipes, dermabrasion, exfoliators on top of Clarisonic. The darker parts hadn't budged or my skin would react but there would be no impact with the problem areas. I do wish there was a comparable eye cream to this gel as my eyelids have darkened some recently and this product advises you to keep away from your eyes. Aside from that, this is a winner and will be part of my routine so long as it keeps working.

reviews.author: Beauty/skincare enthusiast
reviews.text: This is a great chemical exfoliant with glycolic acid! It's a clear gel texture that spreads very easily and makes my skin glow! If you've never used an AHA before, this might sting a little bit the first couple times but after that it doesn't feel like anything. It's not a super intense peel or anything so it won't irritate your skin...unless you have EXTREMELY sensitive skin, then you might have some tingling. But it will not dry you out or make your face peel. Paula's choice put hydrating ingredients in with the aha (glycolic acid) so your getting that chemical exfoliant you want along with hydration so your killing 2 birds with one stone. I have dry sensitive skin so I still need a moisturizer after using this but it would probably be enough if you have oily skin. I use this about 4 times a week in the morning after my cleanser and toner but before my moisturizer. Also DO NOT forget to wear sunscreen if you are using this. That acid is going to make your skin sensitive to the suns rays so it will be much easier for you to get sunburned and hyperpigmentation. It gets rid of the dead skin cells sitting on the top layer of the skin and sloughs them all off. It will allow all the products you put on after absorb that much deeper into the layers of the skin. It's a really great product and I would definitely recommend it!

couche3.note: AHA/BHA doux, estompe cicatrices/taches, éclat
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective as a mild chemical exfoliator and appreciate its ability to fade acne scars and hyperpigmentation, making their skin more vibrant and glowy. The product receives positive feedback for its softening effects, with one customer noting it's gentle enough to leave on overnight. While some customers find it great for sensitive skin, others report stinging sensations, and opinions on value for money are mixed.

reviews.author: Nat Rose
reviews.text:
So far I think I like this, though if you don’t slowly build your skin up to tolerate this harsher treatment, it can cause pain, stinging, redness and irritation. That said, I do believe this is helpful for a lot of skin issues.


I think my skin is a little drier, so I’m working on finding the right combo of moisturizers and proper steps to help remedy this.


IMPORTANT NOTE: Be careful not to mix certain other skincare products or ingredients with it (no matter what the website may say!).


To be helpful, I have made a list of things NOT to mix at the same time and important tips.


However, to be sure, ask a professional dermatologist:


•DO NOT MIX:


-RETINOL should not be mixed with: Vitamin C, Vitamin E, AHA/BHA or Benzol Peroxide.


-AHA/BHA should not be mixed with: Retinol, Vitamin C or Niacinamide (and possibly peptides but I am not 100% sure on that).


-VITAMIN C should not be mixed with Retinol, AHA/BHA or Niacinamide.


-VITAMIN E should not be mixed with Retinol.


-NIACINAMIDE should not be mixed with AHA/BHA or Vitamin C


-BENZOYL PEROXIDE should not be mixed with Retinol.


NOTE: Both Retinol and AHA/BHA can be very helpful for various skin issues. While you should not MIX them, you can switch between them (example: Monday for Retinol, Tuesday for AHA/BHA, etc) nightly once your skin has been safely worked up to tolerating these more intense skin remedies. Do not try to do this all at once, right off the bat.


•SUN AND CARE:


-While the Drunk Elephant website doesn’t always address this, in most cases RETINOL and AHA/BHA should be used at night as they cause sensitivity to the sun that can damage skin. I don’t know if applying sunscreen will fully keep your skin safe (not to mention all the reapplying). While you *may* be able to use it if you’re staying indoors, do understand that harmful rays can enter the home, car and workplace through windows. If you’re going to experience sun, regardless of what you do or do not put on your skin, an SPF of at least 30 should be an everyday routine (with reapplying).


As for if you stay indoors, away from windows, one would assume hanging out in a dark room all day would suffice, but I’m no expert.


-So it’s beat to use Retinol and AHA/BHA at night and wash it off in the morning. A cream or serum containing Vitamin C thrives off sunlight, so that is best to use in the daytime.


•IF YOU OVERDO THE SKIN TREATMENTS AND YOUR SKIN IS FEELING DRY OR IRRITATED: Take a break from the harsh products and use very gentle ones for a little bit. Particularly CERAMIDES. These are gentle and help your skin to heal, calm and moisturize your skin. I personally have used (and liked) “Good Cera Super Ceramide Emulsion (pH 5.5 / 7 free)” which is a nice, gentle moisturizer. There are also things like ceramide masks that can help.

reviews.author: James S.
reviews.text: I’ve known about this stuff for a couple of years and never pulled the trigger because of the price tag. Now that I’m 20 wks pregnant and my skin is a hot mess I decided it’d be worth it to give it a try and I am SO SO glad that I did. Pregnancy wrecks my skin- I get acne like a teenager yet super dry and patchy, and I get “the mask of pregnancy” redness on my cheeks. So I overall just feel crusty and can’t hide my acne with makeup bc it just looks worse. I have been using this stuff for four days and it’s worth every penny. My acne has completely cleared up, I wake up glowing, I don’t feel dry and crusty and the redness is starting to subside. It’s a miracle product and I’ll definitely be buying again when I run out!

reviews.author: Ms Brown
reviews.text: I was one of those dinasours who still used physical exfoliation until I realized my skin was too thin and of course, damaged. This thing made me forget about those endless nights trying to rub/scrub my imperfections off my skin. Now I just put this on a clean face at night and wake up with a softer, cleaner face. It's too soon to say it made my dark spots dissapear (only used it for 2 weeks) but I think the fact that I don't have any other random pimples, will make my face look better because I won't have to deal with scarring anymore. The only thing I don't like is the price...but you get what you pay for, I guess. In this case, I got a high quality product that seems to be working. So far, happy I bought this.

reviews.author: bekah
reviews.text: My skin is like glass in the morning after a night time application. This product combines glycolic and salicylic acids and it didn’t irritate or dry out my skin. I use a moisturizer over the product. It rivals the Paula’s Choice grey bottle of salicylic but better because the glycolic acid target dark spots.

reviews.author: Carole Mathis
reviews.text: I have use this product for several years. Apply at night, not having a scent, a definite plus and moisturizer well. Pricey but worth it.

name: Sunday Riley Good Genes Lactic Acid

couche3.note: AHA lactique premium, éclat/lisse, hydrate; prudence sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers report that the treatment serum makes their skin look and feel healthier, with immediate brightening effects and smooth texture. Moreover, the product effectively moisturizes without leaving an oily residue, and one customer notes it gently exfoliates without irritation. However, customers disagree on whether it's worth the price. Additionally, some customers report burning and stinging sensations on their skin.

reviews.author: MissM
reviews.text:
OMG, I wish I could afford to bathe in Good Genes! It's one of the very few products I've elevated to my "holy grail" list- formulas that actually deliver results and worth their weight in gold.





I initially thought Good Genes was ridiculously overpriced for what appeared to be a fairly basic formula: purified lactic acid combined with a handful of skin-brightening botanicals. There's nothing particularly cutting-edge about the ingredients themselves. Lactic acid, lemongrass, aloe, arnica, and licorice have been used in beauty products for centuries. Even Cleopatra reportedly bathed in sour milk as low-tech lactic acid body peel!





Good Genes was in my cart for weeks before I finally decided to bite the proverbial skincare bullet and order it. Honestly, I almost wish I hadn't…because I absolutely love it.





If SkinCeuticals C E Ferulic has taught me anything, it's that creating a truly exceptional skincare product is about much more than the ingredient list. Percentages, concentrations, formulation, and delivery all matter. While Good Genes seems basic, it outperforms every other leave-on lactic acid treatment I've tried. The results speak for themselves, and that high price is easier to justify.





I'm in my mid-40s and had great skin until I turned 40. Seemingly overnight, a lifetime of questionable decisions appeared etched across my face every time I looked in the mirror. Good Genes restored a youthful glow to my very dry, dull, and probably perimenopausal skin almost IMMEDIATELY.





That's one of the things I love most about it- you get that instant gratification each time you apply it. Your skin looks brighter, smoother, and more radiant right away, while continued use helps soften fine lines and improve overall texture. My skin is noticeably more even, the fine lines around my eyes are smoother, and even my stubborn "1” wrinkle looks much softer.





If you're over 40, haven't yet discovered the magic of lactic acid, and don't want to deal with peels, I highly recommend giving Good Genes a try. It's an easy-to-use, no-rinse treatment that fits seamlessly into almost any skincare routine, whether you're a minimalist or a skincare enthusiast with a 20 step routine.





One pump is enough for my entire face, while two pumps cover my face, neck, upper chest, and hands. It layers beautifully under makeup, but also pairs nicely with retinol or other serums at night. I personally prefer using it in the morning, followed by moisturizer and sunscreen. It never pills and plays nicely with every product I've paired it with.





My only real complaint is the price. If you can, wait for a sale and stock up. I certainly will.





I really hate how much I love this stuff.

reviews.author: Melissa H.
reviews.text: I’ve tried a lot of exfoliating serums, and this one actually lives up to the hype. It makes my skin look smoother, brighter, and more glowy by the next morning without feeling overly harsh. The texture is lightweight and layers well with the rest of my skincare. A little goes a long way, so the bottle lasts awhile. The only downside is the price, but honestly it’s one of the few products I consistently notice a difference with.

reviews.author: B. Beauvais
reviews.text: Once upon a time, I thought this stuff was crazy expensive... Then I tried it and TOTALLY GOT IT! Super effective yet gentle, very moisturizing, and smells ah-mazing! Also-no, just because another serum has lactic doesn't mean it's "basically the same thing as Good Genes" because it's just not. I will always have a bottle of this serum to hand.

reviews.author: Beth Odabashian
reviews.text: LOVE my Good Genes. It makes my skin feel so soft, and i can use it as my moisturizer too.

reviews.author: JANET SAVICKAS
reviews.text: It’s a great product

name: Cetaphil Extra Gentle Daily Scrub
ingredients: Water, Cocamidopropyl Betaine, Disodium Cocoamphodiacetate, Coco-Glucoside, Sodium Cocoamphoacetate, Glycerin, Panthenol (Vitamin B5), Tocopheryl Acetate (Vitamin E), Bambusa Arundinacea Stem Extract, Sodium Hydroxide, Sodium Benzoate, Sodium Citrate, Xanthan Gum, Heliotropine, Phenoxyethanol, Polyquaternium-10, Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer, Citric Acid, Fragrance

couche3.note: gommage doux quotidien, OK sensible; parfumé
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the face wash effective at exfoliating and cleaning while being gentle enough for sensitive skin, with a creamy texture and micro-fine granules. The product receives positive feedback for its effectiveness and value for money. However, the fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some loving the scent while others note a strong perfume smell. Moreover, several customers report skin irritation, mentioning it made their face burn and break out. The bead size also draws mixed opinions, with some appreciating the tiny particles while others find them too small.

reviews.author: Christopher Jack
reviews.text: I have sensitive skin so Cetaphil products are perfect for me. This face wash won't rip off the top lining of your skin, but you'll still feel clean after using it. I never get breakouts from using it.  Overall value is great. 2 bottles lasts me a few months (and I used it every day).

reviews.author: BuyGuy
reviews.text: My daughter turned me on to this. Gets rid of scaley dry skin. Try it!

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text: Very gentle exfoliator that works well. Fragrance free

reviews.author: cheri
reviews.text: Great cleaner. Not really an exfoliant.

reviews.author: Jeanne
reviews.text: Non irritating and nice clean

name: St. Ives Gentle Smoothing Oatmeal Scrub & Mask
ingredients: "Water, Sodium Methyl 2-Sulfolaurate, Glycerin, Hydrated Silica, Decyl Glucoside, Acrylates/Palmeth-25 Acrylate Copolymer, Acrylates Copolymer, Cocamidopropyl Betaine, Disodium 2-Sulfolaurate, Cocamidopropyl PGDimonium Chloride Phosphate, Juglans Regia (Walnut) Shell Powder, Avena Sativa (Oat) Meal Extract, Honey, Fragrance, Polyacrylamide, PPG-2 Methyl Ether, Phenethyl Alcohol, Disodium EDTA, Methylisothiazolinone, Titanium Dioxide."

couche3.note: Gommage doux a lavoine, exfolie et lisse, apprecie peau seche ; exfoliant physique (poudre de noix) - prudence peau sensible ; excellent prix.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this face scrub effective at exfoliating and moisturizing dry skin, with one noting it helped lighten acne scars. The product is gentle enough for sensitive skin, leaving it smooth and soft, and customers appreciate its divine fragrance and good value for money. While some customers say it washes off easily with no mess, others find it difficult to rinse off.

reviews.author: S.M.
reviews.text: I thought I could actually use this as a face mask, too, but that it doesn’t seem to be suited for that. As a gentle scrub, I like it a lot. For those with very sensitive skin, this would probably still be too abrasive. My skin is only slightly sensitive so this caused no issues for me. It’s less drying and irritating than other scrubs due to the oatmeal and honey, but my skin still feels a bit tight after using. I don’t find this to be an issue since I layer several essences, serums, and moisturizers after, but if you’re looking to wash and go, this probably isn’t the right product. I recommend using a normal face wash or cleansing balm before this to be sure all traces of makeup and residue are removed because, on its own, this product isn’t quite enough to accomplish that (especially eye makeup - the skin on your eyes is too delicate for scrubs)! All in all, I like it a lot and find it nice for daily use. Good purchase.

reviews.author: kmhawks26
reviews.text: I have very dry, flaky, fairly sensitive skin especially during the colder months. I've used the St. Ives peach scrub in the past but it's a bit abrasive and specifically meant for acne prone skin. This scrub works so much better for my skin type. It has a gentle exfoliating base made of walnut shell powder and oatmeal. It also smells divine! It's super gentle on my skin, yet works well to remove flaky dry skin and leaves my skin feeling clean, fresh and glowing. Definitely recommended! Especially if you have dry skin.

reviews.author: Ladybugsenior
reviews.text: Very gentle but does the job.  I have used many and this is top grade. Better than more costly ones.  All of their lotions are great too.

reviews.author: Anne
reviews.text:
I like this cleanser/mask, it's soft and not so harsh on the skin.


It leaves your skin smooth and clean all the debris, pollution...

reviews.author: Stephanie Hall
reviews.text: Works great. Is gentle on your skin. Scrubs pretty great. Makes your face feel like you actually cared enough to wash it.

couche3.note: enzymatique doux quotidien, acné/taches, OK sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the exfoliant effective at managing surface acne and hyperpigmentation, while being gentle enough for sensitive skin and suitable for daily use. The product makes skin smooth and bright, with one customer noting immediate texture improvement after one use, and customers appreciate its consistency, with one mentioning it turns into a nice paste when applied to wet skin. While some customers consider it worth the price, others find it expensive.

reviews.author: victoria
reviews.text:
The Dermalogica Daily Microfoliant is a gentle yet effective exfoliator that lives up to the hype and has become a regular step in my skincare routine. It comes as a fine powder that activates with a little water, creating a creamy, silky texture that feels soft on the skin rather than gritty or harsh. What I like most is how it smooths and brightens without irritation  even when I use it a few times a week, my complexion looks more even, fresher, and less dull without that tight, dry feeling you sometimes get with stronger exfoliants.


It does a great job of gently lifting away dead skin cells and helping other products absorb better, and it’s suitable for most skin types, including sensitive skin (just be mindful if you have very reactive skin). It’s pricier than drugstore scrubs, but the results feel worth it: smoother texture, fewer dry patches, and a noticeable glow without redness. If you want a daily friendly exfoliator that’s both gentle and effective, the Dermalogica Daily Microfoliant delivers consistent, reliable results without over stripping your skin. After adding this to my skincare routine my face has never felt fresher.

reviews.author: Big River Shopper
reviews.text:
Dermalogica exfoliating powder has been a great addition to my skincare routine. I really appreciate how gentle it is while still being effective at removing dead skin and leaving my skin feeling smooth and refreshed.





Unlike harsher exfoliants that can cause burning or irritation, this one feels very controlled and skin-friendly. It provides a clean, polished feel without disrupting the skin barrier, which makes it suitable for regular use without overdoing it.





The powder format is also very convenient—it’s easy to control how much you use, and it lathers nicely when activated with water. The packaging is simple, travel-friendly, and practical, which fits well with a daily skincare routine.





As expected from Dermalogica, the formula feels thoughtfully developed and reliable. It also lasts a long time since only a small amount is needed per use, making it a good value over time despite the higher upfront price.





Overall, this is a gentle but effective exfoliator that delivers consistent results without irritation. A solid choice for anyone looking for a skincare-focused exfoliation option from a trusted brand.

reviews.author: J.
reviews.text: This product is amazing and will have you coming back again and again! It helped me to manage surface acne/hyperpigmentation years before i was able to get into a derm consistently to really resolve it. It very gently exfoliants the skin with chemical exfoliants but also insanely refined physical exfoliant that won’t damage skin if used properly. I still use it now but more for polishing my skin and as part of my dermaplane routine. It can be used all over the body to and will leave your skin soft, supple, smooth, and more youthful and radiant after just one use. After consistent use your skin will grow all the time. It’s pricey but definitely worth it. For reference I have a light/medium brown skin tone, naturally oily skin (combo when managed), and have had issues with hormonal acne and post inflammatory hyperpigmentation. I have allergies and sensitive skin and can’t use many products due to fragrance and irritation.

reviews.author: Noel
reviews.text: Excellent abrasion cleanser. Helps to get the dead skin off. I have a dry face and even with moisturizing I still get flakey sometimes. This definitely helps clean all that up. The cleanser comes as a powder you dump onto moist hand and rub onto a wet face. Definitely different than other cleansers. Excellent functionality but it comes at a cost. This stuff totes a hefty price tag compared to just soap.

reviews.author: Leni
reviews.text: Dermalogica never disappoints!  It has been, and still is, one of the best skin care lines on the market. I use many products from Dermalogica and this is one of my favorites. Immediate results, safe for my sensitive skin and truly does leave my skin smooth and glowing by removing dead skin and product residue. It goes a long way so cost effective as well. I can’t do without it.

couche3.note: enzymatique doux, OK sensible, lisse
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the exfoliant effective at removing dead skin and making their face feel fresh and smooth, particularly noting it's gentle on sensitive skin. Moreover, the product foams well and lasts a while. However, opinions about value for money are mixed, with some finding it worth the price while others consider it pricey. Additionally, customers disagree on the product's effect on dryness.

reviews.author: Michelle LaPan
reviews.text:
OMG! What a fabulous product! I’ve been looking for something that would provide phenomenal results and this is it!  Anyone that is looking for a great product and for the results a great price this is it! After the very first use, my face feels completely soft and a totally different I’m only disappointed. I didn’t find this product earlier.





If you’re looking for something that lives up to the results, this is it…👍🏻

reviews.author: Tiffany R.Roberts
reviews.text:
Tatcha’s Rice Polish had my face feeling so smooth I was convinced I’d been secretly sandblasted by skincare angels. One minute I’m a tired human, the next I’m a porcelain teacup from a royal collection.





It gently exfoliates—so gently that I kept checking the mirror like, “Is this doing anything?” Then BAM. My skin went from “Florida humidity survivor” to “soft enough to star in a moisturizer commercial.”

reviews.author: Jo
reviews.text: I am in my late 50s, have dry acne prone skin and am going through menopause. I use this product religiously and have for years. Gets my face squeaky clean, soft and smooths the texture all without drying my skin and without it aggravating my acne. A little goes a long way. I love it so much I have it on subscribe and save. I have this delivered every 5 months.

reviews.author: Leslie
reviews.text: This is the most amazing skin product. It comes out as small white grains and when mixed with water becomes a mild, luxurious exfoliating cleanser. My skin feels amazing after I use it.

reviews.author: Yael
reviews.text: I use this product for around 2 years. It last long because you don't need a lot of it, and it foams really good. It leaves my skin soft and clean :)

name: Pixi Peel & Polish
ingredients: Water/Aqua/Eau, LacticAcid, Polylactic Acid, Helianthus Annuus(Sunflower) Seed Oil, Cetearyl Alcohol,Propanediol, Ceteareth-20, Beraclay LightRed, Hamamelis Virginiana (Witch Hazel)Water, Panax Ginseng Root Extract,Carica Papaya (Papaya) Fruit Extract,Saccharum Officinarum (Sugar Cane)Extract, Acer Saccharum (Sugar Maple)Extract, Cucumis Sativus (Cucumber)Fruit Extract, Citrus Aurantium Dulcis(Orange) Fruit Extract, Citrus Limon(Lemon) Fruit Extract, VacciniumMyrtillus Fruit Extract,Hydroxyethylcellulose, Polyglceryl-3Cocoate, Butylene Glycol, MaltooligosylGlucoside, Glyceryl Stearate, PEG-100Stearate, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride,Dipentaerythrityl Tri-Polyhydroxystearate,Hydrogenated Starch Hydrolysate,Phenoxyethanol, Caprylyl Glycol, SodiumHydroxide, Xanthan Gum, Polyglceryl-4Caprate, Polyglceryl-6 Caprylate,Polyglceryl-6 Ricinoleate, PentyleneGlycol, Sorbic Acid, HydroxyphenylPropamidobenzoic Acid, AscorbylPalmitate, Benzoic Acid, Tocopherol. *Please refer to current packaging for the latest and updated ingredient listing.

couche3.note: enzymatique doux, OK sèche, éclat
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the exfoliant effective for dry skin, with one noting it doesn't cause irritation. The product receives positive feedback for its texture, with one customer describing it as making skin feel like silk, while another mentions it leaves a subtle glow.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text: I keep this in my shower and use it about once a week or once every two weeks and it makes my skin so smooth and it’s so fine that it doesn’t irritate my skin and I have very sensitive skin. It does have a funky smell, but you only have it on for five minutes and it’s gone. I think I’ve been through about seven of these.

reviews.author: Angeline Hernandez
reviews.text: El producto es fantástico remueve la piel muerta y la piel queda muy fresca. El empaque fue lo único que llegó en muy mal estado.

reviews.author: CR
reviews.text: This exfoliating cream makes your skin feel like silk without irritation like some more harsh exfoliating products.

reviews.author: Kristina Rosario
reviews.text: I love this product. It leaves feeling smoother after using it.

reviews.author: Misti Chang
reviews.text: I love this product.  Works very well.

couche3.note: niacinamide top grasse/acné, éclat, anti-rougeurs
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective at brightening, clearing, and reducing redness, while also being good for oily skin and working well with makeup. Moreover, the product receives positive feedback for its value, smooth texture, and moisturizing properties, with one customer noting it holds 1000X moisture in the skin. However, opinions about skin irritation are mixed, with some customers reporting it doesn't irritate sensitive skin while others experienced burning sensations.

reviews.author: Mike
reviews.text:
I’ve been using The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1% consistently for several weeks now, and it has genuinely earned a permanent place in my skincare routine. As someone with blemish-prone and combination skin, I’m always cautious when introducing new products—but this serum has exceeded my expectations in both performance and value.





First, the texture is lightweight and water-based, which makes it easy to layer with other skincare products. It absorbs quickly without leaving a greasy or sticky residue (as long as you don’t over-apply). I use just 2–3 drops for my entire face, and that’s more than enough. It sits well under moisturizer and sunscreen during the day and works seamlessly into my nighttime routine.





What really impressed me is how visibly it helped balance my skin. Within the first couple of weeks, I noticed a reduction in excess oil production—especially in my T-zone. My skin looked less shiny throughout the day, and my pores appeared more refined. Over time, it also helped calm down active breakouts and reduced the frequency of new ones. While it’s not a miracle overnight fix, it works steadily and reliably.





The 10% niacinamide concentration feels effective without being overly harsh. I experienced very minimal irritation, and that was only when I accidentally used too much product. Once I adjusted the amount, my skin tolerated it beautifully. The addition of zinc seems to support oil control and overall skin clarity, making it especially helpful for acne-prone or congested skin types.





Another major advantage is the price point. The Ordinary is known for affordable, ingredient-focused skincare, and this serum delivers high performance without the luxury markup. It feels like you’re getting a clinical-grade formula at a fraction of the cost of similar products.





Constructively speaking, beginners or those with sensitive skin may want to start slowly—perhaps once a day—to ensure their skin adjusts well to the higher concentration of niacinamide. Also, applying too much can sometimes cause a slight pilling effect, so less truly is more with this product.





Overall, I would highly recommend The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1% to anyone struggling with oiliness, enlarged pores, or frequent breakouts. With consistent use, it helps create a smoother, more balanced, and healthier-looking complexion. It’s simple, effective, affordable, and delivers exactly what it promises—which is honestly everything I look for in a skincare product.

reviews.author: Fringe
reviews.text:
This toner has worked miracles for my skin texture. I have suffered from acne for a long time and have struggled to find the right combination of products to help my skin and not make it worse. This toner, in combination with the same brand of moisturizer, has helped IMMENSELY. The redness of my skin and the size of my pores have gone down. I noticed a big difference within around a week. I know everyone’s skin is different, and believe me, I know that products that work for one person will not always work for another. However, for me personally, this product with the moisturizer has done its job, and I am a repeat buyer and will continue to be.





The product is pretty much scentless, feels good on my face , and takes a very small amount to cover my whole face. Another bonus factor is the included dropper- this makes the application of the product VERY easy, and I feel like I waste a lot less product.





The only ‘negative’ was that when I initially used this product alone my skin was a bit dried out, BUT I just added the same brand’s moisturizer and my skin feels almost flawless. I DO still have some acne but the amount is way less, and nothing else has come close to helping as much as this has.





Last thing I'll say is if you're considering it and are unsure, just do it ! You literally cannot beat the price (I know I have tried COUNTLESS products way more expensive than this) and if your skin dries out a bit this company makes great moisturizer that does not seem to clog my pores- try it !

reviews.author: Richard Lykins
reviews.text:
This was another one of my wife’s skincare additions, and while she hasn’t used it long enough yet for some dramatic life-changing transformation review, her first impression has been very positive so far.





The serum itself feels much more “serious skincare product” than random gimmicky beauty items, and overall the quality seems good right out of the bottle.





One thing she especially liked is that it applies smoothly and absorbs fairly quickly without leaving a super greasy or heavy feeling afterward.





The texture also felt lighter than expected, which is nice because some blemish-focused products can feel sticky or overly harsh on the skin.





From what she’s said so far, her skin has tolerated it well without irritation, dryness, or weird reactions during early use.





The bottle size is small, but since only a little product gets used each time, it seems like it should last a decent while.





The Ordinary products also seem to have a reputation for focusing more on straightforward ingredients and less on flashy marketing, which I can appreciate.





Overall, it’s still early for long-term results, but so far this seems like a genuinely good-quality skincare serum and my wife has been happy with it during initial use.

reviews.author: MMorgan
reviews.text:
I’m a big fan of The Ordinary brand, and their Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1% serum does not disappoint! It has become a regular part of my skincare routine. It’s lightweight, absorbs quickly, and layers well with my other products.





I’ve noticed my skin looks more balanced and less oily throughout the day, and it has helped improve the overall appearance of my complexion. A little goes a long way, making the bottle last quite a while.





If you’re looking for an affordable, effective niacinamide serum, this is definitely worth trying.

reviews.author: wiceD
reviews.text: “I’ve been using The Ordinary Niacinamide 10% + Zinc 1% for  5 weeks, and I’m really impressed with the results. It helped reduce the appearance of my pores, control excess oil, and improve the overall texture of my skin. My complexion looks clearer, smoother, and more balanced. The formula is lightweight, absorbs quickly, and works well with the rest of my skincare routine. Plus, it’s very affordable for the quality you get. I would definitely recommend it to anyone looking to improve their skin without spending a fort

couche3.note: niacinamide pores/taches; quelques réactions (prudence sensible)
couche3.customers_say: Customers report that the serum delivers on its promise to shrink pores and help with melasma, while also improving skin tone and making it look better. The product receives positive feedback for its effectiveness as one of the best niacinamide products, and customers appreciate its smoother texture and how it feels on the skin. The consistency receives mixed reactions, with some describing it as water-like while others find it sticky, and customers disagree on whether it's worth the price.

reviews.author: Brelana
reviews.text:
TAKE MY MONEY, PLEASE & THANK YOU! LOL!


Okay, bare with me, this will be a detailed, in-depth, review! Which I don't do often, but this deserves one! Because I have so many things I love about this serum & her products in general! ...





First let me state, it is clear & liquid, like a water consistency! Do not be put off by this! This is a good thing. You just add one dropper's amount into the palm of your hand (using clean hands) and with both hands pat it onto all of your face. (Clean face)... It absorbs really easy & quick! Feels very light, as if nothing is there. Not sticky or thick! Which is why it being like "water" is a GOOD THING! Because after this serum, you want to be able to apply moisturizer, sunscreen, and/or makeup - without difficulty! It wont ruin or clash with the products you go in with afterwards!





I have EXTREMELY sensitive skin! I have to take allergy medicine daily to avoid hives. I have Psoriasis on my elbows & my hairline on the back of my neck. Certain products can make my skin turn red with a burning sensation... Other than certain actual Perfumes, I can not handle fragrance in any of my skincare/hygiene products... It's just, yeah, like I said EXTREME - how sensitive my skin can be.


HOWEVER!!! This stuff does not hurt my skin! My face handles some of the Paula's Choice products I've tried so far, really well! I love that it targets my skin's issues, without any irritation (if used properly) ... I know not everyone's skin is the same, but my sensitive skin handles this particular serum just fine, without any problems!





I also want to compare this product to another expensive brand I've tried, "La Roche-Posay"...


When they were shipped to me, along with Paula's Choice products at the same time, La Roche-Posay's serums came with damaged product boxes & no seal! Their serums are not cheap! So you'd think they could package their stuff better & seal them! It also made me question if they were used, rather than new... On top of that, I did not realize until after they all arrived, that La Roche-Posay's serums all have unnecessary fragrance in them! Not Cool! It made my skin flare up & my skin was irritated as a result! That's when I realized the crap like fragrance... So I stopped using their stuff & continued forth with just my paula's choice & first aid beauty products only!


The Paula's Choice products I bought were all packaged well, no damage, & it was clear they were all new! And they have no fragrance! So I will never buy serums from La Roche-Posay again, and just stick with Paula's Choice! Im not gonna waste tons of money on another expensive brand like that, if I can trust THIS ONE more!





I also know Paula's Choice products are working, because the results were surprisingly FAST! I have never had a product work so quick before. I was shocked, in a very happy way! I have combination skin. Where some areas are oily & some are dry. With hormonal acne, blackheads on my nose & chin, acne scars on my cheeks & forehead, & visible pores... Or at least I did, prior to this stuff! It made my skin feel evenly soft! Moisture in the dry areas, yet not oily! It evened-out my skin, it feels like. My blackheads & pores & acne are all clearing up & way less visible... Just WOW! Left me speechless, honestly! I expected to go through that adjustment phase, where you breakout until your skin gets used to it. But no, I guess I got lucky, because it just immediately started working. I can't wait to see how clear everything is, with more time & more use!





I want to note that I use First Aid Beauty's face wash as a cleanser, First Aid Beauty's Retinol under-eye cream at night, and First Aid Beauty's face ultra moisturizer... HOWEVER, other than that, I am using Paula's choice products (This serum specifically! That dark grey bottle of "Skin Perfecting 2% BHA Liquid Salicylic Acid Exfoliant"... and the light blue bottle of her "Resist Weightless Advanced Repairing Toner, Niacinamide & Hyaluronic Acid") and when my First Aid Beauty's moisturizer is gone, I plan to give Paula's Choice brand of moisturizer a try, to see how it works too!
