I have fallen in-love with this brand, which I do not say lightly; and because of how well it works for me so far, I feel like I will become a new loyal/regular costumer! The reason I mention what brands I am using together, is to help express what all was used to achieve my awesome results!





I do use SPF 30 moisturizer in the La Roche-Posay brand (if Im gonna go outside or be out of the house) but it is unscented, and that was why I originally thought I could trust their serums, until that epically failed for me, because their brand of serums are fragranced & caused my skin to react badly!


So kudos to Paula's Choice for NOT being fragranced lol! My sensitive skin thanks you!

reviews.author: VanillaPepper
reviews.text:
I use this at night as part of my routine, and it layers really well with everything else I’m using.





The quality is excellent — the texture is lightweight, smooth, and absorbs quickly without feeling sticky or heavy. It sits nicely on the skin and doesn’t interfere with other products.





There’s no noticeable scent, which I prefer, and it feels gentle while still being effective over time.





It arrived well-packaged with no leaks, and the bottle is easy to use without wasting product.





For the price, it’s a great value for a targeted step in a nighttime routine. Simple, effective, and easy to incorporate.





This is as effective as any other brand you do not need to spend more money to get this particular product in an excellent quality. You need to know which brand to buy, and Paula’s choice is definitely one of them.





#AmazonFinds #AmazonFine #Chihuahuamom #Dobermanmom


#LuxeLife #MindfulLuxury #IntentionalLiving #ConsciousLiving


#NightRoutine #SkincareRoutine #HealthySkin #SelfCarePractice #CleanLiving #BeautyRitual

reviews.author: Whit
reviews.text:
This serum is amazing! I started using it to help with l pores and uneven skin tone, and within just a couple of days I noticed such a big difference. My skin looks smoother, less oily in the T-zone, and brighter overall. It layers easily with other products without pilling, and it doesn’t feel sticky or heavy.





I also love that it’s fragrance-free and gentle—I have sensitive skin, and it didn’t cause any irritation. The dropper bottle makes it easy to control how much I use, and a little goes a long way.





If you’re looking for something to calm redness, minimize pores, and even out your skin, this is definitely worth trying!

reviews.author: Heather McMahan
reviews.text: This has been a game changer for my mature skin. I lean towards the dry side and have bits of light melasma and large pores in my t zone. This has helped minimize melasma, pores and overall appearance of my skin.

reviews.author: Kristan Pietromonaco
reviews.text: I’m conflicted about this because there seems to be some visible improvement but there’s not enough in the bottle to keep going. I’m thinking probably 3 bottles would be needed to see major results.

name: Beauty of Joseon Glow Serum (Propolis/Niacinamide)
ingredients: Water Dipropylene Glycol Glycerin Butylene Glycol Propolis Extract Niacinamide 1,2-Hexanediol Betaine Salicylate Sodium Polyacryloyldimethyl Taurate Tromethamine Polyglyceryl-10 Laurate Xanthan Gum Caprylyl Glycol Carbomer Ethylhexylglycerin Melia Azadirachta Flower Extract Ocimum Sanctum Leaf Extract Melia Azadirachta Leaf Extract Sodium Hyaluronate Curcuma Longa (Turmeric) Root Extract Dextrin Theobroma Cacao (Cocoa) Extract Corallina Officinalis Extract Melaleuca Alternifolia (Tea Tree) Extract Centella Asiatica Extract Methylpropanediol Lotus Corniculatus Seed Extract Pentylene Glycol Octanediol Calophyllum Inophyllum Seed Oil Tocopherol

couche3.note: glow, uniformise, hydratant, doux
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this serum effective as an addition to their skincare routine, noting its ability to add a perfect amount of glow and even out skin tone. The product receives positive feedback for its moisturizing properties, with one customer mentioning it's great for locking in moisture in the morning. Customers appreciate its lightweight, non-sticky texture that glides on smoothly, and consider it good value for money. Regarding skin sensitivity, while some customers report it doesn't irritate their skin, others mention it made their skin super irritated.

reviews.author: Sahily Ojeda Feria
reviews.text:
This Beauty of Joseon Glow Serum (Propolis + Niacinamide) has quickly become a staple in my routine, and it’s seriously amazing for my sensitive, dry, and mature skin! The texture is lightly honey-like but so lightweight—it glides on, absorbs fast, and never feels heavy or sticky. It leaves my skin feeling deeply hydrated, plump, and super soft all day long.


The propolis soothes and nourishes beautifully, while the niacinamide helps brighten and strengthen my skin barrier. Together, they’re the perfect combination for that natural, healthy radiant glow—my skin looks dewy and lit-from-within without any greasiness. No irritation at all (it’s gentle and has a very subtle scent), and it layers perfectly under moisturizer or makeup.


If you’re looking for a serum that hydrates deeply, calms sensitivity, and gives mature skin that fresh, glowing look without weighing it down, this is it! Highly recommend—5 stars 😍

reviews.author: Stassy Charles
reviews.text: I’ve been using this serum for a little while and I really like how it makes my skin look more radiant and healthy. It has a lightweight texture that absorbs quickly and doesn’t leave a sticky or greasy feeling. I noticed my skin looks more even and less dull, and it layers well with the rest of my skincare. It also feels calming, especially when my skin is a bit irritated. The dropper makes it easy to apply, but you only need a small amount each time. Overall, this is a great serum if you want a natural glow and smoother-looking skin.

reviews.author: Yascia LaFrance
reviews.text: I’ve been using this serum for a few weeks now, and I can honestly say I’m seeing a difference in my skin tone. It goes on smooth, absorbs quickly, and layers well under moisturizer. The formula is gentle — no stinging or irritation — and my dark spots from old acne are starting to fade. My skin feels softer and looks more even. If you’re looking for a hydrating serum that tackles hyperpigmentation without harsh chemicals, this is it!

reviews.author: Key
reviews.text: Zero skin-irritation, moisturizing, lightweight and overall great glow serum. It's easy to apply and gives a smooth consistency every time. Not much fragrance either like some serums. I feel like I've definitely seen a difference in my skin after using it for a few weeks.

reviews.author: Deb Ash
reviews.text: This serum is awesome. Plumps up my skin and gives me a nice glow. This serum is thicker than most. That is the reason why I took off a star. That is going to take some getting used to but I will be purchasing this item again.

name: Maelove The Glow Maker
ingredients: Water (Aqua), Ascorbic Acid, Ethoxydiglycol, Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Juice, Vitis Vinifera (Grape) Seed Extract, Glycerin, Sodium Hyaluronate, Magnolia Officinalis Bark Extract, Tocopherol, Xanthan Gum, Ferulic Acid, Lecithin, Disodium EDTA, Sodium Metabisulfite, Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Callus Culture Extract, Ethylhexyglycerin, Maltodextrin, Sodium Hydroxide, Gluconolactone, Calcium Gluconate, Phenoxyethanol, Sodium Benzoate

couche3.note: vit C éclat/taches, hydrate, doux
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this vitamin C serum effective at brightening and even skin tone, while also hydrating and smoothing their complexion. The product receives positive feedback for its quality, gentle texture, and ability to fade dark spots, with one customer noting it helps keep hyperpigmentation and freckles at bay. Customers appreciate its lightweight formula that layers well under moisturizer and makeup, and its quick absorption that doesn't leave a sticky or greasy feeling.

reviews.author: Mika
reviews.text:
I have sensitive skin and have gone through so many vitamin C serums that either burned, broke me out, or just did nothing. This one is different.





No stinging, no redness, nothing. I use it every morning and my skin has genuinely never looked better. Brighter, more even, and actually hydrated throughout the day. The texture is so lightweight it almost feels like water, which I love because I layer it under moisturizer and sunscreen with zero issues.





The ingredients are super high quality too: 15% vitamin C, vitamin E, ferulic acid, hyaluronic acid, all at the right pH to actually work. Basically the same formula as serums that cost $180+, but this is $33.





I've repurchased twice and have already told like five people about it. If you have sensitive skin and have been scared to try vitamin C, just try this one!!

reviews.author: Rachel
reviews.text:
I’ve tried a lot of vitamin C serums, and this one is hands down one of the best for the price. The formula feels lightweight and absorbs quickly without leaving any sticky or greasy residue, which I love for layering under moisturizer and makeup.





Within a few weeks, I noticed my skin looked noticeably brighter and more even. It really helped fade some of my dark spots and gave me that healthy glow without irritation. I also appreciate that it includes vitamin E and ferulic acid, so it feels like I’m getting a high-end formula without the luxury price tag.





If you’re looking for a reliable vitamin C serum that actually delivers results, this is 100% worth it.

reviews.author: Courtney
reviews.text: I’m loving this serum! Iv had my fair share of trying many vitamin c serums and this one is MY FAVORITE! Iv been using this for almost two weeks and it’s easily found a spot in my routine. It’s the perfect consistency, it’s not sticky at all, and sinks into my skin immediately. I can apply my make up directly after with no issues. It layers great with other products as well. No fragrance and it’s gentle on my skin with no irritation, stinging or tingling. It’s definitely helped even out my skin tone, and hasn’t caused me any breakouts.  It leaves my skin feeling hydrated and smooth. Highly recommend!!

reviews.author: Anna Samsonidze
reviews.text:
I really like the Glow Maker serum overall. It has a lightweight, fast-absorbing texture that sinks into the skin within seconds and doesn’t leave any greasy or sticky residue. It layers perfectly under SPF and makeup without pilling or heaviness.





I also like that it feels gentle on my skin — no stinging or irritation, even with daily use, and my skin stays calm and comfortable.





The scent is very pleasant: a soft, slightly tangy floral fragrance that feels fresh but not overpowering.





After consistent use, my skin looks more hydrated, smoother, and overall brighter.

reviews.author: This tripod is strong and reliable, I use it and am very happy with it
reviews.text: I’ve tried a lot of vitamin C serums, but this one honestly stands out. The texture feels lightweight, hydrating, and gives my skin such a healthy glow without feeling sticky. After using it consistently, my skin looks brighter, smoother, and more even overall. I also love that it layers beautifully under moisturizer and makeup without pilling. The formula feels gentle on my skin, and the glow it gives is so pretty and natural. Definitely one of my favorite serums in my skincare routine ✨

name: Timeless 20% Vitamin C + E + Ferulic
ingredients: Water, Ethoxydiglycol, L-Ascorbic Acid, Propylene Glycol, Alpha Tocopherol, Polysorbate 80, Panthenol, Ferulic Acid, Sodium Hyaluronate, Benzylalcohol, Dehydroacetic Acid.

couche3.note: vit C 20% fort, tone/glow; prudence sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this Vitamin C serum effective, noting it works well under moisturizer and under makeup, with noticeable improvements in skin tone and a healthy glow. The product receives positive feedback for its value, with one customer mentioning it works well with Retin A. While some customers report even skin tone, others mention discoloration issues, and the product tends to oxidize quickly. Customers disagree on the fragrance, with some finding it weird.

reviews.author: C Herm
reviews.text: I have been using the timeless vitamin C for five years now. This should be a staple in every persons anti-aging daily skin care program. Timeless  combines 3 key ingredients;L-Ascorbic Acid, Ferulic Acid and Vitamin E for brightening, evening out the skin and collagen boosting to help diminish fine lines. Due to this being a 20% vitamin C, it may not be a great choice for sensitive skin. But if your skin can tolerate it, this serum is one of the best quality and effective vitamin C products on the market, especially for the price point. When I began using vitamin C, I made sure to try a test spot to see how my skin would react. Then I applied every other day, in the morning after washing my face, allowing my skin to adjust. One pump measures out the proper amount of application to the face. Don’t stop there. I use it on my neck, chest and backs of hands where it can help fade those pesky sun/age spots. After consistent use, my skin noticeably improved.

reviews.author: Daegon
reviews.text: This is best product if you want a stable and high performing l-ascorbic acid serum for the fairest price. Used in combination with a retinoid, azelaic acid, SPF and a good moisturizer you're getting the very best anti-aging routine without breaking the bank on treatments and procedures. This serum can be drying but is never irritating and even after just a couple of uses you get the most radiant glow and even complexion. It's glass skin in one step. Not to mention the antioxidant capacity and collagen renewing benefits. It's just a very capable serum at a fraction of the price of the Skinceuticals and it will be staying in my routine for the long haul.

reviews.author: Sacha Reagan
reviews.text:
I've been faithfully dating Korean skincare for years, but Timeless may have just made me question the relationship. 😂 I'm not here to sell anyone false hope because skincare is different for everyone, but this is one of the best vitamin C serums I've tried. I've seen visible results, including brighter, healthier-looking skin.





What really impressed me is the value. Many of the high-end vitamin C serums contain very similar ingredients but come with a much higher price tag. In my opinion, Timeless gives you some of the best bang for your buck. After trying several top brands, this one has become my go-to and is officially my new main squeeze. ✨

reviews.author: Taylor Ashley
reviews.text: Ordered this after seeing a dermatologist rave about this product! I’ve tried other (much more expensive vitamin C serums) and I can tell you this works as good if not better than those! I apply two small pumps in the morning to my face, it is quick drying and absorbs easily and then I top with a moisturizer and sunscreen. I can already tell a difference after 2 months! My skin definitely has more of a glow and it has evened out my skin texture! I have very sensitive skin and this product has not caused me any irritation. If you are looking for a strong, effective vitamin C serum at an affordable price this is it!!!

reviews.author: Alan
reviews.text: It's a nice serum, though unlike the other brands i've tried i can feel more of the residual residue of the product on my skin when I use this at night.  I try to only use a pea size but yes still feel it.  Fortunately there's no fragrance, but if you want something that feels like it went away after application this might not be it.  It might just be me how i'm applying I will fully admit as i'm inexperienced but if you're also in that same boat and that might bother you, you might want to check around a bit or find smaller sampler bottles

name: Naturium Vitamin C Complex
ingredients: Water (Aqua), Glycerin, Propanediol, Sodium Ascorbyl Phosphate, Ascorbic Acid, Glutathione, Ananas Sativus (Pineapple) Fruit Extract, Carica Papaya (Papaya) Fruit Extract, Mangifera Indica (Mango) Fruit Extract, Terminalia Ferdinandiana Fruit Extract, Pleiogynium Timoriense Fruit Extract, Podocarpus Elatus Fruit Extract, Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Juice, Sodium Hyaluronate, Carbomer, Tocopheryl Acetate, Phenoxyethanol, Caprylyl Glycol, Citric Acid, Hydroxyethylcellulose, Sodium Hydroxide, Beta-Glucan, Potassium Sorbate, Hexylene Glycol, Sorbitol, Xanthan Gum, Algin, Benzoic Acid, Sorbic Acid, 1, 2-Hexanediol, Sodium Benzoate, Disodium Phosphate, Gold, Polysorbate 60, Sodium Phosphate

couche3.note: vit C éclat, hydratant; texture huileuse (prudence grasse)
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this Vitamin C serum to be a high-quality product that works well with other skincare products and shows immediate results. The serum improves skin appearance by brightening and evening out tone, while keeping it soft and hydrated with a light oil-like texture. While some customers report it doesn't irritate sensitive skin, others mention it can cause breakouts. Customers consider it great value for money.

reviews.author: Just Me
reviews.text: Ordered several times. Although I prefer a much more expensive and stronger vitamin C, this one is ALMOST the same; and less harsh. It has a few ingredients that I’m not as keen on. However I formulate skin care and makeup for brands for a living lol; so I’m kinda picky in this department! This one is outstanding though and definitely feels much more expensive than the actual cost. It helps in so many areas as far as improving skin tone, dark spots, and more. There’s no real scent and I appreciate that very much as adding scents is very unnecessary and honestly not good for the skin. It’s not sticky, but it is a thicker consistency than some on the market. This has pros and cons. Pros, great for night time use. It really stays on the skin (especially locked in under a moisturizer.) Cons to a thicker consistency is it’s a little too thick for daytime use (I use a completely different one for day time.) All in all I’ll continue this in my rotation as I switch products from time to time so the skin doesn’t get too comfortable with that particular product. For instance, I’ll use this one every other night. However I use a much stronger one when I’m not using this one at night. That gives the skin a little room to breathe. All in all, I think it would be perfect for someone starting out with vitamin C that’s above the age of 21 (it may be too strong for someone with younger than 21 year old skin unless you’ve had extensive acne scarring and you have used a strong vitamin C and other strong products. Younger people are starting very strong (and not necessarily good for them) skin care much too young. We’re currently doing studies on this. But, what we’re seeing is by the time they are older, their skin won’t react the same to appropriate skin care and they could actually show signs of aging. Sunscreen, cleansing, moisturizer. Those are great for young skin.

reviews.author: Victoria
reviews.text: I have used this product for about a week. I am African American, and I suffer from hyperpigmentation. So, I wanted something moisturizing that would help brighten my face. I want a more even tone. I use Tatcha Dewy as my moisturizer, but I could not bring myself to pay for the Tatcha Brightening Serum. I read about the Tatcha Brightening Serum, and the reviews basically made me feel like it was 50/50 for a lot of people (works for some and not others). When I read the reviews for this product, I became more hopeful because, overall, the reviews are good.  Plus, the price for this product is a lot better. Anyway, I have used this for about a week. My boyfriend told me one morning that my face is looking brighter and more moisturized. I have also noticed as well that my face looks brigher, more even-toned, and my skin feels softer. I still have some hyperpigmentation around my mouth and chin but it truly has improved. I am excited to see what it will look like in the next couple of weeks. The product is sticky, but works for me well. I put Tatcha Dewy over it after it is on my face for a few minutes (sinks in). So, the stickiness leaves. The consistency is perfect in my opinion not too thick nor runny.

reviews.author: Alena Cooper
reviews.text: In general I dislike vitamin c products because my skin never reacts well- it’ll get red, itchy, and my face will break out. However as I’ve just entered my 40’s and recently had some Botox/filler done I know it’s vital to use a vitamin c serum. So I’ve been trying out different ones and this has been the only one that really works well for me! It’s incredibly lightweight but also very hydrating, which is great for me because I have dry skin. I have not used it long enough to know if it’ll help with aging/wrinkles but it definitely imparts and nice healthy glow within a couple uses. A little goes a long way so it is well worth the price. I’ve used a lot of products by Naturium and I feel like they deliver high quality ingredients and products at a very reasonable price; they perform as well as high end brands. I would highly recommend this serum, my only advice is that if you have very oily skin this may not be the product for you but for anyone with normal, dry, or even combination skin I think it will be an amazing product especially for sensitive skinned individuals!

reviews.author: Christian Gavin
reviews.text:
I’ve been using Naturium Vitamin C Complex Face Serum, and it’s one of the better vitamin C serums I’ve tried. The formula feels lightweight but hydrating, and it absorbs quickly without leaving a sticky or greasy finish.





After several weeks of consistent use, my skin looks noticeably brighter and more even. Dark spots have started to fade, and my overall complexion looks healthier and more radiant. I also appreciate that it layers well under moisturizer and sunscreen without pilling.





The jumbo size is a huge bonus — you get a generous amount of product for the price, which makes it feel like a great value compared to other vitamin C serums. If you’re looking for a brightening serum that’s effective but still gentle enough for daily use, this is definitely worth trying.

reviews.author: V
reviews.text: This Vitamin C Complex Face Serum has been such a great addition to my skincare routine. It feels lightweight, absorbs quickly, and leaves my skin looking brighter and more refreshed. After using it consistently, my skin feels smoother and more hydrated, and I’ve noticed a healthy glow. It layers well under moisturizer and makeup without feeling sticky. Definitely worth trying if you want brighter, healthier-looking skin!”

couche3.note: azélaïque rosacée/taches/rougeurs, doux, top sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers report positive results with this skin moisturizer, noting it helps with perioral dermatitis, rosacea, and hyperpigmentation, while reducing redness on cheeks and improving skin tone. The product receives positive feedback for its texture, being velvety smooth and leaving the face soft, and customers find it good value for money. Regarding skin sensitivity, while some find it very tolerable on sensitive skin, others report it burning almost instantly after application. The quantity aspect receives mixed reviews, with several customers noting the product comes less than half full.

reviews.author: Adrienne
reviews.text:
I decided to try The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10% after constantly seeing people talk about it on TikTok, especially people with rosacea and sensitive skin like mine. I was honestly hesitant at first because the texture is definitely thicker than a lot of products I normally use, and thicker creams/products tend to irritate my skin or make me nervous about flare ups.





To be safe, I started off using a very small amount, and I’m really glad I gave it a chance. I’ve only been using it consistently for about a week so far, but I can already see and feel a difference in my skin. Nothing unrealistic or overnight, but enough that my skin feels calmer, healthier, and more comfortable overall. The redness in my face looks less angry, and my skin texture feels smoother too.





Even though the formula is thicker, it doesn’t feel heavy once applied. A little goes a long way, and using too much definitely isn’t necessary. I think easing into it slowly helped my skin adjust really well without irritation.





As someone with rosacea, I’m extremely cautious about trying trending skincare products because my skin reacts to things so easily. A lot of viral products end up being too harsh for me, but this one genuinely deserves the hype it’s getting. It feels effective without destroying my skin barrier, which is hard to find.





If you have sensitive skin or rosacea and have been nervous about trying azelaic acid, I’d definitely recommend introducing this slowly and giving it a shot. So far, it’s been a really positive addition to my routine, and I’m excited to keep using it long term.

reviews.author: Ikram
reviews.text:
I am writing this review because The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10% has absolutely done wonders for my skin! I had been struggling with acne and the frustrating redness and dark marks that inevitably get left behind, but this product has been a complete lifesaver.


It is incredibly effective at calming down active inflammation and clearing up my complexion without being overly harsh or irritating. It fits perfectly into my daily routine alongside my gentle cleanser and sunscreen, and I have noticed a dramatic difference in my skin's overall texture, tone, and clarity since I started using it.


If you are dealing with stubborn breakouts or post-acne marks and need something that actually delivers results, I cannot recommend this highly enough. It is affordable, gentle, and it truly works miracles!

reviews.author: estefany ramirez
reviews.text: This product is worth every penny. I bought it to help with acne marks, dark spots, and uneven skin tone, and the results have been amazing. I have very sensitive skin, so I am always careful when trying new skincare products, but this one has caused absolutely no irritation. No redness, no burning, and no discomfort at all. My skin looks brighter, smoother, and more even-toned. I’ve noticed improvements that I hadn’t seen with many other products. This has become a must-have in my skincare routine, and I will definitely keep repurchasing it. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Cameron Austin
reviews.text:
Within days, this product has worked wonders! I've historically had pretty bad combination skin -- dry and flaky but also prone to acne. I tend to have a good amount of hyperpigmentation and redness on top of that. Within days, this product has evened my skin tone (and maybe also improved my acne?)





My only complaint is that I wish the bottle came in larger sizes. But I think it's a good value for the money, regardless.

reviews.author: Oscar Klaas
reviews.text: Using for my perimenopausal skin. Tones down red and helps my pores.  Does cause dryness with twice daily usage.

couche3.note: azélaïque acné/taches/rougeurs, doux
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective at reducing pimples and perioral dermatitis, while also improving skin tone by evening it out and fading dark spots. Moreover, the product receives positive feedback for its quality and texture, with customers noting it feels smooth on the skin. However, customers disagree on the value for money, with some finding it worth the price while others consider it overpriced. Additionally, experiences with skin irritation are mixed, with some finding it doesn't irritate sensitive skin while others report burning sensations. The size of the product is also a concern, with many noting it's quite small for the price.

reviews.author: Jessica
reviews.text:
I question why I didn’t get this product sooner in my life. Seriously.





This product has made such a positive change in my skin - I feel like crying.





While I have never been officially diagnosed, I’ve always felt that I have a type of rosacea. I don’t get pustules, zits or anything like that (unless I consume a lot of certain sugary drinks, and food) but for as long as I can remember, my face has always dealt with redness. Yes, certain things such as consuming sugar triggers it and makes it worse.





I’ve been using skincare since my mid 20s. I am currently in my early 30s. I feel for the most part I was using products that were benefiting my skin, but as far as the redness went, nothing I used seemed to treat it.





Around a year ago, I heard about how azelaic acid can be beneficial to people who have rosacea - treating redness and the pustules that go with it. So while I was aware of it, I just kept putting off purchasing it. Life stuff always coming up.





After talking with ChatGPT about how sick I was about always walking around with a red/pink face, it recommended azelaic acid. Specifically Paula’s Choice.





Now we all know that with a lot of skin care products it can take time to see differences. No lie… I began seeing results from this within just under two weeks or so.


The redness in my face has decreased A LOT. I am now at a point in my life where I feel more comfortable going out without having to wear foundation or using something like Cicapair by Dr. Jart+ (which by the way is great at covering the redness).





I am extremely pale, so dealing with a face that was red almost constantly - I felt that the difference between my face and body were so dramatically different, it made me self-conscious.





But now? I am much more confident. The redness isn’t completely gone, but when I say it has been dramatically reduced, I mean it! My skin also has this type of glow that I’ve never experienced with other products - not even my tretinoin.





I use this product every other day. I have several products I use for my skin and I cycle through them every other day.


When I do apply this, like with every other skin care product, I always mist my skin lightly with water. Yes, doing this increases the potency of products but for my skin, I can tolerate it just fine. I also feel that by dampening your skin, the product spread, spreads a lot more evenly and easily.





While this is priced at $39 and being only 1 ounce, I know it sounds expensive. But I will say that a little goes a LONG way. You don’t need to be using globs of this stuff. Also, I feel you can get away with using this every other day, like myself, so it can last longer.





Everyone’s skin is different. Like I said, I’ve never been officially diagnosed, but based on what I’ve experienced and what I’ve read, I truly do feel I have some form of rosacea. I also have certain triggers that make it worse; such as consuming certain types of sugary drinks.





So I feel that if you identify your specific triggers, use this product… you should see a definite difference.





Wish I had gotten this product sooner! I absolutely love it.

reviews.author: karen
reviews.text: I usually don't write reviews but I felt strongly about letting others know about this one... had bad rosacea after finishing nursing the baby. It is all hormone related. Happened to me twice after breastfeeding. First time I went to the dermatologist and the stuff she prescribed was making me worse! I was so upset. I was trying to avoid any prescriptions and would just put makeup to cover up. This time I tried honey and other cleansers and was willing to forgo the Rx and pray it would go away. After further research, I came across this product and thought to give it a try. The amount of azelaic acid must be the perfect amount for doing the job to remove the bumps but no so strong as what a dermatologist would prescribe that will cause sensitive skin to get worse. I am satisfied with this purchase. I was more concerned with lessening the bumps then the redness and it is doing just that. Goes on smoothly and is light weight. Doesn't feel like a medicine cream.

reviews.author: Sandy
reviews.text: I’ve suffered with acne scars for decades. Anyone living with them knows how it affects your entire life. I’ve gone to an esthetician for over a decade and my skin health improved but I still had embarrassing hyperpigmentation and scars. So, I went into ChatGPT and asked about softening acne scars. I got tons of advice and product recommendations for my brownish and reddish acne scar discoloration. I purchased the Paula’s Choice BOOST not expecting anything that great BUT I can’t believe the brightness in my skin and a total transformation with the hyperpigmentation. I began to notice it within days. The scars are must less noticeable and some days when I look in the mirror I can believe it’s me. I started out using it every other night to avoid possible irritation but I use it nightly now. It absorbs nicely and isn’t greasy. It’s been life changing for me.

reviews.author: Lauren P
reviews.text:
It is with a heavy heart that I regrettably inform you that this is absolutely worth the price. Yes, it is expensive for the little tube you get, but it has lasted me a couple of months using it both day and night.





This might be one of the only products I've ever used that doesn't give me sensory issues. I put it on and don't get a moment of stickiness or a wet feeling like I do with serum options. It doesn't irritate me. I even LIKE putting it on.





I've used other azelaic acid serums, TXA, and I have a permanent retinoid in my routine. Adding this has been very effective at reducing a lot of the PIE (red hyperpigmentation) I've had leftover from acne since I was a teenager. It's not hydrating, it's not going to do everything— it does one thing, and it does that one thing very well.

reviews.author: Fesan
reviews.text:
Please pay attention to the size that you are ordering. When I purchased this product I thought it would be a decent size based on the photo but I ended up getting a sample sized product. It is so small that it won’t even last a week and I am disappointed because why is a brand selling a 5ml tube for THIRTEEN DOLLARS?? That’s almost the same price as my  cleanser which is 400ml worth of product.





Update: despite the size of the product, it has been working really well on my skin and I’ve noticed a difference with my breakouts & dark marks (they’ve cleared up quite a bit). I use this along with my Retin-A (tretinoin), not at the same time obviously & my skin is clearing up.

couche3.note: Serum acide hyaluronique tres bien tolere, hydratant, absorption rapide, excellent rapport qualite-prix ; convient a tous types de peau.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this hyaluronic acid serum to be better quality than name-brand products, with excellent moisturizing properties that hydrate and make skin look smooth. The serum works well under moisturizer and makeup, and customers appreciate its quick absorption without leaving a sticky feeling. They report improved skin appearance, with one noting visible results after a few weeks, and find it good value for money.

reviews.author: Sydney
reviews.text:
I absolutely love The Ordinary brand and own quite a few of their skincare products, so I was excited to add this hyaluronic acid serum to my routine. I use it after cleansing my face and before applying moisturizer, and it layers really nicely with the rest of my skincare. One of the things I appreciate most about this product is the packaging. The glass bottle feels high-quality, and the dropper makes application easy and convenient.





The serum itself is very lightweight, and a little bit goes a long way, so the bottle should last quite a while. It absorbs nicely into the skin without feeling greasy or sticky afterward. I also really appreciate the clean ingredients and the fact that there are no added perfumes or strong scents, which is important for my sensitive skin and overall preference for non-irritating skincare products.





As with most skincare products, I know it takes consistency and several months of use before you can truly judge long-term results and improvements in your skin. Because of that, I cannot fully speak yet on how effective it is overall, but so far I have been enjoying using it and have had no irritation or issues. For the affordable price point and quality ingredients, I think this is a great skincare product, and I plan to continue using it and update my review later on with long-term results.

reviews.author: Sarah Alcantara
reviews.text:
I’ve tried a lot of hyaluronic acid serums over the years, and honestly, this one surprised me. The Ordinary Hyaluronic Acid 2% + B5 leaves my skin feeling soft, hydrated, and plump without feeling greasy or heavy. As someone in my 50s, I really appreciate products that hydrate without making fine lines look worse, and this one layers beautifully under moisturizer and makeup.





What makes it even better is the price. It’s incredibly affordable compared to other high-end hydrating serums that cost 3–4 times as much. You truly get amazing quality for the money.





My skin feels smoother, looks healthier, and stays hydrated throughout the day. I will definitely keep repurchasing this product and highly recommend it for anyone wanting affordable, effective skincare.

reviews.author: Margie L.
reviews.text:
I have ordered this hyaluronic acid serum over and over again because it consistently delivers great results. It is incredibly hydrating and leaves my skin feeling soft, smooth, and refreshed after every use.





One of the things I love most is how it helps my skin look and feel plump and well-moisturized without feeling heavy or greasy. It absorbs quickly and layers well with the rest of my skincare routine.





After regular use, my skin feels healthier, smoother, and more hydrated throughout the day. This has become a staple in my skincare routine, and I will definitely continue to purchase it.





Highly recommend for anyone looking for an effective, affordable hydrating serum! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

reviews.author: MMorgan
reviews.text:
My face absolutely craves this serum—it’s just that good! I’ve been using it regularly, and it leaves my skin feeling hydrated, plump, and refreshed.





The formula is lightweight, absorbs well, and layers easily with the rest of my skincare routine. A little goes a long way, and my skin always feels noticeably better after using it.





If you’re looking for a simple, effective hydrating serum, this one is definitely worth trying. Your skin will thank you for it!

reviews.author: Jenna Harbeintner
reviews.text:
I’m giving The Ordinary Hyaluronic Acid 2% + B5 5 stars because it’s such a good, simple hydration serum.





It makes my skin feel noticeably more hydrated and plump, especially when I use it on slightly damp skin. I also like that it layers easily with my other skincare and doesn’t feel sticky or heavy.





Overall, it’s an affordable, effective product that I can use every day and always feel good about.

couche3.note: HA hydratant/repulpant, doux, tous types
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective at hydrating and plumping skin, with one noting results within a couple of days. The product receives positive feedback for its silky texture, glowing appearance, and ability to even skin tone out, while also being gentle enough for sensitive skin. The fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some finding it pleasant while others describe it as very strong. Skin compatibility experiences are mixed, with some customers reporting no irritation while others experience redness and rashes.

reviews.author: Eric
reviews.text: One of the best HA serums on the market and nice splurge when you want something French and fancy sounding. It hydrates and plumps fine lines as advertised without being heavy nor greasy. It does have a fragrance, but it is pleasant and mild. I was concerned it would irritate my very sensitive skin and rosacea, but pleasantly surprised after a month of daily use, zero problems. I use it in combination with the Double Repair moisturizer. My dermatologist has been pretty impressed with the health of my skin lately. In complete fairness, there are other HA serums on the market that are as effective, use the same ingredients and concentrations, at half to a quarter of the price (The Ordinary, for example.) YMMV

reviews.author: sandra M Cardenas
reviews.text:
I bought this serum because I needed extra hydration, especially with my busy schedule and long work shifts.





The texture is lightweight but very hydrating. It absorbs quickly and leaves my skin feeling soft and smooth without being greasy. I use it before my moisturizer, and it layers well with other products.





I’ve noticed my skin looks more hydrated and feels more comfortable, especially on days when it feels dry or tired.





Pros: very hydrating, lightweight, smooth texture, good for daily use


Cons: a bit pricey for the size





Overall, this is a great serum if you’re looking to improve hydration and keep your skin looking healthy.

reviews.author: thomas
reviews.text: In my skin care routine, I start with cleansing, then using a toner, followed by a serum, and then a moisturizer followed by sunscreen. I am always searching for the next best thing to replace ones that I am already using. With this serum I have found my perfect one so far. This gives a lovely base line for my hydration to prep for the next moisturizer to wear. Paired with a good moisturizer this serum does wonders. I noticed that my skin stays hydrated longer than if I just used moisturizer alone. And I seen a big difference in my skins complexion. I used this everyday for two months and used it twice a day and the results are hard to argue with.

reviews.author: Brenda
reviews.text:
After several weeks using La Roche-Posay Hyalu B5 Serum, I have noticed a big difference in the hydration and appearance of my skin. Its formula with pure hyaluronic acid, vitamin B5 and madecassoside leaves the skin visibly softer, firmer and more filling from the first applications.


I love that it is light, oil-free and suitable for sensitive skin, as it is rapidly absorbed without leaving a sticky feeling or causing irritation. My skin looks cooler, brighter and the fine lines look less marked.


In addition, a small amount yields a lot, so the bottle lasts a long time. The quality of La Roche-Posay is really noticeable in the results.

reviews.author: Jodi
reviews.text: First week using this serum and wow, my skin feels amazing.  I have been dealing with very dry skin lately that has been patchy and this serum is working its magic plumping and hydrating my skin.  Definitely will be reordering this and the smell is wonderful too!

couche3.note: HA léger, top grasse, hydrate, sous maquillage
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this hyaluronic acid serum effective, noting it delivers results within days and works well under makeup. Moreover, the product receives positive feedback for its moisturizing properties, particularly for oily skin, and its silky smooth texture that absorbs quickly without feeling sticky. Additionally, customers appreciate its compatibility with sensitive skin and its ability to make skin look radiant and youthful. However, opinions on value for money are mixed, with some finding it worth the price while others consider it overpriced.

reviews.author: Magda
reviews.text: This serum feels incredibly lightweight and absorbs quickly into the skin without leaving any greasy residue. It provides excellent hydration and leaves my skin feeling soft, smooth, and refreshed throughout the day. The formula is gentle, making it suitable for sensitive skin, and it layers well under moisturizer and makeup. With regular use, my skin looks healthier and more hydrated. A great addition to any skincare routine and definitely worth trying! ✨💧😊

reviews.author: P. L. Key
reviews.text: They ain't lying.  I'm in my early 70s and have never babied my skin beyond standard moisturizers.  That neglect was beginning to show on my face, so I added this product to my routine.  Within a week there was a noticeable difference!  The dryness was gone, the lines and crinkly texture was softened.  I use it twice a day.  It's lightweight and absorbs fast.  I am very happy with my results.

reviews.author: Britt
reviews.text:
This serum was fine, but nothing special for me. It felt nice going on and absorbed quickly without feeling sticky, but I didn’t really notice any big difference in my skin over time.





It’s a decent basic hydrating step, just didn’t wow me or feel like a must-have.

reviews.author: Donna F
reviews.text: This is my favorite go to every morning it makes my face feel so soft and really helps my skin tone and texture. I am on my 3rd bottle and will continue to use this product regularly. Great for mature skin.

reviews.author: Hatim
reviews.text: Best serum ever. Makes your skin hydrated, smooth and plump and glowy and amazing. Gentle, eczema skin friendly.

couche3.note: HA non gras, hydrate, éclat
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective at keeping skin moist and shiny, with a smooth, non-greasy texture that makes it feel good. The product receives positive feedback for its appearance, with one customer noting it makes their face look brighter and smoother. The pump functionality and fragrance receive mixed reviews, with some saying it works beautifully while others report it doesn't work at all, and while some find the scent fine, others note it smells different. Customers disagree on whether the product is worth the price.

reviews.author: Jamie Stalfort
reviews.text: Okay this stuff may be expensive, but you definitely get your money’s worth. I’m on an acne regimen from the derm that totally dries my skin out & I use this in conjunction with cerave/ceraphil moisturizers. I use it kind of as a serum before my day moisturizer. The texture is almost like a gel, it dries fast leaving your skin with a glowy, dewy finish. The hydration is great, I can’t use it alone bc of my acne prescriptions but if I use the cetaphil/cerave without this, it doesn’t hydrate as well. I would recommend using this as a serum before moisturizing if you have dry skin, and a stand-alone (among with an spf) if you have oily skin. The craziest part - I ran out of this last week and the pump did not give me any sign that the product was running low until the very last pump. I pulled out the inside to see what was left and I’m not joking - there was absolutely no product left. It looked like the bottle was brand new inside and had never even held moisturizer before. It may be expensive, but it lasts until the very last drop!!!

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
I LOVE this serum/moisturizer. 2 pumps for the face and a little down my neck. It sinks right into my skin and leaves it Dewey, not wet or oily until it completely absorbs in just a few minutes, ready for step 2 which is a treatment, acid, retin a depending on the night, then followed by DE bora barrier (pink top) if I’m using retin a because I need the thick moisture over it. Because I torment my skin, it is very sensitive so I am very careful about what goes on it- this is my go to.


I used this after my fraxel face laser to keep my skin moisturized and to make the much more expensive Alastin skin nectar stretch out more. I also used the DE bora barrier because it is a very good thick moisturizer with no irritating actives (exactly was is needed after a laser).

reviews.author: Lucia
reviews.text: I am a 66 year old female with extremely dry skin.  This serum does not feel heavy at all.  My skin is soft and does not feel greasy.  Goes on light.  Lasts forever.....My container is about 2 months old and i use it twice a day.  Great value and great product.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text: I'm a 55-year-old man and this stuff is amazing. Don't worry about the bad reviews, this product is absolutely gentle and dries evenly, no greasy shiny texture left over...and your face feels hydrated. Don't hesitate.

reviews.author: Cathy Block
reviews.text: It feels good going on but it’s not as moisturizing as I need!

name: Purito Centella Unscented Serum
ingredients: Centella Asiatica, Aqua (Water), Glycerin, Butylene Glycol, Pentylene Glycol, 1,2-Hexanediol, Sodium Hyaluronate, Panthenol, Sodium PCA, Allantoin, Ahnfeltia Concinna Extract, Sodium Stearoyl Glutamate, Xanthan Gum, Ethylhexylglycerin, Phenoxyethanol

couche3.note: centella apaisant, top sensible/acné, répare barrière
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this serum effective for various skin types, including sensitive, oily, and acne-prone skin, and appreciate its ability to rebuild the skin barrier and smooth out scars. Moreover, the serum keeps skin moisturized, feels gentle without being sticky, and is fragrance-free. Additionally, customers report that it calms redness, brightens skin, and makes it look glowy.

reviews.author: Olivia
reviews.text: This product is perfect for reactive/sensitive skin. I have pale, easily irritated and red skin. Dryness wise, my skin is normal. This product is hydrating, but also tackles imperfections gently. I started using another product at the same time as this (Terrafique: Bachuchiol Serum) and they work great together, so I can’t say with 100% certainty that this product alone gives these effects, but my skin has cleared, smoothed, and is significantly less red, despite relocating to Florida for the summer. I had a purge phase, but I believe that was from the other product. But my skin has been the clearest and smoothest it’s ever been for the past 3 weeks or so (started use May 15th - using 2x day). So it takes some patience, but very much worth it. Will buy this product again when I run out :)

reviews.author: Melody
reviews.text: I've been using this for about a month now and it's surprisingly good at smoothing out acne scars. I've been looking for something for hyperpigmentation. This product really helps calm the skin. I rarely break out now regardless of what I eat and my skin tone is much more even. It makes my skin smooth and soft and is a great addition to my skincare line. I like that it has no smell and causes no irritation when combined with other products.

reviews.author: Kaylee Allen
reviews.text: This is a nice serum. It is soothing and has good ingredients. It has not been a game changer- I don’t notice a huge difference in my skin. It is definitely nice though. It absorbs fast and calms irritation and redness. I will repurchase. I love that it has no fragrance and is really just simple and effective.

reviews.author: Tianna
reviews.text: Lost count of how many bottles I've gone through over the years. Really excellent fine product. Lightweight Soothing hydrating calming. I do need a cream over this: gel cream if it's summer and a richer cream if it's winter

reviews.author: Jo
reviews.text: I bought this to prevent stretch marks during pregnancy as I had seen some promising research on centella. My son is now 3 and I can say I never did end up with stretch marks, so, worth a shot!

couche3.note: baume répare barrière, apaise, top sensible/sèche, léger
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective for skin barrier repair and appreciate its light texture that doesn't feel heavy or greasy. The product soothes and calms skin, with one customer noting it helps with eczema, and they report no breakouts or irritations. They like its compatibility with sensitive skin and its ability to relieve itching. The consistency receives mixed feedback, with several customers describing it as pretty thick.

reviews.author: VanillaPepper
reviews.text:
This is one of those products that I completely understand the hype around because it truly does work very well for dry, irritated, over-processed, or compromised skin areas. The texture is rich, soothing, and very protective without having a strong fragrance, which I appreciate because heavily scented skincare products usually irritate my skin immediately.





I have found this especially helpful for dry patches, irritation from weather, over-exfoliation, and areas where the skin barrier simply feels stressed or uncomfortable. A very small amount goes a long way and the tube itself lasted much longer than I expected.





That being said, for me personally this product is simply too heavy to use regularly on my face because I am somewhat breakout-prone with richer creams. While it feels incredibly soothing, I personally prefer using it more selectively on very dry or irritated areas rather than as an all-over facial moisturizer.





The packaging arrived perfectly sealed with no leaking or damage during shipping and the product looked exactly as expected upon arrival. For the amount you receive and how little is needed per use, I also think the value is very good compared to many other barrier repair creams.





Overall, I absolutely understand why so many people love this product and I do think it is excellent for repairing and protecting irritated skin — I just personally have to be careful about where and how heavily I apply it.





#FoundItOnAmazon #AmazonFinds #Skincare #SkinBarrier #LaRochePosay #SensitiveSkin #DrySkin #BeautyFavorites #EverydaySkincare #FrenchPharmacy #SelfCare #LuxeLife

reviews.author: Mariela Murillo Castro
reviews.text:
As a facial specialist, I recommend La Roche-Posay Cicaplast Balm B5 more than almost any other product in my arsenal. It’s one of those rare formulas that consistently delivers across skin types, conditions, and ages — and that’s not something I say lightly.





The combination of Panthenol (Vitamin B5), Madecassoside, and thermal spring water creates a truly reparative barrier treatment. I use it professionally on clients post-treatment — after chemical peels, extractions, and microdermabrasion — because it accelerates healing, reduces redness, and restores comfort almost immediately.





In my own daily routine, I apply it as a final step on dry or reactive areas and the results speak for themselves: skin feels protected, plump, and significantly calmer by morning.





What sets this apart from other healing balms is that it’s non-comedogenic and fragrance-free, making it safe even for the most sensitive or compromised skin. It doesn’t feel greasy or occlusive — just deeply nourishing.





Dermatologist-recommended, esthetician-approved. If there’s one product I would tell every client to keep on hand at all times, it’s this one.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
I’ve seen La Roche-Posay Cicaplast Balm B5 recommended countless times online, so I finally decided to try it for irritated skin and a damaged skin barrier. It absolutely lives up to the hype for me. The texture is very rich and moisturizing, but it feels extremely soothing on dry, overtreated, or sensitive areas. I’ve used it on my face, hands, and other irritated patches of skin, and it really helps calm everything down.





The quality feels excellent, which is what I expect from La Roche-Posay. A little goes a long way, so I think it’s a very good value for the price. I also like that it’s versatile enough for the whole family. I’ve even shared it with my daughter, and we both love having it around for dry or irritated skin. Definitely one of those products I can see myself repurchasing regularly.

reviews.author: Adrienne
reviews.text:
Purchased since it was recommended by a dermatologist, and now I know why!! As someone who has to wash my hands and use hand sanitizer all the time at work (healthcare), my hands have become increasingly more dry and irritated. Especially during the winter months. This is the BEST thing I've used for moisturizing my hands. It makes an INCREDIBLE difference in not only how my skin looks, but how much better it makes my hands feel. There is no floral/strong fragrance, but it does have a subtle (medicinal?) scent to it- very tolerable as someone who cannot handle fragrances/scents well.


It's thick, creamy, and keeps my hands feeling so much better for so much longer than other moisturizers!

reviews.author: Yraima Batista Martins
reviews.text: I am very happy with this product. I have been using it on irritated and dry skin, and I noticed a big improvement in a short time. It has a smooth texture, absorbs well, and does not feel greasy. It helps calm the skin quickly and restores the skin barrier. I have used it both on my face and on small sensitive areas of the body, and it works very well in both cases. It is definitely a skincare essential that I will always keep in my routine. I highly recommend it.

couche3.note: cica apaisant, rosacée/sensible; peu d'avis
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective at moisturizing and repairing skin, with one mentioning it helps with rosacea. The product feels gentle on sensitive skin, and customers consider it a great product.

reviews.author: Dacia Edmonson
reviews.text: It wasn’t the holy grail I had heard about, but it is really good. My skin has a softness it hasn’t had in a while. It doesn’t do anything for the redness though.

reviews.author: Wore this for a wedding and it was perfect.
reviews.text: I have combination, dry skin with heavy rosacea.  This product works great and leaves my skin feeling soft and moisturized without being "slick".

reviews.author: Lucetia Waites
reviews.text: This is magic. I have been struggling with slight rosacea and this got rid of the bumps and red in just 3 days

reviews.author: Planchita
reviews.text: Super nice I will definitely but it again. Repairs and enhances skin.

reviews.author: Miranda Segraves
reviews.text: Absolutely fantastic for sensitive skin

couche3.note: peptides anti-âge, hydrate; irritation mitigée
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective for mature skin, with quick results and improved appearance. They appreciate its hydrating properties, smooth texture, and non-greasy formula, while also considering it affordable. Regarding skin irritation, opinions are mixed - some customers report it doesn't irritate their skin, while others experience burning sensations.

reviews.author: Dealie Green
reviews.text: I love this product line. I have ridiculous sensitive skin and it took a long time for me to find a product that didn't irritate my skin. Their products feel so nice and hydrating. None of them are oily. Some are a little tacky when layering but I think that means you have to wait a little longer between layers. I feel the quality is great and I have seen results in a 60 day time span. I have deep forehead wrinkles that I am self conscious about and these products have done such a great job plumping my skin back out. All of the ingredients are safe for people like me with sensitivities. I was unsure how and when to layer so chatgpt whipped up a routine for me that I follow religiously. will be sticking with this line!

reviews.author: Calli Tee
reviews.text:
I’ve been using The Ordinary Hyaluronic Acid 2% + B5 (with Ceramides) for a few weeks now, and it has quickly become a staple in my routine. The texture is lightweight and smooth, and it absorbs nicely without feeling heavy. My skin feels instantly more hydrated and looks noticeably plumper after application.





I love that this formula includes ceramides along with hyaluronic acid — it doesn’t just hydrate, it helps support my skin barrier too. When I apply it on slightly damp skin and follow with moisturizer, my face feels soft, smooth, and balanced all day.





It layers well under makeup and doesn’t pill, which is a huge plus. For the price point, the quality is impressive

reviews.author: YS Daily Finds
reviews.text: This has become one of my favorite serums from The Ordinary. It's lightweight, hydrating, and works well with the rest of my skincare products. After consistent use, my skin feels softer, smoother, and more refreshed. I love that it provides hydration while supporting a healthier-looking complexion. Definitely a product I'll continue repurchasing. ✨💕

reviews.author: Rorie
reviews.text: Excellent product line and maintains supple skin health. I see a difference in the way my skin looks and feels. Great for young and older skin, of all types. Very happy with the Ordinary skincare line.

reviews.author: Kim Baker
reviews.text: Have not finished first bottle and already feel a difference in my face .  Seems firmer and not dry or tight feeling .  Will order again

couche3.note: peptides rides, hydrate, repulpe
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective at reducing forehead wrinkles and making skin noticeably brighter and plumper. They appreciate its hydrating properties, with one customer noting it delivers results without overwhelming the skin. The serum receives positive feedback for its texture, with customers describing it as soft and luxurious, and they consider it worth the cost. The fragrance receives mixed reactions from customers.

reviews.author: Tracy
reviews.text:
The Naturium Multi-Peptide Advanced Serum is a fantastic addition to my skincare routine! If you’re looking to tackle fine lines and wrinkles with a powerful, effective serum, this one is a winner.





The formula is packed with a blend of advanced peptides that work wonders for firming and smoothing the skin. It feels luxurious and hydrating, absorbing quickly without any greasy residue. I’ve noticed my skin looking noticeably plumper and more youthful since I started using it.





The serum’s texture is light yet effective, making it easy to layer under other products. The pump dispenser is user-friendly and dispenses the perfect amount each time.





Since incorporating this serum into my regimen, I’ve seen a reduction in fine lines and an improvement in overall skin texture. It’s become a staple in my skincare arsenal, and I’m thrilled with the results!





If you’re searching for a high-quality serum to address aging concerns and enhance skin’s firmness and smoothness, the Naturium Multi-Peptide Advanced Serum is definitely worth trying. It delivers visible improvements and feels amazing on the skin!

reviews.author: Jennifer Barry
reviews.text:
I’ve been using this serum consistently for a few weeks now and I’m honestly so happy with how my skin is looking and feeling.





The texture is really nice—light but creamy—and it sinks in quickly without feeling sticky or heavy. I apply it after cleansing, and it just makes my skin feel instantly more hydrated and soft.





Over time, I’ve noticed that my skin looks smoother and more even, especially around my forehead and smile lines. I wouldn’t say it erased anything overnight (nothing really does), but my skin definitely looks firmer and more refreshed, like I’ve actually been sleeping enough!





What I also appreciate is how gentle it is. I have pretty sensitive skin and this hasn’t caused any irritation or breakouts. It layers really well with the rest of my routine and just feels like a solid, dependable step that’s quietly doing its job in the background.





I’ll definitely be keeping this one in my rotation—it’s one of those products you don’t realize how much you love until you skip a day and miss how good your skin felt.

reviews.author: Alena Cooper
reviews.text: Feels absolutely amazing! This is such a hydrating yet lightweight serum- no greasiness and absorbs really well! It layers beautifully under different moisturizers (no pilling or stickiness) and for super oily skin it might even work as a lightweight lotion but for my dry skin it is a great booster. I’ve been looking for something that would replace First Aide Beauty’s Bounce Serum since it was discontinued and this is very close to that formula. I’ve only been using it a few days so I can’t say yet how effective it is against wrinkles, but I swear my skin already feels firmer! As a bonus I notice no scent and it does not irritate or bother my skin at all!

reviews.author: serenity
reviews.text: I love this sooooo much I’ve gone thru the little bottles and I use it twice a day everyday and the little 1oz bottle lasted me 5 months. When I seen the 2oz bigger bottle I had to get it. My skin is soft, glowy, and hydrated. Very easy to use(I use after wash and toner), DEFINITELY a great value. My skin used to be very dry and tight all the time but this changed so much. Very lightweight formula and feels great on the skin. I included some before use and after use photos. The first two are before use, then 3 months later, then today (6 month after use). Great at keeping my skin healthy, soft, and reducing redness.

reviews.author: Sophia
reviews.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️





Nice Texture, Still Waiting on Results





I really like the feel of the Naturium Multi-Peptide Advanced Serum—it’s smooth and more like a lotion, not sticky at all, which I love. As for the firming and smoothing effects, I haven’t noticed much of a difference yet, but I’ll keep using it to see if the results improve over time.





Overall, it’s pleasant to apply, and the experience so far has been positive. Hoping to see more noticeable changes with continued use!

couche3.note: arbutine taches/éclat, hydrate, doux
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective at fading dark spots and making skin brighter, while also appreciating its hydrating properties and smooth texture. Moreover, the product receives positive feedback for its quality and value for money. However, experiences with skin irritation are mixed, with some customers reporting no issues while others experience rashes. Additionally, the scent receives mixed reactions, with several customers noting it doesn't have a sweet smell.

reviews.author: C. Rock
reviews.text: My skin had a lot of hyperpigmentation after I kept reacting to no-see-ums (biting insects in Florida where I live) and from acne. I have been using this product with my skincare routine for a month now and the results are great. I know that at some point I need to allow my skin a month break after many months so I'll address other serums as I go about that. In the mean time my skin is glowy and nice. The hyperpigmentation did disappear fairly well and I do wear sunscreen. Sunscreen is a must with this product. It keeps my skin moisturized and soft. The application is very easy and I apply it twice a day. I don't have as much puffiness anymore in my skin. It doesn't have a strong smell.

reviews.author: Jose Luis martinez
reviews.text: I’m very happy with this product. The Alpha Arbutin by  The Ordinary￼ arrived in perfect condition and exactly as described. I’ve been using it consistently, and it’s working 100% for my skin. I’ve noticed my dark spots starting to fade, and my skin looks brighter and healthier. The texture is lightweight, easy to apply, and doesn’t irritate my skin. Definitely a great product and I would recommend it to others.

reviews.author: Alex
reviews.text: I’ve been using this serum for a while now and already repurchased it. It helped make my skin tone look more even and some of my small dark spots started fading gradually. It’s lightweight, gentle on my skin, and layers well with the rest of my skincare. I also noticed my skin looks smoother and healthier overall. Definitely one of the few products I actually kept using consistently.

reviews.author: Edina Kavazovic
reviews.text: This serum surpassed my expectations by a lot! The price is great for the quality of the product. It tightens skin, fades dark sports (in my case Melasma) and I always follow it up with a light retinol cream. My skin care routine is showing improvement on my face so well with this product and it works quickly, about a week in I could tell the difference. Great product!

reviews.author: Rebeka
reviews.text: Works great! Absorbs quickly and makes a noticeable difference in my skins redness and texture. It does have a fishy smell too it, so be prepared. I think this is a bargain for the quality. It does not cause irritation on my dry, sensitive, acne prone skin. I definitely recommend adding to your routine.

name: Good Molecules Discoloration Correcting Serum
ingredients: Water,Propanediol,Glycerin,Niacinamide,Cetyl Tranexamate Mesylate,Cetearyl Alcohol,Ceratonia Siliqua (Carob) Gum,Caprylyl Glycol,Tamarindus Indica Seed Gum,Ethylhexylglycerin

couche3.note: tranexamique taches/teint, hydrate, doux
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective at fading dark spots and improving skin tone, with one noting visible results in less than a week. Moreover, the product receives positive feedback for its hydrating properties, smooth texture, and good value for money. However, customers disagree on its effectiveness, with some reporting no results at all. Additionally, opinions on skin sensitivity are mixed, with some finding it gentle while others report severe irritation.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
At some point in my 30s, my skin decided to start keeping souvenirs.





Old breakouts? Souvenir.


Random sun exposure from “just a quick walk”? Souvenir.


That one time I picked at a zit like I knew better but did it anyway? Permanent postcard.





So while my actual acne calmed down with age, the little shadowy marks stuck around like uninvited guests.





Enter this Good Molecules Discoloration Correcting Serum. I grabbed it because I’ve had weirdly good luck with this brand and also because it was $12, which is very “let’s try it” energy and not “financial commitment.”





What sold me immediately is something small but important. They actually tell you the percentages.





Not “infused with tranexamic acid ✨” like it’s a whisper.


No. It straight up says 3% tranexamic acid and 4% niacinamide.





Thank you. Speak plainly to me.





Texture-wise, it’s super lightweight. Almost watery gel. No stickiness, no fragrance, no drama. It sinks in fast and basically disappears, which I love because I layer a million other things after.





The first few days, nothing exciting happened. Which honestly is how I prefer my skincare. If something works overnight, I get suspicious.





But after a couple weeks, I noticed my skin tone just looked… calmer. More even. The little red and brown marks on my cheeks and chin started softening around the edges instead of screaming “HELLO I USED TO BE A PIMPLE.”





It’s not Photoshop. It’s not magic.


It’s more like someone gently turned down the contrast on my face.





My skin also looked smoother and less oily during the day, probably thanks to the niacinamide. Pores around my nose looked a bit tighter too. Just overall “healthier” vibes.





And I appreciate that it’s gentle. No stinging, no peeling, no “why is my face angry?” phase. I use it morning and night without thinking twice.





If I had to nitpick, you do need consistency. This isn’t a one-week miracle. It’s a slow-and-steady kind of product. But honestly, that’s how most real results work anyway.





For $12 though? It feels kind of ridiculous how well it performs. I’ve used serums triple the price that did way less.





Final thoughts:


If you’re dealing with dark spots, post-acne marks, or uneven tone and don’t want to drop luxury-serum money, this is such a solid, low-risk place to start. It’s simple, effective, and refreshingly honest about what’s inside. One of those quiet overachievers that just works while you go about your life.

reviews.author: NikkiJames023
reviews.text: This stuff is liquid gold! I suffer from acne scarring and used to suffer from melasma on my forehead. This helped clear up both! It's super easy to use but the main thing is that you need to be super consistent. You shake the bottle well, apply a few drops all over your face and wait for it to dry before continuing your skincare routine. Try not to apply too much because it can get tacky on the skin. After a couple of weeks of using this, I noticed a difference in my skin, especially when you pair it with the Good Molecules niacinamide brightening toner. One 1 oz. bottle lasts me about 30 days when used twice a day. My skin texture feels smoother, looks brighter and the best part is it doesn't irritate my skin. This product has been a total game changer for me and I highly recommend giving it a try!

reviews.author: Kaydia.J
reviews.text: I literally just started using the Good Molecules Discoloration Correcting Serum and so far I’m really loving it. The formula feels super lightweight on my skin, absorbs quickly, and doesn’t leave me greasy or sticky at all. I mainly got it to help with my dark marks and uneven skin tone, and I’m excited to keep using it consistently to see the full results. I also love that it’s fragrance-free and gentle on my skin. For the price, it honestly feels like such a good skincare product already. Definitely looking forward to seeing my discoloration fade over time!

reviews.author: Liz Stasieczko
reviews.text: I am so impressed with this serum.  I’ve tried dozens of products on my sun damaged skin and every one was a disappointment until now. In 2 days of use I was already seeing my spots fading. A week later they were almost gone. Still using on my face but now I’ve started using on my hands.  This is an amazing and inexpensive product.  I highly recommend.

reviews.author: Rebecca
reviews.text: Works pretty well. Noticeably improved sun spots. Great moisturizing and skin softness.

name: Naturium Tranexamic Topical Acid 5%
ingredients: Water, Isopentyldiol, Tranexamic Acid, Propanediol, Citric Acid, Kojic Acid, Niacinamide, Hydroxyacetophenone, Isododecane, Polyacrylate Crosspolymer-6, Glycyrrhiza Glabra (Licorice) Root Extract, Panthenol, Glycerin, Xanthan Gum, Tremella Fuciformis Sporocarp Extract, Phenoxyethanol, Ethylhexylglycerin, Betaine, Sodium Hyaluronate, Sodium Phytate, Potassium Sorbate, Tocopherol.

couche3.note: tranexamique taches, lisse; résultats mitigés
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum to be of good quality and excellent value for money, with positive feedback about its texture making skin feel smooth and soft. The product receives mixed reviews for skin improvement, with some reporting dark spots getting lighter while others see no improvement. Customers disagree on its effectiveness, with some noting results within 48 hours while others report no results at all. The skin tone and irritation aspects also receive mixed feedback - while some report brighter skin and no irritation, others mention a very dark yellow color and burning sensations. Several customers report issues with mold growing in the dropper.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
I ordered this because I’ve noticed much more uneven skin tone as I’ve gotten older. Years of sun exposure, the occasional breakout mark, and general skin aging have left me with areas that look a little less uniform than they used to. The combination of tranexamic acid, kojic acid, niacinamide, and licorice root immediately caught my attention since those are all ingredients commonly associated with brightening and improving the appearance of discoloration.





The texture is one of the things I like most about this serum. It has a lightweight gel consistency that spreads easily and absorbs quickly into my skin. It doesn’t leave behind a sticky or greasy layer, which makes it easy to use under moisturizer, sunscreen, and makeup during the day.





I’ve been using it for two weeks, and so far it has fit nicely into my routine. My skin tends to be somewhat sensitive, especially when I combine multiple active ingredients, but I haven’t experienced irritation, stinging, or redness with this formula. The fragrance-free formula is also a plus for me since heavily scented skincare can sometimes be hit or miss.





While dark spots and uneven tone take time to improve, I have noticed that my complexion looks a bit brighter and more even overall. My skin also feels comfortable and well-balanced while using it. I like that Naturium combined several brightening ingredients into a single product rather than requiring multiple separate serums.





In the end, this has been a nice addition to my skincare routine. The lightweight texture, gentle formula, and combination of proven brightening ingredients make it a product I’ll continue reaching for as I work on improving uneven skin tone and discoloration.

reviews.author: Tiffany Fisher
reviews.text:
I purchased this specifically to help with melasma, and I’ve been using it consistently twice a day for about five months. I also want to be transparent that I use it as part of a larger skincare routine that includes daily sunscreen, vitamin C, occasional retinoid use, and micro needling, so it’s difficult to know exactly how much improvement comes from one product alone.





With that said, I do feel like this product has helped keep my melasma from getting worse, which honestly is still valuable to me because melasma can be very stubborn and easily triggered. However, I personally have not noticed dramatic lightening or major fading from this product alone after several months of consistent use.





As far as the formula itself, I really like it. It dries down quickly, doesn’t leave my skin feeling sticky or heavy, and layers well with the rest of my skincare products. I recently added azelaic acid into my routine as well, so I’m interested to see whether that combination gives me more noticeable results over time, but I can’t speak to that yet.





Overall, I think this is a nice, easy-to-use product for someone looking to add tranexamic acid into their skincare routine, just keep expectations realistic if you’re dealing with stubborn melasma like I am.

reviews.author: ji
reviews.text: I have mostly nice things to say about this product. It's effective, My hyperpigmentation has definitely improved. I am only giving it 4 stars  because of the packaging. There is no way to know when you're running out of this product. It's thick, so if you shake it, it doesn't make a sound, and you can't see inside of it. So one day you just wake up and you don't have any, and that's a little bit annoying, because you want to be consistent.

reviews.author: V
reviews.text: “I’ve been using the Naturium Tranexamic Topical Acid 5% and I’m honestly loving the results so far. My skin tone looks more even, dark spots have started to fade, and my complexion looks brighter overall. The formula feels lightweight, absorbs quickly, and layers well with the rest of my skincare routine without feeling sticky. It’s also gentle enough that it didn’t irritate my skin. Definitely a product I’d keep repurchasing!”

reviews.author: K. C. Nelson
reviews.text: My aging brown spots are disappearing. Good stuff!

couche3.note: rétinol débutant doux, acné/taches, OK sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this retinol serum effective at controlling acne and reducing dark spots, while being gentle on sensitive skin without causing irritation. The product receives positive feedback for its moisturizing properties, with one customer noting their face stays soft all day, and its quick-absorbing formula that doesn't feel heavy or greasy. Customers appreciate its value for money and its ability to make skin look bright and beautiful.

reviews.author: JRob
reviews.text: I am 30 days in and so far, no issues. I am going to re-iterate what another customer said as it was spot on for me. This is a good starting dose for people who want to titrate up over time in strength. It also doesn’t dry your skin out like retinol is known to do due to the form. I’m going to use the rest of the bottle then move up to the next strength and so on and so forth. The amount you get for the price is more than sufficient as retinoids can get pricey.
