This is a very low strength, no real scent to it, so I didn’t experience any irritation, burning or rash. It isn't sticky but is like an oil so I just put on like an hour before bed. I haven't noticed a change in my skin yet, I know that will take time. The only odd thing I've found is I am sweater and walk outside daily, I have noticed it leaves a weird yellow tint in my sunscreen and foundation after sweating. I just use a beauty blender to touch up my makeup and then it's fine. Doesn't really bother me.

reviews.author: Ajay R
reviews.text: This is a good starting dose for people who want to titrate up over time in strength. It also doesn’t dry your skin out like retinol is known to do due to the form. I’m going to use the rest of the bottle then move up to the next strength and so on and so forth. The amount you get for the price is more than sufficient as retinoids can get pricey. This is a very low strength so I didn’t experience any irritation, burning or rash. It goes on smoooooootthhh and takes a a little bit to absorb into your skin. It’s not quite sticky but feels like a sheer oil. If you wear to bed, make sure to give yourself some time for it absorb or else your pillow is going to get a face full. Leaves my skin beautiful and glowy in the morning. Make sure to wear sunscreen during the day because your skin will be sensitive to sun and prone to sun exposed injury/ damage. Note that last part. Looking forward to my Benjamin button arc.

reviews.author: Leinis
reviews.text:
I recently started using this retinol about twice a week and so far I’m really liking how it feels on my skin. The texture is lightweight and absorbs nicely without feeling heavy or greasy.





Since I’m still early in the process, I haven’t seen major results yet, but my skin tolerates it well and it hasn’t caused irritation or breakouts. It feels gentle and promising so far.





I plan to keep using it consistently to see more visible improvements over time. Overall, a great start and I’m happy with it.

reviews.author: Anzhelika
reviews.text: I’ve been using this retinol serum and really like the formula. The texture is lightweight and easy to apply, and it absorbs nicely without feeling heavy on the skin. The squalane helps keep my skin feeling moisturized while using retinol. I appreciate that it is fragrance-free and gentle, with good skin compatibility for my skincare routine. Overall, it’s an excellent value for money and a great beginner-friendly retinol serum.

reviews.author: Jashmina pandey
reviews.text: Did good job for me, was gentle to my skin yet worked like a magic

couche3.note: rétinoïde doux rides, ne pique pas
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this retinoid serum effective at reducing fine lines and forehead wrinkles, with their skin looking radiant and brighter over time. The product receives positive feedback for its gentle formula that doesn't cause irritation, and customers appreciate its moisturizing properties that leave skin soft and hydrated. They consider it good value for money and praise its quality, with one customer noting it's particularly suitable for first-time users. The fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some customers finding it pleasant while others mention a strong smell.

reviews.author: Binh
reviews.text:
The Ordinary makes good skincare actually affordable and this is a great example of that. Retinoids at this concentration usually cost a lot more from other brands so the price alone makes it worth trying.





It moisturizes well and the formula is clean with no weird fillers. The only thing I'd flag is the texture is a bit heavier than I'd like on its own. If you mix a small amount into your regular moisturizer it spreads way better and feels lighter on the skin.





Solid product overall. If you want to add a retinoid to your routine without spending a lot this is an easy recommendation.

reviews.author: Nilsa
reviews.text: This has worked wonders for my skin. I use an very drying facial cleanser to really clear my skin of ALL oils, then I use nothing but CeraVe Daily Moisturizer Lotion and this. My skin is so clear that my friends ask me what my skincare routine is. I gave the exact routine to one friend and his skin cleared in just 1 week. I always rebuy this the moment I am about to run out. It soaks in great, has a thin spreadable consistency, and is super easy to work with. If used right, this will CURE YOUR SKIN

reviews.author: Ace
reviews.text: Quality product. No fragrance. I add a full bottle to my Saltair body cream. Mix it really well. Saltair has ceramides and lipids that make it moisturizing enough for a body retinal cream. Works great. Convenient to have it available on Amazon so I’m thankful for that.

reviews.author: Rebecca K
reviews.text: I’ve been using this product for about two weeks now. My skin feels firmer and smoother and it visibly reduced my fine lines and forehead wrinkles. I have MCAS and very sensitive skin and this did not cause any irritation at all. Will definitely be purchasing again.

reviews.author: Suguru Takahashi
reviews.text: I've been repurchasing this product for several years now. It gently cares for my 40s skin, which tends to worry about pores. The price is very reasonable, and it has almost no scent, making it incredibly easy to use every day.

name: CeraVe Resurfacing Retinol Serum
ingredients: AQUA/WATER/EAU, PROPANEDIOL, DIMETHICONE, CETEARYL ETHYLHEXANOATE, NIACINAMIDE, AMMONIUM POLYACRYLOYLDIMETHYL TAURATE, DIPOTASSIUM GLYCYRRHIZATE, HYDROGENATED LECITHIN, POTASSIUM PHOSPHATE, CERAMIDE NP, CERAMIDE AP, CERAMIDE EOP, CARBOMER, CETEARYL ALCOHOL, BEHENTRIMONIUM METHOSULFATE, DIMETHICONOL, LECITHIN, SODIUM CITRATE, RETINOL, SODIUM HYALURONATE, SODIUM LAUROYL LACTYLATE, CHOLESTEROL, PHENOXYETHANOL, ALCOHOL, ISOPROPYL MYRISTATE, CAPRYLYL GLYCOL, CITRIC ACID, TRISODIUM ETHYLENEDIAMINE DISUCCINATE, PENTYLENE GLYCOL, PHYTOSPHINGOSINE, XANTHAN GUM, POLYSORBATE 20, ETHYLHEXYLGLYCERIN

couche3.note: rétinol doux, acné/texture, OK sensible, non gras
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the retinol serum effective at clearing skin and improving texture, with one customer noting its success in treating maskne on sensitive skin. The product receives positive feedback for its gentle formula, brightening effect, and non-greasy texture, while also being fragrance-free and offering good value for money. Opinions about gentleness are mixed, with some customers reporting no irritation while others experienced burning sensations.

reviews.author: Courtney
reviews.text:
I had horrible cystic acne in my early teens that left a lot of scarring and hyperpigmentation on my face; mostly my cheeks, but small dots here and there on my temples too.





I use prescription clindamycin for the occasional cystic acne now, but of course that doesn't do anything for scarring so I decided to branch out. I'm scared of needles so microneedling, while arguably not THAT scary, wasn't an option both because of my fear and because of the price... Hence, this product.





I chose this one because it had a lot of reviews honestly, I am definitely a victim of the "oh, this product has close to five stars and blah blah so many reviews, that must mean it'll work on me!" mentality sometimes, I will admit, so I was a bit skeptical because I've taken this approach before with some products in the past and it didn't go well.





THIS is the best choice I've ever made for a skincare product. While the scarring on my right cheek is still there, it is very minimal now and way less noticeable. As for my left cheek, it's literally almost completely gone. It's crazy. I've only been using this for like maybe 2 months, and just like that, bad acne scarring is gone. I didn't even know that was possible.


That being said, just because this worked super well for me doesn't necessarily mean it'll work super well for you, everyone's skin is different!





I will also add that the serum itself works very well on my oily skin. It's not drying, sticky, and it seems to absorb into my skin well, which is an issue I have with other products. A few minutes after applying it, I don't feel it whatsoever, both in feeling and appearance. You don't need very much of it either, which is pretty common in retinol, to my knowledge, but I really like that I can use just a teeny tiny bit every so often and still have results. Definitely worth a try if you're considering it for acne scar reduction.

reviews.author: Ron
reviews.text: This product, along with four others recommended by ChatGPT to help fade sun and aging spots, has worked remarkably well. One of the other products I use with it, which is an essential product, is Timeless 20% Vitamin C. Along with it, I alternate between CeraVe resurfacing retinol serum and  Paula'sChoice 2% BHA liquid exfoliant for my nose. This combination has worked better in 3 weeks than anything my dermatologist has given me.  The lineup of products or perhaps $50 in total, all purchased on Amazon, adds a good price, and 3 weeks later, I can't begin to tell you how an 85% lightning of age and sunspots on my face and arms, feel so good.

reviews.author: Nicole
reviews.text: This is probably one of my favorite products for skincare! I can’t tolerate a stronger retinol yet postpartum, because I’ve been off of it for a while. This is a great segway product to get me back to a stronger product. It does not irritate your skin at all, leaves a beautiful glowy finish and feels great on the skin. I can even put it on my neck without bothering my skin, which is huge. I would recommend this for anyone in a similar situation, anyone that has never used a type of product like this before and wants to start or has very sensitive skin. It’s fantastic and very easy to use.

reviews.author: Ksenia
reviews.text:
This retinol serum has a lightweight texture that spreads easily and absorbs quickly into the skin. It layers well with other skincare products and doesn’t feel heavy or greasy after application.





With regular use, my skin started to look smoother and more even in tone. The formula feels fairly gentle for a retinol product, although it can cause slight dryness at first, so using a good moisturizer alongside it is helpful.





Overall, it’s a well-formulated retinol serum that works well as part of a consistent skincare routine, especially for those starting with retinol products.

reviews.author: Tsequoi
reviews.text: No skin irritation. Ingredients such as retinol help with wrinkles and are very effective. 2 pumps is all you really need as the consistency isn’t thick, so it spreads easily and the product will last longer.

name: Differin Adapalene Gel 0.1%
ingredients: Active Ingredient: Adapalene 0.1% (retinoid) Inactive ingredients: carbomer 940, edetate disodium, methylparaben, poloxamer 182, propylene glycol, purified water and sodium hydroxide. May contain hydrochloric acid to adjust pH.

couche3.note: adapalène acné efficace; dessèche (prudence sèche/sensible)
couche3.customers_say: Customers report that the acne treatment gel works fast and helps prevent new breakouts, while also improving skin texture and making it feel smooth. The product receives positive feedback for its appearance, with customers noting their skin looks clearer and more radiant. Regarding dryness and sensitivity, while some find it moisturizing and non-irritating, others report it's too drying and causes burning sensations. Moreover, opinions on value for money are divided, with some finding it worth the price while others consider it expensive for its size. Additionally, experiences with skin damage are mixed, with some customers reporting minimal peeling while others experience severe flaking and cracking.

reviews.author: Private Profile
reviews.text:
Here is what I did to avoid the fate of purging and negative skin rashes.....





Don't:


1) Don't use on raw EXFOLIATED skin.  Wait a couple days after exfoliating.


2) Don't use a SUDSING cleansing soap prior. Use a mild hydrating Cleanser.


3) Don't use after getting your skin waxed or shaved.


4) Avoid hot water and steam before use.


5) Do NOT go stand in the sun after applying (retin works by thinning the top layer of your skin which is usually used for protection against the sun). Don't take your chances!


6) do not apply to eyelids or neck or other thin skin areas including the inside of your wrists.  You can apply Vaseline to these areas if needed to protect them.


7) don't freak out if your face flakes and don't scrub the flakes off. Moisturizer is your friend.


8) don't scratch at your skin if itches or burns, stay calm, rinse it off with cool water. Apply a gentle cream and then apply a cool wash cloth. Consider diluting before using again.


9) don't assume because your neighbor next door can use twice a day on fresh scrubbed skin with no adverse effects, that the same will be true for you. Adverse skin reactions can take weeks or months to heal from.


10) do NOT apply generously all over your skin. Pea size only on face.





Do:


1) plan on using once or twice a week at first for the first month.  Remember "sparingly" is your motto.


2) Have a natural moisture barrier on your face first. Be sure you have not scrubbed your skin raw or clean prior and make sure face is danp prior to applying.


3) if you have ultra sensitive skin, apply a gentle moisturizer FIRST before applying differin. Allow to dry before applying differin.


4) do apply a creamy moisturizer after if needed. Wait five minutes before you do.


5) do also apply sunscreen RELIGIOUSLY if you will be outdoors.  So important!!!!


6) do research "purging" and what you can do to minimize or avoid it.


7) do remember that this product is a MEDICATION and not your typical OTC cream. Use sparingly and with caution.


8)  do remember that moisture is your friend. After cleansing, avoid drying your skin with towels. Avoid air drying as well.


9) do remember that with this product "less is more".  It is potent stuff!  Once or twice a week treatments alone will make a difference.


10) keep a good repair Cream on hand in possible event of harsh reaction or irritated skin. I recommend "first aid beauty ultra repair cream". This stuff is amazing. Hydrocortisone cream can also be used.





MOST IMPORTANTLY:  it takes 6-12 weeks of patient and measured use before seeing positive long term results for acne. It can take up to a year before seeing positive results for aged and wrinkled skin.





MY RESULTS:


I started out at a twice a week and had no reaction except for mild redness, itching and tingling the following two days afterwards.  When I tried to increase to three days a week, I started having issues. The solution to this was to revamp my skin care routine (listed below). After that, I was able to switch pretty rapidly to nightly use. The GAME CHANGER was adding First Aid Ultra Hydrating Cream.





I've included my before and six week after photos. My acne is gone and my pores most definitely shrunk. I used the same room and lighting and I notice that my skin has become much brighter and lighter. My guess is in removing the thing top layer of skin, we no longer see the old tanned skin I had. So far, I am happy with the results.





I am 38 years old. My long term goal is to prevent deep wrinkles and shrink my pores and hopefully reduce white Milia bumps under my eyes as well as all random acne and age spots. I know from researching this can take months and years so I'm feeling patient.





MY CURRENT DIFFERIN APPLICATION:





My morning routine:


- CereVe Hydrating Cleanser


- After rinsing, I apply Timewise Vitamin C Serum with the added F acid.


- Then, I apply a thin layer of Sunscreen: La Roche-Posay Anthelios with Mexoryl SX


OR CeraVe SPF 30.





*note: I believe the Vitamin C Serum helped with quicker skin cell turnover rate to prevent purging.





In the evenings:


- I double cleanse: first I use Yes To Coconuts Uktra Hydrating Creme Cleanser. After I rinse that off, I cleanse with CeraVe Hydrating Facial Cleanser.


- I allow skin to stay damp.


- I then apply a pea size amount of differin to my face (never on neck or eyelids).


- after about minutes, I follow that with First Aid Beauty Ultra Repair Cream.


- I apply CeraVe Skin renewing Serum to my eyes and neck and chest.


- note: I only use retin A products at night to decrease sun sensitivity.  In the mornings, I use all my other acids and Vitamin C.





Be aware that most of the negative reviews on here are mostly due to user error and lack of knowledge in the product. There is no reason to have terrible breakouts or harm your skin. I'm horrified people are using this on their NECK right away. Poor skin!  This is a potent product.





Good luck!

reviews.author: lkwilson06
reviews.text:
If you don't read any further in my review, please know this: IT GETS A LOT WORSE before it GETS MUCH BETTER.  HANG IN THERE.





I'm posting this in hopes this helps someone.  First, some history:  I've struggled with moderate acne from my teenage to adult years.  In my early and mid 30s, my acne has turned into painful cysts that predominantly cover my cheeks, jawline and neck.  I also have breakouts on my shoulders, back and chest, though they aren't anything like my face.  The areas I break out in would lead me to believe it's hormonal.  Since I've also had hair loss and have had cysts on my ovaries, there was a thought it was PCOS, but dozens of blood tests and expensive doctor visits later, that was ruled out.





I spend COUNTLESS, and I mean COUNTLESS hours researching about acne.  It's ruined my self-esteem and confidence, I never feel attractive, and I even have trouble looking at people sometimes.  My skin is PAINFUL due to the deep cysts and inflammation.  So much so, some days I just wanted to cut my skin off.  It's been hard not to pick, but of course even if I don't, my skin is left with red marks that are just has hard to cover up as the inflamed bumps.





I've tried EVERYTHING over the years.  You name it, I've tried it, and that includes Acutane (but I wouldn't stay on it due to concerns over liver damage).  The only thing I haven't tried that I heard could work is Spirnolactane.  I was actually prescribed it but decided not to take it, again, over concerns about what it would due to my health.  I don't even know how many dermatologist, primary care and OB/GYN appts I've had to discuss the matter.  One derm went so far as to tell me, "If you still have acne at this age, you'll always have acne."  In other words, I'm hopeless.  I hated that guy.  That was told to me when I was around 22 and I've never forgotten it 13 years later.





Recently, I moved to a new town and got a facial.  The woman that was treating me suggested Differin Gel.  She mentioned she'd seen some success with it and that it was a fairly new OTC product.  I figured, what in the heck do I have to lose.  Desperate, I ordered it from Amazon.





And after using it for the first time, I BROKE OUT EVERYWHERE, particularly my forehead.  It was AWFUL.  My only patch of clear skin was now what seemed to be infinite whiteheads.  Every pore was a whitehead.  It was awwwwwful!!!  I immediately quit the product, and was so angry that another thing failed me.  It took WEEKS for the whitehead breakout to clear up.





While I waited, I kept researching it.  I don't know why.  And I read how people talked about it "purging" the skin so people get a bad initial breakout.  Ok.  Well, I don't know if it was out of frustration or a little nagging gut feeling, I decided to try it again.





AND MY SKIN GOT SO RED AND DRY AND BURNED SO BADLY.  I mean, excruciatingly painful.  And it turns out that's just because I was coating on a thick layer of it all over my skin.  How embarrassing showing up for work and my whole face is just red and massive FLAKES.





But after all that flaking and peeling, and using non-stop aloe on it (also borrowed my husband's thick Aveeno Eczema lotion... it seems to really work!), I had.... clear skin.  I can't believe I'm typing this.  It's been weeks, and my skin has cleared up 98% (I did have a couple neck and jawling cysts this period cycle, though - but they're not as hidden under the surface as they used to be).  My skin doesn't get nearly as oily (overcompensating for the dryness of Differin) or as flaky (because I'm using a little bit for the whole face with a little more added onto my problem areas).  It seems to have finally balanced out.





I really just can't believe it, and I am so unbelievably happy and feeling so much more confident than I have in a long time.  I truly hope this helps someone.  Shoot me a question on here if you have one and I'll be happy to answer.





For the record, after a ton of my own research, I believe I have a sensitivity to DHT.  My blood test DHT levels are normal, but as I understand it, sensitivity to DHT cannot be measured, and I believe that may explain my female hair loss and cystic acne.  I recently purchased Saw Palmetto Extract to see if that can help -- though it made me feel very faint so I stopped after a third day.

reviews.author: Biryani
reviews.text:
I have been using Differin for over a year now (around 14 months giver or take) and did not publish a review until now. If you want an in-depth review, read on. For the tl;dr version: it works, just stick with it. Btw, I was not paid for this nor was I sent this product (although they should be sending it, given how many tubes I've bought).





I had the MOST TERRIBLE acne. No, I'm not talking about a small tiny excuse of a blemish that your friend with great skin complains about when she has her "bad skin" days. My face was RED and PAINFUL. My cheeks were COVERED all the time, and I had no area on my face where acne wouldn't pop up. Nose, brows, chin, jaw, underneath the jaw, you name it. I had tried Curology and while my skin got amazing for the first 6 months, my acne returned with a VENGEANCE. I had tried so many things, clay masks, benzoyl peroxide, salicylic acid, other AHAs and BHAs, sulfur, rosehip oil, and even supplements like zinc and milk thistle, you name it. Nothing worked at all. I was so so embarrassed of my skin, but I couldn't wear makeup either since makeup made my acne SO MUCH worse.





I heard about Differin and I figured "well, might as well give this a try cause it can't get any worse". Boy, I was wrong. It did get worse. Much worse. Suddenly I had SO much more acne and I felt like crap. I couldn't believe my skin could get worse, but it did. Differin was the only thing I was using so I knew this was it. I was depressed but I stuck with it, cause I wanted to test it for at least 4 months. I thought I might as well test the claim, since my skin is so bad already.





The first month was really really bad. It brought all underlying acne to the surface i.e. the purge phase. It did the purging for 3-4 months, but the first month was THE WORST. Not gonna lie to you, that first month will probably be the worst skin you've had in your life. Your skin will become SO SENSITIVE! Anticipate that! My skin has never been sensitive to products but while using this, my regular moisturizers and masks would sting like crazy (get stuff with no fragrance/alcohol or acids!). It didn't affect my skin in any other way, it just stung a lot. Oh, and I used to get my upper lip and brows waxed. Not with this you can't, the wax will tear off your skin since it gets much more sensitive. I learned the hard way. ONLY do threading!





However, after I waited out the terrible, terrible first month, I slowly began to see improvements. Marginal, though. I had acne, but I probably had just a tad less. And it continued that way, it kept improving. Eventually, my skin was so much clearer than before! I still had acne, mind you, it's not a miracle drug, but it was few and far in between. There would be times when I had no active acne, which was HUGE for me!! My face usually looked like a connect the dots game. However, I did hit a plateau. This worked phenomenally at reducing my ugly acne all over my face to a few zits here and there, however, my issue was deeper than that. Eventually, I went to a doctor and got on spironolactone.





Now, I am both on my Spiro and this. The combo of these two have made my skin so clear! I could have never imagined this day! I still need to use this, I have noticed my Spiro is not enough to keep zits at bay and if I skip a couple of nights putting on differin, new zits come up. I am so much happier now that I don't have zillions of gross zits on my face. I still get zits if I eat sugar or near my period but man, I am a long way from before! I have a few blemishes left over from before but I honestly don't care, my skin looks wayyy better. I can hide my blemishes with a sheer foundation of spot concealing if I want.





So if you've read till the end, the lesson is: be PATIENT and be realistic! Your acne did not appear overnight and will not disappear overnight. If you stick with this, it WILL work. However, your mileage may vary. My acne was extremely bad, so after I hit my "acne plateau" I went to the doctor. If your acne is not as bad, it's likely differin is all you need for clear skin! If you're on the fence, confused, depressed, frustrated and/or can't afford a doctor, it's worth a shot! Just be patient and gentle with your skin!

reviews.author: Vy Nguyen
reviews.text:
I have been using Differin since 2022 until now. A little bit does go a long way, and it has been helping me with my acne a lot. In my experience, the purge happened around 6-8 months of using it, and it lasted pretty long on my skin (about 3-4 months) :(((( But when it stopped, my skin started to clear up. I still get acne occasionally, but most of the go away after 1-2 days, so not an issue at all.





I would say that because my skin is very sensitive. I am more prone to having skin reactions or damaged skin barrier everytime the weather changes or the water is a bit hotter than usual, I have to stop using Differin until my skin feels better again. Patience and a good moisturizer are key in this.

reviews.author: logan
reviews.text:
This works really well (so far). I’ve had acne since I was in middle school and I thought once I hit 18 or so that it would go away but sadly it did not.


For the first 7-8 weeks using this my acne was pretty bad and my face was really sensitive. Entering now my 9th week of using this, my acne is almost completely gone and my skin is no longer sensitive. Pretty much a night and day difference. I’m going to continue to use this and see how it goes but for right now I feel amazing and am extremely surprised at the effectiveness of this. I’m hoping with continual use and continual face washing that my acne will disappear completely.

name: RoC Retinol Correxion Deep Wrinkle Night
ingredients: Water, dimethicone, glycerin, ethylhexyl hydroxystearate, isodecyl neopentanoate, tetrahydroxypropyl ethylenediamine, stearyl alcohol, cetearyl alcohol, glyceryl stearate, PEG-100 stearate, ceteareth-20, steareth-10, phenoxyethanol, hydroxyethyl acrylate/sodium acryloyldimethyl taurate copolymer, methyl methacrylate crosspolymer, caprylyl glycol, aluminum starch octenylsuccinate, squalane, glycolic acid, fragrance, butyrospermum parkii (shea) butter, methylparaben, citric acid, xanthan gum, propylparaben, retinol, polysorbate 20, BHT, disodium EDTA, polysorbate 60, ascorbic acid, magnesium aspartate, zinc gluconate, copper gluconate

couche3.note: rétinol rides, non gras; parfumé (prudence sensible)
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this retinol cream effective at reducing wrinkles and fine lines, particularly under the eyes, and appreciate that it's non-greasy with a silky feel. Moreover, they consider it good value for money, noting it works well without expensive dermatologist treatments. However, the fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some finding it beautiful while others describe it as awful. Additionally, customers report mixed results for skin appearance, with some reporting a nice glow while others experience redness. Skin irritation is a significant concern, with multiple customers reporting burning sensations.

reviews.author: K_Ward
reviews.text:
I have spent a large majority of my 22 years in the New Mexican sun, and while I have been very good about sunscreen the last couple of years the sun damage of my youth has caught up to me and I found myself with 3 forehead wrinkles and a scattering of freckles and sunspots. While it might seem insignificant to some I hate to think what I'd look like at 40 if I already have signs of aging.


So I did a lot of research into proven wrinkle treatment and in my search I discovered something. There are millions, MILLIONS of products, home remedies, and concoctions that claim to prevent wrinkles, treat wrinkles, make them disappear, and do a number of other things. However the ONLY FDA APPROVED ANTI AGING INGREDIENT is the various strands of RETINOIDS. That doesnt mean that nothing else out there works, but it means that the only one that has enough strong scientific evidence to be considered a treatment for anti aging is products containing some type of retinoid. When you go to a dermatologist for wrinkle cream, you will very likely receive a prescription strength retinoid because it is proven to work.


What are retinoids exactly and what is the difference between the various types?


Retinoids are essentially vitamin A, their structures vary in each of the three generations but generally all the retinoids found in anti wrinkle creams are first generation. They are in fact natural ingredients found in plants and animals and we consume them in our daily diets. Researching the various types of retinoids for anti aging there are 3 things that pop up.: retinol, tretinoin, and retinoic acid. It took a while to decipher what each of them is and after reading a number of papers about them the short summary is this. Tretinoin is retinoic acid (its also known by its drug name Retin-A) and is the retinoid you will find in perscription cream. The difference between retinol and tretinoin (in relation to their usefulness) is simply their strength. Tretinoin is retinoic acid that is directly applied and effective where retinol is applied and your skin coverts it to retinoic acid to be used.


One of the dermatologist articles I read said she recommends anyone who considers a tretinoin to start with a retinol so you can build up to the full strength.


Retinol and Tretinoin have been found to treat a number of different skin conditions, including acne, wrinkles, stretch marks, and even some studies have been done on its effect on treating skin cancer.


How it treats wrinkles. When its applied your skin has cells that synthesize the retinoic acid and it increases cell turnover, thickens the skin, increases collagen production, Stimulates blood vessels in the skin, and reduces sun spots.


One thing to know though is if applied everyday results will not show for 3-6 months with best results in 6-12 months. So for everyone who says they've used it for a month and haven't seen results, they  haven't been using it long enough, remember sun damage and wrinkles took years and years to appear and are not going to be undone in a month.


The way to apply the product is to spread evenly 30 minutes after washing your face, to ensure your face is completely dry. While I have found different reasons as to why it shouldn't be applied wet most articles say that it: decreases effectiveness, increases risk for side effects like dryness and pealing/scaliness, and one article says it doesn't matter if you apply it wet, but putting it on while your face is completely dry can't hurt. Retinoids also make your skin more sun sensitive, so sunscreen should be applied daily (which if your trying to undo sun damage and wrinkles anyway than you should be putting on sunscreen anyway to prevent even more damage)





Now finally for my personal results. I started using this product every night around 4 months ago and I have noticed a difference. My forehead wrinkles have almost completely disappeared, my sun spots have faded, and even my acne scars are less noticeable. For the price and effectiveness I would recommend this product. The only reason I do not give this product 5 stars is simply because it advertises itself as a deep wrinkle cream, and as I only have fine lines I cannot gauge its effectiveness on deep wrinkles.


I hope some of the information about gives you a little insight into retinoids, but please know this is only basic information gathered by an individual from various scientific articles. I am not a dermatologist and if I was to go into more detail about retinoids this review would be 10 times longer. If you want to know more you should do your own research and talk to your doctor.

reviews.author: MB
reviews.text:
I love this product for fighting wrinkles, but it is so harsh.  I have sensitive/dry skin (which of course, shows wrinkles the most) and being of "that certain age,"  my skin is extra dry and sensitive.  I used this religiously, but ended up with my eyes almost sealed shut from a reaction to it and my skin burned, even when I limited my use to once or twice a week.  Got rid of the wrinkles, but also got rid of my skin!!  I won't give up using it, because it is amazing on wrinkles, but I recommend not using it every night - use it a couple of nights on, then a couple of nights off.  My face would be red after using it this way, but not burned.  A friend of mine recommended another skin care product - she's into organic - and told me to try Bel Essence (comes in an oil or cream, I used both - I've been buying it from their website but it's available here too)(

Bel Essence Anti Aging Treatment - Intensive All Natural Anti-Wrinkle, Facial Lift Skin Care Formula - 1.5oz

).  This solved the reaction problem to Roc.  Not only did it help my skin in general, but using it with Roc - no burning, no bad reactions.  I talked to my doctor about it, and he told me that the natural oils in Bel Essence contain high amounts of Vitamin E and other nutrients that help the skin heal and stay healthy.  If you love Roc (and I am a HUGE fan), I recommend using Bel Essence with it, to get the good effects of Roc without the bad.  There are a lot of all natural skin creams out there, but this worked the best, and absolutely worked the best with Roc.  Not only does it keep the results from the retinol permanent, my skin kept getting better - lines and discolorations were still going away even when I reduced the number of times I used the retinol.  I swear by both products.





Quick update - for Roc burns, I found that the Bel Essence oil 

Bel Essence - (2oz) 100% All-Natural Organic Anti-Wrinkle Formula with Argan, Grapeseed and Sesame Oil

 works a little quicker in helping heal.  Probably because it is 100% oil without the ingredients to turn it into a cream.  So if Roc really burned your skin, the oil will help treat it more quickly.





Another quick update - my skin is fabulous now.  Never thought it would look this good - the Roc has reduced my lines to minor marks that make up covers nicely, and my skin is soft, firm, and a lot brighter.  This is a GREAT combo.  I love the effect of the bel essence on my skin, the roc gets your skin to turnover faster, and the bel essence makes sure that the new skin is firm, smooth, soft and wrinkle free.  I can't believe that a natural product work work this well - I expect it from the roc which uses chemicals, but I am amazed that ingredients from nature can so completely change my skin and make me look years younger!





Ok - so I just need to add another quick rave about the combination of roc and bel essence.  I had deep crevices across my forehead - GONE - only the tiniest of lines that are easily covered with makeup.  I now have NO LINES around my eyes, or on my upper lip.  This combination is amazing.  I've used the Roc alone for a long time and saw results, but when I added the Bel Essence, that's when I really saw results.  And permanent results.  I am so thrilled with my skin now!

reviews.author: gnjames
reviews.text:
I’ve used other retinol products with varying results, but I decided to take a chance on this one because Google listed it as one of the top products for effectiveness against signs of aging.





HOW I USE:


I apply this retinol cream to my wrinkles and lines every evening, then follow-up with a good night cream, (it leaves my skin too dry when used alone). I began with every-night use right away (it is recommended to start gradually with every-other-night vs nightly, but my skin is not sensitive, so I jumped right in without regrets; if you have sensitive skin, I would suggest easing into using, as the instructions state).





MY EXPERIENCE:


The first few times using this cream, I felt a slight burning sensation (mostly between my eyebrows area). I suppose that was my skin getting used to the retinol.





After the very first application, I noticed my smile lines, the lines leading from my nose to my mouth, seemed to reduce substantially. Then, within a couple of days of use, the frown lines between my eyebrows began to lessen. I’ve used this product every night for a little over 2 weeks now, and my skin feels smoother and more youthful. Unfortunately, it has caused a few breakouts, too. Mostly at the end of the first week (maybe it was my skin getting used to this level of retinol).





I haven’t noticed much difference in the wrinkles above my lip (some call them smoker’s lines, but I’m not a smoker). Sometimes, I accidentally lick that area, and let me tell you, retinol does not taste good. And I’m sure it’s not good to ingest. So, I began applying a lighter layer, which may be why I haven’t seen any improvement there.





---------------------------------------


Overall, this is an excellent product. I love that it actually lightens my wrinkles without requiring a prescription or a doctor’s visit fee. The scent is neutral, it's easy to apply, and the consistency is smooth and creamy. I really like this product.

reviews.author: a customer
reviews.text:
I was pleased to receive this product as I have looked for a facial moisturizer that contained a retinol and would soften the rougher skin that comes with aging.


There is barely a scent, absolutely nothing unpleasant. It has moisturized my skin nicely and without any greasy or sticky feelings. I think it is too early to see a true difference in fine lines. (I have used true retin A in the past and even it takes a while to show results, usually beginning with peeling)


If you've read the label, you have noticed that the retinol is certainly not 'top of the label' so that I am unsure how active it actually is. But I have treated it with the respect I show true retin A, using it at night, as directed on label, using about 30 minutes after washing and drying my face. I use a sunscreen daytime with a moisturizer. I started using this cream twice weekly, switched to every other night per label, and will wait a bit before using it nightly. I do expect possible peeling and perhaps a bit of redness. Personally, I avoid the undereye area as I tend to be most sensitive there but I use an Amazon eye cream that I really like.


This cream certainly gets positive reviews on beauty sites and I plan to continue using it. I like that it is a thick cream, so that a dab goes far. I got it during a Prime sale because it is usually a bit expensive for such a small tube.


The moisturizing itself lends a nice glow to my skin and I think it was a great buy.

reviews.author: Jeanie
reviews.text:
I found myself growing desperate. At 70 years old, my skin was no longer responding to exfoliation. It was as if my skin was as dry as a desert bone and no amount of moisturizer would penetrate it.


I thought to myself: 'So this is what it's like to turn 70, as the skin cells harden like old dry leather. It was very uncomfortable physically, the sensation of unyielding, tight dryness.


Then I read the good reviews on this particular product, RoC Retinol Correction Deep Wrinkle Night Cream. I thought, Why not try it - it's not too pricey and I could afford this.


So glad I did!


Almost immediately it began to work wonders! My skin feels normal again, like tender, soft, clean young skin - well, as soft as a 70 year old can get.


My skin looks and feels better after only a week's worth of bedtime application. No lines are disappearing, but they appear softer, if that makes sense.


My makeup glides on more smoothly and my skin looks distinctly softer/better, more luminous.


I would definitely recommend this one for the over 65 crowd.


Note: There is a mild burning after its applied. At first, apply it every other night. Then apply every night. After the application dries down on your face, in 10 minutes or so, you can apply a natural, healthy none toxic moisturizer. It helps to relieve some of the burning.

couche3.note: rétinol 1% fort rides/acné; irritation (prudence sensible)
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the retinol serum effective, with one mentioning it works well for acne and fine lines, while others note it helps with wrinkles and even skin tone. The product receives positive feedback for its texture, making skin smooth and soft, though opinions about its size are mixed, with many finding it very small for the price. While some customers report it doesn't irritate their skin and lasts longer, others experience rashes and note it's not suitable for long-term use. The strength of the retinol receives mixed reviews, with some finding it extremely strong while others say it's too strong for their skin.

reviews.author: clukhard
reviews.text:
I was introduced to this product by a friend who has similar skin/complexion. She looks fabulous so I decided to give it a whirl.





Things you should know:


1. My beauty routine consisted of occasionally washing my face. And by occasional— I mean rarely.


2. I was always fortunate to have ‘good’ skin it didn’t break out or have big pores BUT it was super sensitive.


3. I’m lazy and will not do anything that requires a lot of work.


4. I’m 50.  The pictures are in order of progress — blue top was day of first application —the next 3 are from the first 10 days and the last one is as of tonight.





With all that said — I was instructed in detail about using the product.


1. Start slow — every other night


2. Keep it away from your eyes.


3.  Use sunscreen and be very careful going out in the sun after a treatment.





I started slow (2 applications following the instructions) and did not put it ‘near’ my eyes.  I stop at my brow and just below my darks circles.  It burned my skin off as if I was in Chernobyl when it exploded.  It was AWFUL and it took 10 days to heal.  (See pics)





What I realized was with my sensitive skin I really should have put more time between applications.  So once I healed — I then waited 6 days between applications as I had some dryness and a little sensitivity on my cheeks.





I’ve been using the product now for a little over 3 weeks only had 4 applications and I’m already seeing marked improvement.





My “11s” are much less obvious (see pics). My skin looks brighter and healthier.  Lastly it’s a smooth as a baby’s rump — I suspect that has a lot to do with burning off the top 4 layers of skin, but I digress.





This product is intense but effective. It will burn if you don’t ease into using it.  Also — when they say don’t put it on your eyes — what they mean is anywhere near your eyes.  Give a wide birth or else you risk having your face look like mine did. My eyelids were literally burned and I never touched them with the product that’s how strong it is.  Now I keep it focused on my  cheeks and a very tiny amount on the side of my eye for those dreaded crows feet.





Other things of note:  this is part of a very simple routine.


Morning


1. Wash face


2. Use hyaluronic acid serum


3. Use Vitamin C serum





Evening


1. Wash face


2. Use Retinol (slow at first - then work up to the right cadence for you)


3. Use moisturizer





I use Beauty Counter products for the serums I use in the morning.





I use Verse for my sunscreen and moisturizer.





Obviously, PC’s retinol





Cerave Moisturizing Face Wash





I purchased everything except the Beauty Counter products through Amazon.





I love all of the products and will continue using them.





Hope this helps you make good decisions.

reviews.author: Lanni Ames
reviews.text:
PROS: great product! I have large pores, I’m prone to blackheads in my T-zone area, and also have always had horrible redness in my T-zone and within a week of using I noticed a slight difference in the appearance of my pores, my blackheads have gotten a tad better and my redness was almost completely gone. My face looked noticeably brighter and less dull. My husband said my skin was glowing! It’s light on your skin which is great because I have extremely oily skin and most products make me so oily I end up having to wash it off. This however did not which I loved.





CONS: The bottle is sooo small. By the time you start noticing a change it’s empty. I realize there is a bigger bottle but I can’t always afford that one as they are quite pricey.





Overall, I do really enjoy this product. I had no reaction to it and it was my first time using any kind of retinol. FYI, I did put a light moisturizer on right before I applied to create a barrier for the first week or so, after that I started applying without and had no issues. I will continue to use this product as I am pleased with the results but I will branch out and try similar products that may be less expensive 🙂

reviews.author: Alexia Winston
George
reviews.text: If you don't see burning on your face as some people report, that was my case, and then I thought I could apply it under the eyes, wow, don't do it, It will burn for days and will damage that sensitive skin. It won't have that bounce it once had.

reviews.author: Monica R.
reviews.text: After one week, I could see the difference! My pores were smaller my skin looks tighter! A very small quantity, yet last a decent amount of time. Make sure you use moisturizer after applying this product as is strong.I would highly recommend.

couche3.customers_say: Customers find the moisturizer effective and appreciate its silky texture, with one noting it performs as well as high-end products. The cream is fragrance-free and leaves skin feeling moisturized and soft. While some customers report their skin looks fantastic and feels like a baby's butt, others experience redness and breakouts. The product receives mixed feedback about its non-greasy nature and value for money, with some finding it worth the cost while others consider it overpriced. Skin reactions are also mixed, with some customers saying it doesn't bother their sensitive skin while others report burning sensations.

reviews.author: F. Panter
reviews.text: I used to spend 3x the $ on another brand of moisturizer and this works better. Give it a try.  I’m 73 and I see an improvement in my skins smoothness and the dark spots are lighter after using this 4-5 times weekly over the course of a month.

reviews.author: Jennjc
reviews.text: My favorite night cream,been using Olay products for years. They are affordable and easy to apply. This night cream is nice and light and a little goes along way. Makes my skin feel hydrated as it absorbs quickly.

reviews.author: Grisaida Gómez romero
reviews.text: I like Olay night cream with retinol because it leaves my skin softer, hydrated and with a firmer appearance. I use it at night and I feel my face more renewed and luminous.

reviews.author: RPinNC
reviews.text: Really like this cream.  Moisturizes well without being greasy.  My skin feels nice and smooth after application.  It has no fragrance.  This is my go-to night cream.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text: Great skin care product. Rejuvenates the skin.

couche3.note: rétinol 1% rides, hydrate; prudence sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the retinol cream effective, with one reporting dramatic skin improvements and another noting fine lines fade quickly. The product receives positive feedback for its texture, leaving faces smooth and fresh, and one customer mentions it hydrates beautifully. While some customers consider it good value for money, others find it expensive. Skin sensitivity is a concern, with multiple customers reporting irritation.

reviews.author: Poker Panda
reviews.text:
This is the best retinol product I've ever tried and one of the highest quality skincare products I've ever used in my life. It makes my dry skin look and feel softer and smoother. It genuinely works magic on fine lines. However, it runs out quickly and is too expensive to repurchase with any regularity. This allows/forces me to try various other, more affordable quality brands, like Cocokind and Good Molecule.





At every Amazon sale and every holiday, this is the first product page I look at. It is never, EVER on sale. Give me a $20 coupon and I will purchase this product over and over and over, give it as gifts, promote it on my socials, and make product videos and help bring it to a wider audience. However, this isn't the case.





If you're rich and skin-conscious, this product is for you. If not, you can only dream, as it is never affordable to the average American woman. I feel sad because I want to use it in my middle age. Oh, well.🤷‍♀️

reviews.author: C. Taylor
reviews.text: I was really skeptical as about using this. I’m Black and white biracial, so I’m prone to hyperpigmentation, and the only thing that has ever worked is one specific laser. No creams, peels, etc. I’m even going through a different kind of laser now and I feel like that isn’t working. Anyway - back to the retinol. I had a keratosis pop up on my face for the first time ever after vacationing in the Mediterranean with tons of sunscreen. The laser didn’t even remove it. But two weeks of this retinol and it flaked away! I’m also getting amazing comments on how beautiful and glowy my skin looks. This stuff is so worth it. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Kabycat
reviews.text: Love the face product and already seeing results. Also not irritating my highly sensitive skin. Use lotion with it if you need.

reviews.author: CDBrown
reviews.text: This product is very good. It hydrates the facial skin beautifully

reviews.author: Jane Rago
reviews.text: Daughter loves it!
