ingredients: Water, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride, Glycerin, Isododecane, Cetearyl Olivate, Sodium Acrylate/Sodium Acryloyldimethyl Taurate Copolymer, Cetearyl Alcohol, PPG-12/SMDI Copolymer, Sorbitan Olivate, Tocopheryl Acetate, Cyclodextrin, Squalane, Hydroxyethyl Acrylate/Sodium Acryloyldimethyl Taurate Copolymer, Hydroxyacetophenone, 3-O-Ethyl Ascorbic Acid, Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose, Sodium Hyaluronate, Phenoxyethanol, Tetrahexyldecyl Ascorbate, Daucus Carota Sativa (Carrot) Seed Oil, Retinal, Rubus Chamaemorus Seed Oil, Alumina, Isostearic Acid, Lecithin, Polyglyceryl-3 Polyricinoleate, Lonicera Japonica (Honeysuckle) Flower Extract, Lonicera Caprifolium (Honeysuckle) Flower Extract, Polyhydroxystearic Acid, Stearic Acid, Sodium Polyaspartate, Pentylene Glycol, Citric Acid, Polysorbate 60, Disodium EDTA, Ethylhexylglycerin, BHT, Sorbitan Isostearate, Vanilla Planifolia Fruit Extract, Dipteryx Odorata Bean Extract, Coumarin, Red 4, Titanium Dioxide/CI 77891.

couche3.note: rétinal doux, taches/éclat, OK sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers report noticeable improvements within two weeks, including clearer skin and reduced dark spots, with their skin looking brighter and overall tone improving. The serum is non-irritating for sensitive skin, keeps it soft, and customers feel rejuvenated and refreshed every morning. While many find the scent pleasant, opinions about value for money are mixed, with some saying it's worth the price while others find it too expensive.

reviews.author: TheFaceLadyG
reviews.text:
I’m 52 with post-menopausal mature skin, and while I’ve used prescription Retin-A in the past, it always ended up being too irritating for me. I eventually switched to Arazlo (which is amazing), but my insurance stopped covering it — and paying hundreds of dollars out of pocket just isn’t realistic.





So I decided to try Medik8 Crystal Retinal, and I went straight for the strongest version (20), since it’s considered just one step below prescription-strength retinoic acid. I honestly wasn’t sure what to expect, but I’ve been pleasantly surprised.





First, the tolerance:


Even at level 20, I’m experiencing no irritation — no peeling, no redness, no dryness. The formula feels creamy and elegant, not harsh or stripping, which makes it worlds easier to incorporate consistently.





Second, the results:


After consistent nightly use, my skin looks noticeably smoother and more refined. Fine lines look softer, the overall texture looks more even, and everything just feels firmer and more resilient. It truly performs like a high-strength retinoid without the usual misery.





The only downside:


The color is VERY yellow — almost startling at first. The higher the concentration, the deeper the color, so level 20 is intense. But since it’s a nighttime product, it really doesn’t matter, and it hasn’t transferred to my pillow.





If you’ve struggled with irritation from Retin-A or can’t justify the cost of prescription options like Arazlo, this is an excellent alternative. Strong, effective, and surprisingly gentle on sensitive, mature, post-menopausal skin. I’m genuinely impressed.

reviews.author: Cindy N.
reviews.text:
I’ve used retinol for years, and this is one of the best formulas I’ve found for getting results without the irritation. The texture is lightweight, absorbs quickly, and doesn’t leave behind a greasy film. It layers beautifully under moisturizer without pilling, which isn’t always the case with retinol products.





What I love most is that it comes in different strengths, making it easy to choose the right level for your skin or gradually work your way up over time. Even as a veteran retinol user, I appreciate that this formula is effective while still being gentle. I hardly ever experience peeling, excessive dryness, or irritation, and my skin doesn’t feel overly dry the next morning. I also appreciate that there is no fragrance to it as I do tend to be sensitive to strong scents.





With consistent use, I’ve noticed improvements in my skin’s texture, smoothness, and the appearance of fine lines. My overall skin tone feels brighter. It’s gentle enough that you actually stick with it, which is one of the most important factors when using retinol because results come from consistency. I also think it’s a great option for people who have struggled with irritation from stronger retinol products in the past.





The pump packaging works well and dispenses the right amount each time without any issues. While it’s not the least expensive retinol on the market, I think the gentle formula, multiple strength options, and consistent results make it worth the investment. Overall, this has become a staple in my skincare routine. Based on my experience so far, i would purchase this again.

reviews.author: Ashap
reviews.text:
Medik8 Crystal Retinal 3 is hands down one of the best skincare products I’ve ever used. As someone with sensitive skin, I was hesitant to try a retinal product, but this serum exceeded my expectations. It’s gentle enough for beginners while still delivering powerful results.





After just a few weeks of use, my skin looks noticeably brighter and more radiant. Fine lines are less visible, and my complexion feels firmer and smoother. It’s almost like I’ve discovered a real-life filter for my face! The texture of the serum is luxurious, and it absorbs quickly without leaving any greasy residue. Plus, the soothing ingredients prevent any irritation—a huge win for my reactive skin.





I also love how Medik8 incorporates retinaldehyde, a more advanced form of vitamin A, making it more effective than traditional retinols but still easy to use. My skin feels rejuvenated and refreshed every morning, and it pairs beautifully with my moisturizer.





If you’re looking for a high-performance serum to elevate your skincare routine, Medik8 Crystal Retinal 3 is worth every penny. It’s perfect for anyone starting out with retinal or wanting serious results without compromising skin comfort. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Iris Hijar  de Finch
reviews.text: I've been using it for 3 weeks and my skin looks fantastic! I have the 6; I plan to get the 10 and then the 20... I hope they keep producing more...

reviews.author: Carol Randall
reviews.text: I love the product but the tube is always half empty when I get it. It's not a full tube so it's a little deceiving.

couche3.note: rétinal apaisant, texture/éclat, marque sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the retinol cream effective at improving skin texture, leaving it smoother and more radiant with consistent use. The product receives positive feedback for its quality, with one customer noting it's a high-quality retinol product. While some customers find it gentle on sensitive skin, others report it irritates their skin. The fragrance receives mixed reactions, with several customers noting a heavy scent, and opinions on value for money are divided between those who find it a great value and those who consider it pricey.

reviews.author: Sara V.
reviews.text:
I’ve been using this for about 3 months now and I can honestly say I’ve seen a noticeable difference, especially with a dark spot on my nose. It’s still there, but it’s definitely lighter than it was before.





My routine is pretty simple. At night I start with a clean face, then use a pea size amount of Avène Retrinal all over (as shown). I focus a little extra on the dark spot on my nose and right under my eyes for fine lines. After that I go in with a milky toner, then seal everything in with a moisturizer.





*I was convinced I'd use more than a pea size amount, but it's truly all that you need. The moisturizer will help spread it further and seal it is.





The biggest difference I’ve noticed is in the texture and softness of my skin. It just feels smoother overall. The key with this product is consistency. It’s not something you use a few times and expect instant results, but if you stick with it, it works.





In the mornings I usually use vitamin C instead, but I will sometimes still put this on targeted spots. Just make sure you’re following up with sunscreen. This can only do so much if you’re not protecting your skin from the sun.





I’m not even through my first bottle yet and I already bought a second when I saw it on sale because I know I’ll keep using it. It's worth every dollar.





As far as smell, I really don't even notice a fragrance. It's pretty neutral.

reviews.author: Rich S
reviews.text: I’ve been using Avène Retrinal Intensive Multi‑Corrective Cream for about three years. My dermatologist originally recommended it, and it’s been a regular part of my evening washing-up routine ever since. I do think the price is a bit high, which is why I’m giving it four stars instead of five, though buying it on Amazon is cheaper than getting it through my doctor’s office. It's hard to say what the results are from using it. In other words, if it's making my skin look younger. I guess I would only know if I stopped using it which I don't plan to do anytime soon. I do think it makes my skin look healthier. I use it on my forehead, around my eyes and on my nose.

reviews.author: alicia murphy
reviews.text:
This Avène Retrinal Intensive Cream has been such a game‑changer in my routine. The texture is cushioned and creamy, so it never irritates my skin the way other retinoids can. The retinaldehyde is strong enough to make a real difference in texture, fine lines, and overall glow, but still gentle on sensitive or reactive skin.





It absorbs well, layers easily, and leaves my skin looking smoother and more even by morning. If you want real results without the dryness or peeling, this formula is absolutely worth it.

reviews.author: Pia
reviews.text: I have been using this product for approximately three years now and it has definitely stood the test of time for me. I recommend this product highly...and I did do the research on it. It is non-greasy and performs as stated. I use this product at least oncer per day and my skin is firmer and more elastic than ever before.

reviews.author: LH Designs
reviews.text: This is a high quality retinol that I use five or so times a week. I frequently follow up with a session under my red light mask. Avene makes a stellar eye cream, as well: RetrinAL Eyes. I use the two together.

name: PanOxyl Acne Foaming Wash 10% BPO
ingredients: aqua, carbomer homopolymer type C, carbomer interpolymer type A, decyl glucoside, dimethicone, dioctyl sodium sulfosuccinate, glycerin, oleic acid, palmitic acid, polyacrylate crosspolymer-6, polyoxyl 40 stearate, propanediol, silica, sodium chloride, sodium citrate, sodium hydroxide, sodium laurylglucosides hydroxypropylsulfonate, sorbitan stearate, stearic acid, xanthan gum

couche3.note: BPO 10% top acné/grasse; peut piquer (prudence sèche/sensible)
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this acne wash effective, particularly for oily skin, and consider it good value for money. The product receives mixed feedback regarding skin sensitivity, with some saying it doesn't irritate sensitive skin while others report burning sensations. Customers disagree on the cleaning effectiveness, with some reporting clearer skin while others note it bleaches clothes. The fragrance and moisturizing properties also receive mixed reviews, with some appreciating the gentle scent while others find it too strong, and some saying it keeps skin moisturized while others find it drying.

reviews.author: lexy
reviews.text:
Awesome product ONLY IF YOU HAVE SEVERE BREAKOUTS AND OILY SKIN. STAY AWAY IF YOU HAVE SLIGHTLY DRY SKIN AND/OR MINOR BREAKOUTS- not worth the risk.





I used it for back acne and it really helped. DO NOT MIX WITH OTHER ACNE PRODUCTS.





But HUGE HUGE HUGE HUGE WARNING!!!!!——


This is NOT a great play for your face or truly anyone who doesn’t have severe acne and oily skin. Yes, it eliminates acne. If your face is extremely oil and acne prone, every few days would be fine.





I, myself, always thought my face was a mix of oily and dry- but I had a lot of hormonal acne from birth control. At first, I would use this every few days (every other, every 3, depending), and I noticed it improving my facial acne as well. I used it for 3+ months. THEN *TRIGGER WARNING* I suddenly one day broke out in HIVES. It completely STRIPS YOUR SKIN BARRIER IF YOU OVERUSE IT OR IF YOUR SKIN IS NOT SEVERELY BROKEN OUT.  Red, swollen, bumps, all over my face for a week straight. My face burned so bad I couldn’t even cry it hurt. I couldn’t sleep good. Couldn’t touch my face. Couldn’t even put on moisturizer. Not even an ice pack helped. I had to stop hot showers and every single facial product/makeup for multiple weeks to heal my face. I noticed after I stopped using it, it almost made my acne better on my face- like it truly did strip my skin barrier.  I will no longer be putting this on my face UNLESS I have severe acne breakouts, and if that is the case it would be a ONE TIME use. I also DO NOT use it everyday on my back or chest, it’s more of an every other- every few days type of deal.





BE ADVISED- does the job, but can also make things worse.

reviews.author: Emelisse Love
reviews.text: It works amazingly! Where was this when I was 14??? I bought this because I was experiencing insane breakouts from hormones during my time of the month. My entire chin and mouth area was covered in cystic acne that I haven’t had since I was a teen. I started using this and literally only three days later of using this only once a day, the acne is completely gone! It’s crazy how effective this is. It doesn’t foam up very much though, and I’ve noticed my skin is definitely pretty dry which is weird because I have naturally oily skin. It’s a big bottle and will last a while for the price. It’s a pretty medium thickness, almost resembling Elmer’s glue consistency. My skin is not very sensitive but I’ve noticed it’s definitely dry but not too bad where it’s flaking.

reviews.author: Tash
reviews.text: I’ve been using this acne face wash for a little while now and I’m really happy with it. It leaves my skin feeling clean and I’ve noticed fewer breakouts since I started using it. A little goes a long way so the bottle lasts a long time which is a big plus. It works well as part of my daily routine I am definitely purchasing it again.

reviews.author: Nneka
reviews.text: This wash works great for acne-prone skin. After a few weeks of use, I noticed fewer breakouts and smoother skin. It lathers well, cleans thoroughly, and leaves my skin feeling fresh. Just be sure to follow up with a moisturizer since it can be a bit drying. Overall, a very effective acne wash that I would purchase again.

reviews.author: Kristen
reviews.text: Bacne? Say no more.  I did a lot of research and settled on buying this to help my hormonal back acne.  Within a week it was almost completely gone.  No side effects, no gritty feeling.  No scent either.  Just does what it's supposed to do.

name: La Roche-Posay Effaclar (acné)
brand: Factory EU - France COSMETIQUE ACTIVE PRODUCTION

couche3.note: gel acné rapide, léger; prudence sèche
couche3.customers_say: Customers report that the acne treatment works wonders, completely clearing their skin within 2 days. The product receives positive feedback for its texture, with one customer noting its lightweight gel-cream formula that absorbs quickly, and they find it worth the price. While the product is gentle enough for sensitive skin, some customers mention it has a strong scent.

reviews.author: Anthony Colletti Jr.
reviews.text:
La Roche-Posay Effaclar Duo was an absolute game-changer for me. Out of nowhere, I developed painful cystic acne in my twenties, and after trying countless products with little to no success, I was feeling frustrated and discouraged. This product was recommended to me, and it ended up being the one thing that actually worked.





Within a short period of time, I noticed a significant improvement in my skin. The deep, painful cystic breakouts that seemed impossible to get rid of became less frequent and eventually cleared up. Not only did it help treat existing blemishes, but it also seemed to prevent new ones from forming.





Years later, I still keep Effaclar Duo on hand and use it whenever I feel a blemish starting to develop. It continues to work incredibly well and remains one of the few skincare products that has consistently delivered results for me. If you've struggled with stubborn acne and feel like you've tried everything, I can't recommend this product highly enough. It truly changed my skin—and my confidence.

reviews.author: Chia Martxz
reviews.text:
I have sensitive, rosacea, prone skin-slight irritation at first, but it calms and clears my occasional breakouts. I love this product. The texture is light, almost not fragance/scent.





Fast absorption, never clogging, and and keeps great cleanliness on my skin. Amazing value. Reliable, effective, and worth it.

reviews.author: Cherry B.
reviews.text: Official product. Works perfectly for adult acne. I use it in accordance with other la Roche acne products for my skin routine. It has a light fresh scent. It doesn’t leave any sticky residue on the skin. A little goes a long way. So worth the price.

reviews.author: Super bueno
reviews.text: La Roche-Posay Effaclar reduces pimples, blackheads and visible pores with salicylic acid. Its light and non-greasy texture leaves the skin matte and soft, ideal for sensitive

reviews.author: Kamolros Saiworn
reviews.text: Overall, it’s a reliable skincare option if you struggle with acne or oily skin. It may take some time to see full results, but it’s definitely worth trying.

name: Naturium Azelaic Topical Acid 10%
ingredients: Water, Propanediol, Potassium Azeloyl Diglycinate, Polyacrylate Crosspolymer-6, Niacinamide, Ethyl Ascorbic Acid, Caprylyl Glycol, Coffea Arabica (Coffee) Seed Extract, Sodium Hyaluronate, Phenylpropanol, Phenoxyethanol, Citric Acid, Potassium Chloride, Glycerin, Chlorphenesin, Tocopherol, Potassium Sorbate, Sodium Benzoate.

couche3.note: azélaïque rougeurs/taches, doux, top sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective at reducing redness, irritation, and hyperpigmentation, while also improving skin texture and making it look brighter and more glowy. The product receives positive feedback for its quality and value for money, with customers noting it works well under makeup. The fragrance and moisturizing effects receive mixed reviews - while some find the scent tolerable, others describe it as horrific, and while some say their skin feels moisturized, others report dryness.

reviews.author: Alissa
reviews.text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐


I’ve been using this azelaic acid consistently for a few weeks and I’m genuinely impressed with the results. My skin is very sensitive and dry, but also acne-prone postpartum, and this has helped calm redness, smooth texture, and fade stubborn marks without irritating my skin.





The formula feels lightweight and layers well with the rest of my skincare. I also love that it includes niacinamide and vitamin C, so my skin looks brighter and more even overall. Unlike some other azelaic acid products I’ve tried, this one doesn’t pill or leave my face feeling tight and dry.





I noticed my skin looking calmer within the first week, and after continued use my pores and hormonal breakouts around my nose and upper lip have improved a lot. A little goes a long way, so the jumbo size is worth it.





Definitely a staple in my routine now!

reviews.author: Gigi
reviews.text: I read the reviews before buying and there was mentions about this smelling weird and confirm it’s true but I looked it up and it’s cause it’s in its pure form and it’s medical so no fragrances added. This doesn’t sting or burn, I was scared for that but everything was fine my skin reacted good. I like how the bottle is glass like and I’ve seen improvement already since using it. It hasn’t dried my face or made it red but I am using 2-3 times weekly. Good quantity for the price and it’s not heavy on the skin.

reviews.author: Sarah
reviews.text: I first read about azelaic acid on Reddit and thought I’d give it a try and it works! It is a great exfoliant and this product also makes my skin smooth and feels so good after a shower. The ingredients moisturize my skin and it’s never looked so good. My dermatologist also recommends azelaic acid but said this product is better than the prescription because of it’s formula with niacinamide.

reviews.author: Dawoonah Pierre
reviews.text: This azelaic acid serum feels lightweight on the skin and absorbs quickly without leaving a greasy residue. It helps improve the appearance of skin texture and tone while fitting easily into a daily skincare routine. The packaging is convenient, and a little goes a long way. Great quality product.

reviews.author: Edward J Hines
reviews.text: Naturium Azelaic Acid treatment works!!  It fades sun damaged skin and evens out overall tone.  Goes on easily, and dries quickly.  Does not leave a shiny appearance and is not greasy.  I use on my neck and arms, where i had the most sun damage.  Very good product.

name: Murad Rapid Dark Spot Correcting Serum

couche3.note: taches, doux; résultats mitigés
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the serum effective at fading dark spots and appreciate its smooth, silky texture. The product receives positive feedback for its quality, with one customer describing it as a skincare staple. Opinions are mixed regarding effectiveness, value for money, skin irritation, fragrance, and stickiness.

reviews.author: alrightalrightalright
reviews.text:
My skin went through hell last year. It was painful, embarrassing, and left me with tons of hyperpigmentation that wasn’t there before. My skin texture also suffered a bunch. I tried an expensive laser treatment that only made things worse, which left me even more depressed about my skin situation. At this point, I figured I didn’t have anything to lose by trying the “slow and steady” approach. I turned to this hoping it would address the hyperpigmentation problems. This wasn’t the only product I started with - I also added a micellar water to help cleanse my skin beforehand, daily extra strength peel pads by another brand, and a liquid to help clear my pores from another. Let’s just say I didn’t have high hopes going in, especially after shelling out big bucks for someone to shoot lasers at my face, but I figured I had nothing to lose. I think I maybe tried another product or two, but I’ve already forgotten what they are because I quickly determined which ones were working for me. I could tell pretty quickly that this was working for me. I actually made a point to take “before” pictures so that I could take “after” ones so I would be able to tell for myself if anything was working. The pics I’ve added here are almost exactly one month apart, are not edited except to zoom in and black out portions of my face, and are taken in the same position in the same room that only has one light source so the lighting  and everything is as close to the the same as I could get it. I was happily shocked after I started seeing actual results! I was so excited that I could count myself among the people who had good experiences with this product! I used this almost every night of the week, and used it as the final step in my skincare routine.





While I still have a ways to go with my skin, I’m fine with that. I haven’t had any cystic acne since I started this new skincare journey. My skin looks almost normal again. The spots of hyperpigmentation are definitely fading. My skin texture is improving. After I was feeling like this had done everything it could for me, I switched back to two RX creams through a D2C subscription service: one with tretinoin/azelaic acid/niacinimide/tranexamic acid, and the other with hydroquinone/kojic acid/hydrocortisone/azelaic acid. The only thing that I know can’t be helped by topicals is the issue of skin laxity that I think came from the laser treatment (or at least it was made much worse by it), so I would like to try a series of professional microneedling to address that soon.


Anyway, all in all, I think this did exactly what I hoped it would do, and it was worth the investment for me. It’s not cheap, so that alone makes it not for everyone, but if you have similar issues I was having, and you manage your expectations appropriately, *and* are able to afford this, I say don’t hesitate. My only gripe with it is that it is a little sticky, and sometimes I feel like it’s hard to get the past but out of the bottle. While I do love this product and want to cycle it back into my skincare regimen, the price has me looking for alternatives since there is a lot of competition in the marketplace. I’ll edit this in the future if I end up circling back here after an unsuccessful search. However, I personally do not regret starting with this product (and the others I mentioned) in order to get my skin to a good place again. I’m thankful for this product, and it’s good to know it’s there if I should ever need to return to it, though I hope I won’t! Oh - and whether you try this or not, **make sure you use sunscreen every day**!!! That might be one of the single best things you can do for your skin. Whatever you try, good luck! I hope you find what works for your skin!

reviews.author: Nataska U. Walters
reviews.text: It’s working. My sister and I got sun poisoning while we were young on our face.  It is slowly moving a large spot on my face so I recommended it to my sister. Tell the people who shop to please wear Sunscreen all year long. Yes, folks even in the winter and if you are driving. It’s doing a great job on my hands. Yes! Your hands and face tans or burn even when you’re driving. Please wear as much white as you can during the summer. It really deflects the sun rays. Don’t forget the sunglasses. Important. Have a nice day.

reviews.author: Sue1130
reviews.text:
This is the only product I’ve ever used that has actually lightened my dark spots


I have had CO2 laser which was painful and expensive Murad produced similar lightening with no pain and way less expensive yes it works slowly but for me It worked

reviews.author: Gloria Dakroub
reviews.text: I've had a brown spot on my face for years.  However, with Murad used daily, it's barely visibly noticeable.  You have to use daily or the spot will darken rapidly.  Good product but quite expensive.

reviews.author: Nancy
reviews.text: I’ve been struggling with facial hyperpigmentation for 30 years. Thoughthus helped lighten some doors overall not effective after 5 months of consistent wear usage.

name: Aztec Secret Indian Healing Clay
ingredients: 100% pure bentonite clay.

couche3.note: argile forte pores/acné; décape (prudence sèche/sensible)
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the clay mask effective and say it works wonders for their skin, leaving it feeling great and smooth. Moreover, the product reduces pores and helps with acne, while offering good value with plenty of product for the price. However, opinions are mixed regarding skin appearance, with some reporting a glowy effect while others experience redness, and regarding skin sensitivity, with some finding it gentle while others report irritation. Additionally, customers disagree on how easy it is to remove, with some saying it's not hard to rinse off while others find it messy to clean.

reviews.author: Deborah Ippolito
reviews.text:
So, I never ever write amazon reviews BUT I read A TON of reviews on this BEFORE getting it and was a little discouraged. Many people said it made their skin worse and I got very frightened because I have been actively trying to heal some dark scars on my face and close up some pores.  Essentially I want skin that looks healthy enough to not wear makeup. Anyways, as I said, I read a bunch of really good reviews and a bunch of really unhappy reviews and I was hesitant. I have tried white clay masks from Kiehls and that didn’t do much for my skin and I have tried charcoal peel off masks and that didn’t do much either. I have pores that are visible in between my eye brows, on my nose, and chin and I live in NYC so I am ALWAYS aware of the fact that my face gets dirty really fast with the nasty nasty pollution in the air.





So, after a lot of deliberation I figured what the heck, I’ll buy it (because it’s a good price) do it PROPERLY and see what happens.


The first night I did it,  I focused mainly on my trouble spots to see if it would reduce the pores and I did not leave it on for longer than ten minutes (honestly it was probably like under ten), I mixed in a glass bowl with a plastic spoon and Braggs ACV ( absolutely no water). I washed it very gently afterwards (saw only a slight redness) and immediately used some vitamin e oil and very natural lotion to prevent dryness. ( I also MADE sure I had CLEAN hands when I lotioned up my face.)


The next morning the spots on my skin were slightly lighter and my pores were not as visible. I have to admit I often stare at my face in the mirror with the horrible bright light on the maginified side and when I did that my pores looked smaller!! This wasn’t a HUGE difference but there was a change, and it made me happy.





I’ve done it a couple more times (yes I know it’s supposed to be once a week), but I really like it. My skin between my eye brows was a little dry today, but a little lotion fixed that up. There has been no extended amounts of redness or breakouts and overall, I like the results.





I definitely recommend this if you are willing, able and patient to test it and go slow. I did not use it on my entire face until the third time because I wanted to see how my problem areas reacted first. Because I didn’t have any problem with it, I moved on to a full face mask. This isn’t a mask that you can just peel on and off, and yes you do have to make sure that chunks of clay don’t go down your drain because it will harden and clog the drain. It takes a little tender, love and care, but it is totally worth it.





Do not be afraid to try it, for 8 dollars it’s really well priced and has done good things for my skin!!





I hope this helps! ❤️ Happy face mask hunting!

reviews.author: Sean Bozarth
reviews.text:
Aztec Secret Indian Healing Clay is one of those products that’s been around forever for a reason—it really works. I’ve been using this clay mask on and off, and every time I come back to it, I’m reminded why it has such a loyal following.





The clay itself is incredibly pure and simple. You just mix it with apple cider vinegar or water, and it turns into a smooth, spreadable paste. There’s something almost therapeutic about how basic the process is—no fancy additives, no strong fragrances, just straight-up clay that feels like it’s doing real work.





Once applied, you can feel it tightening as it dries, which definitely lets you know it’s active. It’s intense, but not in a bad way. My skin always feels deeply cleaned afterward—pores look smaller, excess oil is reduced, and my face feels refreshed rather than stripped. I especially notice a difference when my skin feels congested or dull.





I also appreciate how versatile this clay is. I’ve used it as a face mask, a spot treatment, and even on my body, and it delivers the same deep-cleaning effect every time. A little goes a long way, so the one-pound container lasts forever, making it a great value.





This isn’t a gentle, daily-use mask, but that’s not what it’s meant to be. It’s more of a reset button for your skin—something you use when you want a serious cleanse. If you’re looking for a natural, no-nonsense mask that leaves your skin feeling truly clean and refreshed, Aztec Secret Indian Healing Clay absolutely earns its reputation.

reviews.author: DaveisHere
reviews.text:
This is my go to (2x weekly)  face mask/bentonite mud treatment.





It's a  pulsating, rejuvenating face mask that will blow your mind, and it's priced economically! A little of this product goes a long way!





Don't order the off-brand names that are cheaper and claim to be something that they clearly are not!





This is the original,  purist, and most trustworthy Bentonite product on the market!


It doesn't stink like swamp gas  (like the cheaper off-brand one I tried)!





If you want the best, buy the best  —  cause the rest don't stack up!  I want to apologize to you, Aztec Secret, I knew the secret was you, it always has been. I strayed away and I'll never do it again.





Mix a small amount of this Aztec "Gold" with Apple Cider Vinegar to form a paste — slather that stuff on your face about 1/4" thick and wait 5-15 minutes! As it dries on your skin you'll  literally feel your face start to tighten up and then you start to feel your face tingle (ooh feels good already just thinking  about it!  The  blood vessels on your face tend to like heat up like a flushing feeling —, blood is rushing to your capillaries for a unique feel! If you have sensitive skin test this somewhere inconspicuous on your body.





I gotta tell you it brings blackheads and impurities that are sitting irght below the surface of your skin to the top.





I sing my praises to thee AZTEC SECRET!  In the future, I willl always buy this and never stray from you again.





Thank you Aztec Secret!!! Love you guys!

reviews.author: Hewitt T
reviews.text:
Pros:


Mama Mia, this mask, like, sucks the stubborn tiny bumps on my oily T-zone like nothing else after the 1st mask, which even months of consistent use of PHA, AHA & BHA wouldn’t achieve. It rinses off very easily with warm water.





Cons:


Hurts like hell, for 6 hours+, even 100% Rose water spray & rose water mask can’t calm my face down. Before 10 minutes is up, the pulsation was too overwhelming to continue so I wash off. My face was not too red but inflamed, like you just had a manual & thorough acne extraction session at a spa. I have super sensitive skin, but decided not to add water to the ACV for optimal results, because I know that would not work on my T-zone bumps. The day after, my skin is showing a kinda reaction similar to the first time I used propolis, which went away after a week: red in some spots, but the whole face looks darkened and not smooth, like there’s a layer of something on top of my skin.





Verdict: Will continue & hope my skin will get used to it. Nothing else has and will ever work such magic on these tiny T-zone bumps, I just know it. However, I suggest if you have very sensitive skin, maybe dilute the mixture with some water for the first few applications, then if you need extra deep cleansing, progress to just ACV.

reviews.author: Cece - Honest & Verified Reviews ✨
reviews.text:
I’ve been using this clay mask for a years now, and it’s one of those products I always keep on hand. It delivers a deep, thorough cleanse that leaves my skin feeling incredibly fresh and smooth after each use. It’s powerful but effective, especially when my body needs a reset. When the kids are sick I put a scoop in their bathtub to detox. We use on any scrapes, wounds and a carbuncles. Try this mother natures earth clay before any prescription meds.





Pros: very deep pore cleansing, simple natural ingredients.


Cons: It is too harsh for the skin on my face





I highly recommend this skincare product & I hope this review is helpful 👍🏻

couche3.note: masque BHA pores/acné, doux
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the mask effective at clearing pores and calming acne conditions, while also appreciating how it leaves skin soft and smooth without causing irritation. Moreover, the product receives positive feedback for its quality. However, customers disagree on the value for money, with some considering it a great value while others say it's not worth the price. Additionally, several customers report receiving the product almost empty.

reviews.author: nicole
reviews.text:
I’ve never done a face mask at home before, but I kept hearing good things about this one—especially for skin that’s a little breakout-prone but still needs something gentle. I wanted to try this before jumping into something more intense like microneedling.





I saw some reviews where people said they broke out badly after using it, so I was super nervous. I tested it first by putting a little on a couple of my breakouts instead of doing my whole face, just to see how it reacted. Thankfully, I had zero issues. It worked great for me.





After using it fully, my skin felt tight (in a good way), smooth, and really clean. I actually really like this product and definitely recommend it if you’re looking to try a mask without going too harsh.

reviews.author: BROOKE
reviews.text:
This stuff has helped me so much. There’s no potent smell, it didn’t cause any skin irritation and believe me I have some sensitive skin. It made my skin feel soft after washing it off, I feel like it’s made my under eyes look semi-brighter.


I also have oil prone skin, this mask has helped me so much with oil control!


I would say get the bigger size though, because the original size comes in a small to medium sized bottle.


Definitely would spend my money on this if I were you!

reviews.author: shakinah
reviews.text: This masque works wonders. This dried up my stubborn jawline breakout. I noticed a difference after just 1 use. Now I use it weekly in my trouble areas to keep my skin clear. Great value for the results. It is easy to apply, dries quickly and rinses off cleanly.

reviews.author: megan sanchez
reviews.text: This mask helped clean out pores, dry up pimples and prevent future breakouts. Using it 2x a week and have noticed such a difference in my acne. This brand doesn't miss, and it's so affordable.

reviews.author: jo
reviews.text: I dont love masks in general, but at least this one does mot get hard and tight. So i like it.

name: Innisfree Super Volcanic Pore Clay Mask

couche3.note: argile volcanique pores, ne dessèche pas, éclat
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this clay mask effective at reducing pores and leaving skin smooth and hydrated without dryness. The mask is easy to remove with cotton pads and dries quickly without feeling too tight, while also brightening complexion and reducing redness. They appreciate that it doesn't irritate sensitive skin and consider it worth the price.

reviews.author: Darkmane
reviews.text:
I did not think I would enjoy this as much as I do. Usually I'm a little wary of mud masks because they dry up the skin a lot. I used to have oily skin as a teen but over the years and pregnancies my skin has become really dry and left enlarged pores.


I needed something that would give me a good clean without drying it out and I'm so surprised at what a wonderful job this does.


After a couple uses I can visibly see cleaner pores on my cheeks. I have not experienced any extra dryness apart from the kind that I already have.





Its fuss free, easy application and removal with a quick glow up!


I absolutely recommend getting this for yourself or for the mom you know who probably hasn't washed her face in a week.

reviews.author: Mary Rae Fouts
reviews.text:
Innisfree Super Volcanic Clay Masks are a must have in my skin care routine.  I have mature (age 60), dry, sensitive skin.  This facial mask always leaves my face with baby smooth skin that is refreshed, more plump, and calm.  My whole body simply feels better!  Incredible quality and consistent spectacular results.





Pro Tips:





- Cleanse your face before applying the mask for best results.


- I  gently remove the mask with warm water and a washcloth, then cleanse my face with a gentle facial cleanser.  Save the volcanic mask water for watering indoor or outdoor plants.  Plants love the volcanic clay minerals!





This mask is a terrific value, too.  I highly recommend this product.

reviews.author: Janelle Ferra
reviews.text:
This mask really lives up to its claims for deep cleansing. My skin feels noticeably smoother, my pores look smaller, and there’s a fresh, clean finish that lasts. The texture spreads easily and dries down quickly without feeling too tight.





That said, it can be a bit drying if you have sensitive or combination skin, so I follow with a hydrating serum or moisturizer. The jar lasts a long time since you only need a thin layer each use.





TL;DR: Excellent for a deep detox and smoother texture — just moisturize after to avoid dryness.

reviews.author: Vee M
reviews.text:
I used to think that the face masks usually just provide a placebo effect, until I tried this face mask. I apply this to all the family members in my house hold and we can see the improvement in everyone skin.


This product is ideal for people who wants to reduce or minimize skin pores, I use it twice a week for approximately 7 minutes per session and I have seen a very noticeable improvement in my skin.


Light texture, light smell and very easy to remove

reviews.author: JB
reviews.text: Good clay mask. Doesn’t strip my skin. Makes my skin glow and feels and looks tighter. Get it.

couche3.note: charbon pores; peut dessécher

Moreover, the mask dries quickly and is easy to wash off, leaving skin feeling smoother. However, opinions on value for money are mixed, with some finding it worth the price while others consider it expensive.

reviews.author: Nataliia Krainyk
reviews.text: This mask really does draw out impurities – my T-zone looks cleaner and pores seem smaller after just a few uses. The texture is smooth and feels refreshing when applied. It doesn’t leave the skin feeling too tight, as long as you moisturize afterward. However, if you have sensitive skin, leave it on for less time or use it less often. Worth the price for the results

reviews.author: katie pitcock
reviews.text: I bought this item for the first time because my other charcoal mess that I use got discontinued. I was having an active continued breakout during my pregnancy and needed something to heal that and this stuff worked. This has truly been amazing and I love it. Super easy to apply, then it dries pretty quickly and it's easy to wash off. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: Kristen Nelson
reviews.text: I have loved this face mask for 10+ years. I struggle with acne and when it gets bad, this mask helps so much! It calms any inflammation I have while also being gentle on my sensitive skin.

reviews.author: pmw
reviews.text: I like Origins Charcoal Face Mask.  It is a bit expensive, but I space it out to make it last.

reviews.author: CHREAmazon Customer
reviews.text: Great product. Good price.

ingredients: Water, Dipropylene Glycol, Propanediol, Ceratonia Siliqua (Carob) Gum, XanthanGum, Chondrus Crispus Extract, Chlorphenesin, PEG-60 Hydrogenated Castor Oil, Phenoxyethanol, Potassium Chloride, Fragrance, Disodium EDTA, Hydrolyzed Hyaluronic Acid, Tremella Fuciformis Sporocarp Extract, Plukenetia Volubilis Seed Oil, Sucrose Cocoate, Tocopherol, Blue 1

The adhesiveness and ease of use receive mixed reviews - while some say it adheres well and is easy to use, others report it slides off and find it difficult to apply.

reviews.author: amilee
reviews.text:
I order 12. I have ordered 12 many times now. I received  one. Again.  Again.





I love these masks. I pay for 12 yet again because I want them. And again, I am sent one.





And for a return I have to spend half an hour driving across town.





Once was fine- twice I could understand. We all make mistakes. And now I have ordered 12 face masks many times  because I love this product.





I assumed the company would get its act together.


They haven’t. I want this . I love these face masks。 I can’t order them because when I buy 12, I ALWAYS receive 1.





Then I ask for a replacement and get the full 12x and if I don’t spend my time and energy to return the single mask, which takes a lot of my time, I then sm charged twice.





Why can the company send correct replacements every time and fail to send my initial order correctly every time?  Because they get paid twice when O do t have time to drive thei single mask they send me?





I haven’t found a better product. And I am done I assumed that ordering would get better. And  I have now received one  face mask while ordering and being charged for 12, so many times that I’m out over $200 and I hold active hate for Neutrogena.





And I love these face masks. I eod have been a repeated and loyal customer and I gave them so many chances to just send what I purchased from them.





I’m disappointed red that I can’t get their masks. I’m angry that I gave them the benefit of the doubt. And I’m angry that I have spent t my time and money either returning the single mask or not having the energy or time to return it and then being charged  for the (suspiciously always correct) 12 pack.





Ordering something I want is not possible . Unless I am willing to spend half an hour returning g it and asking for a replacement EVERY time I try to order these.





Why?!?

reviews.author: DK33
reviews.text:
I never ever write reviews. Ever. But this is the best face mask I have ever used (and I’ve tried many including expensive products that aren’t all they are hyped up to be)! Seriously, this one is so soothing and hydrating. I have dry skin and use a prescribed retinoid not to mention live in a super dry climate and this mask made me glow and really cut down on the peeling I’ve been having trouble keeping at bay lately. And that was after only one use.





The only complaint I have is about the delivery and not the product itself. I ordered the 12 pack  with my Amazon Fresh order but only received a single mask. I called Amazon customer service though telling them I wanted to keep the single but didn’t want to pay for 12 when I only got one.  They just refunded me without making me drive somewhere to return the one I received which I did appreciate. I just wish they sold the 12 packs for delivery instead of just through Fresh as now I want more but don’t need groceries and don’t want to pay the $9.70 delivery fee for one Fresh product. On Amazon through regular Prime you can order them but they cost more and I can only find a pack of 10 not 12.

reviews.author: Just my opinion
reviews.text:
This mask is cut in half with a top half and lower half.  It is a gel or like a jelly feel.  A con of the mask is it is too small for my face.  The edges on my forehead, cheeks, and jawline do not get the mask coverage.  A plus of the mask is that it is non-comedogenic so it doesn’t clog your pores.  It moisturizes the are it covers.  It is somewhat difficult to apply but I’m still learning how to apply this cut in half and slippery mask.





There is another mask on Amazon that I love but it’s not non-comedogenic so it could flog my pores.  It is one piece and easy to apply as well as covers my whole face.  So I just have to decide if this mask’s negative attributes are worth it being non-comedogenic.





I might go back to the mask that is not non comedogenic and use pore stripping strips.  I will use all 12 of these gel masks and see if I notice a better outcome than when I used the other mask.  It will be a hard decision for me.  I don’t want my pores to clog up and get big.  But I’d like my whole face covered by the mask also.  I will decide on the best moisturizing performance of the 2 masks.

reviews.author: C. M.
reviews.text: These are a great value for the money! They are comparable to much more expensive masks. They were recommended to me by a Hollywood makeup artist and I have been buying them ever since. They are extremely hydrating and non-irritating and leave a nice glow. I have dry, sometimes sensitive, skin and have always had a very good experience with these. They do not leave a sticky residue and have a very generous amount of serum in the packets. You could even use them twice by putting the mask in a plastic baggy with the leftover serum. It can be a little tricky to get them off of the backing and onto your face, but with a little practice, it is no problem. They don't completely cover my face so I just use some of the extra serum on the places the mask doesn't reach like the skin by my ears and around my nose. Of course I look silly with the mask on and scare my dog, but that is the price of beauty! ;-)

reviews.author: Bea
reviews.text: LOVE these for throwing in a summer bag. They are WET an amazing and such great value for the price. My dermatologist suggested this brand for their moisturizers, so I’m experimenting with other products and this is such a great summer / night time pick me up. Leaves your skin hydrated and plump and glossy!!!

couche3.note: masque nuit hydratant léger, tous types
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the sleeping mask hydrating, with one noting it absorbs well without leaving an oily residue. The product receives positive feedback for its texture, with one customer describing it as a lightweight gel that feels refreshing, and its effectiveness in making skin look better and illuminating. They appreciate its suitability for sensitive skin and pleasant fragrance, while also praising its smooth application.

reviews.author: Carolyn C.
reviews.text:
This sleeping mask has become a staple in my nighttime skincare routine. The lightweight gel texture feels incredibly refreshing and absorbs beautifully without leaving my skin sticky or greasy. I apply a thin layer before bed, and by morning my skin looks noticeably more hydrated, plump, and radiant.





The formula provides deep, long-lasting moisture thanks to the squalane and probiotic-derived complex, and it really helps support my skin barrier during dry or stressful weeks. I’ve noticed fewer dry patches and a smoother overall texture after consistent use.





A little goes a long way, so the jar lasts much longer than expected and offers great value for the price. It layers well over the rest of my skincare products and hasn’t caused any irritation or breakouts on my sensitive skin.





If you’re looking for an easy overnight treatment that delivers softer, healthier-looking skin by morning, this mask is absolutely worth trying. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Mario
reviews.text: I’m very tactile and I like this product so much, that I tried their other products. I did not like their other products that much. But this one’s still works so well. I like the way my skin absorbs it without feeling oily, but does feel very moisturized. I don’t know if I use it the right way because it’s supposed to be some sort of sleep mask, but I just use it like a moisturizing cream. I love the way it smells so fresh and airy, but mostly I like how it absorbs in my skin. I find that a lot of other moisturizing cream seem to be very oily and take a long time to be absorbed, but this one always feels like such a luxury every time I put it on. To me finding that kind of quality is worth the price, which I do find to be a lot of money, but worth it.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text:
The LANEIGE Water Sleeping Mask has a cool, lightweight gel texture that feels instantly soothing as it sinks in. I wake up to skin that looks refreshed, soft, and smooth without any greasy residue. The squalane, hyaluronic acid, and probiotic-derived ingredients really boost hydration and help keep my skin barrier calm and balanced.





It’s perfect for normal or combination skin and gives that healthy, well-rested glow overnight. I think it’s an excellent value for the results and quality. I truly love Laneige products.





Thank you for reading my review :)

reviews.author: April
reviews.text: I truly love this stuff. It’s better on my face than the rose one. Leaves me baby soft too! Will repurchase!

reviews.author: Korin
reviews.text: Great Clinique moisture surge dupe! So smooth and great for sensitive skin.
