ingredients: Active: Benzoyl Peroxide 2.5%: Acne Treatment. Inactive: Water, Bentonite, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride, Glycerin, Emulsifying Wax Nf, Polysorbate 20, Glyceryl Laurate, Cetyl Dimethicone, Magnesium Aluminum Silicate, Xanthan Gum, Sodium Citrate, Disodium Edta, Citric Acid, Methylparaben, Propylaraben.

couche3.note: BPO spot rapide, doux sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the acne spot treatment effective, working almost instantly and clearing pimples within 48 hours, while being gentle on sensitive skin and not causing irritation. The product is easy to use and offers good value, with one customer noting it layers well under moisturizer or makeup. Customers disagree on the product's stain resistance, with some reporting it can stain pillowcases and towels. The product's duration receives mixed feedback, with some saying it lasts a long time while others find it doesn't last very long.

reviews.author: Alina Demydko
reviews.text: I’ve been using this acne cream for a few weeks and I’m very happy with the results. It helped reduce redness and breakouts without drying out my skin too much. The texture is lightweight, absorbs quickly, and doesn’t leave a greasy feeling. My skin looks clearer and smoother, and new pimples seem to heal faster. I would definitely recommend it to anyone struggling with mild to moderate acne.

reviews.author: Vivian SB
reviews.text: This is the best acne spot treatment I’ve ever used. I always keep a fresh tube on hand. It works quickly and the 2.5% benzoyl peroxide is effective but a lot gentler than most acne products. The tube is designed with a small opening which is great for dispensing only the tiny amount needed to spot treat. Follow directions, be patient and you’ll be very satisfied with the results.

reviews.author: Barbie
reviews.text: This stuff works well on my breakouts. I use it mostly as a spot treatment on my highly sensitive face and I like it a lot. I’m hesitant to put things on my face but this is a product I will keep using. It seems to minimize my breakouts quickly without drying out my skin.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text: Works fast and effectively! It reduces redness overnight and helps prevent new breakouts. The cream absorbs quickly and doesn’t dry out my skin. A must-have for anyone managing acne.

reviews.author: MSBrown
reviews.text: Works well not harsh on skin. Takes longer to work compared to a higher concentration of benzoyl. With consistent use you see improvement.

name: Clinique Acne Solutions Clinical Clearing Gel

couche3.note: gel BHA spot; irritation variable
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the acne treatment effective, particularly as a quick spot treatment, with one mentioning it helps calm and shrink pimples over time. The product receives mixed feedback regarding skin irritation, with some customers reporting that it burns their skin.

reviews.author: Daniel
reviews.text: The Clinique Acne Solutions Clinical Clearing Gel is like the overzealous law-and-order officer in your skincare routine — it means business when it comes to blemishes. Packed with acne-fighting salicylic acid and soothing witch hazel, this clear gel zips into pores to help dissolve oil and dead skin that lead to breakouts while keeping things lightweight and non-greasy. I find it helps calm and shrink pimples over time without turning your face into the Sahara, though a bit of dryness or tightness can occur (so moisturize like it’s your job). It isn’t some magical overnight miracle, but with regular use it works hard at clearing and preventing spots without feeling like a sticky science experiment.

reviews.author: Evelyn T.
reviews.text: I’ve used this since I was a teen & am now 65+, it is the best treatment for blemishes (yes, older people do get zits too!).  It does dry the skin out a bit, but can be used under a moisturizer and makeup.  No scent.  Hypoallergenic, no animal testing.  It is effective in drying up zits, easy to apply .  It does cost a bit much, but it’s the best I have ever found.  My son who is 32 has his own tube & has been using since he was a teen.  It does not irritate the skin after shaving.

reviews.author: Liz
reviews.text: Not just great for hyperkeratotic acne, this is a vital product for other hyperkeratotic conditions including some types of psoriasis (but talk to your dermatologist about this, of course). I guess Clinique got so many complaints when they discontinued it that they brought it back. Good for Clinique!!! Now they just need to bring back the 2% salicylic acid cover product. (And sell the gel in larger amounts.)

reviews.author: Rahema C Senegal
reviews.text: My daughter likes it. Works well, but be careful not to use it too much, it may damage your skin barrier if you leave it on your skin to the point where it burns. (That doesn’t mean it’s working!) :)

reviews.author: SotRH
reviews.text: Is really good for acne and clears it up fast for those small breakout we women get. They need to make a body wash out of this stuff. I purchase this frequently when I get low. ( Mrs. Sotrh)

reviews.author: LL
reviews.text:
Works amazingly well to absorb, calm, and heal acne blemishes.  The day version is much less noticeable to the eye for daily use.





I find the standard healing dots work better for overnight use as they absorb even more but are definitely more visible to the eye.

reviews.author: Ryder Mac
reviews.text: Works like magic! If I have a pimple starting I’ll put one of these on at night and by morning it’s gone or almost gone depending on the size. Mon irritating to my skin. They stick well.

reviews.author: Brandi Norris
reviews.text: These things work miracles! I haven’t bad inflammatory acne and these get rid of them or bring them to a head with a quickness.

reviews.author: PhiloGal
reviews.text: This product is a game-changer. If you ever get some hormonal breakouts, do NOT sleep on these! Fantastic product.

reviews.author: Jessy
reviews.text: Worked, barely can see it. I was skeptical but i will buy again

couche3.note: soufre boutons overnight; assèche (prudence sèche/sensible)
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the acne treatment very effective, noting it works overnight. The product's drying properties receive mixed feedback - while some customers say it's not overly drying, others mention it tends to dry out the skin pretty good.

reviews.author: Courtney
reviews.text:
After coming off of birth control (which I'd been on for 15+ years), I began struggling with acne for the first time in my life. I'm not talking about a few breakouts, which are frustrating in their own right. I'm talking about full blown acne, all across my forehead, my jawline, my neck, and my chest. I knew it would take a while for my hormone levels to even out and my skin to go back to normal, but after two months of this acne, I was feeling pretty low. I didn't want to even leave my house to get the mail - I just felt so self-conscious.





I can't say how well this treatment works long term yet, because I only just started using it. No, it did not eliminate all of my acne overnight, but the difference it made was immediately noticeable the next day. My pimples were smaller, and some of them had come to a head, a sure sign that this treatment was fighting them. My chest acne was nearly gone, which made me cry of sheer happiness. I still have raised acne and it didn't help much with discoloration (but I'm extremely pale and was not expecting a miracle), however it's the first treatment that has actually made any difference. I've tried everything - washing my face with oatmeal and honey, charcoal cleansers, and oil cleansing. I've also used spot treatments including one from Origins and a tea tree spot treatment for Lush. No change in over a month.





Now, I'm oil cleansing twice a week, using a skin cleanser fro Lush (Aqua Marina, I believe), and a tea tree toner sparingly (I have DRY skin), and then applying this treatment. I do use moisturizer and sometimes oil as moisturizer, but this has not seemed to help or hurt. I can write back to let you guys know how long it takes for my skin to clear with this, to give any acne sufferers out there some realistic expectations.





Please keep in mind, I know my acne is hormonal, but it hasn't budged in months. If your acne isn't hormonal or stress-related, I can't claim this will work. Many of the cleansers/treatments I tried were rumored to be HOLY GRAIL treatments and they did absolutely nothing. Everyone's skin is different, but if you've tried everything else, give this a shot.

reviews.author: Anthony Haer
reviews.text: Works wonderfully to dissolve pimples in a day or two, I use it overnight as I sleep and usually see a reduction by the next morning! It does have a strong sulfur smell, but it’s sulfur, that’s kind of hard to avoid. I’ve been using this item for years and it’s something I always order and keep on hand.

reviews.author: Tekoah
reviews.text: Great product. I suffer from hormonal acne at 48 years old and recently discovered sulfur and thought I would try this. It is amazing at clearing up old acne and preventing new acne. It’s a little pricey but FSA/HSA covers it so it’s not so bad. I will be purchasing more for sure

reviews.author: Beka
reviews.text: This stuff is incredible! It visibly reduced the size of my pimple overnight. It was a little drying on my sensitive skin, but after some moisturizer I was good to go!

reviews.author: Shannon
reviews.text: This product works very well and noticeably clears my skin overnight.

name: Hero Cosmetics Micropoint for Blemishes
ingredients: Sodium Hyaluronate, Water, Niacinamide, Butylene Glycol, Salicylic Acid, Salix Alba (Willow) Bark Extract, Madecassoside, Centella Asiatica Extract, Pentylene Glycol, 1,2-Hexanediol, Sodium Dehydroacetate.

reviews.author: Ash
reviews.text:
These pimple patches are a must for those with hormonal/cystic acne. They are specifically designed to get rid of those deep, painful spots that never come to the surface to pop— you know the ones I’m talking about!





The material is sticky enough that it’s not going to move while you are sleeping. Though they are slightly bulky in size and thickness, so definitely more effective for night wear. And yes, they WORK! Those large, agitating cysts go down usually 50-70% in size with one use, and then completely gone with a second patch. No more picking, scarring, and headaches. Yes they are pricey, but the quality of these microderm patches compared to competitors has me coming back to these again and again

reviews.author: Sprklindiamond
reviews.text: I’ve been very impressed with this blemish patch for early stage blemishes. I have somewhat sensitive skin, and I didn’t have any issues with pain or discomfort when using these. I typically use these overnight, and it seemed to help prevent further development of  blemishes, and redness/swelling was even reduced. I wasn’t bothered by the micro points at all. I could feel them upon application, but it wasn’t painful and gave me the impression that they were working.

reviews.author: oignonne
reviews.text: They work pretty well at absorbing things and helping penetrate beneath the skin. They are better than any other patch I’ve tried, though sometimes do still have issues with sticking well or working well without pre-popping.

reviews.author: Gaelle
reviews.text: Very easy to use and super effective. They have great adhesiveness, so once they’re on they won’t just fall off. I just wish they were more affordable, as a price is a little steep for the amount of patches you get.

reviews.author: Rachel Eddinger
reviews.text: SAVING GRACE!! Any deep pimples or HS spots have flatten by the next morning using one of these. Worth the price. I will always continue to purchase!

name: TruSkin Vitamin C Serum
ingredients: INCI FORMAT: Water (Aqua), Organic Aloe Barbendensis (Aloe Vera) Leaf Juice Powder, Sodium Ascorbyl Phosphate (Vitamin C), Hamamelis Virginiana (Witch Hazel) Water, Dimethyl Sulfone (MSM), Cassia Angustifolia Seed Polysaccharide (Botanical Hyaluronic Acid Alternative), Tocopheryl Acetate (Vitamin E), Glycerin (Vegetable Source), Carbomer, Arginine, Centella Asiatica (Gotu Kola) Extract, Equisetum Arvense (Horsetail) Extract, Taraxacum Officinale (Dandelion) Extract, Sodium Hyaluronate (Hyaluronic Acid), Geranium Maculatum (Geranium) Extract, Simmondsia Chinensis (Jojoba) Seed Oil, Phenoxyethanol, Ethylhexylglycerin, Sodium Hydroxide (pH adjuster)

couche3.note: vit C best-seller taches/éclat; certains desséchant (prudence sèche)
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this vitamin C serum to be a fabulous product that delivers visible improvements in skin texture and appearance. The serum works quickly and helps fade dark spots, with customers noting their skin looks more radiant and youthful with a beautiful glow. While some customers report their skin feels smooth and hydrated, others mention it can be drying. Customers consider the product well worth its price.

reviews.author: Bethany
reviews.text:
Update -


So I bought my second bottle , it was so good & I don’t ever get something twice because I love trying new things but this was an exception! The first serum was a 2oz bottle & I don’t recommend doing that size because vitamin C oxidizes pretty quickly, so the 1 oz is perfect so you get the highest quality in my opinion. I had started using another vitamin C for a little while & before I noticed my complexion had significantly changed , it wasn’t as bright & my sun spots wasn’t fading & I realized it was because I wasn’t using the Truskin vitamin C & that Was the difference ! This will forever be in my Rotation - even if I try new ones because that’s what I do lol , this will definitely have its place & I won’t be without ever again !! It’s one of the very few that made such a difference, I personally don’t like products that use droppers so I am going to put this in a pump bottle for better quality… because this is one you just can’t beat as far as dewy , fresh looking skin that actually fade’s hyperpigmentation ❤️❤️


So far my favorite vitamin C serum , and I have tried several ! This one hydrates your skin without causing any irritation ! It glides on so smooth & soaks into your skin . My stubborn dark spots are fading & my overall complexion is so dewy ! I do recommend getting the 1ounce  bottle instead of the bigger one because vitamin C oxidizes Rather quickly & it’s in a dropper instead of a pump nozzle.Next time I will probably put it into a pump bottle instead of using the dropper to make it stay fresh longer . I have used this several months now & I'm about to get my second bottle . Also , I did use Medicube vitamin C capsule moisturizer over this serum during my morning routine every other day  & they go nice together. I had zero irritation as well .

reviews.author: Jessica
reviews.text: I’m not a skin care person because I’m lazy and could never get myself to commit to a routine, but this serum has me hooked. It makes my skin feel so smooth, soft, and refreshed. It dries quickly and is not oily. This is the only serum I can see myself consistently using long term. I’m on my second bottle and think I notice a difference. I’m 39 and naturally look younger, plus I am vegan (which is why I also love that this formula uses plant based ingredients and doesn’t test on animals), but I have these sun spots and my skin was feeling dull and dry. This serum makes my skin feel rejuvenated and it does seem to be lightening my sun spots a little. I am hoping that that with long term use the sun spots will mostly go away.

reviews.author: Mayowa O.
reviews.text:
The results are unbelievable, Was a bit skeptical because all the reviews were from Caucasian women. I was slightly worried that my skin would not respond as well but I was quite mistaken.


With or without makeup, my skin is bright, smooth and my spots are disappearing.


One other result i am sure a number of people have not noticed is that your palms get softer too, i guess the residue on the palms makes it soft and supple.

reviews.author: Maine-iac
reviews.text: I have very sensitive skin and this serum did not make me red and dry.  It seems to have lightened several age spots on my face.  I do note, that I am not having as many breakouts as before.  These reviewers ask for a review long before you can tell if a product is working or doing the job that it is touted to do, so that Is why I have only given it 4 stars.  It may be a five later on . . . time will tell.

reviews.author: Jennifer
reviews.text: I find this gentle on my skin and have noticed my skin seems brighter. Dark spots lighter. I use on my face, neck and chest. No harsh smells. Not sure if any reduction in lines or wrinkles but feel my skin looks better. Price is very reasonable.

name: Heimish All Clean Balm
ingredients: Ethylhexyl Palmitate, Cetyl Ethylhexanoate, PEG-20 Glyceryl Triisostearate, Polyethylene, PEG-8 Isostearate, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter, Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Fruit Extract, Citrus Aurantifolia (Lime) Fruit Extract, Freesia Refracta Extract, Iris Versicolor Extract, Jasminum Officinale (Jasmine) Extract, Lilium Tigrinum Extract, Leontopodium Alpinum Extract, Nelumbium Speciosum Flower Extract, Narcissus Pseudo-Narcissus (Daffodil) Flower Extract, Rose Extract, Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Peel Oil, Lavandula Angustifolia (Lavender) Oil, Pelargonium Graveolens Flower Oil, Amyris Balsamifera Bark Oil, Citrus Paradisi (Grapefruit) Peel Oil, Eucalyptus Globulus Leaf Oil, Melaleuca Alternifolia (Tea Tree) Leaf Oil, Juniperus Mexicana Oil, Boswellia Carterii Oil, Citrus Aurantium Bergamia (Bergamot) Fruit Oil, Tocopheryl Acetate, 1, 2-Hexanediol.

couche3.note: baume top démaquillage, hydratant, doux sensible
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this cleansing balm effective at removing even waterproof mascara and appreciate its lovely mild scent. The product leaves skin clean, hydrated, and moisturized, while being gentle around the eyes and non-irritating for sensitive skin. Moreover, they praise its creamy texture and ease of removal, noting it dissolves makeup without leaving residue. Additionally, customers consider it good value for money, comparing favorably to cleansing oils.

reviews.author: Jacky Z
reviews.text:
This is the best cleansing balm I've ever tried. Hands down. The absolute tiniest amount spread effortlessly over the skin, just straight up melting my most stubborn waterproof mascara with zero scrubbing. It also left my skin feeling so soft and hydrated. Absolutely fantastic product.





But there was just one little fly in the ointment (ha ha). The scent is not my favorite and it's just SO STRONG. It was pretty overwhelming at first, especially the eucalyptus,  which is of course the part I actually dislike. And it lingered for a while after too, maybe half an hour, definitely longer than I would've liked. I'm no anti-scent fanatic either, I really love a good scented soap or cleanser. I almost didn't knock off the one star, but I'd be lying if I didn't say the smell really hurt what would've otherwise been a sublime experience.





Don't get me wrong though, I'm absolutely gonna be repurchasing this in the future. Scent or no, until I find something else that works this well I'm sticking with it. But next time I'm getting the mandarin scented version, even if it takes longer to ship.

reviews.author: Jill
reviews.text: Love, love, love this balm.  I've tried several other balms that were fine, but this is my favorite.  It's gentle and has clean ingredients, removes waterproof makeup & sunscreen without having to rub or tug, doesn't cloud or irritate the eyes, and a little goes a very long way - my daughter & I use it daily and a jar lasts us about two months - so it's worth every penny.  I'm sensitive to scents and this one is *extremely* light and rinses completely away.  I have dry/sensitive skin, my daughter has combo skin, and our faces feel clean but not stripped after using this.  It's also a great first step if you double cleanse.  Just an excellent product, highly recommend.

reviews.author: RDSunset
reviews.text: I like simple face cleansing routines and this balm does it. It’s easy to apply, just dip your fingers in the pot ( not too much) and rub into your skin and even over your eyes. No stinging and it is easily removed with water. ( Removes all my makeup but I don’t use heavy foundation. ) My skin feels clean but not taut after use. I highly recommend.

reviews.author: J
reviews.text: Facial balm cleanses and refreshes and is gentle to skin.  Balm effectively removes make up.  Balm has a pleasing texture and is non-greasy.

reviews.author: Beth D
reviews.text: Removes makeup. Smells great. Removes with water/ Easy peasy.

name: La Roche-Posay Mela B3 Dark Spot Serum
brand: Factory EU - France COSMETIQUE ACTIVE PRODUCTION

couche3.note: taches, doux sensible; résultats variables
couche3.customers_say: Customers give positive feedback about the serum's texture, noting it makes skin feel smooth and leaves a noticeable difference, while also appreciating its smooth application and gentle formula for sensitive skin. The product receives mixed reviews for its effectiveness in treating dark spots, with some reporting improvement while others see no change, and opinions vary on whether it's worth the price. The fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some finding it fantastic while others note a strong scent. Skin sensitivity experiences are also mixed, with some customers reporting no irritation while others experienced redness and irritation.

reviews.author: NWSwallow
reviews.text:
Magical.





I recently saw a dermatologist regarding my middle aged face.





She sent me out the door with a few samples of this and a few samples of sunscreens.





Loved them all.  But this.  Wow.  If you are treating your skin with prescriptions that cause redness or dryness but you must push through to see the results you want- use this too!  Cicaplast B5 and this magical serum will have your skin feeling soft, smooth, moisturized and a whole lot happier.





Initially, smoothing this into my skin feels luxurious and lovely.  The serum moves easily, absorbs and feels hydrating.  Immediately, my skin is happier.  It’s even happier 20 minutes later.  I put this on while getting ready for bed and in the morning after washing my face.  1 week in and I think there is a visible difference.





I am using four new products on my face to help with the case of Getting Old and this is one of them.  Cicaplast B5, a sunscreen and face wash- from this brand, La Roche-Posay.  I never tried them till the dermatologist recommended and this brand seems fantastic.





The dermatologist I saw was younger than me but her skin was amazing.  She glowed and her skin was translucent and utterly blemish free.  I was listening to everything she said and La Roche-Posay was her favorite brand for herself and to recommend.  What else does she do to keep her skin supple and radiant?  I should have asked!





My skin has improved and I am buying another bottle for a friend and probably for my Mother.





I do have sensitive skin (Oil of Olay moisturizer used to turn my face red and burn like fire).  La Roche Posay products have been excellent and irritant free.  Recommended!  Try it. It’s good.


(I have tried Chanel’s La Blanc line.  Lancome.  Philosophy.  Murad.  I like the La Blanc face wash and everything Shiseido.  Everything else was forgettable and ineffective.)





The La Roche Posay products I have used make me curious to try more of their products.

reviews.author: Kenia gonzalez
reviews.text:
I have to share this with you!!!!!. This is my 5th bottle, and I can honestly say I’m beyond impressed with the results. At first, I had my doubts, but this product has truly exceeded my expectations.


I’ve seen amazing improvements, especially on my décolletage area, which used to be one of my biggest insecurities. The appearance of dark spots and uneven tone has consistently improved over time, and my skin now looks much more even and radiant.


I use it every night before going to bed, and on alternating days I also apply it in the morning, always followed by sunscreen. This routine has made a huge difference.


The texture is silky and not sticky at all, and it has a very delicate, pleasant scent, which makes it even more enjoyable to use.


If you’re dealing with sun spots or discoloration like I was, this product is definitely worth trying. I’m extremely happy with the results and will absolutely keep repurchasing!

reviews.author: ANGIE
reviews.text: Dermatoligist recommend, so I've been using for a few weeks two times a day. I haven't really noticed much, maybe a little lighter dark spots, that's why only 4 stars.  It doesn't irritate my skin, not itchy or sticky. Just a few drops and it absorbs quickly. It does not protect against the sun or moisturize so after applying Mela B3 to clean skin you will need to apply moisturizer and sun screen, just as well because I like my brand now. I will continue to use it to 1-see if dark spots will lighten as promised and 2-to keep new ones from appearing.

reviews.author: Chad R Gaskey
reviews.text:
This serum is amazing! I noticed visible decrease in my dark spots after a month of use. The key is to maintain consistency - ensure that you incorporate into your daily routine and stick with it!





It does not have a bad odor like some of the other products I’ve tried. It takes a little time to dry but does not leave your face feeling greasy.





In my opinion this is a fabulous option for those looking to fade their dark spots!

reviews.author: Kamolros Saiworn
reviews.text: Gentle and lightweight serum that helps brighten skin and fade dark spots over time. Great for sensitive skin and daily use, but results are gradual.

name: CeraVe Skin Renewing Night Cream
ingredients: AQUA / WATER / EAU, GLYCERIN, CAPRYLIC/CAPRIC TRIGLYCERIDE, BUTYROSPERMUM PARKII BUTTER / SHEA BUTTER, DIMETHICONE/VINYL DIMETHICONE CROSSPOLYMER, CYCLOPENTASILOXANE, GLYCERYL STEARATE SE, CETYL ALCOHOL, DIMETHICONE, SACCHARIDE ISOMERATE, STEARIC ACID, PALMITIC ACID, GLYCINE SOJA STEROLS / SOYBEAN STEROLS, ALLANTOIN, CERAMIDE NP, CERAMIDE AP, CERAMIDE EOP, CARBOMER, NIACINAMIDE, CETEARYL ALCOHOL, BEHENTRIMONIUM METHOSULFATE, SODIUM HYDROXIDE, MYRISTIC ACID, SODIUM LAUROYL LACTYLATE, SODIUM BENZOATE, SODIUM CITRATE, SODIUM HYALURONATE, CHOLESTEROL, PHENOXYETHANOL, TOCOPHEROL, TRIPEPTIDE-1, LAURETH-9, CITRIC ACID, CAPROOYL TETRAPEPTIDE-3, BIOSACCHARIDE GUM-1, PHYTOSPHINGOSINE, XANTHAN GUM, DEXTRAN, ETHYLHEXYLGLYCERIN, BUTYLENE GLYCOL

couche3.note: Creme de nuit peptides+ceramides, reparation barriere, sans parfum; ideale seche/sensible, un peu riche pour peau grasse.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this night cream moisturizing and effective, particularly for dry skin and eczema, with a luxurious feel that's not greasy. They appreciate its quality and consider it reasonably priced. Regarding skin compatibility, while some customers report it doesn't irritate sensitive skin, others mention it causes stinging sensations.

reviews.author: Patilu
reviews.text:
My mature, sensitive skin and dry eyes don't play well with most beauty products and cosmetics. CeraVe has been a godsend for me. I'm reviewing the night cream here but also mentioning some other CeraVe products to share with other folks who have sensitive skin like mine.


When my go-to extra moisturizing skin cleansing balm was recently discontinued I was bereft, thinking I was not going to find a suitable replacement. However, I now use CeraVe foaming oil face cleanser and this CeraVe moisturizing night cream at night, with satisfactory results. The oil face cleanser removes makeup and leaves my face clean but calm and soft. [Not that tight, stripped-of-oils feeling that I get from so many other products, which unfortunately includes Cerave skin cleansing balm. The cleansing balm took makeup off and didn't irritate my skin but gradually stripped it of natural oil]. Then I apply the CeraVe moisturizing night cream which soothes, moisturizes and helps retain moisture throughout the night and into the next day. I can't emphasize how much of a breakthrough this is for me. Even though the extra moisturizing cleansing balm I previously used at night didn't strip my skin, I really haven't had a suitable product for a long time that could retain  moisture overnight without irritating my skin and eyes. With the CeraVe night cream, my skin stays moisturized until I am ready to clean and prepare my skin for makeup the next day. If I don't do that until noon it doesn't matter because my skin stays moisturized until then.


Since starting the use of the CeraVe cleansing oil and CeraVe night cream, the appearance of my skin has improved. I have my naturally rosy cheeks and  soft, clear skin without either the redness or dullness that have resulted when I used some other facial products.





I round out my skin care routine in the morning, splashing my face with warm water and very gently wiping with soft washcloth soaked in warm water, then applying a skin barrier repair lotion to my face and under eyes before applying a popular tinted CC cream.


[I once used CeraVe's face serum at night with amazing results. My skin was soft and it diminished the look of wrinkles. But my skin became sensitized to it and I can no longer use it. I do highly recommend it for people who do not have skin as ultra sensitive as mine.]


CeraVe also has a Moisturizing Cream for Face and Body that I use on my neck, upper chest and hands. That, too, has been a game changer for me. It's the best product I have found for keeping crepey appearance at bay on my neck and it lasts beyond 24 hours. It also keeps my dry, eczema-prone hands calm and moisturized. I generally just have to use it once a day. [My hands really are sensitive. I use household gloves religiously and use CeraVe's non-foaming Hydrating  Facial Cleanser as a hand soap.]  The moisturized feeling from the CeraVe cream doesn't disappear when I wash my hands. The cream seems to actually treat my hands, not just attempt to hold in moisture.

reviews.author: Stephanie Ann
reviews.text:
Let me just say this upfront: you do not need a $98 cream in a fancy jar to have soft, glowing, younger-looking skin. I’m 54, my skin is dry, and I get asked if I’m in my 30s on the regular. This CeraVe Night Cream is a big part of why.





I know it’s technically marketed for nighttime, but my skin is Sahara-level dry, so I use it during the day too. I layer a tiny bit of castor oil underneath (an aesthetician recommended that combo to me), and my face stays moisturized, calm, and honestly as soft as a baby’s bottom.





What I Love:





• Deep hydration without feeling greasy


It’s thick in the jar but melts right into your skin — perfect under makeup or on its own.





• Gentle, no-fragrance formula


My skin is sensitive, and this doesn’t irritate or break me out at all.





• Peptides + ceramides + hyaluronic acid


Basically all the things every expensive brand brags about… except this one is actually affordable.





• Skin looks smoother and more rested


After a few weeks, my fine lines looked less noticeable and my skin barrier felt stronger.





A quick heads up:





It doesn’t come with an inner seal inside the jar — that’s normal for this product, but good to mention for anyone wondering.





Bottom Line:





This is one of the best moisturizers you can buy at this price point. If you have dry or mature skin, skip the pricey stuff and try this first. Your skin (and your wallet) will thank you.

reviews.author: Jamie Jorgensen
reviews.text:
I've been using CeraVe Skin Renewing Night Cream for a while now, and overall I'm very happy with it. I have psoriasis, so finding a moisturizer that actually helps without irritating my skin can be difficult. Many lotions and creams I've tried in the past cause a burning or stinging sensation when I first apply them, especially on areas where my skin is dry or irritated. This night cream does not do that at all.





The cream feels rich and nourishing, but it absorbs well and doesn't leave my skin feeling greasy. It helps keep my skin soft, smooth, and hydrated throughout the night, and I've noticed that my skin feels much more comfortable and less tight by morning. The combination of ceramides, hyaluronic acid, and peptides seems to work very well for maintaining moisture and supporting my skin barrier.





The only reason I'm giving it 4 stars instead of 5 is because the jar was much smaller than I expected. I was surprised by the size when it arrived, especially considering the price. Since I use moisturizer regularly due to my psoriasis, I know I'll go through it fairly quickly.





Despite the smaller size, I would still recommend this product to anyone looking for a gentle, moisturizing night cream, especially those with sensitive or dry skin. The fact that it keeps my skin soft and doesn't burn when applied makes it stand out from many other moisturizers I've tried.

reviews.author: Many
reviews.text: I purchased this long time ago but waiting until I opened the box and tried it before posting review since I wanted to see a real result. I can say that this night cream suits my skin and it makes my skin hydrated at night. I woke up with good feeling on my face. It doesn't make my skin feel irritated at all while it helps my skin look softer and minimize skin's pore. I keep using it for a month, still feel so good with its consistency and how mild of ingredients while its strength improves my skin texture. The price is also reasonable, I think it will be last for like 6 months. So totally worth it.

reviews.author: Jasminia Jimbo
reviews.text: This night cream arrived in good condition and properly sealed. The container was not damaged, and the product looked fresh. The cream feels smooth and moisturizing without feeling too heavy. It is easy to apply at night and works well as part of a skincare routine. Overall, it arrived safely and was a good product for daily nighttime use.

name: Cetaphil Healthy Renew Night Cream
ingredients: Water, Glycerin, Propanediol, Panthenol, 1,2-Hexandiol, Niacinamide, C13-15 Alkane, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter, Coco Nucifera (Coconut) Oil, Sodium Polyacryloyldimethyl Taurate, Hydrogenated Polydecene, Silica, Tocopheryl Acetate, Vinyl Dimethicone, C14-22 Alcohols, Leontopodium Alpinum Extract, Polyglyceryl-2 Stearate, Polyacrylate-13, C12-20 Alkyl Glucoside, Oryza Sativa (Rice) Lees Extract, Glyceryl Stearate, Stearyl Alcohol, Polyisobutene, Hydrolyzed Soy Flour, Adenosine, Dimethicone/Vinyl Dimehicone Crosspolymer, Butylene Glycol, Trisodium Ethylenediamine Disuccinate, Acacia Senegal Gum, Citric Acid, Agar, Caprylyl Glycol, Polysorbate20, Sodium Benzoate, Sorbitan Isostearatec, Potassium Sorbate, Sodium Hyaluronate, Xanthan Gum, Ethylhexylglycerin, Buddleja Davidii Extract, Thymus Vulgaris (Thyme) Extract

couche3.note: Creme de nuit anti-age (peptides, alt-retinol doux), sans parfum, sensible OK; riche -> prudence peau grasse.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the night cream effective and moisturizing, making their skin smooth and hydrated, with one noting improvements in texture and fine lines. The cream has a thick consistency that's not too heavy, feels luxurious, and customers consider it good value for money. Regarding skin compatibility, while some say it doesn't irritate sensitive skin, others report it makes their face feel itchy. The product receives mixed feedback about skin sensitivity, with some reporting improvements in skin tone while others experienced breakouts.

reviews.author: Julie Adamson
reviews.text:
I’ve been using the entire Cetaphil Healthy Renew line for over a year now, and this night cream has become an essential part of my routine. As someone who can’t tolerate retinol, I was looking for an effective alternative that would help with aging skin concerns without the irritation.





Results I’ve seen:


After consistent use over the past year, I’ve noticed genuine improvements in both my skin texture and fine lines. My skin feels smoother and looks more refined, and the fine lines around my eyes and forehead are visibly reduced. These aren’t overnight results - it took a few months to really see the difference - but the improvements have been steady and lasting.


What I appreciate about this cream:


The formula is gentle enough for nightly use without any of the sensitivity, redness, or peeling I experienced when I tried retinol products in the past. It absorbs well without feeling heavy or greasy, which is important for a night cream since I don’t want it transferring to my pillowcase.


The texture is rich but not overly thick. A little goes a long way, so the jar lasts longer than I initially expected. It layers nicely with the other products in the Healthy Renew line.





Worth noting:


This is a commitment product - you need patience and consistency to see results. If you’re looking for dramatic overnight changes, this isn’t it. But if you want steady, sustainable improvement in skin texture and fine lines without harsh active ingredients, it delivers.





The jar packaging isn’t the most hygienic compared to a pump, though I use a small spatula to keep things clean.





Bottom line:


For anyone who can’t use retinol but still wants anti-aging benefits, this night cream is worth trying. After a year of use, I can confidently say it’s made a real difference in my skin’s appearance and texture. I’ll continue repurchasing this and using it as part of my nighttime routine.

reviews.author: NIceNuzy
reviews.text:
I recently incorporated Cetaphil Healthy Renew Skin Tightening Night Cream into my skincare routine, and I am thrilled with the results. As someone with sensitive skin, I've always been cautious when it comes to anti-aging products. However, this night cream has proven to be a gentle yet highly effective solution.





The standout feature of this night cream is its formula enriched with peptides, providing an excellent alternative to traditional retinol creams. Peptides are known for their ability to stimulate collagen production, which is essential for maintaining youthful and firm skin. This cream harnesses the power of peptides without the potential irritation that can come with retinol-based products.





One of the first things I noticed after using this night cream consistently is the visible reduction in the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. My skin feels smoother and firmer, and the results have been noticeable within just a few weeks of use. It's reassuring to know that I can achieve these benefits without subjecting my sensitive skin to harsh treatments.





The texture of the cream is luxurious and hydrating, and it absorbs quickly into the skin without leaving a heavy or greasy residue. I appreciate that it doesn't cause any redness or irritation, even when used nightly. It's a comforting feeling to wake up with refreshed and rejuvenated skin every morning.





Cetaphil has once again impressed me with their commitment to creating products specifically designed for sensitive skin. This night cream is hypoallergenic, which gives me peace of mind knowing that I won't experience any adverse reactions. It's also fragrance-free, which is another bonus for those of us with sensitive skin.

reviews.author: Kelli H
reviews.text: This is a great product. I have a lot of allergies and chemical sensitivities, and am always cautious when trying new products. I have had no reactions to this product at all. In other reviews, customers remarked on the scent. While it does have a slight unpleasant odor, it is not strong and goes away quickly. I'm sensitive to smells and am unable to wear any perfumes or body sprays. The odor of this product does not bother me at all. Other reviews also stated that the product does not produce results quickly. I disagree. After the first night, I noticed improved hydration, firmness, and a more even skin tone. I am extremely happy with the product and highly recommend it.

reviews.author: Deanna Miller
reviews.text:
I love this product, it’s not greasy at all.


The scent is awful!! It almost smells like bad cheese.


I will continue to use just hold my breath when I apply.

reviews.author: Queen Anne
reviews.text: Texture is nice, but I find the odor a bit off putting.  I think it's a good product but not my favorite.

name: Olay Regenerist Night Recovery Cream
ingredients: Water,Dimethicone,Glycerin,Niacinamide*,Dimethicone Crosspolymer,Polymethylsilsesquioxane,Polyacrylamide,Panthenol**,Polyethylene,Tocopheryl Acetate***,Palmitoyl Pentapeptide-4,Allantoin,Camellia Sinensis Leaf Extract,Sodium PEG-7 Olive Oil Carboxylate,Dimethiconol,Laureth-4,Laureth-7,Disodium EDTA,Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer,Aminomethyl Propanol,Titanium Dioxide,C13-14 Isoparaffin,DMDM Hydantoin,Iodopropynyl

couche3.note: Creme de nuit amino-peptides, sans parfum, anti-rides; texture riche -> prudence peau grasse.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this night cream effective, noting it works wonders throughout the night and shows results within the first week of use. They appreciate its moisturizing properties, particularly for oily skin, and its soft, smooth texture that's not greasy or sticky. Customers praise its value, saying it outperforms higher-priced alternatives, and appreciate that it's fragrance-free.

reviews.author: Azeba
reviews.text:
I love this product. It's become a go-to for me.





The consistency is thick and definitely leaves my face feeling a bit greasy, so I wouldn't recommend trying to use it as a substitute for your daytime face cream.


It spreads evenly and easily, and most of it absorbed into my skin pretty fast.





I did notice that this is supposed to be a 30 day supply, but I did not find myself going through it that fast. I used about one to two pea sized amounts to cover my entire face, and it lasted almost 60 days of daily use.





Due to how greasy/oily it left my skin, if I forgot to thoroughly wash my face FIRST thing in the morning, I did break out more. However, as long I washed my face almost as soon as I got up for the day, I didn't have any acne issues with this product.





My face texture appeared smoother, brighter, and overall more useful while using this product. Highly recommend.

reviews.author: Marily
reviews.text: I love this night cream. As to it stopping my aging, that actually is impossible. One cannot stop the wheels of time. Why did I choose olay?  Mainly because it enters into the dermis quickly and leaves no oil or residue. My skin is soft. I think it has slowed down the raviges of aging and if  your skin is fine and smooth or a rough texture I do believe it does help both.  Trying to stop aging is all still there but shows change in a different way . I have very white, sensitive skin. I believe I have experienced many positive occurrences using the Olay products. About fragrance, it is very nice and subtle. I pick one product to have  fragrance the others are non.

reviews.author: Jessica
reviews.text: I bought this because I use adapalene on my acne and this was recommended to go with it. This product is....strange. I say that because it leaves a weird feeling on my skin after I apply it. The best I can describe it is when you rub 2 pieces of silk together. It's slippery, but not greasy. It's odd. It IS moisturizing and a little bit goes a long way. It's a thick cream and did not cause any breakouts on my acne prone skin, but the feeling it leaves is just strange. I've been using it for 2 weeks and am not disappointed with the product except for that weird feeling. Not sure if I will buy again or try another olay product.

reviews.author: Sparky
reviews.text: I wake up in the morning after using this Night Cream with vibrant, very smooth skin.  I love that Olay has the Fragrance Free option as I have very sensitive skin and this doesn't bother it at all.  I have found this night cream to be very moisturizing and helps the texture of my skin as I aged (I use this nightly).  A little bit goes a long way and is easy to use .... apply this right after washing my face in the evening and it doesn't feel greasy at all.  Would recommend as a nightly face cream.

reviews.author: Jeanine J. Kuhrts
reviews.text: Product is great. However the shipping box it came in is nearly impossible to open. Bad glue. I had to use a strong knife to leverage to open it.

name: Naturium Plant Ceramide Rich Moisture Cream

couche3.note: Creme riche aux ceramides vegetales, barriere; ideale seche/sensible, lourde pour peau grasse.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the moisturizer hydrates dry skin perfectly and has a luxurious, silky texture that feels nice on normal-dry skin. The product receives mixed feedback regarding its greasiness, with some finding it buttery while others say it's too greasy, and its fragrance is virtually scentless. Customers disagree on the value for money, and while some find it good for sensitive skin, others report it stings a little. The product's thickness and consistency receive mixed reviews, with some praising its amazing consistency while others note it separates over time.

reviews.author: Ann Opinion
reviews.text:
I have sensitive skin that tends to immediately react if a product doesn't agree with it. After testing on my neck for a couple days without breaking out, I decided to use it on my face. I had no issues when I woke up in the next morning. My skin felt moisturized and soft. Because my skin leans toward being dry, it will definitely be dry if a moisturizer is ineffective. I like that this product is unscented because my husband and I are both scent sensitive.


I will continue to see how long term use of this product is to determine if I will repurchase. If long time use is consistent with their serums, I'll probably be repurchasing because I love their Niacinamide, vitamin C, hyaluronic and retinol serums. I only gave four stars instead of five because of the smaller product size. But I will say not a lot of product is needed to effectively cover my face and neck.





Addendum:As I stated earlier I have what I would describe as sensitive skin that if not moisturized is dry. I was specifically looking for an unscented moisturizer to use after I used the Naturium retinol serum. Naturium suggested this combination: Niacinamide serum, retinol serum, plant squalene oil and lastly the plant ceramide rich moisture cream.


I was a bit hesitant to use the squalene oil with the cream, but I decided to give it a try.


I can't say enough about this combination.


I don't need much of each, and it leaves my skin incredibly plump, soft, smooth and moisturized when I wake up. I use this combination 3x/ week and an alternate less complex routine on opposite nights. I would never go through the combination of steps if it wasn't so surprisingly effective.

reviews.author: isabelle
reviews.text:
I’ve been obsessed with Naturium ever since my daughter introduced me to their shower gel, and this Plant Ceramide Rich Moisture Cream is another fantastic product. It’s not greasy, feels amazing on the skin, and has a nice, clean scent. I use it daily, and my skin absolutely loves it.


I also appreciate how easy it is to grab on Amazon—fast, convenient, and always in stock. Overall, another Naturium product I’m happily adding to my routine!

reviews.author: rebecca l.
reviews.text: Love this product line and this cream is no exception.  It's great for dry skin and it's vegan.  I'm 66, have dry skin, rosacea and I'm allergic to everything.  This is highly moisturizing and fragrance free.  I use it as a night cream.  Terrific product, highly concentrated so lasts a long time.

reviews.author: MB
reviews.text: Very moisturizing, doesn’t feel heavy. It makes my 50+ skin feel plump and supple. I layer it with the peptide serum.

reviews.author: Amazon Customer
reviews.text: Great night time moisturizer.  Doesn't clog my face. Good price.

name: CeraVe Oil Control Moisturizing Gel-Cream
ingredients: Aqua/Water/Eau, Niacinamide, Glycerin, Cetearyl Isononanoate, C14-22 Alcohols, Isopropyl Myristate, Zea Mays Starch/Corn Starch, Ceramide Np, Ceramide Ap, Ceramide Eop, Carbomer, Cetearyl Alcohol, Behentrimonium Methosulfate, Triethyl Citrate, Silica, Sodium Hydroxide, Sodium Hyaluronate, Sodium Lauroyl Lactylate, Cholesterol, Phenoxyethanol, Citric Acid, Caprylyl Glycol, Trisodium Ethylenediamine Disuccinate, Xanthan Gum, Phytosphingosine, Polyacrylate Crosspolymer-6, Benzoic Acid, C12-20 Alkyl Glucoside (Code F.I.L. N70040237/1).

couche3.note: Gel-creme controle sebum (niacinamide), sans parfum, non comedogene; ideale grasse/mixte le soir, trop legere pour tres seche.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the moisturizer effective for oily skin, keeping it hydrated and fresh-looking, while appreciating its lightweight, soft texture, and good value. Moreover, the product absorbs instantly, with one customer noting it leaves a matte finish. However, experiences with oiliness are mixed - while some report their skin isn't super oily anymore, others mention their face becomes full of oil. Additionally, the product receives mixed feedback regarding skin irritation, with some finding it good for sensitive skin while others report stinging sensations. The oil control aspect is also mixed, with some finding it effective while others say it doesn't work at all.

reviews.author: Wildflower
reviews.text:
Believe the great reviews, it really is that good. At 42 I'd always thought by now my skin would FINALLY stop being oily, but nope, genetics determined it's for life apparently. I'm just as crazy oily at this age as ever. *sigh*





Obviously, as we get older though, minimizing fine lines and wrinkles become more of a priority, so I don't love using extremely drying or harsh products for the oil issue either.





Normally I always carry with me oil blotting papers (which I highly recommend). I use those within 2 hours of doing my makeup and that's even when I've already used an oil absorbing primer, etc. Then I continue to have to use blotting papers to touch up throughout the day, every couple of hours or so. I'm THAT freaking oily. It's such a pain and a little embarrassing to get shiny so fast around others, as I'm sure many reading this can relate to, no doubt.





Suffice it to say that I've tried tons, and I mean TONS, of oil absorbing products over the years. While some work a bit better than others (by the way, if you're looking for a matte primer that does indeed control oil longer, I'd suggest trying Rimmel's Stay Matte in the purple and white tube), but none of the primers or moisturizers or treatment creams I've tried ever really worked wonders for how quickly my face produces oil. So I'd basically given up on the hope that any product was going to really knock it out of the park for my particular needs.





Enter CeraVe oil control moisturizer.





I'm shocked. Totally shocked. The first time I put it on, I made sure my face was super clean and I applied nothing else but this product. I checked the time so I could gauge how long the matte effect lasted.





For starters, this moisturizer feels amazing! Smooth and hydrating, your skin drinks it in immediately, and you're left with an instant matte yet moisturized and baby-smooth feel that leaves you wanting to touch your face just to enjoy it. You don't have to wait forever for it to dry and it plays beautifully under makeup without pilling.





But the real shock occurred when I touched my face a little over two hours after application. AND IT WAS


STILL DRY. No oil. My jaw dropped.





No other products I've tried have done this for me.





Needless to say, I'm going to be stockpiling this stuff!!!! It's so perfect in nearly every way. I freaking love it. I knew CeraVe was a good brand, but wow, they nailed it with this one!

reviews.author: Betiana jean pierre
reviews.text:
As someone with oily and sensitive skin, finding a moisturizer that hydrates without adding shine is always a challenge. This CeraVe Oil Control Gel-Cream is fantastic! The gel-cream texture is incredibly light, absorbs instantly, and feels refreshing. It provides essential moisture without any heavy or greasy feeling, and it creates a lovely matte base for makeup.


​I definitely noticed a reduction in midday shine compared to other moisturizers I've used. I gave it 4 stars instead of 5 because I wished the oil control effect lasted slightly longer into the evening. However, it's a staple in my morning routine and a very effective, non-comedogenic option. I highly recommend it for daily use on oily or combination skin

reviews.author: Deborah S.
reviews.text: Great little lotion, not too expensive and seems like it will last a long time. Only need to use a little bit of it and it's such an easy to use bottle with easy application. I have very oily skin and so far this seems to be very compatible with my skin. It hydrates without making my oily skin worse. No irritation, it seems like a good, simple, fragrance free face lotion.

reviews.author: Tiann
reviews.text: I’ve always struggled with a shiny T-zone, but this gel-cream is a game-changer. It feels so lightweight and absorbs almost instantly. I love the oil-absorbing technology because it keeps my skin looking fresh and matte without feeling dry. If you have combination to oily skin, you need to try this!

reviews.author: Michelle
reviews.text: This helps control the oil on my face for makeup application.  It works really well

couche3.note: Creme de nuit intense apaisante (ex-Toleriane Ultra), peau reactive; riche -> prudence grasse.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the moisturizer super hydrating and appreciate its smooth, supple texture and lightweight feel. The product receives mixed feedback regarding skin sensitivity - while some report no irritation, others experience burning sensations. Customers disagree on whether it's non-greasy, with some finding it sticky. The fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some appreciating the lack of scent while others find it funky. Skin reactions are also mixed, with some customers reporting cleared redness while others experience breakouts.

reviews.author: DKH
reviews.text:
LOVE IT! PLEASE DON'T EVER STOP MAKING THIS HOLY GRAIL!!!


I have tried sooooo many brands and moisturizers trying to find one that would agree with my sensitive skin and aggravated red cheeks, and just nothing worked. Not even the ones that promise to work on sensitive skin!


I found I was able to use La Roche-Posay Tolerance Double Repair Face Moisturizer and so I have been using it daily for a while and continued to look for something maybe for night that would be a bit richer for my dry red cheeks.


I also tried La Roche-Posay Tolerance ROSALIAC AR for under makeup trying to help out to hide my red cheeks. I found that it helped to hide them a bit, (it's green) but really, not a lot. I also found it was actually a bit drying and I had to use it with the Double Repair, and then it wanted to pill under the makeup. So really, the ROSALIAC AR, as much as I tried over and under the other moisturizer, it just is a strong NO HELP.


SOOOOO, I finally tried the La Roche-Posay Tolerance DERMALLERGO NIGHT Ultra Soothing Repair Moisturizer last night for the first time.


First, it really is a small bottle; 1.35 fluid ounces. The good news is that it comes out in a small measured pump, and it takes very, very little. A LITTLE REALLY GOES A LONG WAY! It states it's gentle enough to even use on eyes, and I did. I used no other serums or creams, I used that alone for the night. I found as it began to soak into my skin, it has a nice feel to it, but also felt kinda sticky like others have said. However, when I got up this morning, zero sticky feeling to my skin; and my skin has an amazing hydrated feeling to it, even my cheeks (which never happens!) - and lo and behold... MY CHEEKS HAVE ZERO RED TO THEM! My cheeks that are forever aggravated and dry are soft, normal colored skin, and actually feel good to the touch, not their normal soreness to them.


Sooooo, if I have to buy this every month, I will! I am so grateful to have finally found a moisturizer that really, REALLY was kind to my face; moisturized wonderful and especially my dry sensitive tender cheeks!


I'm in!!!

reviews.author: Yakelin Hernandez
reviews.text:
I have very sensitive skin and struggle with rosacea, so finding a product that actually calms my skin instead of irritating it is extremely difficult. After trying countless creams and serums, this La Roche-Posay Toleriane Dermallergo Night moisturizer has become one of the few products that truly delivers noticeable results.





From the very first days of using it, I felt my skin becoming more balanced and less reactive. The formula is incredibly gentle, yet deeply hydrating. It absorbs quickly without leaving any greasy residue, and it gives my skin an instant soothing feeling. What impressed me the most is how effectively it reduces redness. I wake up with my skin looking calmer, more even-toned, and without the hot, irritated flare-ups I used to experience almost daily.





This cream has become a lifesaver during my rosacea flare days. It doesn’t sting, it doesn’t cause breakouts, and it doesn’t trigger any irritation — which is rare for me. Instead, it strengthens my skin barrier and keeps my face feeling comfortable throughout the night. I’ve also noticed that my makeup applies more smoothly in the morning because my skin is not inflamed or dry.





If you have sensitive skin or suffer from rosacea, I genuinely recommend giving this product a try. It has brought me relief, confidence, and consistency in my skincare routine. For me, this moisturizer has been a true game changer.

reviews.author: kayliep7
reviews.text:
skin type: i have dry sensitive skin.





Pros:


- pump dispenser is easy to use and more sanitary compared to jar lotions.


- Pump dispenser has a twist lock so it won’t leak or explode if it’s in a bag.


- “gel cream” is thick but not sticky and glides on well.


- fragrance free/non scented makes it good for sensitive skin.


- no preservatives or parabens





Cons:


- price for the amount of lotion: i bought it at $29.99, which is $22.21 per fluid ounce. that is super expensive compared to other lotions that are just a few dollars per fluid ounce.


- frequency of purchasing: i went through one of these in about a month. so that would be roughly $360 (not including tax) just for facial lotion if i continued at this rate for the next year.


- each pump dispenses a very small amount of lotion. i’d compare it to less than a dime size amount (see picture). i need at least five pumps to throughly moisturize my face.





although i have found more pros than cons, i’m not sure if it is sustainable to continue buying this lotion given the price. i have bought my second bottle, but will likely be searching for an equally effective cheaper alternative.

reviews.author: Just my opinion
reviews.text:
I like that it absorbs quickly so I’m not sitting up until late at night before I go to bed and lay my head on my pillow after I put my CPAP mask on.





You only need a little bit to cover your face and neck - about the same or a little more than a creamy serum.  My skin still feels hydrated without feeling oily or greasy.  It is thin to a little medium weight.





The quality is good and just what I was looking for so I can go to bed earlier than when I wear thick and oily night creams or light night creams that don’t absorb in my skin.





This is a bottle a little on the smaller side but it doesn’t make a difference to me because I only use about 4-5 small pumps to cover my face and neck.  Most night creams of this value are around $50 and you get 2 of these bottles for the price of 1 of those bottles because this one is about $25.





It’s also non-comedogenic so your pores are much less likely to get clogged, get acne, or get fleshy colored bumps on your skin.  It’s good for sensitive skin too.





So for all these reasons, I think it’s a good value for the money charged.  I recommend this product.

reviews.author: Patricia Johnson
reviews.text: this stuff is wow. I like the way it feels

couche3.note: Hydratant peptides leger, sans parfum, anti-rides doux; bien pour grasse/mixte, un peu juste pour tres seche.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective at tackling dry skin and appreciate its light, smooth texture that absorbs quickly. They consider it good value for money and like how it feels on their face. The fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some customers noting a strong chemical smell. Several customers report receiving the product under half full.

reviews.author: RCGirl
reviews.text: This moisturizer really delivers the goods.  I use it both morning and night.  It's usually my last step in both my morning and evening routines.  It's hydrates and gives the skin all those good anti aging peptides.  A great price, too.

reviews.author: Laura C
reviews.text: I love how soft this moisturizer makes me skin feel. There’s no scent, it isn’t greasy at all, and it’s a great size for cost. I have incredibly sensitive skin and this does not irritate my skin at all.

reviews.author: Natalia Fajardo
reviews.text: I really like this product, it’s lightweight, absorbs quickly, leaves my skin looking healthy. I will buy it again

reviews.author: Matie Hampton
reviews.text: Great product! I love it!

reviews.author: Lindy
reviews.text: Affordable and works well

name: Naturium Intense Overnight Sleeping Cream
ingredients: Aqua, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride, Glycerin, Caprylic/Capric/Myristic/Stearic Triglyceride, Cetearyl Alcohol, Squalane, Propanediol, Glyceryl Stearate, Polymethyl Methacrylate, Terminalia Ferdinandiana Fruit Extract, Ammonium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/VP Copolymer, Caprylyl Glycol, Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer, Tremella Fuciformis Sporocarp Extract, Glycolipids, Sodium Hyaluronate, Glycine Soja Sterols, Sodium Hydroxide, Phenoxyethanol, Betaine, Potassium Sorbate, Polysorbate 20, Hexylene Glycol.

couche3.note: Creme de nuit overnight (ceramides+peptides), hydratation intense; ideale seche, riche pour grasse.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the moisturizer provides solid overnight hydration and leaves skin feeling soft, with one noting it's particularly effective for cold, dry winter air. The cream receives positive feedback for its quality and fragrance, being unscented. However, customers disagree on the consistency, with some finding it silky while others say it's not creamy. Moreover, experiences with skin irritation are mixed, with some reporting no breakouts while others experienced strong burning sensations. Additionally, opinions on absorption are divided, with some saying it absorbs well while others report it doesn't absorb at all.

reviews.author: Kimberly Cabe
reviews.text:
I think this product, and this brand overall, is the best thing that's ever happened to my skin.  I have dry skin overall, but can have some T-zone oiliness in the summer thanks to Deep South humidity.  I am acne-prone if my skin is occluded overnight.  For example, if I sleep with my satin hair bonnet pulled too low on my forehead, I will wake up with a blemish where it was resting all night.  But, what I struggle with the most is dryness and dullness.  This overnight cream has never made me break out, but I do wake up with luxuriously hydrated and glowing skin. I haven't been using it long enough to see if it helps with firmness of the skin, but will update later.





I am sensitive to fragrances and this has none.  It has a light, natural smell that is hardly noticeable. It goes on smoothly and absorbs beautifully.  At night, I use a cleansing balm, wipe my face with the First Aid Beauty Radiance pads (exfoliation), apply Naturium Multi-Peptide serum, then the overnight cream.  In the morning, I wipe with the exfoliation pads, apply Multi-Peptide serum, then Naturium Multi-Peptide moisturizer.





I am now a loyal Naturium customer for life.

reviews.author: Tracy
reviews.text:
The Naturium Intense Overnight Sleeping Cream is a dream come true! I was looking for an overnight moisturizer that could deliver serious hydration and anti-aging benefits, and this one has exceeded my expectations. It has a rich, creamy texture that feels luxurious going on, but it absorbs beautifully without feeling heavy or greasy.





Each morning, my skin feels so much softer, smoother, and has a healthy glow—like I just had a spa treatment! Fine lines look softened, and my skin feels plumper and more refreshed. I also appreciate that this cream doesn’t clog my pores or cause breakouts, which is a huge plus for someone with combination skin.





The jumbo size is a fantastic value, too. It feels like it’ll last a long time, making it a great investment in my skincare routine. Highly recommend this for anyone looking to amp up their hydration game and wake up with radiant, youthful skin!

reviews.author: Robin in FL
reviews.text:
I wish this came with a little scoop like some jars do - the texture is so odd, it's hard to get out of the jar and doesn't want to spread out.  Seems to do a good job of keeping the tretinoin from drying my skin too much overnight but I don't enjoy the texture.  It's almost like there is wax in there?  I can't quite describe it as a cream, it's not creamy.  No irritation, no problems with it, not breaking me out and does the job as far as I can tell but I don't think I would repurchase.





Their body lotion, on the other hand - my husband stole it from me and he doesn't even like lotion, it's incredible, unscented, feels like nothing yet does moisturize, skin doesn't dry out.  Maybe the best one I have ever tried.

reviews.author: Missy Gibbs
reviews.text: Do not hesitate to buy this. I have spent hundreds of dollars on night crèmes that were nothing like they said they were and did nothing for my skin. This is super thick, perfect for nighttime and my skin has never looked or felt better. I’m stunned at the price, I have wasted a LOT of money, wish I could have found sooner. I do have extremely dry skin and this plumped it right up. HIGHLY RECOMMEND!!!

reviews.author: Devon
reviews.text: So happy with the Naturium Intense Overnight Sleeping Cream. Not only does it keep my skin feeling and looking extremely soft and even but it feels so good knowing that it's made with such clean ingredients! The consistency is non-greasy which is nice too. I find that rubbing a small dab of the cream between my fingers then applying to my face works really well for easy coverage.

name: elf Holy Hydration Nourishing Night Cream
ingredients: Water (Aqua), Dimethicone, Butylene Glycol, Hydrogenated Polyisobutene, Ethylhexyl Palmitate, Glycerin, Simmondisa Chinensis (Jojoba) Seed Oil, Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter, Cetearyl Olivate, Dimethiconol, Sorbitan Olivate, Stearic Acid, Cetyl Alcohol, Aloe Barbadensis (Aloe Vera) Leaf Extract, Cucumis Sativus (Cucumber) Fruit Extract, Vaccinium Myrtillus (Bilberry) Fruit/Leaf Extract, Saccharum Officinarum (Sugar Cane) Extract, Citrus Medica Limonum (Lemon) Fruit Extract, Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Fruit Extract, Acer Saccharum (Sugar Maple) Extract, Tocopheryl Acetate, Phenoxyethanol, Caprylyl Glycol, Tromethamine, Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer, Sodium Dehydroacetate, Xanthan Gum, Sodium Hyaluronate, Disodium EDTA

couche3.note: Creme de nuit nourrissante (karite, AH), eco; polyvalente, douce.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective at solving dry skin problems and appreciate its smooth, non-greasy texture that leaves faces feeling soft and clean. The cream absorbs easily into the skin, and customers consider it good value for money with a very mild scent. Regarding skin sensitivity, while some customers find it fabulous for sensitive skin, others report burning sensations.

reviews.author: S. B. Simkins
reviews.text:
This stuff is great! I'm buying my 3rd jar. I have super dry skin and i'm using 0.025% tretinoin and even though it's mild, it really dries me out. I wanted an inexpensive night cream to wear over my tretinoin which is expensive.


Even the creamiest expensive moisturizers don't for my skin, they leave it greasy yet rough. It smells nice, not overpowering or perfumey.  Holy hydration night cream has a creamy, gel-like texture; it's lightweight and absorbs well. I even wear it during the day!





My skin is soft, supple, and hydrated. I wake up every morning looking beautiful.





I do wish there was a refill for the jar because plastic waste is destroying our planet. This jar seems to be bigger on the outside than on the inside--meaning there is a lot of plastic in this jar.

reviews.author: TAC
reviews.text: At age 65, and after trying dozens of night creams- only to be disappointed time and time again- this product did not disappoint!! It feels so good when I apply it to my face before bed, nice and cooling. My skin, which is terribly dry, soaks up the moisturizer.  It is not greasy, which is a huge plus. My skin looks and feels so soft and plump... or should I say hydrated. Finally, a great product at a very affordable price. PTL.

reviews.author: Laurin Brannon
reviews.text: I have tried all of ELFS hydrating moisturizers and this is the best onee!!! I have extremely dry skin and it just drinks this in. For a night cream it is very lightweight and actually does very well under makeup.! I give it a 4 because it is supposed to be a nightcream and super hydrating but I find it to be more lightweight and will actually do 2 uses before bed. ELF needs some new products!

reviews.author: Sherry
reviews.text: Best product I have used. Non greasy formula and can be used day and night. Very low odor and can be worn under foundation without an issue.

reviews.author: le
reviews.text: Moisture, and no strong smell.

name: LOreal Age Perfect Collagen Night
ingredients: Aqua / Water / Eau, Dimethicone, Glycerin, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride, Octyldodecanol, Niacinamide, Myristyl Myristate, Butyrospermum Parkii Butter / Shea Butter, Stearic Acid, Palmitic Acid, Ammonium Polyacryloyldimethyl Taurate, Cera Alba / Beeswax / Cire Dabeille, Glyceryl Stearate, Peg-100 Stearate, Ethylhexyl Palmitate, Capryloyl Salicylic Acid, Palmitoyl Tetrapeptide-7, Palmitoyl Tripeptide-1, Pentaerythrityl Tetra-Di-T-Butyl Hydroxyhydrocinnamate, Sodium Hydroxide, Calcium Pantothenate, Cetyl Alcohol, Stearyl Alcohol, Silica, Aluminum Starch Octenylsuccinate, Butylene Glycol, Caprylyl Glycol, Carbomer, Citric Acid, Myristic Acid, Peg-20 Stearate, Polysorbate 20, Sodium Lactate, Citronellol, Limonene, Phenoxyethanol, Parfum / Fragrance F.I.L. # T2778833/2

couche3.note: Creme de nuit collagene/peptides, fermete mature; parfumee -> prudence sensible, riche -> prudence grasse.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find the moisturizer effective at preventing dryness and improving skin appearance, making it look better in the morning with brighter and more even tone. The texture is creamy and soft, and customers consider it reasonably priced. However, the fragrance receives mixed reactions, with some finding it lovely while others note a strong perfume smell. Moreover, the consistency and greasiness receive mixed feedback, with some appreciating its thick luxurious feel while others find it too thick and greasy. Additionally, several customers report skin irritation, including burning sensations and rashes.

reviews.author: Ann
reviews.text: Received the L’Oreal in perfect condition. It is a night cream and only takes a small amount and the small amount leaves the face greasy, but soft feeling skin. I have used this face cream some time ago and don’t remember it being as greasy with only a tiny amount. I’ve only used this jar three times and I’m not happy with it at all. I also used it on my dry hands and it has helped the dryness a lot, but too greasy for me and my dry face. It’s not a bad product and every store sells it too. Virtually no smell with it.  It is a nice product, but this time, it’s just not for me. Maybe my skin has changed?

reviews.author: RoyalSmash
reviews.text: The face lotion I’ve been using at night was discontinued so I’ve been on the hunt for a new one. In my late 30s and have somewhat reactive skin so a lot of creams make me break out. Two weeks into this one, no issues. It’s very moisturizing and has a thick consistency. Only complaint is it smells like what I’d imagine my grandma’s face cream to smell like. VERY scented and perfumed. Not a deal breaker but they need to tone down the aroma for me to give it 5 stars. My husband even commented on how strongly it smelled.

reviews.author: Kim DeBusk
reviews.text: I love this night cream  I think is smells so good and makes my skin feel so soft.  It moisturizes my face while I sleep  and this last a long time.  You don't need to use too much and so easy before bed to apply.

reviews.author: Jackie Lewandowski
reviews.text: Great ,great make my skin say I love this cream every time I use it. Never miss a night and am I have am and pm

reviews.author: lyssa  koscher
reviews.text: I use it during the day time. It’s not greasy and it’ feels good  on dry skin.

name: Cetaphil Rich Hydrating Night Cream
ingredients: Water, Hydrogenated Polydecene, Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride, Dimethicone, Glycerin, Butylene Glycol, Cetearyl Olivate, Sorbitan Olivate, Cyclopentasiloxane, Marrubium Vulgare Extract, Hydrolyzed Hyaluronic Acid, Olea Europaea (Olive) Fruit Oil, Tocopheryl Acetate (Vitamin E), Ascorbyl Palmitate (Vitamin C), Leuconostoc/Radish Root Ferment Filtrate, Phospholipids, Isopropyl Lauroyl Sarcosinate, Cetearyl Alcohol, Sodium Polyacrylate, 1,2-Hexanediol, Caprylyl Glycol, Polymethylsilsesquioxane, Isohexadecane, Glyceryl Stearate, PEG-100 Stearate, Cetearyl Methicone, Phenoxyethanol, PEG-40 Stearate, Steareth-2, Steareth-21, Retinyl Palmitate, Linoleic Acid, Lycopene

couche3.note: Creme de nuit riche a l AH, sans parfum, peau seche a tres seche/sensible; lourde pour grasse.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this night cream effective, noting it works wonders overnight and is particularly good for dry skin. The texture receives positive feedback, with customers describing it as silky and soft, and they appreciate that it doesn't irritate sensitive skin. The consistency and value for money receive mixed reviews - while some praise its thick body cream texture, others find it watery and grainy, and while some consider it a great product for the money, others say it's not worth the price. Customers disagree on whether it leaves a greasy feeling, with some finding it non-greasy while others report a musty smell.

reviews.author: darla reynolds
reviews.text: Best out there. This is the one to buy if you want hydrated skin. This does not cost an arm and a leg to buy.  Living in Arizona where sometimes the humidity is 10 %....my skin is still glowing . I am a fair skinned blond who can not handle heavy parfum or  chemicals in skin care products. This has neither.  Can not say enough about this one. I buy it on Amazon because I can buy several at a time. The store never seems  to have more that two on the shelf.  No wonder. This is  the perfect facial moisturizer hands down.

reviews.author: rae
reviews.text: This isn’t the thickest cream so as someone with dry skin I use it during the day in winter. It is hydrating enough for that but I like something thicker for night. Feels nice on the skin and can be used near the eyes without irritation. I wish you got more product for the price. I do think there are better night creams that are more moisturizing and you get more for the price.

reviews.author: HarperLeeFan
reviews.text: I use this at night after cleansing my face. It feels wonderful going on and absorbs well without being sticky or greasy.

reviews.author: eve mcginn
reviews.text: The best night cream for older women

reviews.author: Alejandra
reviews.text: Very good moisturizer

name: Neutrogena Hydro Boost Water Cream
ingredients: Water, Glycerin, Isopropyl Palmitate, Cetyl Alcohol, Cetearyl Olivate, Sorbitan Olivate, C10-30 Cholesterol/Lanosterol Esters, Ceramide NP, Stearic Acid, Palmitic Acid, Sodium Hyaluronate, Serine, Glycine, Alanine, Threonine, Arginine, Proline, Lysine HCI, Glutamic Acid, Sodium PCA, PCA, | Sodium Lactate, Urea, Sodium Chloride, Citric Acid, Betaine, | Allantoin, Fructose, Maltose, Trehalose, Glucose, Pentylene Glycol, Dimethicone, Polyacrylate Crosspolymer-6, Ethylhexylglycerin, Caprylyl Glycol, 1,2-Hexanediol, Tropolone, Sodium Hydroxide

couche3.note: Water cream a l AH, sensible/legere; ideale grasse/mixte, juste pour tres seche.
couche3.customers_say: Customers find this moisturizer effective, providing all-day hydration and working well for dry and sensitive skin. The cream is lightweight, silky, and absorbs quickly into the skin, while being non-greasy and fragrance-free. They appreciate its value, noting it's cheaper than Clinique but works well, and report no skin irritation.

reviews.author: VG
reviews.text:
Ok, so full disclosure—I have spent so much money on skincare products and tried all the big and expensive names. Now, I won't call out any specifics but most of them have led me to break-out, get greased up beyond belief, or just plain old smell weird. Incoming Neutrogena Hydro Boost, my pride and comfortably affordable joy. I don't know why or how it works so well but it just does.





Keep in mind, the only downside is a slight fragrance so it is definitely not "fragrance-free". All this in mind, for my dry-in-the-winter/normal skin, this is the only moisturizer that 1. absorbs into my skin right away, 2. doesn't feel greasy, 3. and is so affordable that you can buy bulk on Amazon without declaring bankruptcy faster than Greece. I love it.

reviews.author: DA
reviews.text: I had been looking forever for something to use on my very very dry and sensitive skin during this last winter when my dermatologist recommended this cream. She gave me samples and omg, what a game changer! This is the first face cream that hydrates my skin all day, no flaking midday!! Its also so nice for my sensitive skin, it's the only cream I trust around my eye area because of how gentle it is. Highly recommend!!

reviews.author: Placeholder
reviews.text: I absolutely love this face cream. I have used it for a couple years now and have no desire to choose something else. I love the Hydro boost aspect. It does not need to be rubbed in. Just put a little bit on the skin and let it soak in. Super lightweight not heavy, not greasy and fragrance free

reviews.author: Quiltmaker
reviews.text: I love the feel of this skin cream and it leaves my facefeeling good - no stickiness, no odor - just a comfortable and moisturizing feel.  I would recommend this cream.  And a little bit goes a long way so it's lasting longer than I expcted from the size of the jar.

reviews.author: Mermaid88
reviews.text: My dermatologist recommended this. He said it's a great product for the ingredients and price. I love how it feels on my face. Lightweight feel and moisturizing. Great product.

name: Aveeno Calm + Restore Oat Gel Moisturizer
